title: [bug] current-adapter return type: impl returns map, spec says keyword (needs Mike-decision)
description:
## Direct semantic mismatch, observable to consumers

`(current-adapter)` impl returns the full adapter spec map. Spec 006 §965-969 and API.md row 565 both say it returns a discriminator keyword (`:reagent` / `:plain-atom` / `:custom`).

Tests assert the map contract (`(identical? plain-atom/adapter (adapter/current-adapter))`) — so the spec is what's misaligned, not the impl behaviour, but the surface contract needs reconciliation.

## Needs Mike's decision

Two roads:

- **(a) Keep impl's map form (more useful for tools) and update Spec 006 §965-969 + API.md row 565 to publish the map shape.**
- **(b) Add a discriminator keyword (`:adapter-kind`) inside the adapter spec map and project that for `current-adapter`. Keeps the spec's keyword-shape return type but lets tools that need fn-handles reach for `current-adapter-spec` or similar.**

The current impl shape is more useful for tools that need to call adapter fns; the spec shape is more useful for "branch on host" predicate code.

## Sites

- `implementation/core/src/re_frame/substrate/adapter.cljc` L49-52 — the impl returns the spec map
- `implementation/core/test/.../boot_test.clj` L74, L91 — tests assert with `identical?` against the spec-map var
- `spec/006-ReactiveSubstrate.md` L965-969 — claims keyword return
- `spec/API.md` row 565 — mirrors the keyword-return claim

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-spec-impl-alignment-2026-05-13.md` §Finding 3.2
notes: PR #627: current-adapter now returns :kind keyword per Spec 006; new current-adapter-spec returns the map. Six adapter specs gained :kind; route-hook! migrated; specs + tests updated. Awaiting review.
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] frame-meta returns nested shape; spec says flat (needs Mike-decision)
description:
## Direct semantic mismatch, observable to consumers

`(frame-meta id)` impl returns `{:id :config :lifecycle}` (nested); both Spec 002 worked example (L268-273) and Spec-Schemas `:rf/frame-meta` (L1823-1839) require a flat effective-metadata shape with `:preset`/`:fx-overrides`/`:drain-depth`/`:doc`/`:tags`/`:url-bound?`/`:platform`/`:on-error`/etc. at the top level.

Tools that read `frame-meta` per Tool-Pair (Causa, Story, 10x successors) are seeing a different shape than the schema describes. The discrepancy needs to be resolved before consuming code stabilises.

## Needs Mike's decision

Two roads:

- **(a) Preferred — Spec wins.** Flatten the impl in `frame.cljc:frame-meta` to produce the canonical `:rf/frame-meta` shape per Spec-Schemas. Also fix call sites that currently read `(:config ...)` / `(:lifecycle ...)` from the return value. The flat shape is more ergonomic for tools and matches Spec 002's user-facing prose.
- **(b) Impl wins.** Update Spec-Schemas `:rf/frame-meta` definition and Spec 002 worked example to publish the nested shape. Less work but loses ergonomics.

Sweep recommends (a).

## Sites

- `implementation/core/src/re_frame/frame.cljc` L63-70 — the impl
- `spec/002-Frames.md` L268-273 — the spec example showing flat shape
- `spec/Spec-Schemas.md` L1823-1839 — the `:rf/frame-meta` schema (flat shape)

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-spec-impl-alignment-2026-05-13.md` §Finding 3.1 / §Finding 5.1
notes: PR #625: flat shape per Spec-Schemas (Mike's path (a)). frame-meta merges :id + :config + :lifecycle. 7 caller sites rewritten; round-trip + new preset-expansion-flat-shape tests pin the canonical shape. JVM (core 355/ssr 31/machines 119/story 259) + CLJS (1365) suites all green.
close_reason: Closed

title: [conformance] Reconcile fixture :actor/spawn vs spec :actor/spawn-destroy vocabulary (needs Mike-decision)
description:
## Direct AI-implementability blocker

The spec corpus and the conformance fixture corpus DISAGREE on the actor capability vocabulary, which means an AI port author cannot reliably claim conformance.

### Spec says

The authoritative capability list is enumerated three times — all three name `:actor/spawn-destroy`, `:actor/own-state`, `:actor/cross-actor-fx`:

- `spec/005-StateMachines.md:2828` — "Imperative spawn / destroy" row, manifest example `#{:actor/spawn-destroy ...}`
- `spec/Implementor-Checklist.md:470` — actor axis enumeration `(:actor/own-state, :actor/spawn-destroy, :actor/cross-actor-fx, :actor/invoke, :actor/spawn-and-join, :actor/system-id)`
- `spec/conformance/README.md:148` — same list

### Fixtures use

14 fixtures tag with `:actor/spawn` — none use `:actor/spawn-destroy`, `:actor/own-state`, or `:actor/cross-actor-fx`. The README's worked example also shows `:core/trace` and `:core/frame` capability tags but no fixtures carry those either.

### Why it bites AI one-shot

An AI implementor following 005 + Implementor-Checklist declares `:actor/spawn-destroy` in its harness manifest. The harness then filters fixtures via `(subset? fixture-capabilities claimed-capabilities)`. Since `:actor/spawn` is not in the claimed set, ALL 14 spawn fixtures are skipped as "not exercised". Conversely a port that declares `:actor/spawn` matching the fixtures has its claim diverge from the spec's vocabulary.

This is the canonical SA-6 violation under SPEC-AUTHORING — harness MUST honour the claim.

### Affected fixtures

`cross-actor-send`, `destroy-clears-snapshot`, `destroy-machine-clears-system-id-index`, `invoke-all-{join-all-completes, join-any-fails-cancels, n-of-cancels-extras}`, `invoke-spawn-on-entry-destroy-on-exit`, `machine-actor-isolation`, `spawn-{from-action, on-spawn-callback, tracked-without-data-pending, with-system-id-then-lookup-resolves, without-system-id-leaves-index-empty}`, `system-id-collision-warns-and-rebinds`.

## Proposed fix

Pick one vocabulary as canonical and align the other:

- **(a) Preferred:** rename all fixture tags `:actor/spawn` → `:actor/spawn-destroy` to match the spec's normative capability list. The spec is the contract.
- **(b) Alternative:** reword 005 / Implementor-Checklist / conformance/README.md to use `:actor/spawn`.

Also reconcile the README example's `:core/trace` and `:core/frame` mentions — either fix the README example, or add those tags to appropriate fixtures.

## Needs Mike's decision

Pick (a) spec-wins or (b) fixtures-win.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-spec-completeness-2026-05-13.md` §Bucket 1 / Finding 1.1 — flagged HIGH, the only P0 in stream 4.
notes: PR #624 (rename + claim-set updates) + follow-on bead rf2-3pnob (core/trace + core/frame tagging)
close_reason: Closed

title: [skill] re-frame-pair2 README: fix stale paths + missing MCP pointer + install-path contradiction
description:
## Scope

`skills/re-frame-pair2/README.md` carries multiple staleness defects flagged by the readmes-currency sweep:

### Problem 1 — wrong spec paths AND wrong branch

Three URLs use the OLD `docs/specification/` path and the OLD `master` branch:

- `https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/blob/master/docs/specification/Tool-Pair.md`
- `https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/blob/master/docs/specification/009-Instrumentation.md`

Real paths: `spec/Tool-Pair.md` and `spec/009-Instrumentation.md`. Real branch: `main` (not `master`). Three occurrences.

### Problem 2 — missing MCP-server pointer

The skill README presents the babashka-based ops dispatcher (`scripts/ops.clj` + thin shell shims) as the only delivery mechanism. The structural successor — the MCP server at `tools/pair2-mcp/` — is documented as a back-compat wrapper there, but `skills/re-frame-pair2/README.md` never mentions it. A reader landing on the skill README would not know the MCP path exists.

### Problem 3 — install-path contradiction

"There is no separate npm package or plugin registry entry — clone re-frame2 and reference the skill from `skills/re-frame-pair2/`." This is true for the skill itself but reads as if there's no npm path at all; the MCP server DOES have an npm path (`@day8/re-frame-pair2-mcp`) per `tools/pair2-mcp/README.md`. Sequencing of the two paths in the user-facing README would help.

## Proposed fix

- Replace `docs/specification/` paths with `spec/` paths; switch `master` → `main`.
- Add a top-of-doc note pointing to `tools/pair2-mcp/` as the modern delivery path.
- Differentiate "skill (markdown)" from "MCP server (npm)" in the install section.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-readmes-currency-2026-05-13.md` §"`skills/re-frame-pair2/README.md` — Stale (significant)"
notes: PR #550: docs(skills): pair2 README — fixed stale docs/specification→spec + master→main URLs (5 occurrences), added top-of-doc MCP-server pointer to tools/pair2-mcp/, reworked Install section to differentiate MCP server (npm) vs skill (clone) paths.
close_reason: Merged via PR (this session sweep wave). Verified on origin/main.

title: [docs] Causa rename sweep — purge stale 're-frame-10x v2' / 're-frame-causa' / 'tools/10x/' references repo-wide
description:
## Scope

Sweep the repo for stale references to `re-frame-10x v2`, `re-frame-causa` (hyphenated), `tools/10x/`, and similar variants that pre-date the Causa rename lock. The on-disk reality is `tools/causa/` with Maven coord `day8/re-frame2-causa` and namespace `day8.re-frame2-causa.*`.

## Sites identified by the readmes-currency sweep

- `README.md` (root) §Tooling — replace "re-frame-10x v2 (in design)" with "Causa (in design); the structural successor to re-frame-10x".
- `README.md` (root) Project-layout block — line `10x/  day8/re-frame2-10x — re-frame-10x v2 (in design)` does not match on-disk; replace with `causa/  day8/re-frame2-causa — Causa, the structural successor to re-frame-10x`.
- `README.md` (root) Project-layout block — omits `tools/pair2-mcp/` (exists on disk with substantial spec / src / test / pilot subtree). Add the row.
- `README.md` (root) Project-layout block — lists `machines-viz/` and `machines-viz-mcp/` as if they were present (they are not on disk). Either drop or flag as "in design — no directory yet."
- `tools/README.md` — "Future homes" still lists Causa as in-design even though `tools/causa/` exists with full spec corpus and src/. Promote Causa out of "Future homes". Also list `pair2-mcp` (currently omitted from Shipped).
- `tools/template/src/clj/new/re_frame2/shared/README.md` — replace `re-frame-causa preload` (hyphenated form) with `Causa` (or the Maven coord `day8/re-frame2-causa`). This README is the template the tool emits into every generated user project — downstream impact on every new app.
- `docs/guide/README.md` chapter 15 row — same hyphenated `re-frame-causa` form; rename to `Causa`. Likely echoes through `docs/guide/15-devtools-and-pair-tools.md` and other guide chapters; sweep all.
- `skills/re-frame-pair-improver2/references/known-frictions.md` — flagged for review.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-readmes-currency-2026-05-13.md` §"Cross-cutting observation" identifies the Causa rename as the single biggest source of drift across the readme sweep. Cleanup is mechanical: global-replace `re-frame-10x v2` → `Causa`, `re-frame-causa` → `Causa`, `tools/10x/` → `tools/causa/`.

## Pre-alpha

Cut the stale strings freely. No back-compat to old naming; readers should always land on the current name.
notes: PR #556: docs Causa rename sweep across 21 .md files — UX/branding, Maven coords, namespaces. https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/556
close_reason: Merged via PR (this session sweep wave). Verified on origin/main.

title: Examples (causa.spec) step 5: trace panel row count does not grow after dispatch — rf2-iw5ym regression
description:
On current main, examples/reagent/counter/causa.spec.cjs fails at line 153 — 'Trace panel row count did not grow after dispatch (before=0, after=0)'. Surfaced AFTER rf2-iw5ym (commit 769ba9d4) landed.

## Root cause

rf2-iw5ym moved `:rf.causa/trace-buffer` sub from thunking `(trace-bus/buffer)` (atom) to reading `(get db :trace-buffer [])`. Sub now requires the trace-buffer to be in app-db.

trace-bus/collect-trace! dispatches `:rf.causa/note-trace-event` to `:rf/causa` to mirror the buffer into app-db. But:

- The dispatch is guarded on `(frame/frame :rf/causa)` existing.
- re-frame2 doesn't auto-create frames on dispatch.
- The preload can't `(rf/reg-frame :rf/causa {})` because reg-frame requires a substrate adapter installed via rf/init!, and the preload runs at ns-load time BEFORE that.
- So in production, `:rf/causa` is NEVER registered, the dispatch is silently skipped, and `db.:trace-buffer` is always empty.

The trace panel reads via `:rf.causa/trace-feed` -> `:rf.causa/trace-buffer` sub, both of which fall through to `:rf/default` for plain-fn shell views. Sub returns [], panel renders no rows.

## Constraints

- The Causa shell currently uses plain Reagent fns for all panel views. Their `(rf/subscribe ...)` calls fall through to `:rf/default` (the framework emits `:rf.warning/plain-fn-under-non-default-frame-once` confirming this).
- Even if `:rf/causa` is registered (e.g. lazily at mount time), the plain-fn sub still reads from `:rf/default`.

## Possible fixes (need design decision)

1. **Revert iw5ym sub change**: thunk the atom again. Requires also reverting 5 tests in registry_cljs_test.cljs (sub-trace-buffer-reads-app-db, sub-trace-buffer-evicts-on-overflow-via-event, sub-trace-buffer-survives-hot-reload, sub-trace-buffer-restarts-from-clean-state, event-sync-trace-buffer-replaces-slot) + 14 helpers across panel view tests. Locally invasive — undoes most of iw5ym's surface.

2. **Register `:rf/causa` at mount time + reg-view-register shell views**: Causa shell panels become reg-view-wrapped so their subscribes carry the `:rf/causa` frame via React context. Aligns with the framework's plain-fn warning intent. Wider refactor — touches every shell panel.

3. **Make the trace-buffer sub read from a fixed-frame app-db**: `(get (frame/frame-app-db-value :rf/causa) :trace-buffer [])`. Requires `:rf/causa` registered AND a custom signal source (re-frame's reactive layer tracks the subscriber's frame, not arbitrary frames). Probably requires registering `:rf/causa` AND a sub graph hack — complex.

Same regression hits `:rf.causa/suppressed-sensitive-count` (rf2-0vxdn — same recipe); rf2-higwg patched `config/note-suppressed!` to drop the `:rf/causa` binding so the redacted-indicator works without registering the frame, but the trace-buffer path needs more design thought.

## Suggested next steps

1. Lock the architecture decision (which option above).
2. If Option 2, write a new bead for reg-view-wrapping each Causa panel.
3. If Option 3, write a new bead for the fixed-frame sub mechanism.

Source: rf2-higwg PR investigation, 2026-05-13.

The new prose adds three skip-checks before `git worktree remove`: (a) permission-denied probe, (b) .claude-agent.lock sentinel, (c) process listing showing cwd inside worktree. title: SSR: spec the parallel-loader pattern — async data fetch fan-out during drain
description:
Per ssr-audit-20260513-1757 findings (rf2-ul137) gap #3. Spec 011's drain model is sequential: :on-create event → :fx dispatched in source order → drain to fixed point → render. The Next.js / Remix model — multiple loaders fan out in parallel, framework awaits all, then renders — has no specced equivalent. :rf.http/managed on JVM (Spec 014, java.net.http.HttpClient transport) blocks the drain thread per HTTP call.

An article-page that wants Promise.all([getArticle, getComments, getRelated]) translates to 3 sequential dispatches in re-frame2 today, multiplying latency by N.

## The primitives exist
- :invoke-all (spawn-and-join machine pattern, Spec 005 §:invoke-all + §invoke-all-join-all-completes / invoke-all-join-any-fails-cancels fixtures) does parallel-await.
- The drain settles when all spawned actors complete.
- :rf.http/managed can be invoked from spawned actors.

What's missing is the DOC that says: 'this is the canonical SSR parallel-loader pattern; here's how to spawn fetch-actors from :on-create and join their results.'

## Recommendation
File: spec/Pattern-SSR-Loaders.md (new pattern doc — same convention as Pattern-RemoteData.md / Pattern-Forms.md).

Content:
- 'The SSR drain runs to fixed point; sequential awaits inside it serialise. For parallel fetches, spawn-and-join via :invoke-all.'
- Worked example showing :rf/server-init that spawns 3 fetch actors and joins on settle.
- Cross-ref to Spec 005 §:invoke-all and Spec 014 §:rf.http/managed.
- Cross-ref from Spec 011 §80–141 (server flow).

## Effort
- 1 new Pattern doc (~200 lines).
- 1 new conformance fixture: parallel-loader-ssr.edn (3 spawned :rf.http/managed-canned-success actors join → app-db carries all three results → render).
- 1 worked example: examples/reagent/ssr-parallel-loader/.

## Surfaces touched
- spec/Pattern-SSR-Loaders.md (new file)
- spec/011-SSR.md §Server flow (cross-ref insertion)
- spec/conformance/fixtures/parallel-loader-ssr.edn (new)
- examples/reagent/ssr-parallel-loader/ (new)

## Acceptance
- Pattern doc covers the fan-out shape end-to-end.
- Fixture demonstrates 3 parallel :rf.http/managed dispatches resolving in parallel under SSR.
- Worked example shows the per-route loader idiom.
notes: PR #665 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/665. Pattern doc 'Pattern-SSR-Loaders.md' (237 lines) + index entry in spec/README.md. check_doc_slugs.py exits 0. Bead remains OPEN pending merge.
close_reason: Closed

title: UIx + Helix adapter render slots: audit for React-18 create-root migration (rf2-fn5rk parity)
description:
Per rf2-fn5rk PR #671's findings: the React-18 create-root bug ONLY ever lived in the Reagent adapter. UIx + Helix already follow Spec 006 §871 + §895 (react-dom/client.createRoot + root.render). All example specs (counter-uix, login-uix, counter-helix, login-helix) pass — no observable symptom suggests latent bug.

**This bead becomes a CONFIRMATION AUDIT, not a fix.** Scope:
1. Read implementation/adapters/uix/src/.../render fn — confirm it uses createRoot + root.render (matches Spec 006 §871)
2. Read implementation/adapters/helix/src/.../render fn — same for Spec 006 §895
3. If both confirm: close this bead with no PR + a note saying audit passed
4. If either diverges from spec: file a focused fix bead with the specific divergence

Likely a 10-minute read-only audit. May not need its own PR.

Source: rf2-fn5rk PR #671 'Follow-on for rf2-uwozn' note.
close_reason: Audit PASS for both UIx and Helix adapters. Per Spec 006 §UIx line 876 ('render wraps react-dom/client.createRoot + root.render') and §Helix line 900 (same): both adapters already use the canonical create-root path that rf2-fn5rk retrofitted into Reagent. UIx render fn at implementation/adapters/uix/src/re_frame/adapter/uix.cljs lines 140-147 — let-binds the createRoot result, calls .render, returns an unmount thunk closing over the cached Root. Helix render fn at implementation/adapters/helix/src/re_frame/adapter/helix.cljs lines 143-150 — structurally identical. Both hydrate paths correctly capture and reuse the Root returned by hydrateRoot. End-to-end: the 'Examples (browser, headless Chromium, rf2-lyj0)' CI job, which exercises counter-uix + login-uix + counter-helix + login-helix specs, passes on main. No fix required, no PR.

title: SSR: implement :rf.server/request cofx — spec contract present, impl absent
description:
Per ssr-audit-20260513-1757 findings (rf2-ul137) gap #2. Spec 011 §311–323 documents the :rf.server/request cofx:

  (rf/reg-cofx :rf.server/request
    {:doc       "The active HTTP request. Server only."
     :platforms #{:server}}
    (fn [coeffects _]
      (assoc coeffects :rf.server/request *current-request*)))

re-frame.ssr never registers it. The worked example side-steps by passing 'request' as an event arg to :rf/server-init, but downstream handlers (e.g. :auth/check-session, :load-user-from-session) have no way to read URL / headers / session without that explicit arg threading. This breaks the canonical SSR auth pattern Spec 011 §687–699 describes ('Server middleware extracts the session from the request; :rf/server-init reads via cofx').

## Reproduction
(rf/reg-event-fx :load-user-from-session
  [(rf/inject-cofx :rf.server/request)]
  (fn [{:keys [rf.server/request]} _]
    ;; request is always nil — cofx unregistered
    ...))

## Recommendation
- Implement :rf.server/request as a registered cofx in re-frame.ssr OR in the host adapter (rf2-NEW-1).
- The host adapter binds *current-request* per-request before the drain runs.
- The cofx reads from that binding.
- Document the binding contract in spec.

## Dependencies
- rf2-NEW-1 (host adapter) — *current-request* binding lives in the host adapter; the cofx is the consumer surface.

## Surfaces touched
- implementation/ssr/src/re_frame/ssr.cljc (cofx registration)
- implementation/ssr-ring/ (binding site)
- spec/011-SSR.md §311–323 (note where *current-request* gets bound)

## Acceptance
- (rf/reg-event-fx :foo [(rf/inject-cofx :rf.server/request)] handler) — handler receives :rf.server/request bound to the active request map under SSR.
- :rf.cofx/skipped-on-platform fires when invoked client-side.
notes: PR #667 https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/667 — :rf.server/request cofx + per-frame request slot (set-request!/get-request/clear-request!) + :platforms #{:server} gating. 8 tests, all passing. Leaves open per dispatch protocol — Mike closes after review.
close_reason: Closed

title: SSR: ship a Ring/Pedestal host adapter — no canonical SSR boot today
description:
Per ssr-audit-20260513-1757 findings (rf2-ul137) gap #1. Spec 011 §357–363 says 'host adapter is responsible for materialising :response into the wire format' but NO host adapter ships. Users wanting SSR must hand-roll: HTTP server choice (Ring/Pedestal/Jetty/HttpKit), per-request frame-creation glue, :rf/response → Ring-response translation, cookie serialisation (RFC 6265 wire form), static-asset serving. The worked example examples/reagent/ssr/core.cljc rolls its own handle-request fn that returns a Ring-shaped map — but it's per-app. This is the gap that turns 'we have SSR' into 'we have an SSR primitive.'

## Recommendation

Ship day8/re-frame2-ssr-ring as a per-feature artefact:

- (ns re-frame.ssr.ring)
- (defn handler [opts] ...) — returns a Ring handler that per-request: make-frames, dispatches :on-create with the Ring request bound as cofx, drains, calls render-to-string, builds the Ring response from get-response + the rendered HTML + the hydration payload.
- Wraps cookie serialisation (RFC 6265) so cookie maps in :cookies become Set-Cookie: headers on the wire.
- Handles redirect short-circuit (:html absent → status+location response).
- Calls destroy-frame in a finally block.

Pedestal/HttpKit variants would follow the same shape; Ring is the v1 commit.

## Effort
- 1 spec section in 011 (host-adapter contract) OR a new spec doc (Spec-SSR-Host-Adapter.md)
- 1 new artefact dir (implementation/ssr-ring/)
- 1 example that uses the adapter end-to-end (examples/reagent/ssr-with-ring/)

## Dependencies
- rf2-NEW-2 (:rf.server/request cofx) — the host adapter is the natural site to register the cofx.
- Coordinates with rf2-0m5ea (template variant for SSR scaffolding).

## Surfaces touched (sequential — hot zone)
- spec/011-SSR.md (response contract section + host-adapter contract addition)
- implementation/ssr-ring/ (new artefact)

## Acceptance
- An app added to deps {day8/re-frame2-ssr-ring {:mvn/version ...}} and (re-frame.ssr.ring/handler {...}) gets a working Ring handler with NO per-app SSR-glue code.
- Cookies in :cookies → Set-Cookie: wire format.
- Redirect short-circuit → status+location response with no body.
- Frame destroyed in finally block (covered by leak test from rf2-NEW-7).
notes: PR #666 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/666. Ships day8/re-frame2-ssr-ring as new artefact under implementation/ssr-ring/. ssr-handler + ssr-middleware + cookie->set-cookie-header public surface. *current-request* binding site for rf2-e825b coordination. 11 deftests / 50 assertions / all green. Existing ssr suite unchanged. Hot-zone touches (implementation/deps.edn + shadow-cljs.edn) additive only. Out of scope: streaming SSR (rf2-olb64), async Ring 3-arity, reg-head (rf2-gr0n).
close_reason: Closed

title: Decision: file skills/re-frame2-improver/ as a new sibling skill for framework-pattern critique
description:
Source: rf2-zf7zd investigation (ai/findings/improver-architecture-20260513-1752.md §Angle 2).

## Decision needed

Currently the skills matrix has six entries. Five are authoring/operational; one (`re-frame-pair-improver2`) is retrospective. The base `re-frame2` skill is strictly authoring with no critique surface.

Proposal: file a new `skills/re-frame2-improver/` (sibling to `re-frame2`, not a sub-mode) that retrospects on re-frame2 code Mike authored and suggests framework-pattern improvements.

## Why a sibling skill, not a sub-mode of re-frame2

Inline critique mode in `re-frame2/SKILL.md` would:
- inflate the skill past the ~250-line leaf ceiling claimed in its own SKILL.md
- blur the trigger fingerprint (re-frame2 would fire on both authoring and critique vocabulary)
- contaminate every authoring session with critique chatter
- complicate the allowed-tools surface

A sibling skill keeps both surfaces clean. Same reasoning as rf2-ncp5n's HYBRID precedent, applied inverted: prefer separation when trigger fingerprints are mutually exclusive.

## Narrow scope at launch

- **Trigger**: explicit pull only — "any improvements?", "review my re-frame2 code", "is there a better re-frame2 pattern for this?"
- **Activation precondition**: a body of re-frame2 source in conversation (read/edited `.cljs`/`.cljc`, or user-supplied snippet)
- **Output**: 7-section structured retro (Goal / Observed shape / Pattern suggestions / Higher-leverage redesigns / Bead candidates / Other / Filing offer)
- **Pattern catalogue at launch**: 6 anti-patterns (see follow-on bead from rf2-zf7zd rec #4):
  1. Manual HTTP retry loops → Managed HTTP (Spec 014)
  2. Boolean-discriminator subs for FSM states → Tags layer (Spec 005)
  3. Manual loading flags → Nine States pattern
  4. Schemaless events at HTTP boundary → Spec 010 schemas
  5. Imperative effects in event handlers → data-only fx (Spec 003)
  6. View-side state via reagent atom/useState for non-render-local concerns → app-db + reg-sub
- **Trigger rejections**: no inline interruption, no periodic check-ins, no auto-end-of-session detection

## Why P1

Adds an 8th skill to the matrix. Decision-shaped (Mike approves/rejects). High-leverage if approved: every authoring session can be retro'd for framework adoption; pre-alpha is when this is most valuable (the framework learns from how it's used).

## If approved, follow-on implementation beads

- Build the SKILL.md, spec/, references/ scaffolding (P2 implementation)
- Pre-populate the anti-pattern catalogue (rec #4 from rf2-zf7zd)
- Land alongside the shared retro-protocol leaf (rec #3 from rf2-zf7zd)

## If rejected

Mike's call to keep the matrix at 6+1 retrospect skill (pair2-retro only). No framework-pattern critique surface exists; users get framework guidance only via the (authoring-time, no-critique) `re-frame2` skill.

## Cross-refs

- ai/findings/improver-architecture-20260513-1752.md (full investigation)
- ai/findings/re-frame-pair-story-skill-investigation-2026-05-13.md (HYBRID precedent)
- skills/re-frame-pair-improver2/spec/design.md (template for the new skill's spec)
close_reason: Closed

title: fix(adapter-reagent): substrate render must use rdc/create-root before render
description:
Discovered by rf2-s2bhn (Causa Playwright spec): the Reagent adapter's `render` slot calls `(rdc/render mount-point render-tree)` directly, but React 18's `reagent.dom.client/render` expects a `Root` (from `create-root`) as the first arg — not a raw DOM element.

When Causa's `mount/open!` calls `(substrate-adapter/render [shell/shell-view] node nil)` the adapter hits `(rdc/render <div> tree)` and React throws `TypeError: root.render is not a function`. The mount silently fails — Causa's `#rf-causa-root` div lands in the DOM but the shell never renders.

Failure stack:
  TypeError: root.render is not a function
      at reagent\$dom\$client\$render
      at re_frame\$adapter\$reagent\$render
      at re_frame\$substrate\$adapter\$render
      at day8\$re_frame2_causa\$mount\$open_BANG_
      at day8\$re_frame2_causa\$mount\$toggle_BANG_

File: implementation/adapters/reagent/src/re_frame/adapter/reagent.cljs ~line 55

Fix: create a Root from the mount-point first, render into the Root, and call .unmount() on the Root in the returned unmount fn.

    (defn- render [render-tree mount-point opts]
      (let [hydrate? (boolean (:hydrate? opts))
            root (rdc/create-root mount-point)]
        (if hydrate?
          (rdc/hydrate-root mount-point render-tree)
          (rdc/render root render-tree))
        (fn unmount [] (rdc/unmount root))))

(Plus the hydrate path also needs review — hydrate-root returns its own root.)

The rf2-s2bhn Playwright spec is shipped with `skip: 'awaiting <this-bead>'` and will go live when the adapter is fixed.

Source of discovery: rf2-s2bhn, the Causa Playwright spec running through the substrate adapter's render slot for the first time end-to-end. Aligns with the rf2-otcbz audit finding that Causa had zero browser-test coverage — the spec immediately surfaced a real bug.
close_reason: Closed

Adds :large? as a boolean per-slot metadata key on Malli app-schema slots. Defines the schema-driven nomination path for rf2-vnmt6 (option (c) in the findings survey).


title: [spec/014] HTTP trace events MUST specify :sensitive? honoring
description:
Audit rf2-ok47g §Completeness matrix flagged HTTP as the canonical privacy surface — passwords go over HTTP, not into app-db. Spec 014 §HTTP has zero mention of :sensitive?. :rf.http/* trace events ride the trace bus carrying request bodies (auth, payment) and response bodies (PII).

Required:
- Specify how :sensitive? declared on the dispatching event handler propagates to :rf.http/* trace events emitted within the cascade (inheritance via :rf.trace/trigger-handler).
- Consider request-side declaration on the :rf.http/managed fx-args map ( per-call) for cases where the handler isn't sensitive but the specific HTTP call is.
- Specify response-side scrub (which slots carry user data; with-redacted-style sentinel substitution).
- Cross-reference from spec/014-HTTPRequests.md to spec/009-Instrumentation.md §Privacy.

Source: ai/findings/sensitive-elision-audit-20260513-1748.md §G3
notes: PR #675 — feat(http): honour :sensitive? in HTTP trace events + redact known-sensitive headers. Spec 014 §Privacy added; new re-frame.http-privacy namespace with 12-name header denylist; per-call/per-request/per-handler :sensitive? OR-reduction; transport-cljs + transport-jvm stamping + redaction; in-flight handle propagation for actor-destroy abort emit. 30 unit + 5 integration tests; 105 http JVM tests pass; 1688 CLJS tests pass. Leaving OPEN per dispatch protocol.
close_reason: Closed

title: [spec/010] Schema-validation errors MUST honour :sensitive? flag
description:
Audit rf2-ok47g §Completeness matrix found that :rf.error/schema-validation-failure trace events typically carry the failing value verbatim (Malli's default behaviour). Spec 010 and Spec 009 have zero mention of :sensitive? interaction with schema-validation errors. A registration carrying :sensitive? true with a schema that fails on user input will emit a trace event containing the raw failing value.

Required:
- Either (a) scrub the failing-value slot when :sensitive? is in scope at emit time, or (b) drop the schema-validation-failure event entirely.
- Decide the per-key fate (path-of-failure should remain visible; only the failing-value slot is sensitive).
- Cross-reference from spec/010-Schemas.md to spec/009-Instrumentation.md §Privacy.

Source: ai/findings/sensitive-elision-audit-20260513-1748.md §G2
notes: PR #673 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/673
close_reason: Closed

title: [runtime] Implement :sensitive? top-level stamping per Spec 009 §Privacy
description:
Audit rf2-ok47g surfaced this as the highest-priority completeness gap. Spec 009 §Privacy (lines 1149-1268, resolved by rf2-a32kd) describes runtime stamping of :sensitive? at the top level of every trace event emitted within a registration carrying :sensitive? true on its metadata. **No such stamping logic exists in implementation/core/src/** — grep finds zero matches for :sensitive? in implementation/core/src/. Every consumer (Causa, Story, pair2 preload, 3 MCP servers) has wired against a future-shaped event; their default-drop filters are no-ops today.

Required:
- Implement :sensitive? hoisting at every trace emit point inside a handler's scope (use :rf.trace/trigger-handler to find the innermost in-scope handler, read :sensitive? from (handler-meta kind id)).
- Implement the :rf.warning/sensitive-without-redaction emission at registration time per (kind, id) pair with suppression cache reset on frame destroy (catalogued at spec/009-Instrumentation.md:870).
- Implement filter-vocab support for (rf/trace-buffer {:sensitive? bool}) per spec/009 §Filter vocabulary line 1245.
- Implement with-redacted interceptor scrub of :event/dispatched :tags :event, :event/db-changed :tags :app-db-before/:app-db-after, and :rf.error/handler-exception :tags :event.
- Tests: every emit-site that the spec calls out must be exercised.

Source: ai/findings/sensitive-elision-audit-20260513-1748.md §G1
close_reason: Closed

title: test(causa): add view-tree tests for trace, performance, issues_ribbon panels
description:
Per rf2-otcbz findings doc, recommendation #3.

Three Causa panels ship `.cljs` view files at 308–428 LoC each with **no view-tree test**:

  - `panels/trace.cljs` (428 LoC, 17 data-testid attrs) — helpers tested at 31 t / 97 a in `trace_helpers_cljs_test.cljc`.
  - `panels/performance.cljs` (308 LoC, 16 data-testid attrs) — helpers tested at 32 t / 119 a in `performance_helpers_cljs_test.cljc`.
  - `panels/issues_ribbon.cljs` (403 LoC, 17 data-testid attrs) — helpers tested at 24 t / 89 a in `issues_ribbon_helpers_cljs_test.cljc`.

Each carries 16-17 `data-testid` attributes signalling they were designed for tests that never landed. Helpers are well-covered; the view-level wiring is not: empty-state rendering, filter-chip toggle, row-click → `:rf.causa/select-dispatch-id`, source-coord chip click, severity badge rendering.

Add (matching the pattern of `subscriptions_view_cljs_test.cljs` and `effects_view_cljs_test.cljs`):

  - `panels/trace_view_cljs_test.cljs`
  - `panels/performance_view_cljs_test.cljs`
  - `panels/issues_ribbon_view_cljs_test.cljs`

These three are the only Causa panels still missing the view layer in the helpers+view convention.

Source: rf2-otcbz findings doc at `ai/findings/causa-test-coverage-20260513-1706.md`, recommendation #3.

title: test(causa): add dedicated registry.cljs tests — 125 framework registrations
description:
Per rf2-otcbz findings doc, recommendation #2.

`tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/registry.cljs` (1,818 LoC — the heaviest single file in tools/causa/) registers 64 reg-subs + 58 reg-event-fx/db + 3 reg-fx (125 framework registrations) under the `:rf.causa/*` namespace on the `:rf/causa` frame. There is **no dedicated test file**.

Coverage is currently transitive through panel view tests (each panel test calls `(registry/reset-for-test!)` then exercises the panel-specific subset via subscribe/dispatch). The transitive route covers most user-facing paths but does NOT isolate:

  - Cross-panel composite subscriptions that no single panel test exercises.
  - The 3 `reg-fx` handlers (`:rf.causa.fx/restore-epoch`, `:rf.causa.fx/reset-frame-db!`, etc.) — `time_travel_cljs_test.cljs` capture-stubs two of them but doesn't drive the registered delegations themselves.
  - Handler-meta source-coord stamping on the registrations.
  - Failure paths in event handlers (assoc into missing nested map, unknown dispatch ids, etc.).

Add `tools/causa/test/day8/re_frame2_causa/registry_cljs_test.cljs` that drives each registered sub / event in isolation against a fresh `:rf/causa` frame and asserts the contract. Mirror the shape of `re-frame.events-cljs-test` for the per-handler assertion pattern.

Source: rf2-otcbz findings doc at `ai/findings/causa-test-coverage-20260513-1706.md`, recommendation #2.
notes: PR #651 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/651

title: test(causa): add shell.cljs tests — panel routing, REDACTED indicator, hydration dormant flag
description:
Per rf2-otcbz findings doc, recommendation #1.

`tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/shell.cljs` (392 LoC) has zero direct tests. Logic that lives only in shell.cljs:

  - `canvas` panel-routing `case` (16 panel ids → 16 view fns + the `stub-panel` fallback). A typo in one keyword silently routes to the stub.
  - `sidebar`'s `hydration-awake?` flag that toggles the `:dormant?` marker once `:has-mismatch?` arrives (tools/causa/spec/006-Hydration-Debugger.md §Visibility).
  - `bottom-rail`'s `[● REDACTED N]` indicator gated on `(pos? redacted-count)` — the trace-bus counter is tested; the *render* is not.
  - `top-strip`'s collapsed-cue affordance for the co-pilot rail gated on `:rf.causa/copilot-open?`.

Add `tools/causa/test/day8/re_frame2_causa/shell_cljs_test.cljs` covering:
  - Every panel id in `sidebar-items` routes to its view function from `canvas`.
  - An unknown `:rf.causa/selected-panel` value falls through to `stub-panel`.
  - The redacted-indicator renders iff `(pos? redacted-count)` and pluralises the title text correctly (singular at 1, plural at N>1).
  - The hydration sidebar entry's dormant marker (`◌`) drops once the hydration composite returns `:has-mismatch? true`.

Source: rf2-otcbz findings doc at `ai/findings/causa-test-coverage-20260513-1706.md`, recommendation #1.

description: Investigation rf2-j41ya found ~80 :rf.causa/* subs/events/fxs registered in tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/registry.cljs (1818 LOC, 14 spec-prefixed phases) with no spec enumeration. tools/causa/spec/008-Embedding-Contract.md enforces the prefix as a convention but lists nothing. title: Causa spec: trace-bus + collector contract has no normative home
description: Investigation rf2-j41ya found Causa's trace-bus + collector lifecycle (tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/trace_bus.cljc, 229 LOC) lives entirely in impl docstrings. No spec covers: (1) the decision to maintain a separate Causa ring buffer alongside (rf/trace-buffer); (2) buffer depth (impl: 1000, 'five times framework default'); (3) collect-trace! registration as :rf.causa/trace-collector; (4) the :sensitive? gate; (5) the filter-events consumer-side filter algebra (9 axes); (6) clear-buffer! semantics. Recommendation: new spec doc tools/causa/spec/013-Trace-Bus.md normatively describing the consumer contract. Models: pair2-mcp/spec/001-Wire-Protocol.md + 002-nREPL-Transport.md (substrate-integration contracts get their own normative doc). P1 because this is the data plane every panel reads from; an AI handed only the spec couldn't reconstruct Causa's buffer story. Findings: ai/findings/causa-impl-spec-gap-20260513-1705.md Gap #1.

title: [pair2-mcp] Diff-encode :epochs slice by default; full snapshots opt-in
description:
Source: rf2-jlq5j findings doc (ai/findings/wire-protocol-bigapp-20260513-1541.md), recommendation #3

THE SINGLE BIGGEST WIRE-BYTE WIN. Each `:rf/epoch-record` today carries full `:db-before` AND `:db-after` (`spec/Spec-Schemas.md:2082`). Default epoch-history depth is 50 (`implementation/epoch/src/re_frame/epoch.cljc:45`). So the `:epochs` slice of `snapshot` is 100x app-db in worst case — a 1MB app-db → 100MB on the wire just from this slice. `pr-str` does NOT preserve structural sharing across records, so the in-memory savings are erased on serialisation.

## Scope

`skills/re-frame-pair2/preload/re_frame_pair2/runtime.cljs:1236` (`snapshot-frame-slice :epochs`) projects each record as:

```
{:epoch-id <id>
 :event-id <id>
 :committed-at <ts>
 :trigger-event <vec>
 :db-before-hash <hash>
 :db-after-hash <hash>
 :db-delta <clojure.data/diff projection>
 ... other slots unchanged ...}
```

The existing `runtime.cljs:441 epoch-diff` helper already computes the diff in the v1 vocabulary; reuse it.

Opt-back-in via `:full-epoch-snapshots? true` arg on `snapshot` and `trace-window`. Agents that need full snapshots for time-travel restore call out explicitly.

## Scope boundary

This is a WIRE-LAYER transform at the MCP/runtime boundary. The in-memory `:rf/epoch-record` schema stays unchanged — no Spec-Schemas.md change needed. The diff projection happens in the runtime preload, before `pr-str`. Time-travel restore (which needs the full snapshot) goes via the existing `rf/restore-epoch` path, not via re-reading the snapshot wire response.

## Acceptance

- snapshot `:epochs` slice returns diff-encoded by default.
- `:full-epoch-snapshots? true` opt-in returns the full pair.
- Test: 5MB app-db, single-key change, diff-encoded payload < 1% the full-encoded payload.
- Spec/003-Tool-Catalogue (and the runtime ns docstring) document the change.
close_reason: Closed

title: [pair2-mcp] Path-based slicing for snapshot :app-db (and new get-path tool)
description:
Source: rf2-jlq5j findings doc (ai/findings/wire-protocol-bigapp-20260513-1541.md), recommendation #2

Today the snapshot tool returns full app-db via `rf/get-frame-db` (`skills/re-frame-pair2/preload/re_frame_pair2/runtime.cljs:1226`). Agents that want one key fetch everything.

## Scope

Two parts:

1. Add `:path [:user :profile]` arg to the `snapshot` tool's `:app-db` slice. Server-side `get-in` before serialising. Defaults unchanged.
2. Add a dedicated `get-path` MCP tool — small, focused primitive for targeted reads. Signature: `{:frame :stories :path [:cart :items 0]}` → `{:ok? true :value <subtree> :exists? true}`.

## Why path is the cleaner primitive

Slicing a tree by path is the operation re-frame agents already think in (subscribe vectors, `get-in`, etc.). Adding it elsewhere (subs cache reads, future causa-mcp tools) is the same pattern.

## Acceptance

- snapshot tool accepts `:path`; smoke test in test/snapshot_test.cljs covers nil (default), present path, missing path, root path.
- get-path tool exists with its own descriptor; smoke test covers same.
- spec/003-Tool-Catalogue + the runtime preload's docstring document the new path arg.
notes: PR #648 https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/648 — path slicing on snapshot + new get-path tool. 84 tests / 198 assertions green; production build clean. Path vocabulary shared with causa-mcp §2. Spec updates in pair2-mcp tree; runtime preload docstring update in skills/ filed as follow-up (out of bead scope).
close_reason: Closed

title: [pair2-mcp] Enforce 5K-token cap at wire boundary
description:
Source: rf2-jlq5j findings doc (ai/findings/wire-protocol-bigapp-20260513-1541.md), recommendation #1

The 5K-token cap declared normative in `tools/pair2-mcp/spec/Principles.md:127` §"Tight token budget per response" is documentation only — no code enforces it. A `snapshot` call against a real-app's 5 MB app-db produces ~1.4M tokens (290x the cap), silently corrupting the agent conversation.

## Scope

Size-check rendered `pr-str` output in `tools/pair2-mcp/src/re_frame_pair2_mcp/tools.cljs:57 ok-text`. On overflow:
- Return `:ok? false :reason :budget-exceeded :estimated-tokens N :hint <next-step>`
- Hints should be tool-specific: snapshot → "try `:include [:app-db]` + `:path [...]`"; trace-window → "narrow `:ms` or use `:since-id` with explicit `:limit`".
- Configurable cap with sensible default (5K tokens ≈ 17.5KB EDN).

## Failure mode being fixed

Today: silent agent-context corruption. Agent host either truncates middle of payload or wedges conversation. Answer is lost without warning.

After fix: structured error with next-step hint. Agent retries with a narrower request.

## Acceptance

- ok-text checks size pre-return.
- Unit test in `test/re_frame_pair2_mcp/` exercises both under-cap (passes through) and over-cap (returns budget-exceeded with structured data) paths.
- spec/003-Tool-Catalogue updated to document the bound on every tool.
notes: PR #641 (https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/641). Wire-boundary 5K-token cap enforced in tools.cljs invoke. Pluggable :strategy (today :truncate-with-marker; extensible for path-slicing rf2-tygdv, lazy summary rf2-u2029, diff encoding rf2-rl7y). Default cap 5000, per-call max-tokens arg, 0 disables. Overflow shape: {:rf.mcp/overflow {:limit :reached :token-count N :cap-tokens C :tool T :hint H}}. 17 new tests (57 total, 132 assertions, 0 fail). Spec Principles.md §Tight token budget tightened from should → must enforced; 003-Tool-Catalogue.md gained universal wire-cap section. Note: another worktree had begun uncommitted work on the original rf2-rvyzy-pair2-mcp-wire-cap branch with a per-tool-plumbed approach; this PR's centralised-at-invoke design supersedes it.
close_reason: Closed

title: Investigation: wire protocol scalability for big app-dbs (MB-scale) — MCP surface, pagination, caching, dedup
description:
Audit the wire protocol surfaces used by skills ↔ MCP servers to handle large app-db payloads (multi-MB scale). Open questions:

**Capacity**: Can the current MCP wire protocol handle a few-MB app-db without truncation, choking the conversation, or breaking the 5K-token response budget (rf2-ll0yq)? What's the failure mode today — silent truncation, error, blowup?

**Surface design**: Do MCP server tool signatures need to change for big payloads? Options:
  - **(a) Slicing**: tools accept a path/selector (e.g. EDN path, Specter-style) and return only the requested slice. The agent navigates the tree incrementally.
  - **(b) Pagination**: tools return a cursor for sequential chunks of large collections.
  - **(c) Lazy summary + on-demand drill-down**: default response is a tree summary (keys + sizes + types); agent asks for specific subtrees by id/path.
  - Mix of the above.

**Caching**: If the app-db is largely stable across consecutive tool calls, can the server cache a hash and return 'unchanged since last fetch' instead of the full payload? What's the invalidation rule (per-event? per-epoch? per-path?)? Where would this state live — per-frame, per-MCP-session, or global?

**Trace surface**: trace events also carry app-db payloads (or do they?). Same questions apply.

**Structural dedup**: Should we use an improved version of https://github.com/day8/de-dupe? That library dedupes structurally-shared data on the wire. Could collapse repeated subtrees, common keysets, common values across events in a trace window. Investigate: is de-dupe still maintained? What's its CLJS+JS reach? Is a v2 worth doing?

**Output**: Write findings to ai/findings/wire-protocol-bigapp-<timestamp>.md with:
  - Today's per-tool wire shape (concrete bytes for a worked example with a 5MB app-db)
  - Failure-mode catalogue
  - Design proposal: which combination of (slice / paginate / lazy / cache / dedup) fits which surface
  - de-dupe assessment: alive? CLJS reach? v2 scope?
  - Prioritised recommendations → bead each one

**Surfaces to read**: tools/pair2-mcp/, tools/story-mcp/, tools/causa-mcp/ (spec-only), spec/Tool-Pair.md, spec/009-Instrumentation.md (5K-token budget rule), the wire-encoding paths in each MCP server, day8/de-dupe README.
close_reason: Investigation complete. Findings at ai/findings/wire-protocol-bigapp-20260513-1541.md. 12 follow-on beads filed: rf2-rvyzy (P1 cap enforcement), rf2-tygdv (P1 path slicing), rf2-1wdzp (P1 epoch diff encoding), rf2-obpa9 (P2 de-dupe integration), rf2-u2029 (P2 lazy summary), rf2-kbqq3 (P2 pagination), rf2-ho4ve (P2 size-aware overflow), rf2-nw6sj (P2 de-dupe .cljc port), rf2-3rt1f (P2 hash-keyed cache), rf2-lwgg8 (P3 causa-mcp spec), rf2-6sddv (P3 typical-tokens hints), rf2-f3djn (P3 skill docs). day8/de-dupe is alive (April 2026), CLJS-only, no v2 needed — just .cljc port for JVM reach.

title: Decide: who owns the story-mcp consumer story (existing skills vs new re-frame-story skill)
description:
**Source**: rf2-fvy7o findings doc, finding #2 + recommendation #3.

Decision needed; not a clear implementation task.

`tools/story-mcp/src/re_frame/story_mcp/tools.cljc` exposes 16 MCP tools across Dev / Docs / Testing / Write. **No skill in `skills/` declares any of them in `allowed-tools`.** The agent self-healing loop (generate variant → register-variant → run-variant → read-failures → iterate) is fully specified in `tools/story-mcp/spec/` but has zero agent-side glue.

## Options

1. **Extend the existing skills.** Add `mcp__story-mcp__*` tool ids to `skills/re-frame2/SKILL.md` (for the authoring + register loop) and `skills/re-frame-pair2/SKILL.md` (for run-variant + read-failures in a live session). Plus new leaves: `skills/re-frame2/reference/tooling/story-mcp-loop.md` and `skills/re-frame-pair2/references/stories.md`.
2. **Spin a new `skills/re-frame-story/` skill.** Scope: component-development companion using story-MCP, no runtime attach required, complements `re-frame2` (authoring side) but doesn't replace it. Trigger: agent-driven Storybook MCP loop (generate → register → run → fix).

## Context

`ai/findings/re-frame-pair-story-skill-investigation-2026-05-13.md` (bead rf2-ncp5n) explicitly recommends option 1 and rejects option 2's borderline argument unless the self-healing loop becomes a dominant pattern with its own decision-flow. The findings doc's reasoning is sound; the operational confirmation in rf2-fvy7o is that **none** of the binding work has happened yet — option 1 needs to be picked up and actioned, or option 2 needs to be revisited.

## Inputs for the decision

- How dominant is the agent self-healing loop expected to be vs. ad-hoc authoring?
- Does the existing 6-skill matrix have room for a 7th without making routing harder?
- Are there workflows that exclusively need story-mcp (no `re-frame2` authoring context, no `re-frame-pair2` runtime context)?

## Next step

Mike-decision. Once locked: file the implementation beads (one per leaf to add, one per `allowed-tools` edit).
close_reason: Closed

title: re-frame-pair2: add subscribe/unsubscribe MCP tools + streaming recipes
description:
**Source**: rf2-fvy7o findings doc, finding #1.

`tools/pair2-mcp/src/re_frame_pair2_mcp/tools.cljs:609-708` exposes 9 MCP tools but `skills/re-frame-pair2/SKILL.md:15-29` declares only 7 in `allowed-tools`. The two missing tools are `subscribe` and `unsubscribe` — the rf2-hq49 streaming pair. The agent host's allow-list will block both even though the server advertises them.

## What to do

1. Add `mcp__re-frame-pair2__subscribe` and `mcp__re-frame-pair2__unsubscribe` to `skills/re-frame-pair2/SKILL.md` front-matter `allowed-tools`.
2. Add a new `references/streaming-subscriptions.md` leaf (≤250 lines per the leaf-size policy) covering:
   - When to use subscribe vs. watch-epochs (push-mode vs. pull-mode)
   - Topic vocabulary: `:trace`, `:epoch`, `:fx`, `:error`
   - Filter shape per topic (cross-link to ops.md / mcp-transport.md)
   - The `notifications/progress` correlation model
   - Termination: client abort, `max-events`, `max-ms`, `unsubscribe`
3. Update the three recipes in `references/recipes.md` to prefer the streaming MCP path over the bash-shim `scripts/watch-epochs.sh --stream`:
   - "Narrate the next N events"
   - "Alert me on slow events"
   - "Watch for X while I interact"
4. Update `references/mcp-transport.md` §"Bash-shim → MCP tool mapping" to add rows for the streaming pair.

## Verification

After landing: `grep mcp__re-frame-pair2__ skills/re-frame-pair2/SKILL.md | wc -l` should equal 9. The new leaf should be reachable from `SKILL.md` §"Where the depth lives — loading map".
notes: PR #633: rf2-aks1t-pair2-streaming-recipes — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/633

title: CI flake: counter-with-stories time-travel scrub — 'still 4'
description:
Intermittent CI failure on 'Examples (browser, headless Chromium, rf2-lyj0)' job. Reproduces on the same SHA — fails roughly 16% of the last 50 main runs (8/50). Three open PRs (#581, #603, #609) all hit it today; the PRs themselves are unrelated and red purely because of this flake.

Signature (consistent across every failed run):

    FAIL counter-with-stories: time-travel scrub did not visibly change :count (still "4")
    Error: time-travel scrub did not visibly change :count (still "4")
        at Object.run (examples/reagent/counter_with_stories/counter_with_stories.spec.cjs:866:15)

Hits §11 of counter_with_stories.spec.cjs. The scrubber click should restore the loaded variant frame (init 7 + §10 click = 8) back to count=7, but the visible count is observed as '4' and unchanged after the scrub.

Hypothesis: when the test runs §10 inc.click() after the §6 workspace + §7 clickVariant('/loaded') sequence, main.locator([data-test='inc']).first() resolves to a workspace cell's canvas (the clicked-three-times variant: init 0 + play's 3 incs = 3) rather than the loaded variant's canvas. The click bumps count 3→4 in the wrong frame. The scrubber's restore-epoch targets the active variant (loaded) whose frame is sitting at count=7, but the count cell the test reads is still showing the workspace cell's '4'.

Next steps:
- Local Playwright reproduction (npm run test:examples after shadow-cljs compile).
- Add a console probe to dump the count of [data-test='inc'] under <main> at the §10 moment.
- Either anchor §10/§11 selectors to the active variant's canvas explicitly (e.g., scope by the canvas pane's data-test once one is added), or guarantee workspace teardown finishes before clickVariant assertions proceed.

Not introduced by the three PRs surfacing it today; the same SHA passes and fails. Affects PR queue throughput — every PR sees ~16% red on this job through no fault of its own.
notes: PR #616: data-test-variant anchor on Story canvas + workspace cells; §10/§11 spec selectors scoped to active variant. Path (b) per bead. 20/20 local repeat runs pass. Root cause: shell's :selected-variant and :selected-workspace are independent slots — variant click after workspace selection leaves workspace rendered under <main>, alphabetically-first cell of :auto-grid (clicked-three-times, count=3 after :play) absorbs the §10 inc click → count=4, but right-pane slider scrubs /loaded → canvas count stays at 4. Fix anchors selectors to /loaded explicitly via data-test-variant, robust regardless of state hazard.
close_reason: Closed

title: [skill] re-frame-pair-improver2: tighten description precondition + add 'when NOT to use' section
description:
## Weakest activation guard in the corpus

`skills/re-frame-pair-improver2/SKILL.md`:

- `description:` is generic — "Analyze a user's recent work with re-frame-pair2 to identify friction..." Will trigger on ANY retrospective phrasing ("retro on my session", "what went wrong", "how could this be better").
- The skill only makes sense AFTER a `re-frame-pair2` session, but the description doesn't encode that precondition.
- No "When NOT to use" section in the body.

A user could invoke this skill after a generic re-frame2 authoring session and get nonsense.

## Proposed fix

- Tighten `description:` to require an `re-frame-pair2`-session precondition explicitly (model on `re-frame-pair2`'s own description, which is the strongest false-activation guard in the corpus).
- Add a "When NOT to use this skill" section in the SKILL.md body.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-skills-best-practice-2026-05-13.md` §CC-9 + bead B-skill-improver2-precondition / B-skill-improver2-add-when-not-to-use — P1
notes: PR #566 (combined with rf2-hvzsx) — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/566. Tightened description: now requires explicit re-frame-pair2-session precondition (attach + dispatch/trace/epoch/hot-swap/restore-epoch artefacts) and calls out vocabulary-only matches as insufficient — modelled on re-frame-pair2's own description (strongest guard in corpus). Added new '## When NOT to use this skill' body section enumerating routing cases with explicit alternative skills.
close_reason: Closed

title: [skill] Corpus-wide SKILL.md trim pass — target sizes per skill
description:
## Skills body bloat

Every SKILL.md in the corpus exceeds Anthropic's recommended ~3-5K-byte router size by 2-4×. Total bytes loaded on activation across the six skills: 84,994 (1,031 lines). Mean SKILL.md = 14,166 B / 172 L.

Recurring offenders:

1. **Cardinal rules** with 5-line entries that re-explain a one-line rule.
2. **Workflow / Phase enumerations** that duplicate the leaf describing the same phase.
3. **Deep-dive descriptions** that paragraph-describe each leaf.
4. **Anti-patterns** that restate cardinal rules in the negative.

The actual triggers for SKILL bloat are:

- `re-frame2-implementor` (185 L, 19,518 B) — largest. Cardinal rules + Phase 1 enumeration + Phase 2 enumeration + done checklist + anti-patterns all overlap heavily with leaves. Phase 1 decisions listed twice (SKILL §Phase 1 + `reference/phase-1-decisions.md` 211 L).
- `re-frame-migration` (166 L, 15,765 B) — `MIGRATION.md` framing duplicated 5+ paragraphs; Phase 2/3/5 re-derive what each `reference/*.md` already states.
- `re-frame2` (176 L, 15,317 B) — cardinal-rules overlaps leaves; long ~80-line loading-map table.
- `re-frame-pair-improver2` (171 L, 11,035 B) — 35-row "lens table" + improvement patterns belong in `references/analysis-lenses.md` (which already partially has them).
- `re-frame2-setup` (173 L, 11,929 B) — `When-the-author-asks-anything-beyond-setup` table duplicates the frontmatter routing.

## Proposed fix — target sizes

- `re-frame2` → ~110 L
- `re-frame-migration` → ~110 L
- `re-frame2-implementor` → ~110 L
- `re-frame2-setup` → ~130 L
- `re-frame-pair-improver2` → ~100 L
- `re-frame-pair2` → keep (best in corpus at 160 L / 11,430 B; already lean)

Method:

- Pull cardinal-rules detail to `reference/cardinal-rules.md` per skill.
- Remove duplicate phase enumerations from SKILL.md when leaves carry them in full.
- Compress deep-dive section to one bullet per leaf (not a paragraph).
- Remove anti-patterns sections; let cardinal rules carry the positive form.

## Cross-stream dedupe

This bead consolidates the per-skill trim beads from `sweep-skills-best-practice-2026-05-13.md`:
- B-skill-re-frame2-trim-skill
- B-skill-implementor-trim-skill
- B-skill-migration-dedupe-workflow
- B-skill-corpus-trim-skills (the umbrella)

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-skills-best-practice-2026-05-13.md` §CC-3 + per-skill bead candidates
notes: PR #558: trim corpus -42% (84,994B -> 49,082B); 5 of 6 skills trimmed (pair2 left per bead instruction). DO NOT CLOSE.
close_reason: Merged via PR (this session sweep wave). Verified on origin/main.

title: [spec] Cross-link conformance README capability-tag example to actual fixture tags
description:
## Conformance README example carries unused capability tags

`spec/conformance/README.md` worked-example section shows capability tags `:core/trace` and `:core/frame` in its sample manifest, but no fixture in the corpus carries either tag. The example is aspirational; a reader following it will produce a manifest claim with tags that match nothing.

Ties to the conformance vocab drift bead — the P0 `:actor/spawn` vs `:actor/spawn-destroy` reconciliation should bring this README example into agreement with reality.

## Proposed fix

Either:
- Add `:core/trace` and `:core/frame` tags to appropriate fixtures (e.g., trace-emit fixtures, multi-frame fixtures).
- Or rewrite the README example to use tags that actually appear on real fixtures.

The README is the first-read doc for an AI port author; the example MUST match reality.

title: [spec] Consolidate error-event vocabulary into single normative catalogue (G-A closure)
description:
## G-A closure

The error envelope is gestured at across Spec 002, 009, 010 (P7 ◐) but the complete cross-product table of `(category, :tags shape, recovery, owning-Spec)` is split across 9 files.

`Spec-Schemas.md` defines `:rf/error-event` (L282) and per-category tags schemas (`DrainInterruptedTags` etc.) but the catalogue is incomplete — the audit calls for "validation failure, handler exception, hydration mismatch, drain depth exceeded, override fallthrough" all in one place.

Per the spec-impl alignment sweep, API.md L422-453 lists ~30 categories; Spec 009 §Error categories L829-917 lists ~60+ that the impl actually emits. Consumers using API.md as a "complete" error map will under-handle.

## Proposed fix

Consolidate the error-event vocabulary into a single normative `§Error vocabulary catalogue` — either in Spec 009 or `Spec-Schemas.md`. Include for every `:rf.error/*` and `:rf.warning/*`:

- Category keyword
- `:tags` shape (schema reference)
- Owning Spec (for context)
- Recovery posture (retry, drop, escalate, surface to :on-error)
- Emit-site file references (kept fresh; see spec-impl alignment sweep §Finding 4.1 about stale citations)

Update `spec/API.md` L422-453 to either reproduce the full list, or replace the partial list with a "See [09 §Error vocabulary catalogue] for the complete list" pointer.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-spec-completeness-2026-05-13.md` §Bucket 3 / G-A — BC-11 — P1
- `ai/findings/sweep-spec-impl-alignment-2026-05-13.md` §5 / Finding 5 — same defect from impl-side
notes: PR #575 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/575

title: [spec] Pin Tool-Pair multi-frame operating-frame v1 contract (hybrid posture)
description:
## SA-4 closure

`spec/Tool-Pair.md` §Open questions L477: "Multi-frame pair UX — operating frame?" carries "Lean: hybrid (explicit-set, implicit-until-reset)" but the v1 contract isn't pinned.

Tool-Pair is the spec downstream tools (pair2-mcp, Causa, Story) consume to operate on a re-frame2 app with multiple frames. Without a pinned posture, every tool re-invents the operating-frame state.

## Proposed fix

Land the hybrid posture as the v1 normative contract:

- The default operating frame is `:rf/default` until the user explicitly sets it.
- An explicit `set-operating-frame!` (or per-call `:frame` opt) overrides until the next `reset-operating-frame!` or session reload.
- The operating frame is implicit-until-reset within a session; one set affects subsequent ops in the same session.

Then enumerate the corresponding MCP tool surface (`set-operating-frame`, `reset-operating-frame`, `get-operating-frame`) in pair2-mcp's 003-Tool-Catalogue.md.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-spec-completeness-2026-05-13.md` §Bucket 2 / Finding 2.9 — BC-10 — P1
notes: PR #561: spec(Tool-Pair) §Operating frame — multi-frame resolution. Four-tier resolver pinned (explicit > session > sole-frame > nil); reads and writes both refuse :ambiguous-frame at tier 4; matches shipped impl from rf2-19xl. Resolved decisions trailer added (Tool-Pair.md's first).
close_reason: Stale in_progress cleanup — PR merged earlier this session, bead status never auto-flipped.

title: [spec] Resolve OQ 009 §Privacy / sensitive data in traces — :sensitive? tag + with-redacted interceptor
description:
## SA-4 closure — intersects security

Spec 009 §Open questions L1163: "Privacy / sensitive data in traces?" carries "Recommendation: `:sensitive?` tag + `with-redacted` interceptor" but the schema / interceptor is not specified.

This matters because trace surfaces ride into developer tooling (Causa, 10x, story-mcp); when sensitive data is in app-db, the trace bus can leak it through any consuming surface.

## Proposed fix

- Specify the `:sensitive?` event-handler / registration tag (schema in Spec-Schemas).
- Specify the `with-redacted` interceptor — its API, redaction algorithm, default redactor, override hook.
- Document the trace-side handling: when `:sensitive?` is on a registration, the trace event carries `:rf.trace/redacted true` and the payload is the redactor output, not the original payload.
- Cross-reference from Spec 009 §Error contract and any Story/Causa panel docs that display traces.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-spec-completeness-2026-05-13.md` §Bucket 2 / Finding 2.7 — BC-8 — P1
notes: PR #564: resolved OQ §Privacy / sensitive data — :sensitive? reg-* metadata + with-redacted interceptor; trace-event :sensitive? top-level; listener default-drop policy; dev-only elision; new :rf.warning/sensitive-without-redaction advisory
close_reason: Merged via PR (this session sweep wave). Verified on origin/main.

title: [spec] Add per-kind registration-metadata schemas to Spec-Schemas (SA-3/SA-4 closure)
description:
## SA-4 + SA-3 closure

Spec 001 §Open questions L297: "Per-kind metadata schemas?" carries "Lean: yes, add per-kind schemas to Spec-Schemas" but the schemas are not added.

This ties to SA-3 (every shape on the wire MUST have a schema in Spec-Schemas). The metadata stamped on each registration kind (`:rf/event-metadata`, `:rf/sub-metadata`, `:rf/fx-metadata`, `:rf/cofx-metadata`, `:rf/machine-metadata`, `:rf/flow-metadata`, `:rf/route-metadata`, `:rf/app-schema-metadata`, `:rf/view-metadata`, `:rf/frame-metadata`, `:rf/error-projector-metadata`) is currently implicit.

## Proposed fix

- Add per-kind schemas to `spec/Spec-Schemas.md` enumerating which metadata keys are valid for each registration kind.
- Cross-reference from `spec/001-Registration.md` §Open questions (mark RESOLVED) + §Registration grammar.
- Optionally — depending on rf2-jzs9 plural-form discussions — surface a `(rf/kind-metadata-schema :event)` reflection helper for tooling.

Each :merges the base open shape additively. Resolved decisions trailer added; 001 open-question marked RESOLVED.

title: [spec] Resolve OQ 001 §:doc required in dev (SA-4 closure)
description:
## SA-4 closure

Spec 001 §Open questions L291: "Should `:doc` be required, not just recommended?" carries "Lean: yes, in dev" but no warning is specified. Per SPEC-AUTHORING SA-4, every OQ must resolve to either a landed decision or an explicit "host-choice" framing.

## Proposed fix

Decide: land a dev-mode warning when `:doc` is absent on `reg-event-*` / `reg-sub` / `reg-fx` / `reg-cofx` / `reg-machine` / `reg-flow` / `reg-route` / `reg-app-schema(s)` / `reg-view`. Or explicitly mark the question as "host-choice — v1 CLJS reference does not warn; ports may choose to enforce."

If landing the warn: file the warning key (e.g. `:rf.warning/missing-doc`), enumerate the slot in Spec 009, gate behind goog.DEBUG.

## Source

notes: PR #559: resolved :doc OQ — SHOULD (dev-warned); :rf.warning/missing-doc once per (kind, id); production-elided

title: [test] Run conformance corpus on CLJS — port conformance_test.clj to :node-test
description:
## Conformance corpus only runs on JVM

`implementation/core/test/re_frame/conformance_test.clj` is JVM-only. The CLJS counterpart (`conformance_dsl_cljs_test.cljs`) only tests the DSL `resolve-value*` micro-shape; it never loads or runs any `.edn` fixture.

All 111 fixtures are validated against ONE host. Per the conformance README's own framing — fixtures are meant to be the portable cross-host capability proof — this is a serious gap.

CLJS has materially different async semantics (the executor isn't a thread, sub-cache disposal scheduling differs, view-render trigger-handler rides via a different binding mechanism). A second-host re-frame2 port author can't trust the corpus until CLJS is the second host.

## Proposed fix

Mirror `conformance_test.clj` as `conformance_corpus_cljs_test.cljs`, target `:node-test`. Adapt the runner's reset routine for CLJS async semantics (especially the sub-cache disposal scheduling and view-render trigger-handler binding).

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-test-coverage-rigour-2026-05-13.md` §Finding 1 / B-1 — P1, the highest-value coverage gap.
notes: PR #562: CLJS conformance corpus runner ports the JVM logic to :node-test; 110/110 runnable fixtures pass on both hosts (matching skip: :flow/lifecycle-emits-traces, out-of-claim).
close_reason: Merged via PR (this session sweep wave). Verified on origin/main.

title: [docs] examples READMEs: reconcile 11-vs-16-vs-20 example count; add missing rows
description:
## Stale catalogues

The examples READMEs disagree with the directory listing:

- Top-level `examples/README.md` enumerates 16 examples in the Reagent table.
- `examples/reagent/README.md` Layout block enumerates 11 (`counter`, `login`, `todomvc`, `routing`, `ssr`, `managed_http_counter`, `state_machine_walkthrough`, `nine_states`, `7Guis`, `realworld`) and the line "the full set of fifteen worked examples".
- Directory on disk holds 16+ subdirectories — including `boot/`, `counter_slim_and_fast/`, `counter_with_stories/`, `long_running_work/`, `websocket/`.

Three of those four extras have their own per-example READMEs (boot, websocket, long_running_work). `counter_slim_and_fast/` lacks a README.

## Proposed fix

- Reconcile the count in both catalogue READMEs.
- Add the missing rows (`boot`, `counter_slim_and_fast`, `counter_with_stories`, `long_running_work`, `websocket`) to both catalogues with build-ids and spec cross-refs.
- Decide whether `counter_slim_and_fast/` should get its own README (likely yes — it's a worked example like the others) or whether the directory should be merged into `counter/` with a flag-driven variant. May split into a P2 follow-up bead.

## Source

title: [docs] tools/causa/README.md: drop 1.0.0 version pin + unshipped causa-mcp claims
description:
## Over-claiming README

`tools/causa/README.md` advertises a v1.0 artefact that doesn't exist:

### Problem 1 — version pin

Quick-start dev-deps snippet:

```clojure
{:aliases {:dev {:extra-deps {day8/re-frame2-causa {:mvn/version "1.0.0"}}}}}
```

But the repo VERSION is `0.0.1.alpha`. Clojars has not yet been cut. The Status footer says "In design. Spec corpus landed via rf2-1lls (2026-05-12). Implementation work begins after the spec ratifies." — contradicts the version pin in the quick-start.

### Problem 2 — unshipped MCP artefact

"MCP (Causa as an agent surface)" section:

```bash
npm install -g @day8/re-frame2-causa-mcp
```

`tools/causa-mcp/` does not exist on disk. The README references `spec/010-MCP-Server.md` which does exist, but the artefact it describes is not shipped.

## Proposed fix

Replace the version pin in the dev-deps snippet with a comment indicating "pre-alpha, not yet on Clojars; use `:local/root \"tools/causa\"` until the alpha publish lands." Mirror the posture in `tools/template/README.md` which carries the right idiom: "Until the alpha publish lands on Clojars, use the `:local/root` route."

Same posture for the MCP section — clarify "once published" or point to a local-build path.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-readmes-currency-2026-05-13.md` §"`tools/causa/README.md` — Over-claiming"

title: [ci] CI coverage for tools/* — add per-tool jobs in test.yml
description:
## CI gap

None of `tools/causa/`, `tools/story/`, `tools/story-mcp/`, `tools/template/`, `tools/pair2-mcp/` has any PR-time CI surface. Each ships test trees (`tools/causa/test/`, etc.) but failures will surface only when shadow-cljs's combined build catches them.

`tools/causa/src/` and `tools/story/src/` are on `implementation/shadow-cljs.edn` `:source-paths`, so their tests DO run as part of the consolidated `:node-test`. But `tools/story-mcp/`, `tools/template/`, `tools/pair2-mcp/` are NOT — their tests are not run by any current CI step.

## Proposed fix

Add per-tool CI jobs to `.github/workflows/test.yml`:

- `jvm-causa` — JVM clojure -M:test from `tools/causa/`
- `jvm-story` — JVM clojure -M:test from `tools/story/`
- `jvm-story-mcp` — JVM clojure -M:test from `tools/story-mcp/`
- `jvm-template` — JVM clojure -M:test from `tools/template/` (the existing `core_test.clj`)
- `node-test-pair2-mcp` — node-test from `tools/pair2-mcp/` (it's an npm package; shadow-cljs `:node-test` target)

Where the consolidated shadow-cljs `:node-test` already covers the CLJS-side, no extra CLJS job needed. JVM and out-of-consolidated-tree node-test surfaces are the gaps.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-repo-best-practice-2026-05-13.md` §5 / Bead candidate 2 — P1
notes: PR #552: ci(tools): add per-tool test jobs to test.yml — jvm-tools-causa/story/story-mcp/template + node-test-tools-pair2-mcp. https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/552

title: [ci] release.yml: add reagent-slim to deploy-leaf matrix + pre-release JVM test gate

title: [bug] :platforms metadata not honoured on reg-cofx (Spec 011 SSR contract)
description:
## Spec contract violated

`:platforms` metadata on registrations should gate fx execution by the active frame's `:platform`. Spec 011 §634-638 and `Spec-Schemas.md` `:rf/registration-metadata` `:platforms` row both require the gate on BOTH `reg-fx` AND `reg-cofx`.

Impl honours `:platforms` only inside `fx.cljc` (gates fx execution). `cofx.cljc` reads no `:platforms` key and runs cofx universally regardless of frame `:platform`.

This is a hard SSR contract — a cofx tagged `:platforms #{:client}` should no-op on a server-side render.

## Proposed fix

Add a `:platforms` filter in `cofx.cljc`'s injection path so a cofx with `:platforms #{:client}` no-ops on a `:platform :server` frame. Likely a 3-line addition mirroring `fx.cljc` L114.

## Sites

- `implementation/core/src/re_frame/fx.cljc` L114 — the existing fx gate (model)
- `implementation/core/src/re_frame/cofx.cljc` L92-99 — the missing gate
- `spec/011-SSR.md` L634-638 — the normative contract
- `spec/Spec-Schemas.md` L134 — schema row

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-spec-impl-alignment-2026-05-13.md` §Findings 1.2 / 3.3 / 5.3 (cross-listed three times)
notes: PR #557: gate inject-cofx on :platforms (mirror fx.cljc); emit :rf.cofx/skipped-on-platform; spec 009 + 011 updated; tests for matching/non-matching/absent/per-frame-override/2-arity-value cases.
