title: [BLOCKING] Bump CI Java from 17 to 21 — closure-compiler v20250407 needs Java 21+
description:
**Severity**: BLOCKING — every CLJS test job currently fails on `origin/main`.

**Root cause**: rf2-rlpo bumped ClojureScript to 1.12.145, which ships closure-compiler v20250407. That compiler is built for class file version 65.0 (Java 21). CI runs `java-version: '17'` everywhere (class file 61.0).

**Error**: `UnsupportedClassVersionError: com/google/javascript/jscomp/CompilerOptions has been compiled by a more recent version of the Java Runtime (class file version 65.0), this version of the Java Runtime only recognizes class file versions up to 61.0`.

**Evidence**: PR #477 (doc-only spec/API.md edit) sees 7 CLJS jobs fail with this exact error. The failure is not caused by the PR — every CLJS PR will hit this.

**Fix scope**: Sweep `java-version: '17'` → `'21'` across:
- `.github/workflows/test.yml` — 21 instances
- `.github/workflows/release.yml` — 3+ instances

Total ~24 occurrences.

**Validation**: After bump, re-run the failed jobs on PR #477; they should pass (the PR change is doc-only).

**Risk**: Java 21 is LTS and Temurin-supported; no functional impact on Clojure builds.

**Action**: Single PR. Title: `ci: bump Java 17 → 21 — required by closure-compiler v20250407 (CLJS 1.12.145)`.

**Adjacent**: rf2-3aks bumped Node 20 → 24 in workflows but did not also bump Java. The deps.edn Clojure bump (rf2-rlpo) implicitly required Java 21 but CI didn't follow.
notes: Identified during PR #477 review. Blocks all CLJS PR merges until landed. Pre-alpha — fix directly; no back-compat concerns.
close_reason: merged in 2026-05-13 batch landing wave

title: REGRESSION: browser-test plain-fn-under-non-default-frame fails post-Stage-4-E
description:
PR #250 (Stage 4-E render-to-static-markup) broke a browser test on main.

## The failure

CLJS browser-test (shadow-cljs :browser-test, headless Chromium) job fails on main with:

  FAIL in (plain-fn-under-non-default-frame) (re_frame/cross_spec_cljs_test.cljs:498:17)
  FAIL in (plain-fn-under-non-default-frame) (re_frame/cross_spec_cljs_test.cljs:512:17)

Plus warnings:
- 'You are calling ReactDOMClient.createRoot() on a container that has already been passed to createRoot() before.'
- 'The current testing environment is not configured to support act(...)'

## Likely cause

Stage 4-E added the dom.server ns + parity tests. May have:
- Disturbed the test harness's mount/unmount lifecycle (the createRoot warning suggests a root re-creation issue)
- Introduced a test that doesn't clean up properly, leaking state into plain-fn-under-non-default-frame

## Reproducer

cd implementation && npm run test:browser

The failure surfaces consistently on main as of 2026-05-11.

## Acceptance

- plain-fn-under-non-default-frame test passes
- ReactDOMClient.createRoot warning gone (or proven unrelated)
- act(...) warning gone (or proven unrelated)
- All other browser tests still pass

P1 — main CI is red.
notes:
Root cause was NOT mount-leakage from Stage 4-E parity tests; it was a load-order-dependent bug in the :adapter/current-frame and :adapter/current-component late-bind hooks. Each of the four adapter ns's (reagent, reagent-slim, uix, helix) overwrote those hooks at ns-load time. In the in-tree shadow-cljs build that loads all four, the LAST-LOADED adapter silently won — and the cross-spec test runs the bridge but the hook was reading slim's *current-component* (always nil during stock-Reagent renders). Stage 4-E shifted the load order; the underlying fragility predated it. Fix wraps each adapter's reader in a routing closure that consults (substrate-adapter/current-adapter) and runs THIS adapter's reader only when this adapter is the installed one — otherwise chains to the previously-installed reader. PR #256: https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/256. Tests: 406/982/0 browser-test (was 404/980/2). 406/961/0 node-test. Elision green.
PR #256 CI: all 18 checks GREEN (run 25637297170, conclusion: success). browser-test 1m28s pass, node-test 46s pass, examples 3m19s pass, elision pass, bundle isolation pass, all 11 JVM jobs pass, lockstep pass.
close_reason: Closed

title: rf2-froe pre-existing elision regression: schemas reason strings survive in prod bundle
description:
Following rf2-froe (set-schema-validator! seam), the production-elision verifier (npm run test:elision) reports 4 failures on baseline main:

- [FAIL] re-frame.schemas/validate-app-db!: sentinel "App-db at path " expected ABSENT, was PRESENT
- [FAIL] re-frame.schemas/validate-event!: sentinel " payload failed schema " expected ABSENT, was PRESENT
- [FAIL] re-frame.schemas/validate-sub-return!: sentinel " return value failed schema " expected ABSENT, was PRESENT
- [FAIL] re-frame.schemas/validate-cofx!: sentinel " injected value failed schema " expected ABSENT, was PRESENT

These were ABSENT before rf2-froe — verified by running test:elision against the rf2-froe parent commit. The likely cause: rf2-froe restructured the validate-*! fns to gate on `(when (and interop/debug-enabled? @validator-fn) ...)` instead of the simple `(when interop/debug-enabled? ...)`. Per Spec 009 §Production builds L329: `(when (and X interop/debug-enabled?) ...)` does NOT constant-fold reliably — "Closure can't statically rule out X, and the dead branch survives into the bundle."

The fix is likely to invert the gate — outer `(when interop/debug-enabled? ...)` for DCE, inner `(when @validator-fn ...)` for the runtime check.

Discovered while implementing rf2-r2uh (boundary-validation interceptor). My change does NOT regress this — same 4 failures appear in baseline and with my changes. Filing as a separate bead per CLAUDE.md guidance: "when implementing surfaces a spec gap/inconsistency, file a bd bead".
close_reason: Fixed by rf2-qlx2 (615bc82): restore C-000.35 elision gate for validate-*! sentinels — landed on main between my discovery and PR. My rf2-r2uh branch rebased onto it; elision passes.

title: REGRESSION: rf2-froe broke production elision — 4 schema sentinels leak into prod bundle
description:
PR #237 (rf2-froe) broke C-000.35 strict elision. The CLJS production elision CI test (Spec 009 / rf2-11hn) now fails on main with 4 sentinel violations:

[FAIL] re-frame.schemas/validate-app-db!: sentinel 'App-db at path ' expected ABSENT, was PRESENT
[FAIL] re-frame.schemas/validate-event!: sentinel ' payload failed schema ' expected ABSENT, was PRESENT
[FAIL] re-frame.schemas/validate-sub-return!: sentinel ' return value failed schema ' expected ABSENT, was PRESENT
[FAIL] re-frame.schemas/validate-cofx!: sentinel ' injected value failed schema ' expected ABSENT, was PRESENT

Root cause hypothesis: the new validator-fn / explainer-fn atom-based seam in re-frame.schemas no longer presents Closure with a clear DCE-able shape for the validate-*! function bodies. The old hard-coded resolve-style pattern was either inside a goog.DEBUG branch or yielded a static call site that DCE could eliminate. The new atom-deref pattern is dynamic and Closure can't prove the body is unreachable in :advanced.

Fix path:
- Wrap the dev-time validate-*! fns' bodies in (when ^boolean js/goog.DEBUG ...) or similar elision gate so Closure can drop the entire body in prod.
- Verify the new validate-with-registered-fn path (the boundary-validation seam) does NOT take the gate — that fn intentionally runs in prod (boundary validation IS prod validation).
- Two distinct call paths needed: dev-time validate-*! (gated) + boundary validate-with-registered-fn (ungated). Already separated per rf2-froe's design but the gate apparently fell off.

Run the test locally: cd implementation && npm run test:elision

Severity: P1 — constitutional C-000.35 violation. Every push to main since PR #237 has had red CI on this step.
notes:
Fix landed in PR #240 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/240

Root cause confirmed: the combined gate predicate (and interop/debug-enabled? @validator-fn) was not a compile-time constant. The atom deref defeated Closure's reachability proof, so the four reason strings survived advanced compilation with goog.DEBUG=false.

Fix shape: restructured each of the four validate-*! entry points so interop/debug-enabled? is the OUTERMOST gate (matching the pattern in registrar.cljc, trace.cljc, router.cljc, subs.cljc, views.cljs). The @validator-fn deref now lives inside that gate.

Boundary path (validate-with-registered-fn, explain-with-registered-fn) intentionally untouched per rf2-r2uh contract.

Local verification:
- npm run test:elision: PASS — all 4 sentinels ABSENT in production, PRESENT in control.
- cd implementation/schemas && clojure -M:test: 38 tests, 136 assertions, 0 failures, 0 errors.
close_reason: Closed

title: Consider reimplementing re-frame-pair2 app-db/reset on top of rf/reset-frame-db!
description:
Why this suggestion exists:
`app-db/reset` is documented as a working pair-tool write op (`skills/re-frame-pair2/SKILL.md:106`, `skills/re-frame-pair2/docs/capabilities.md:86`), but its implementation appears to target the wrong API surface. In `skills/re-frame-pair2/scripts/runtime.cljs:118-138`, `app-db-reset!` logs the change and then tries to locate a frame-local `:app-db` container through `(rf/handler-meta :frame frame-id)`. The public implementation API does not expose the container that way: frame metadata is config/lifecycle data, while `rf/get-frame-db` is a value-form accessor. re-frame2 already exposes `rf/reset-frame-db!` as the canonical Tool-Pair write surface for this exact use case (`implementation/core/src/re_frame/core.cljc:1166`).

As written, the helper can plausibly report `{:ok? true}` without actually mutating state or recording the synthetic epoch that `restore-epoch` depends on.

Suggestions to consider:
- Rebuild `app-db-reset!` on top of `rf/reset-frame-db!` instead of trying to reach a frame container through registrar metadata.
- Surface the real success/failure result from `rf/reset-frame-db!` rather than always claiming success.
- Add a runtime or fixture test that asserts `app-db/reset` both changes `app-db` and appends the expected synthetic epoch record.
close_reason: Stale in_progress cleanup — fix/docs/test commits all merged to origin/main per git log sweep 2026-05-13. Bead status was never flipped after PR merge. Spot-checked rf2-cfig: all 4 Phase 2 sub-deliverables landed (5-fixtures, CLJS-smoke, Playwright example, elision-probe). Mike can re-open any that turn out partial.

title: Consider tightening multi-frame operating-frame semantics in re-frame-pair2 runtime
description:
Why this suggestion exists:
The runtime says multi-frame sessions should treat ambiguity as unsafe for writes, but the current implementation does not consistently enforce that contract. In `skills/re-frame-pair2/scripts/runtime.cljs:69`, `current-frame` falls back to `:rf/default` whenever that frame is registered, even when multiple frames exist and no frame has been selected. `pair-dispatch-sync!` then only raises `:ambiguous-frame` when frame resolution returns nil (`skills/re-frame-pair2/scripts/runtime.cljs:454-456`), so mutating ops can silently land in `:rf/default` instead of refusing.

There is a second inconsistency in the same area: `subs-sample` calls `@(rf/subscribe query-v)` with no frame threading (`skills/re-frame-pair2/scripts/runtime.cljs:194-200`). That means a prior `select-frame!` does not affect this op the way the runtime's operating-frame model suggests. The docs currently promise the opposite: `SKILL.md:76`, `docs/initial-spec.md:205`, and `docs/capabilities.md:88` all say mutating ops refuse on `:ambiguous-frame`, and the capabilities matrix marks wrong-frame routing as done.

Suggestions to consider:
- Make `current-frame` return nil when multiple frames are registered and no explicit/session frame has been chosen, so mutating ops really refuse.
- Thread the selected or explicit frame through `subs-sample` and any similar helper that currently relies on `rf/subscribe`'s default frame resolution.
- Add runtime-level tests for ambiguous multi-frame sessions and for `select-frame!` affecting read helpers.
notes: PR #190 (merged 2026-05-10): fix landed on main — current-frame returns nil under ambiguity, subs-sample threads the operating frame, 17-test multi_frame_test suite passes (bb). Bead is now effectively resolved; leaving open for Mike to close.
close_reason: Already shipped in PR #190 (2026-05-10). Fix + 17-test bb suite landed on main. Bead never closed; stale in_progress cleanup.

title: Fix UIx/Helix derived-value baseline and add regression coverage
description:
Why this issue exists:
Both non-Reagent adapters seed their derived-value watch baseline from the raw source container instead of the derived value itself. In implementation/adapters/uix/src/re_frame/adapter/uix.cljs:84 and implementation/adapters/helix/src/re_frame/adapter/helix.cljs:88, `prev-state` starts as `(deref s)`, but the watch callback compares that baseline against `new-derived`. On the first source update, a derived computation that stays value-equal can still emit a spurious downstream notification whenever the derived value is not identical to the raw source value, for example `(odd? x)`, counts, projections, or maps.

What needs to be done:
- Seed the baseline from the current derived value rather than the raw source value in both adapters.
- Add regression coverage for the first-update/no-op case where the source changes but the derived value remains `=`.
- Prefer direct adapter-level tests under implementation/adapters/uix/test and implementation/adapters/helix/test; example-smoke coverage alone is too indirect for this behavior.

Notes:
This is easy to miss because the Reagent adapter has different internals and the UIx/Helix test trees currently provide little or no focused coverage for derived-container notification semantics.
notes: PR #192 (MERGED 2026-05-10): seeded UIx + Helix derived-value baselines from derived value, added 7 regression cases each adapter under implementation/adapters/{uix,helix}/test/re_frame/adapter/*_derived_value_baseline_cljs_test.cljs
close_reason: Already shipped in PR #192 (2026-05-10) — fix + uix/helix derived-value regression tests landed. Bead never closed; stale in_progress cleanup.

title: Spec 009 production-elision constitutional contradiction: schema-validation-failure recovery splits dev/prod despite "all tracing eliminated in prod" claim
description:
Audit rf2-ra02 finding F-1, ranked second of top-3 most load-bearing.

Quote (audit-009 line 45): '§Default behaviour for :rf.error/schema-validation-failure says recovery is :no-recovery (dev) / :replaced-with-default (prod) (line 579). But the spec's lead claim (line 13) is "All tracing is compile-time eliminated in production builds. No exceptions." Spec-Schemas line 250 lists validate-app-db! / validate-event! / validate-cofx! / validate-sub-return! behind the same interop/debug-enabled? gate — i.e. schema validation IS dev-only. So either (a) there is no "(prod)" recovery row to discuss because validation isn't running, or (b) validation runs in prod and the §Production builds claim is overstated. This is the rf2-uuoj C-000.35 territory ("behavioural-equivalence-under-elision"). 009 has not ratified what behaviour, if any, survives the gate.'

Why load-bearing (audit-009 §3 line 72-81): :rf.error/schema-validation-failure is the only category in §Default behaviour by category that splits '(dev) / (prod)' on its recovery — every other row is single-valued. The split contradicts the spec's lead claim that production elides ALL tracing. The decision matters because Spec 010 (Schemas) builds boundary-validation policy on top of it.

Constitutional violation per audit-criterion #5: 000-Vision Goal-tier commitment ('production-elision') contradicted by the §Default behaviour table.

Resolution per audit R-L1: (a) strict elision — recovery split is fictional, remove it; (b) schemas survive elision — narrow the lead claim; (c) per-frame opt. Auditor leans (a). This is rf2-uuoj's C-000.35 held clause.

title: Spec 005 documents [:rf.machine/spawn ...] as canonical fx-id but impl registers only :spawn / :destroy-machine
description:
Audit rf2-goxn finding #1, ranked top-2 most load-bearing.

Quote (audit-005 §3 #2, line 46-47): '§Top-level boot-time spawn (L1116) presents [:rf.machine/spawn ...] as canonical from outside a machine handler. Impl registers only :spawn / :destroy-machine (machines.cljc:1172-1173). A user following the L1116 worked example gets :rf.error/no-such-fx. Capability matrix :actor/spawn-destroy row (L1796) overstates ("Already specced"). Either the spec drops the namespaced form or the impl aliases. Decision is the gating question.'

Audit finding #1 quote (audit-005 line 16): 'Two parallel canonical fx-id surfaces — :spawn / :destroy-machine (internal) AND :rf.machine/spawn / :rf.machine/destroy (top-level) — only the unqualified pair is wired. Impl: machines.cljc:1172-73 registers only :spawn / :destroy-machine.'

Worked-example-fails per audit criterion #6: a user copying the L1116 boot-time worked example gets :rf.error/no-such-fx because :rf.machine/spawn is not registered.

Cross-spec inconsistency report (audit-005 §4): Conventions §Reserved fx-ids row is wrong — real fx-ids are :raise / :spawn / :destroy-machine (internal) plus the unregistered :rf.machine/spawn / :rf.machine/destroy.

Resolution per audit §5 medium: drop :rf.machine/spawn from the spec, OR alias it to :spawn in impl. Either fix is small; the decision is the work.

title: Spec 005 :invoke destroy resolves actor id via magic key :pending; auth-flow worked example silently leaks
description:
Audit rf2-goxn finding #2, ranked top-1 most load-bearing.

Quote (audit-005 §3 #1, line 42-43): '§Desugaring rules (L1278) says the runtime "tracks which key the user's :on-spawn wrote". Impl just reads (get-in snapshot [:data :pending]) (machines.cljc:521). The user MUST write :pending as their slot key, or destroy silently no-ops. The auth-flow worked example (L1366) writes :auth-actor and would silently leak a child actor on every :authenticating → :authenticated transition. Highest-severity correctness drift; user-visible from following the spec literally.'

Spec citation: 005-StateMachines.md L1278 (the prose claim) and L1366 (the auth-flow worked example using :auth-actor).
Impl citation: implementation/machines/src/re_frame/machines.cljc:521 (unconditional (get-in snapshot [:data :pending])).

This is a worked-example-fails bug per audit criterion #6: an example in the spec, executed as written, would fail / produce wrong behaviour (silent leak).

Resolution options per audit §5 medium: A) commit to :pending and document loudly; B) add an explicit :id-key slot to :invoke; C) pass the resolved id to a destroy-time fn. Pick one.

title: Spec 002 drain-loop pseudocode :dispatch fx fails to forward envelope, contradicts cascade-propagation contract
description:
Audit rf2-8syd finding F18, ranked top-3 most load-bearing.

Quote (audit-002 line 40): '§Per-frame and per-call overrides commits to "All three override types propagate transitively through any depth of :fx [:dispatch ...] cascade … envelope-field-copying when queueing children." But the §Drain-loop pseudocode (lines 857–858) shows :dispatch fx implementation as (dispatch frame {:event ev}) — only :event and frame are forwarded. Where do :fx-overrides, :interceptor-overrides, :trace-id, :origin, :source get into the child envelope? An AI literally implementing the pseudocode produces children with NO inherited overrides — directly violating the cascade-propagation contract.'

Why load-bearing (audit-002 line 53): tests, story fixtures, and per-frame stubbing rely on cascade-propagation. The pseudocode at line 857 (:dispatch fx) builds a child envelope with only :event — no :fx-overrides, no :interceptor-overrides, no :trace-id, no :origin. An AI that implements 002's pseudocode literally produces a runtime where :fx-overrides only apply to depth-0 dispatches. The conformance corpus may catch this if there's a depth-1 fixture, but the prose claim and the pseudocode disagree, and the pseudocode is what the AI will copy.

Resolution: fix the pseudocode at line 857 to forward the full envelope (or copy the inheritable subset). Alternatively, hoist a merge-from-parent-envelope step into the pseudocode and name it. Add a fixture asserting that depth-2+ dispatches inherit :fx-overrides.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #150 — 002 drain-loop pseudocode :dispatch fx envelope-forwarding fix (commit eb70ca8).

title: Spec 002 fx-handler signature contradiction: documented binary (fn [m event]) vs drain-loop pseudocode (fx-handler frame args)
description:
Audit rf2-8syd finding F5, ranked top-3 most load-bearing.

Quote (audit-002 line 27): 'Line 540: "reg-fx's primary signature in re-frame2 is binary: (fn [m event] ...)" and line 553: "m is the same map the originating event handler received." Line 838–839 pseudocode: (let [fx-handler (lookup-fx frame fx-id)] (fx-handler frame args)) — calls with the frame record, not the handler's context map. These cannot both be the contract. The handler context map and the frame record are different shapes; an AI implementor would pick one and the conformance would diverge.'

Why load-bearing (audit-002 line 50): every fx in the runtime — the four reserved (:dispatch, :dispatch-later, :db, :raise/:spawn) and every user fx — depends on the calling convention. The §Async effects section painstakingly establishes that the binary signature is 'm, the originating handler's context map' and cites this for why (:frame m) works. The drain-loop pseudocode then passes the frame record. An AI implementor running :fx [[:dispatch [:foo]]] from the pseudocode would write (rf/dispatch event {:frame (:frame m)}) against an m that IS the frame record, which would fail. This is the kind of contradiction that breaks the whole 'AI-implementable from spec alone' goal (000-Vision Goal 2).

Resolution: pick one. Either (a) the fx-handler receives the handler context map (m) and the pseudocode should pass envelope or the assembled context, or (b) the fx-handler receives the frame record and the documented signature should say so. The documented (:frame m) reads in §Async fx (line 564) imply (a).

title: [bug] timer 7guis #4 Playwright spec fails on CI — Reset doesn't return elapsed to 0 within 2s
description:
Main branch CI has been RED since PR #50 (rf2-lyj0 examples runnable + Playwright). The Examples (browser, headless Chromium) job consistently fails with:

  FAIL timer (7guis #4): Reset did not return elapsed to 0.0 within 2s
  Error: Reset did not return elapsed to 0.0 within 2s
    at Object.run (examples/playwright/timer.spec.cjs:51:11)

Other 6 example specs (counter, flight-booker, nine-states, routing, temperature, todomvc) all PASS.

## Local-vs-CI signal

The rf2-cfig agent (PR #53) reported: 'timer 7guis spec was flaky on the first run, passed on subsequent runs' on a local Windows worktree. On Linux CI runners, it fails every run for ~4 consecutive main commits.

## Three plausible causes

1. **Real bug** — the Reset event handler doesn't actually clear :elapsed in app-db, OR the state-machine that drives ticking keeps producing :tick events that re-increment :elapsed faster than the spec can observe a 0 value.

2. **CI timing** — Linux runner is slow enough (combined with Reagent re-render / Playwright DOM-poll cadence) that the 2s assertion window expires before :elapsed is observably 0.

3. **Element-locator issue** — the Playwright spec is querying the wrong element, or querying a stale render.

Investigation order: read examples/seven_guis/src/timer/* (or wherever the timer impl lives), then examples/playwright/timer.spec.cjs:51 and the surrounding test code, then check whether the Reset event/transition actually pure-zeroes :elapsed in app-db.

## Fixes by cause

- Real bug: fix the Reset semantics. Probably a state-machine action that doesn't run, or an :elapsed slot that's reseeded by an in-flight :tick.
- CI timing: bump assertion timeout to 5s; add Playwright auto-retry on first failure of this spec; OR add a settle wait before the assertion.
- Locator issue: pin the assertion to the correct DOM element with a stable test-id.

## Why P1

- Main branch has been RED for 4 commits.
- New PRs that don't touch the timer still inherit a red main-branch CI signal at merge time.
- The 'green main' invariant is foundational; restoring it is more important than landing the next feature.

## Out of scope

- Don't disable the timer spec to make CI green. Fix it or document the flake-and-retry path properly.

Discovered from main-branch CI runs 25553697711 (and 3 prior).
close_reason: Already shipped on main — fix commit e296c072 (timer 7guis #4 retire stale ticks on Reset). Bead never closed; stale in_progress cleanup. Note: another timer-related fix shipped under rf2-u95c (823f8bcd) — separate concern.
