title: Cross-MCP naming conventions: lock verb shapes across pair2-mcp / story-mcp / causa-mcp
description:
**Source**: rf2-fvy7o findings doc, finding #4 + recommendation #5.

The MCP triplet (pair2-mcp shipped, story-mcp shipped, causa-mcp planned) should feel like one product. Today the naming axes drift:

- **Read-shape verbs.** pair2 uses bare names (`snapshot`, `trace-window`, `watch-epochs`). story uses `list-` / `get-` / `read-` prefixes (`list-stories`, `get-variant`, `read-failures`). causa-mcp plans `get-` for everything (`get-trace-buffer`, `get-epoch-history`, `get-app-db`). No rule.
- **`snapshot` overloaded.** pair2-mcp's `snapshot` is a coarse multi-frame read. story-mcp's `snapshot-identity` is a content-hash. Same verb, two meanings across siblings.
- **`dispatch` shared.** Both pair2-mcp (shipped) and causa-mcp (planned) call it `dispatch` with different `:origin` tags. Naming is consistent — good — but the convention isn't documented.
- **Streaming pair.** Both pair2-mcp (shipped) and causa-mcp (planned) call them `subscribe` / `unsubscribe`. story-mcp has no streaming. Convention isn't documented.

## What to do

Add a `spec/Conventions.md` §"Cross-MCP naming conventions" subsection (or equivalent in `tools/README.md` — the latter may be the better home since `tools/` is where the MCP servers live). Lock:

- **Read verbs**: `get-<thing>` for single-entity reads (id → record); `list-<things>` for collection reads (no id, returns vector); bare verbs (`snapshot`, `trace-window`) reserved for derived projections / mega-ops that span multiple registry kinds.
- **Mutation verbs**: `dispatch`, `restore-<thing>`, `reset-<thing>`, `register-<thing>`, `unregister-<thing>`.
- **Streaming pair**: `subscribe` / `unsubscribe`. Topic vocabulary documented per-server but the pair is universal.
- **Escape hatch**: `eval-cljs` everywhere; always carries `:origin :<server-name>` tag.
- **Error category prefixes**: `:rf.error/*` for runtime; `:rf.epoch/*` for epoch-machinery; `:<server-name>.error/*` for server-specific failures.

## Audit + alignment

Audit the existing pair2-mcp and story-mcp catalogues against the locked convention. File follow-up rename beads for any drift (renames must keep back-compat aliases for one release minimum). causa-mcp's `003-Tool-Catalogue.md` (not yet written per `tools/causa-mcp/README.md` §Status) inherits the convention by reference.

## Why P2 not P1

No tool is broken today. The cost of misalignment is reasoner-side: the agent picks the wrong verb when both servers are attached. Worth fixing before causa-mcp implementation lands — that's the moment the triplet's surface area triples.
notes: PR: https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/735
close_reason: Closed

title: re-frame-pair2 references: lead with MCP form, demote bash-shim invocations
description:
**Source**: rf2-fvy7o findings doc, finding #3 + recommendation #4.

`references/mcp-transport.md:8` declares MCP path is preferred (~14× faster than the bash shims). But the canonical examples in `references/ops.md`, `references/recipes.md`, and `references/hot-reload-protocol.md` all use the bash-shim form throughout. An agent following the canonical recipes verbatim takes the slow path even when the MCP server is wired.

Examples:
- `references/ops.md:19-29` — every read op as `scripts/eval-cljs.sh '...'`.
- `references/recipes.md:61-63` — post-mortem recipe uses `scripts/eval-cljs.sh ...`.
- `references/recipes.md:135` — experiment loop uses `scripts/tail-build.sh --probe '...'`.
- `references/hot-reload-protocol.md:6` — canonical post-edit invocation uses `scripts/tail-build.sh`.

## What to do

Rewrite the three references to lead with the MCP-tool call form (e.g., `eval-cljs {form: "(rf/...)"}`) and demote the bash-shim form to a single back-compat appendix at the end of each file. The MCP→bash-shim mapping table currently in `references/mcp-transport.md:43-56` can stay where it is, but each individual recipe / op example should show MCP form first.

## Relation to other beads

- rf2-2d6na ("retire bash shims from allowed-tools post-MCP-rollout") covers the retirement-wiring change. This bead is the prose change that should land alongside or before that retirement so the recipes match the surviving surface.
- After the recipes flip, rf2-2d6na can move the bash shims to a back-compat tier with confidence that no canonical doc still routes through them.

## Verification

After landing: `grep -rn 'scripts/' skills/re-frame-pair2/references/` should match only an explicit "back-compat" section in each file, not in any of the canonical example positions.
notes: PR #704 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/704

title: CI: diff MCP tool descriptors against skill allowed-tools (drift smoke-test)
description:
**Source**: rf2-fvy7o findings doc, finding #6 + recommendation #2.

When pair2-mcp added `subscribe` / `unsubscribe` (rf2-hq49) the skill's `allowed-tools` front-matter didn't update. The drift went undetected until rf2-fvy7o audited the surfaces. Same shape will repeat when story-mcp or causa-mcp grow tools.

## What to do

Add a CI gate that fails when the MCP server's exposed tool catalogue is not a subset (or strict equal — pick the policy) of the skill's `allowed-tools` for the canonical consumer skill.

Concretely:

1. Write a test (placement TBD — likely `tools/pair2-mcp/test/` or repo-level `tests/skills/`) that:
   - Reads `tools/pair2-mcp/src/re_frame_pair2_mcp/tools.cljs` and extracts the `tool-descriptors` `:name` values.
   - Reads `skills/re-frame-pair2/SKILL.md` front-matter (YAML parse) and extracts the `allowed-tools` whose ids match `mcp__re-frame-pair2__*`.
   - Fails if the two sets diverge.
2. Mirror for story-mcp once a consumer skill exists (see rf2-fvy7o decision rec #3).
3. Mirror for causa-mcp once it ships.
4. Wire into `.github/workflows/` alongside the existing bundle-isolation / elision smoke tests.

## Policy decision

Strict equality (every server tool must be allowed by the canonical consumer) is the cleaner contract. Alternative is "subset" (skill may opt out of tools the server exposes). Prefer strict equality — drift either way is a surface a maintainer wants to see.
close_reason: Closed

title: pair2 streaming: extend :sensitive? guard to epoch records (defense-in-depth)
description:
Follow-on from rf2-3cted (PR #631). The streaming guard added in #631 drops trace events tagged :sensitive? before they hit subs. Epoch records currently don't carry a top-level :sensitive? — :with-redacted handles per-event payload redaction inside the epoch. This is intentional per the bead's scope hold.

Question to settle: should the streaming pipe also gate epoch records themselves on something like 'any constituent trace is :sensitive?' or 'epoch carries :rf.privacy/sensitive? tag'? If yes, decide the propagation rule (is an epoch sensitive if any of its events were? if its event-vector was? if a specific interceptor flagged it?) and add a streaming-side filter.

If not, document the deliberate separation in spec/Conventions.md or spec/009-Instrumentation.md so future implementers don't re-litigate.

Source: rf2-3cted PR #631 scope-held note. See skills/re-frame-pair2/references/vocabulary.md §Privacy posture for the current separation rationale.
notes:
Audit rf2-ok47g (ai/findings/sensitive-elision-audit-20260513-1748.md §G4) recommends extending scope:
- Spec the epoch-level :sensitive? propagation rule using OR-reduce per spec/009 line 1174 — 'epoch is sensitive iff any constituent trace event is sensitive'.
- Compute once at epoch-assembly time so consumers do one read (no per-event walk on the consumer side).
- Push the cascade-wide rollup into the epoch assembler, not into each consumer.
- Upgraded P3 -> P2 in priority because the consumer-side defensive gates (pair2-mcp scrub-snapshot-sensitive) are currently no-ops until both this and rf2-isdwf (runtime stamping) land.

title: Investigation: skills ↔ MCP-server interaction — efficient, elegant, complete?
description:
Audit how the three Claude Code skills (re-frame-pair2, re-frame-pair-improver2, story-related skills if any) interact with the three MCP servers (pair2-mcp, story-mcp, causa-mcp). Specifically:

**Efficiency**: Is each skill calling the right tools? Are response budgets respected (rf2-ll0yq 5K-token posture)? Are there round-trip patterns that could be collapsed into a single MCP tool? Are skills loading reference docs they don't need on every invocation?

**Elegance**: Are the contracts crisp? Does the skill 'know' what the MCP server can do, or does it have to grope? Is the tool naming consistent across the three MCP triplet? Do skills duplicate logic that should live in the MCP server (or vice versa)?

**Completeness**: Does every MCP tool have a skill consumer? Does every skill responsibility have an MCP tool backing it? Are there obvious gaps — e.g. skills needing to read app-DB but no tool exposing it, or MCP tools no skill knows how to invoke?

**Output**: Write findings to ai/findings/skills-mcp-interaction-<timestamp>.md with: matrix of skills × MCP tools, list of concrete drift/gap/duplication observations, prioritised recommendations. File beads for each actionable insight.

**Surfaces to read**: skills/*/SKILL.md, skills/*/references/, tools/pair2-mcp/, tools/story-mcp/, tools/causa-mcp/ (if exists), spec/Tool-Pair.md, spec/009-Instrumentation.md.
close_reason:
Investigation complete. Findings doc at ai/findings/skills-mcp-interaction-20260513-1519.md.

Six beads filed:

- rf2-aks1t (P1) — re-frame-pair2: add subscribe/unsubscribe MCP tools + streaming recipes
- rf2-1v7tu (P1) — Decide: who owns the story-mcp consumer story (Mike-decision)
- rf2-flzdp (P2) — CI: diff MCP tool descriptors against skill allowed-tools (drift smoke-test)
- rf2-qdgwz (P2) — re-frame-pair2 references: lead with MCP form, demote bash-shim invocations
- rf2-mzf1r (P2) — Cross-MCP naming conventions: lock verb shapes across triplet
- rf2-87kv7 (P3) — re-frame-pair-improver2: allow discover-app for live tool-catalogue introspection

Confirmed existing beads cover overlap: rf2-ll0yq (token budget), rf2-2d6na (retire bash shims), rf2-bba4s (frame-meta sync), rf2-hvzsx (taxonomy dedupe), rf2-p6f0h (frame-meta spec drift). No duplicates filed.

Top takeaways:

1. pair2-mcp ↔ re-frame-pair2 skill is the only working axis — subscribe/unsubscribe added to MCP but not skill (rf2-aks1t).
2. story-mcp has zero declared consumer in any skill — 16 orphaned tools (rf2-1v7tu).
3. References still lead with bash-shim form despite MCP being recommended; 14× latency hit (rf2-qdgwz).

description:

## Sites
- tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/trace_bus.cljc — collect-trace! pushes every event onto the buffer unconditionally (line 63-71). Add a default :sensitive? filter at push time, with a Causa config flag to opt-in for engineers debugging redaction.

Causa already has a separate AI Co-Pilot redaction layer (default-redaction-settings in ai_co_pilot_helpers.cljc) — that's panel-side, redacts already-visible content. This bead is about the framework's :sensitive? flag that comes pre-stamped by the registrar; Causa's buffer needs to honour it before any panel touches the events.

title: [causa] Phase 5 — MCP Server UI hookup (panel-side consumer)
description:
Phase 5 of rf2-5aw5v (Causa v1.0 epic). Implement the **UI-side** MCP Server hookup — the Causa panel surfaces that consume / expose the causa-mcp surface. **Distinct** from rf2-006p0 (which scaffolds the `tools/causa-mcp/` spec) and from the eventual `tools/causa-mcp/` jar implementation.

## SPEC DEFICIENCY — read before dispatching

`tools/causa/spec/010-MCP-Server.md` (449 lines) is the **server-side** contract — the catalogue of tools the causa-mcp jar exposes to AI agents over stdio JSON-RPC. The Causa **panel-side** UI for the MCP integration is **not deeply specified**. Specifically the spec doesn't articulate:

- A dedicated "MCP" panel in the sidebar (the 16-panel inventory in `000-Vision.md` doesn't include one)
- A UI surface for seeing active MCP subscriptions (`subscribe` / `unsubscribe` lifecycle from L141-156 of the MCP spec)
- A UI surface for filtering by `:origin :causa-mcp` (the spec describes the colour-coding in `007-UX-IA.md` §Colour system L209 — `:pair-origin` gets indigo; `:causa-mcp` needs a colour assigned)
- A UI surface for the eight `notifications/progress`-shaped streaming subscriptions an agent might have open

The strongest UI hooks that DO exist in the spec:
- `tools/causa/spec/000-Vision.md` §How re-frame2 unlocks: "`:origin` opt on dispatch — Filter by actor: 'show me only the dispatches Claude issued.'"
- `tools/causa/spec/010-MCP-Server.md` §Origin tagging L284-323: trace-bus filtering by `:origin :causa-mcp` is the canonical surface
- `tools/causa/spec/007-UX-IA.md` §Colour system L209 — `:pair-origin` is indigo; causa-mcp needs a parallel colour (likely a second indigo variant or a distinct hue — DECISION NEEDED)
- `tools/causa/spec/010-MCP-Server.md` §Chrome DevTools MCP co-install L327-377 — co-installation UX hint

Recommend filing a sibling bead "spec — tools/causa/spec/MCP-UI-Surface.md" before code-dispatch. The spec should articulate:

1. **Does an MCP-dedicated panel exist?** Or is the MCP surface only manifest via (a) the `:origin :causa-mcp` colour-coding axis and (b) a Settings → MCP section showing connection status / active subscriptions?
2. **What colour does `:origin :causa-mcp` get?** (`007-UX-IA.md` doesn't lock this)
3. **How are active MCP subscriptions surfaced?** A small ribbon? A Settings tab?
4. **Does Causa expose any MCP surfaces back to the agent?** (The spec is silent — the prevailing assumption is causa-mcp is the only direction, but the task wording "consumes/exposes" suggests Mike may want bidirectional.)

## Inferred minimum-viable hookup (if Mike chooses to proceed without spec-first)

If Mike wants to proceed inferentially:

- Add an MCP **subsection in Settings** (not a sidebar panel) showing: connection status (causa-mcp jar attached over nREPL? yes/no); active subscriptions (id + topic + filter + delivered count); origin-filter quick toggles ("show only :causa-mcp" / "hide :causa-mcp")
- Add a `:origin :causa-mcp` colour to the colour palette (lift from §Colour system; recommend a second indigo variant `#3D5BCC` or a distinct shade)
- Wire the trace-bus colour-coding in event-detail / causality-graph / event-log / trace panels to honour the new origin
- A small top-strip indicator: `🔌 MCP` chip when the causa-mcp jar is attached; tooltip shows session-id + active subscription count

## Implementation surface (assuming inferred path)

- `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/mcp_hookup.cljs` — connection-status detector + origin-filter store
- `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/settings/mcp_section.cljs` — Settings UI subsection
- Updates to `shell.cljs` for the top-strip `🔌 MCP` indicator
- Updates to colour-coding wiring in `event_detail.cljs`, `causality_graph.cljs`, future `event_log.cljs`, future `trace.cljs` panels

## Subscriptions to register

- `:rf.causa/mcp-connection-status` — boolean + session-id (probes for `re-frame2-causa-mcp.runtime/session-id` sentinel per `010-MCP-Server.md` L82-87)
- `:rf.causa/mcp-active-subscriptions` — vector of `{:sub-id :topic :filter :delivered}` per the streaming subscription surface
- `:rf.causa/origin-filter` — current origin-filter state (set of origins to include/exclude)

## Events to register

- `:rf.causa/set-origin-filter` (event-db) — toggle which `:origin` values are visible across panels
- `:rf.causa/mcp-connection-detected` (event-db) — fired when sentinel detected
- `:rf.causa/mcp-connection-lost` (event-db) — fired when sentinel disappears

## Fx to register

- `:rf.causa.fx/probe-mcp-runtime!` — periodic (10s) probe for the causa-mcp runtime sentinel via the framework's existing primitives (NOT a new framework surface)

## Data sources

- `re-frame2-causa-mcp.runtime/session-id` sentinel (set by the causa-mcp jar when it attaches)
- `:tags :origin` on trace events (the canonical Spec 009 axis)
- `tools/causa-mcp/`'s `subscribe` / `unsubscribe` lifecycle if any in-process notification surface exists (verify; may be agent-host-side only, in which case the UI sub is a no-op until causa-mcp publishes one)

## Test surface

`tools/causa/test/day8/re_frame2_causa/mcp_hookup_cljs_test.cljs`. Canonical cases:
- Connection detector correctly fires `:rf.causa/mcp-connection-detected` when sentinel appears
- Origin-filter restricts visible events across event-detail / causality-graph / trace panels
- `:origin :causa-mcp` colour-coding applied correctly
- Settings MCP subsection renders connection status + active subscriptions
- Top-strip `🔌 MCP` indicator visibility toggle correct

## Cross-references

- `tools/causa/spec/010-MCP-Server.md` — server-side contract
- `tools/causa/spec/000-Vision.md` §How re-frame2 unlocks — `:origin` filter UI hook
- `tools/causa/spec/007-UX-IA.md` §Colour system — `:origin` axis colour-coding (causa-mcp colour NEEDS A DECISION)
- rf2-006p0 — causa-mcp spec scaffold (sibling, separate concern)
- `spec/009-Instrumentation.md` §Filter vocabulary — `:origin` axis
- Parent epic: rf2-5aw5v

## Acceptance criteria

- MCP connection detector wired (sentinel probe runs)
- Settings → MCP subsection renders connection status + active subscriptions
- Top-strip `🔌 MCP` indicator works when causa-mcp attached
- `:origin :causa-mcp` colour-coding applied across panels
- Origin-filter toggles work across all relevant panels
- Tests cover the detector, the colour-coding, the filter
- `npm run test:cljs`, `test:browser`, `test:elision`, `test:examples` pass

## Constraints

- NO AI attribution
- Don't commit `ai/`, `decision-beads.md`, `findings/`
- Pre-alpha

## Decision needed (file separately if pursuing path-a)

If Mike wants the masterpiece-grade route, file a sibling bead "spec — tools/causa/spec/MCP-UI-Surface.md (panel + Settings + colour)" first; that spec resolves:
- Whether there's an MCP-dedicated sidebar panel or just Settings + origin filtering
- The colour assignment for `:origin :causa-mcp`
- The active-subscriptions UX
- Any bidirectional surface (Causa→agent) implied by "exposes" in the task wording
notes: PR #619 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/619. Three inferential decisions documented inline + in PR body: (a) dedicated sidebar panel — yes; (b) origin colour for :causa-mcp — cyan #06B6D4; (c) bidirectional Causa→agent surface — out of scope (causa-mcp jar concern). Each is a follow-on bead candidate.
close_reason: Closed

title: [causa] Phase 5 — AI Co-Pilot panel
description:
Phase 5 of rf2-5aw5v (Causa v1.0 epic). Implement the AI Co-Pilot panel per `tools/causa/spec/009-AI-CoPilot.md`.

## Scope

`tools/causa/spec/009-AI-CoPilot.md` (full file, 479 lines) is the contract. Per Lock #2 (ship at v1.0 in pull-only form) and Lock #8 (collapsed by default). Lock #12 (ephemeral conversation, no persistence). Lock #13 (NO voice / STT at v1.0).

Headline contracts:

- §Default state (L13-19): collapsed; subtle magenta `◇` cue glyph pulses every 8 seconds until first use. Toggle: `Ctrl+Shift+/` or click sidebar `Co-pilot` entry.
- §Panel layout (L21-51): 320px right rail when open. Title bar with model picker `⌗`, expand `⛶`, close `✕`. Conversation newest-at-bottom. Each turn: question (▸ in violet) → response.
- §Pull-only model (L53-74): Causa NEVER sends a token unless user submits. NO background narration, NO proactive alerts, NO auto-summaries, NO conversation resumption prompts. Model speaks only when spoken to (Lock #10).
- §Slash commands (L76-94): `/explain`, `/diff`, `/find`, `/rewind`, `/state`, `/why`, `/whatif`, `/clear`.
- §Provider abstraction (L96-113): Claude (default), OpenAI, Gemini, Local (Ollama), Custom. API keys stored ONLY in localStorage; never sent to Day8.
- §System prompt (L115-154): user-editable bundled default; includes the chip-encoding instruction (model emits `{:rf.copilot/chip <key> <value>}` for chip handles).
- §Tool / function calling (L156-182): read-only tool catalogue (`get-trace-buffer`, `get-epoch-history`, `get-app-db`, `get-machine-state`, `get-issues`, `get-handlers`, `get-source-coord`). NO `restore-epoch`, NO `reset-frame-db!`, NO `dispatch` through tool-use. Model PROPOSES via action chips; user clicks to confirm.
- §Result formatting (L184-206): markdown render; code blocks (cljs/edn/json); source-coord chips; **Causa data chips** (structured runtime identifiers); action chips. Streaming ~25 tokens/sec; first words ~600ms.
- §Causa data chips (L208-307): the model emits structured citations as inline edn fragments `{:rf.copilot/chip <key> <value>}`. Renderer parses at stream time and swaps for chip component. Four chip types:
  - `:dispatch-id` → ◆ → Events panel
  - `:path` → ▥ → App-db panel
  - `:epoch-number` → ⏵ → Bottom scrubber (passive)
  - `:handler-id` → ⚙ → Events panel → Open source
  Failure modes: unresolvable identifier renders greyed-out with `⚠`; empty path opens at deepest existing ancestor; cross-frame identifier shows frame badge.
- §Ephemeral conversation (L309-328): Lock #12 — per-session in-memory only. NOT persisted; NOT exported; cleared on tab reload + Causa close.
- §Redaction defaults (L330-368): privacy-by-default — event-vector args + app-db slice values are `<redacted>` by default; opt-in toggles per-category. Source coords + handler IDs + trace metadata always sent.
- §Voice / STT (L370-378): NOT at v1.0 (Lock #13). Text only. NO mic permission prompts. NO `🎙` button.
- §When the model is wrong (L380-421): three error states — API error / Model says I-don't-know / Model is confidently wrong. Mitigation: every claim references verifiable data; chips are live handles.
- §Performance (L436-444): zero CPU/network when collapsed or idle.
- §Empty state (L446-465).

## Implementation surface

- `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/copilot.cljs` — view (rail + conversation + input + chip renderer)
- `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/copilot_helpers.cljc` — chip parsing (`{:rf.copilot/chip ...}` extraction from streamed text); slash-command tokeniser; redaction filter
- `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/copilot_provider.cljs` — provider abstraction (Claude / OpenAI / Gemini / Local / Custom); tool-call dispatcher
- `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/copilot_tools.cljs` — read-only tool catalogue implementations (one tool per `get-*` surface)

## Subscriptions to register

- `:rf.causa/copilot-open?` — boolean (drives the collapsed/open state)
- `:rf.causa/copilot-conversation` — vector of `{:role :question/:answer :text ... :streaming? bool}` turns (ephemeral, in-memory only)
- `:rf.causa/copilot-provider` — current provider config
- `:rf.causa/copilot-cue-active?` — boolean (drives the `◇` pulse; goes to false after first use)
- `:rf.causa/copilot-redaction-settings` — per-category toggles
- `:rf.causa/copilot-streaming-token-count` — for the streaming progress UI

## Events to register

- `:rf.causa/copilot-toggle` (event-db) — open/close rail
- `:rf.causa/copilot-submit-question` (event-fx) — fires the LLM call (with redaction applied)
- `:rf.causa/copilot-tool-call` (event-fx) — handles model tool-call requests (read-only catalogue)
- `:rf.causa/copilot-tool-call-response` (event-db) — feeds response back to streaming
- `:rf.causa/copilot-stream-token` (event-db) — append a token to current answer turn
- `:rf.causa/copilot-stream-end` (event-db) — mark answer turn complete
- `:rf.causa/copilot-clear-conversation` (event-db) — Ctrl+L
- `:rf.causa/copilot-mark-first-use` (event-db) — stops the cue pulse forever
- `:rf.causa/copilot-set-provider` (event-db)
- `:rf.causa/copilot-set-redaction` (event-db)
- `:rf.causa/copilot-chip-clicked` (event-fx) — chip click handler; opens the relevant panel pointed at the chip's referent

## Fx to register

- `:rf.causa.fx/llm-stream` — wraps the chosen provider's streaming API (Claude / OpenAI / Gemini / Local Ollama / Custom URL). Reads API key from localStorage. Never sends to Day8. Tool-call round-trips happen client-side.

## Data sources (read-only tool catalogue mirrors causa-mcp's surface)

- `(rf/trace-buffer opts)` — for `get-trace-buffer`
- `(rf/epoch-history frame-id)` — for `get-epoch-history`
- `(rf/get-frame-db frame-id)` — for `get-app-db`
- `[:rf/machines <id>]` slot — for `get-machine-state`
- `(rf/trace-buffer {:op-type :error})` etc. — for `get-issues`
- `(rf/handlers kind)` + `(rf/handler-meta kind id)` — for `get-handlers`
- Source-coord registry — for `get-source-coord`

## Test surface

`tools/causa/test/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/copilot_cljs_test.cljs` + `copilot_helpers_cljc_test.cljc`. Canonical cases:
- Collapsed by default; toggle on `Ctrl+Shift+/`; cue `◇` pulses until first use then stops
- Pull-only: NO outbound LLM call without user submit (assert via fx-spy)
- Slash commands: each of the 8 commands routes to the right tool / format
- Tool catalogue read-only: assert NO `restore-epoch` / `reset-frame-db!` / `dispatch` in the tool set
- Action chips for mutation always require user click (no auto-execute)
- Chip parser: extracts `{:rf.copilot/chip <key> <value>}` fragments correctly; malformed edn renders literal (not a chip)
- Chip types: all four (`:dispatch-id`, `:path`, `:epoch-number`, `:handler-id`) produce correct click-targets
- Chip failure modes: unresolvable identifier greys out with `⚠`; empty path opens at deepest ancestor; cross-frame shows frame badge
- Redaction: event-vector args + app-db values are `<redacted>` by default; opt-in toggles unmask
- Conversation ephemeral: clear-on-reload + clear-on-close + Ctrl+L work
- No persistence: assert NO localStorage write for conversation buffer
- No voice surface: assert NO `🎙` button rendered; NO mic permission requested

## Cross-references

- `tools/causa/spec/009-AI-CoPilot.md` — full contract (full 479 lines is normative)
- `tools/causa/spec/DESIGN-RATIONALE.md` Lock #2, #8, #10, #12, #13 (all four shape this panel)

- Redaction defaults applied; opt-in toggles work

title: [causa] Phase 5 — Hydration Debugger panel
description:
Phase 5 of rf2-5aw5v (Causa v1.0 epic). Implement the Hydration Debugger panel per `tools/causa/spec/006-Hydration-Debugger.md`.

## Scope

`tools/causa/spec/006-Hydration-Debugger.md` (full file, 176 lines) is the contract. Headline contracts:

- §Visibility (L10-23): panel is **dormant** until first `:rf.ssr/hydration-mismatch` trace lands. Sidebar entry shows `◌` until then. On first mismatch: sidebar icon flashes red 600ms; Issues badge increments; co-pilot (if open) surfaces a contextual suggestion (no auto-open).
- §Substrate (L25-44): runtime emits `:rf.ssr/hydration-mismatch` with `:path` / `:server-tree` / `:client-tree` / `:server-hash` / `:client-hash` / `:frame` / `:view-id`. Plus the SSR payload stamped on the document at hydration time. Causa's preload captures the first client render-tree before any dispatch.
- §Layout (L46-65): side-by-side render-tree view. Divergent node pulses red (600ms expand-fade) and flagged with `⚠`. Hover divergent node → tooltip with the render-tree hash (bisectable).
- §Render-tree hash bisector (L67-86): every parent has a hash summarising its subtree. Hashes match at common ancestors and diverge at the first differing descendant — this is the bisection property per Spec 011 §Hydration equivalence rule. Causa renders hashes inline as faint chips; divergence path highlighted root → first differing node; click any hash chip → re-root at that node.
- §Multi-mismatch case (L88-100): list at panel top; click an entry → side-by-side rebases; arrow keys walk the list
- §Source-coord drilldown (L102-119): mismatch surfaces source coords for the divergent view's registration; falls back to dispatch / `:rf/hydrate` cofx via handler-coord fallback (Lock #11) with `(?)` annotation
- §Cause hypothesis (L121-137): one-line hypothesis based on divergence kind (different text / tag differs / attribute differs / children missing client / children missing server). The hypothesis is a hint, NOT an answer; links to panel pivots to verify.
- §Frame awareness (L139-145): per-frame hydration; switching frame picker re-filters. Cross-frame SSR renders both swimlanes.
- §Production posture (L147-156): dev-only. `:rf.ssr/hydration-mismatch` never emitted in prod. Causa's panel renders empty state in prod-like builds. Lazy-loaded on first activity (per `007-UX-IA.md` §Bundle splitting target <100KB extra).
- §Empty states (L158-176): two empty states — "No SSR in this app" / "SSR present, no mismatches" (the second is deliberately a positive result message — same as Schema-Timeline's "all schemas clean").

## Implementation surface

- `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/hydration.cljs` — view (side-by-side trees + mismatch list)
- `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/hydration_helpers.cljc` — render-tree diffing + hash-bisector walk + hypothesis classification (pure data)

## Subscriptions to register

- `:rf.causa/hydration-mismatches` — composite over `:rf.causa/trace-buffer` filtered to `:operation :rf.ssr/hydration-mismatch`
- `:rf.causa/selected-mismatch-detail` — composite over `:rf.causa/selected-mismatch-id`
- `:rf.causa/server-render-tree` — projection of the SSR payload stamped on the document
- `:rf.causa/first-client-render-tree` — captured by preload hook (must be wired in `preload.cljs`)
- `:rf.causa/divergence-hypothesis` — composite that classifies the divergence kind and produces the hypothesis line

## Events to register

- `:rf.causa/select-mismatch` (event-db)
- `:rf.causa/clear-mismatch-selection` (event-db)
- `:rf.causa/reroot-tree-view` (event-db) — click a hash chip → re-root
- `:rf.causa/capture-first-client-render-tree` (event-db) — fired by preload before first dispatch

## Data sources

- `:rf.ssr/hydration-mismatch` trace operation per Spec 011 §Hydration equivalence rule
- The SSR payload stamped on the document (server-side render tree)
- The first client render-tree captured by Causa's preload hook
- Spec 011 §Hydration equivalence rule — bisection property of render-tree hashes

## Test surface

`tools/causa/test/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/hydration_cljs_test.cljs` + `hydration_helpers_cljc_test.cljc`. Canonical cases:
- Panel dormant until first `:rf.ssr/hydration-mismatch`
- Sidebar icon flash + Issues badge increment on first mismatch
- Side-by-side renders divergent node with `⚠` flag + red pulse
- Hash bisector: divergence path correctly highlighted root → first differing node
- Click hash chip → re-root works
- Multi-mismatch list: arrow keys walk; selecting an entry rebases
- Source-coord fallback (Lock #11) renders `(?)` annotation correctly
- Hypothesis classification covers all five divergence kinds (different text / tag / attribute / children-missing-client / children-missing-server)
- Both empty states render correctly (no SSR / SSR present no mismatches)
- Per-frame filter works; cross-frame SSR renders both swimlanes
- Production-build path: empty state renders; `:rf.ssr/hydration-mismatch` never emitted (verified via `npm run test:elision`)

## Cross-references

- `tools/causa/spec/006-Hydration-Debugger.md` — full contract
- `tools/causa/spec/000-Vision.md` L95 — panel-inventory row
- `spec/011-SSR.md` §Hydration equivalence rule — render-tree hash semantics
- `tools/causa/spec/DESIGN-RATIONALE.md` Lock #11 — handler-coord fallback
- `tools/causa/spec/007-UX-IA.md` §Sidebar groups — Hydration is dormant `◌` until first activity
- Parent epic: rf2-5aw5v

## Acceptance criteria

- Panel mounts under `:rf/causa` frame-provider; dormant by default
- First-mismatch activation cues (sidebar flash + Issues badge + co-pilot suggestion) all fire
- Side-by-side render-tree view with divergent-node pulse + `⚠` flag
- Hash bisector: divergence path correctly highlighted; click-to-reroot works
- Multi-mismatch list walks correctly
- Hypothesis line classifies the five divergence kinds correctly
- Empty states render correctly
- Lazy-loaded bundle <100KB extra per spec
- Tests cover the diff, the bisector, the hypothesis classification, the empty states, the production elision path
- `npm run test:cljs`, `test:browser`, `test:elision`, `test:examples` pass

## Constraints

- NO AI attribution
- Don't commit `ai/`, `decision-beads.md`, `findings/`
- Pre-alpha
notes:
PR #586 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/586

Phase 5 Hydration Debugger panel landed. Visibility gate impl: sidebar reads composite sub's :has-mismatch? flag and flips :hydration entry's :dormant? off when truthy; the sidebar-item renderer paints ◌ when dormant, ◉ when active, ○ when live-not-active (per spec §Visibility).

Tests: 99 JVM / 849 CLJS — all pass. 30 helper-test assertions + 30 view-test assertions added.

Surface: panel + helpers + view-test + helper-test + registry composite sub + shell sidebar gate + canvas wiring. All wrapped with hydration-debugger panel begin/end markers in registry.cljs / shell.cljs for clean-rebase per bead's convention.

title: [causa] Phase 5 — Schema-violation Timeline panel
description:
Phase 5 of rf2-5aw5v (Causa v1.0 epic). Implement the Schema-violation Timeline panel per `tools/causa/spec/005-Schema-Timeline.md`.

## Scope

`tools/causa/spec/005-Schema-Timeline.md` (full file, 173 lines) is the contract. Headline contracts:

- §Substrate (L7-25): every schema validation failure emits a structured trace event per Spec 010 (Schemas) + Spec 009 §Error contract:
  ```
  {:op-type :error
   :operation :rf.error/schema-validation-failure
   :recovery <one of :skip-handler :skip-fx :rollback-db :replaced-with-default :re-raised>
   :tags {:where ... :path ... :value ... :schema ... :explanation ... :frame ...}}
  ```
- §Layout (L29-57): horizontal timeline with one row per registered schema; coloured dot per failure; time axis scrolls horizontally; default 60s window; drag-zoom; **time-axis selection synchronises with the Trace panel** (L36)
- §Recovery → colour mapping (L42-52):
  - `:skip-handler` → red
  - `:skip-fx` → red
  - `:rollback-db` → red
  - `:replaced-with-default` → yellow
  - `:re-raised` → red, thicker stroke
- §Per-violation detail (L58-95): click a dot → side panel with schema / path / value / expected / recovery + Malli explanation + Triggered-by (epoch + dispatch + Open-in-causality-graph link) + Registered-at (source coord + Open-source)
- §Hover tooltip (L97-106): 240ms tooltip with one-line cause ("at [:auth :email], expected :string, got nil")
- §The five recovery categories (L108-122): closed set per Spec 010; panel does NOT invent additional categories
- §Reading the timeline at a glance (L124-132): empty row = clean; new-row first-violation flashes label once (600ms)
- §Aging out (L134-141): reads from trace buffer (bounded by Spec 009 at 200 entries); ages out with everything else; no parallel buffer
- §Performance (L143-149): O(visible violations) render; O(0) hot-path cost
- §Empty state (L152-173): three empty states — no schemas registered / schemas-registered-but-no-failures / "all schemas clean" message is deliberately a positive result

## Implementation surface

- `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/schema_timeline.cljs` — view (per-schema row timeline + detail panel)
- `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/schema_timeline_helpers.cljc` — violation projection + recovery → colour mapping (pure data, JVM-runnable)

## Subscriptions to register

- `:rf.causa/registered-schemas` — composite over `(rf/app-schemas frame-id)` for the row enumeration
- `:rf.causa/schema-violations-window` — composite over `:rf.causa/trace-buffer` filtered to `:operation :rf.error/schema-validation-failure`, restricted to the current time-window
- `:rf.causa/selected-violation-detail` — composite over `:rf.causa/selected-violation-id`
- `:rf.causa/schema-timeline-window` — current time-axis window state (synchronised with Trace panel's `:rf.causa/trace-time-axis`)

## Events to register

- `:rf.causa/select-violation` (event-db)
- `:rf.causa/clear-violation-selection` (event-db)
- `:rf.causa/set-schema-filter` (event-db) — filter to the focused schema row ("Show me all violations of this schema")
- `:rf.causa/set-schema-timeline-window` (event-db) — drag-zoom (synchronises with Trace panel)

## Data sources

- `(rf/app-schemas frame-id)` per Spec 010 — registered schemas
- `(rf/trace-buffer {:operation :rf.error/schema-validation-failure})` — violation feed
- Schema registration source coords for the "Registered at" detail line

## Test surface

`tools/causa/test/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/schema_timeline_cljs_test.cljs` + `schema_timeline_helpers_cljc_test.cljc`. Canonical cases:
- Row enumeration matches `(rf/app-schemas frame-id)` ordering
- Recovery → colour mapping correct for all five recovery modes
- `:re-raised` rendered with thicker stroke (visual differentiator)
- Time-axis sync with Trace panel verified bi-directional
- Per-violation detail renders Malli explanation correctly
- "Show me all violations of this schema" filter narrows the timeline
- New-row first-violation flashes label once (visual cue; assert dispatch of the flash event)
- All three empty states render correctly (no schemas / no violations in window / all clean)

## Cross-references

- `tools/causa/spec/005-Schema-Timeline.md` — full contract
- `tools/causa/spec/000-Vision.md` L93 — panel-inventory row
- `spec/010-Schemas.md` §Per-step recovery — the five recovery categories (closed set)
- `spec/009-Instrumentation.md` §Error contract — error-event taxonomy
- `tools/causa/spec/007-UX-IA.md` §Sidebar groups — Schemas is dormant with `◌` until first violation
- Parent epic: rf2-5aw5v

## Acceptance criteria

- Panel mounts under `:rf/causa` frame-provider
- Horizontal timeline with per-schema rows renders correctly
- All five recovery → colour mappings honoured
- Time-axis sync with Trace panel works (selection synchronises)
- Click a dot → side panel with full Malli explanation + click-through to causality + source
- "Show me all violations" filter works
- Three empty states render correctly
- Tests cover the recovery mapping, the time-axis sync, the detail panel, the empty states
- `npm run test:cljs`, `test:browser`, `test:elision`, `test:examples` pass

## Constraints

- NO AI attribution
- Don't commit `ai/`, `decision-beads.md`, `findings/`
- Pre-alpha

title: [causa] Phase 5 — Routes panel
description:
Phase 5 of rf2-5aw5v (Causa v1.0 epic). Implement the Routes panel.

## SPEC DEFICIENCY — read before dispatching

The Routes panel has **no dedicated spec doc** in `tools/causa/spec/`. The only normative content is:

- `tools/causa/spec/000-Vision.md` L97 (panel-inventory row): "Routing inspector — `:rf/route` as breadcrumb; nav-token timeline with stale-result suppression. Data: `(rf/sub :rf/route)` + `:route.nav-token/*` traces."
- `tools/causa/spec/000-Vision.md` §What re-frame2 unlocks: "Routing (Spec 012) — URL ↔ frame state visualisation; nav-token timeline with stale-result suppression in flight."
- `tools/causa/spec/007-UX-IA.md` §Sidebar groups — Routes is in the conditional-with-activity group
- `tools/causa/spec/007-UX-IA.md` §Panel jumps — `r` key (collision-aware with event-action `r` rewind — global `r` only when no event is focused)

The runtime substrate is **Spec 012-Routing** (not the `012-Subscriptions.md` doc in `tools/causa/spec/` but the framework Spec 012 routing doc). The panel is the **UI consumer** of `:rf/route` + `:route.nav-token/*` traces.

Recommend filing a sibling `008-Routes.md` per-panel spec first if Mike wants masterpiece-grade. Inferable contract from the above:

- Breadcrumb of current `:rf/route` for the active frame
- Nav-token timeline: per-nav-token, start → resolution; visually distinguish in-flight / resolved / stale-suppressed
- Registered routes registry browser with click-to-source
- Route history: scrollable list of previous routes (back/forward navigation surface)

## Implementation surface

- `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/routes.cljs` — view (breadcrumb + nav-token timeline + history list)
- `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/routes_helpers.cljc` — route projection from `:rf/route` + nav-token correlation

## Subscriptions to register

- `:rf.causa/active-route` — composite over `(rf/sub :rf/route)` for the active frame
- `:rf.causa/registered-routes` — composite over the routing-registry handlers (verify with Spec 012)
- `:rf.causa/nav-token-timeline` — composite over `:route.nav-token/*` traces; correlates start → resolution per token
- `:rf.causa/route-history` — projection over epoch-history of `:rf/route` value changes

## Events to register

- `:rf.causa/select-route-history-entry` (event-db)
- `:rf.causa/clear-route-selection` (event-db)
- `:rf.causa/select-nav-token` (event-db) — focus a nav-token in the timeline

## Data sources

- `(rf/sub :rf/route)` — active route
- `:route.nav-token/*` trace operations — nav-token lifecycle
- Routing registry per Spec 012 — registered routes (verify the registrar surface name in `spec/012-Routing.md`)
- `:rf/route` slot from `:rf/epoch-record :db-after` — route history projection

## Test surface

`tools/causa/test/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/routes_cljs_test.cljs` + `routes_helpers_cljc_test.cljc`. Canonical cases:
- Active-route breadcrumb derives correctly from `(rf/sub :rf/route)`
- Nav-token timeline correlates `:route.nav-token/start` with `:route.nav-token/resolve` / `:route.nav-token/stale-suppressed`
- Route history scrolls back through epoch-history's `:rf/route` slot changes
- Empty state when no routes registered
- Per-frame: switching frame in the picker switches the route view

## Cross-references

- `tools/causa/spec/000-Vision.md` L97 — panel-inventory row
- `tools/causa/spec/007-UX-IA.md` §Sidebar groups + §Panel jumps (`r` collision)
- `spec/012-Routing.md` — runtime routing contract + nav-token semantics
- Parent epic: rf2-5aw5v

## Acceptance criteria

- Panel mounts under `:rf/causa` frame-provider
- Active route renders as a breadcrumb
- Nav-token timeline correctly distinguishes in-flight / resolved / stale-suppressed states
- Route history list scrollable; click rebases panel view
- Per-frame awareness verified
- Tests cover the breadcrumb, the timeline correlation, the history projection
- `npm run test:cljs`, `test:browser`, `test:elision`, `test:examples` pass

## Constraints

- NO AI attribution
- Don't commit `ai/`, `decision-beads.md`, `findings/`
- Pre-alpha

## Decision needed (file separately if pursuing path-a)

If Mike wants the masterpiece-grade route, file a sibling bead "spec — tools/causa/spec/008-Routes.md per-panel spec" first.

title: [causa] Phase 5 — Performance panel
description:
Phase 5 of rf2-5aw5v (Causa v1.0 epic). Implement the Performance panel.

## SPEC DEFICIENCY — read before dispatching

The Performance panel has **no dedicated spec doc** in `tools/causa/spec/`. The only normative content is:

- `tools/causa/spec/000-Vision.md` L92 (panel-inventory row): "Performance ribbon — INP, long tasks, layout shifts, re-render counts per epoch. Data: `PerformanceObserver` watching `rf:*` + browser entries."

  - Perf colour scale: `fast` (<16ms), `medium` (16-50), `slow` (50-100), `blocking` (>100ms, INP threshold)
- `tools/causa/spec/007-UX-IA.md` §Performance budget L599-610: opening Causa must not change observable INP

The runtime substrate is **Spec 009 §Performance** — the framework's performance surface (per-cascade duration capture, budget warnings). The panel is the **UI consumer** of this surface.

Recommend filing a sibling `008-Performance.md` per-panel spec first if Mike wants masterpiece-grade. Inferable contract from the above:

title: [causa] Phase 5 — Trace panel
description:
Phase 5 of rf2-5aw5v (Causa v1.0 epic). Implement the Trace panel.

## SPEC DEFICIENCY — read before dispatching

The Trace panel has **no dedicated spec doc** in `tools/causa/spec/`. The only normative content is:

- `tools/causa/spec/000-Vision.md` L89 (panel-inventory row): "Trace timeline — Raw trace events with timing. Structured filter on `:op-type` / `:operation` / `:frame` / `:dispatch-id` / `:origin`. Data: `(rf/trace-buffer)` + `PerformanceObserver` on `rf:*` measures."
- `tools/causa/spec/007-UX-IA.md` §Sidebar groups — "Trace" is always-active
- `tools/causa/spec/007-UX-IA.md` §Panel jumps — `t` key
- `tools/causa/spec/005-Schema-Timeline.md` L34-36 mentions time-axis synchronisation: "The Trace-timeline panel governs the same time axis when both are visible — selection synchronises."

The strong external anchor is **rf2-97ah0** (filter vocabulary just landed) and `spec/009-Instrumentation.md` §Filter vocabulary which defines the canonical 9-axis filter: `:operation`, `:op-type`, `:since`, `:frame`, `:severity`, `:event-id`, `:handler-id`, `:source`, `:origin`, `:dispatch-id`, `:since-ms`, `:between`, `:pred`. The panel is the **UI consumer** of this filter vocabulary.

Recommend filing a sibling `008-Trace.md` per-panel spec first if Mike wants masterpiece-grade. Inferable contract from the above:
- Vertical timeline of raw trace events; mono-format; timestamped
- Filter controls: chips / multi-select for each of the 9 filter axes
- `PerformanceObserver` integration for `rf:*` browser performance measures (the spec inventory mentions this for INP / long-task overlay)
- Time-axis sync with Schema-Timeline panel

`tools/causa/test/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/trace_cljs_test.cljs` + `trace_helpers_cljc_test.cljc`. Canonical cases:

title: [causa] Phase 5 — Machine Inspector panel
description:
Phase 5 of rf2-5aw5v (Causa v1.0 epic). Implement the Machine Inspector panel per `tools/causa/spec/003-Machine-Inspector.md`. Embeds `tools/machines-viz/`'s `MachineChart` component.

## Scope

`tools/causa/spec/003-Machine-Inspector.md` (full file, 280 lines) is the contract. Headline contracts:

- §Embedding posture (L9-41): dependency arrow is `tools/causa/ → tools/machines-viz/ → implementation/machines/`. `machines-viz` MUST NOT depend on Causa. The contract: `machines-viz` exports `MachineChart` accepting `machine-id` + `frame-id` + `:on-state-click` / `:on-transition-click` callbacks. **PRECONDITION**: `tools/machines-viz/` must exist before this panel can land. See rf2-x50eu (open: "scaffold tools/machines-viz/spec/ — MachineChart contract + share-URL encoding"). This panel BLOCKS until rf2-x50eu's implementation child lands.
- §What the panel shows (L43-60): directional state-chart; current state pulses; compound states nested visually; hover edge → guard/action; `:invoke` / `:invoke-all` spawned children as smaller machines; `:after` timer countdown rings; transition history ribbon
- §Data sources (L62-77): `(rf/machines frame-id)`, `[:rf/machines <id>]` app-db slot, `:rf.machine/transition` traces, `:rf.machine.microstep/transition`, `:rf.machine.timer/*`, `:rf.machine.invoke-all/*`, `:rf.machine/spawned` / `-destroyed`, `:rf.machine/done`, `:rf.machine/system-id-bound` / `-released`
- §Selection and switching (L79-90): panel header carries a machine picker over `(rf/machines frame-id)`; cross-frame; `:system-id` parenthetical
- §Transition history ribbon (L92-109): horizontal scrubbable list; click rewinds chart (view-only — passive scrubbing per Time-Travel spec); microstep entries indented under outer
- §Source-coord integration (L111-130): state node / edge / guard / action → click jumps to source; handler-coord fallback per Lock #11 with `(?)` annotation for synthetic dispatches
- §`:invoke-all` viz (L132-146): horizontal row of mini-machines; join condition label; live colour updates as children complete/fail; join resolution collapses to summary
- §Auto-pan (L148-152): chart auto-pans on transition so active state stays in view; per-machine toggle in localStorage
- §Performance (L154-163): chart re-layout only on registry change; `:after` rings 60Hz only when panel visible; transition history virtualises past 200
- §Share affordance (L165-258): right-click → Copy machine as PNG / SVG / Share URL. NOT a session export (Lock #4 — only topology + current-state encoded in URL fragment).
- §Empty state (L260-272), §Accessibility (L274-280)

## Implementation surface

- `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/machine.cljs` — thin wrapper around `machines-viz/MachineChart` + transition-history ribbon + source-coord jump
- (No `_helpers.cljc` expected — the chart logic lives in `machines-viz`)

## Subscriptions to register

- `:rf.causa/registered-machines` — composite over `(rf/machines frame-id)` for the picker
- `:rf.causa/selected-machine-id` (sub from the panel registry)
- `:rf.causa/machine-transitions` — composite over `(rf/trace-buffer {:operation :rf.machine/transition})` filtered to selected machine
- `:rf.causa/machine-microstep-transitions` — same shape for `:rf.machine.microstep/transition`
- `:rf.causa/machine-active-timers` — composite for `:after` countdown rings
- `:rf.causa/machine-invoke-all-state` — composite for parallel-child join visualisation

## Events to register

- `:rf.causa/select-machine-id` (event-db)
- `:rf.causa/clear-machine-selection` (event-db)
- `:rf.causa/scrub-machine-transition` (event-db) — view-only transition-history scrub (passive, NOT a rewind)
- `:rf.causa/toggle-auto-pan` (event-db) — per-machine localStorage flag
- `:rf.causa/copy-machine-as-png` (event-fx) — share-affordance
- `:rf.causa/copy-machine-as-svg` (event-fx)
- `:rf.causa/copy-machine-share-url` (event-fx)

## Data sources

- All of Spec 005 (State Machines) + Spec 009 (Instrumentation) machine traces
- `tools/machines-viz/` exports (the rendering primitive)

## Test surface

`tools/causa/test/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/machine_cljs_test.cljs`. Canonical cases:
- Picker enumerates registered machines correctly per active frame
- Transition history ribbon populates from `:rf.machine/transition` traces
- Microstep entries render indented under their outer transition
- `:invoke-all` parallel-child viz: child progress maps correctly
- `:after` countdown ring visible only when panel mounted (60Hz render gate)
- Share-affordance: PNG / SVG / URL encoding produces topology-only payload (NOT a session export — verify Lock #4)
- Empty state when no machines registered

## Cross-references

- `tools/causa/spec/003-Machine-Inspector.md` — full contract
- `tools/causa/spec/000-Vision.md` L90 — panel-inventory row
- `tools/causa/spec/DESIGN-RATIONALE.md` Lock #11 (handler-coord fallback), Lock #4 (no session export)
- `spec/005-StateMachines.md` — runtime contract
- `tools/machines-viz/` — rendering primitive (precondition)
- rf2-x50eu — `tools/machines-viz/spec/` scaffold (precondition; this bead BLOCKS until that work's implementation child lands)
- Parent epic: rf2-5aw5v

## Acceptance criteria

- Panel mounts under `:rf/causa` frame-provider
- Embeds `machines-viz/MachineChart` correctly (Causa → machines-viz dep only; no reverse dep)
- Machine picker enumerates + switches active machine

title: [causa] Phase 5 — Effects (fx-detail) panel
description:
Phase 5 of rf2-5aw5v (Causa v1.0 epic). Implement the Effects (fx-detail) panel.

## SPEC DEFICIENCY — read before dispatching

The Effects panel has **no dedicated spec doc** in `tools/causa/spec/`. The only normative content is:

- `tools/causa/spec/000-Vision.md` L88 (the panel-inventory table row): "Effect log — Every fx invocation; filter by fx-id; outcome (`:ok` / `:error` / `:skipped-on-platform`). Data: `(rf/trace-buffer {:op-type :fx})` + `:effects` projection."
- `tools/causa/spec/007-UX-IA.md` §Sidebar groups L107 — "Effects" is in the always-active group
- `tools/causa/spec/007-UX-IA.md` §Panel jumps (mnemonics) L329 — `f` key
- `tools/causa/spec/010-MCP-Server.md` L113 mentions the panel as the surface mirroring `(rf/trace-buffer ...)` fx slices

The implementing agent should **either** (a) flag back to Mike that a `008-Effects.md` per-panel spec is needed first, **or** (b) implement a minimum-viable Effects panel inferred from the panel-inventory row + the `:rf.fx/*` trace operations in `spec/009-Instrumentation.md` (the trace bus spec defines `:rf.fx/handled` / `:rf.fx/override` / `:rf.fx/skipped-on-platform` operations). Recommend (a) for masterpiece-grade — file a spec bead first if the panel needs design depth comparable to App-DB Diff / Subscriptions.

The shape inferred from the inventory row is:
- List of fx invocations per cascade, filterable by `:fx-id` and by outcome (`:ok` / `:error` / `:skipped-on-platform`)
- Per-fx-row click → fx-detail showing the input args, the registered handler's source coord, the outcome trace
- "Stub indicator" mentioned in task — Spec 009 has a `:rf.fx/stub` mode for tests/stories; surface when an fx invocation was stubbed vs ran real

## Implementation surface (assuming inferred spec proceeds)

- `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/effects.cljs` — view (pure hiccup)
- `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/effects_helpers.cljc` — fx projection from trace buffer (CLJC)

## Subscriptions to register

- `:rf.causa/fx-invocations` — composite from `:rf.causa/trace-buffer` filtered to `:op-type :fx` (vector of `{:fx-id :outcome :dispatch-id :tags ...}`)
- `:rf.causa/fx-by-id` — group-by `:fx-id` for the filter UI
- `:rf.causa/selected-fx-detail` — composite over `:rf.causa/selected-fx-id` + trace events
- `:rf.causa/registered-fxs` — composite over `(rf/handlers :fx)` for the empty state + registry-browser

## Events to register

- `:rf.causa/select-fx-id` (event-db)
- `:rf.causa/clear-fx-selection` (event-db)
- `:rf.causa/set-fx-filter` (event-db) — by fx-id + by outcome (`:ok` / `:error` / `:skipped-on-platform` / `:stubbed`)

## Data sources

- `(rf/trace-buffer {:op-type :fx})` — the canonical fx trace stream
- `:rf/epoch-record :effects` projection — per-cascade fx slices
- `(rf/handlers :fx)` — registered-fx registry browser
- Spec 009 §Filter vocabulary — `:fx-id` / `:operation` filter axes

## Test surface

`tools/causa/test/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/effects_cljs_test.cljs`. Canonical cases:
- List populates from `:rf.causa/trace-buffer` filtered to `:op-type :fx`

title: [causa] Phase 5 — Subscriptions panel
description:
Phase 5 of rf2-5aw5v (Causa v1.0 epic). Implement the Subscriptions panel per `tools/causa/spec/012-Subscriptions.md`. Builds on Phase 1-4 panels.

## Scope

`tools/causa/spec/012-Subscriptions.md` (full file, 403 lines) is the contract. Answers canonical questions #3 ("Why is this subscription returning the wrong value?") and #4 ("Why is this view re-rendering?"). Headline contracts:

- §Data sources (L29-41): three surfaces — `(rf/sub-cache frame-id)` (CLJS only), `:rf/epoch-record :sub-runs`, `:rf/epoch-record :db-before/:db-after`. No new framework instrumentation.
- §Sub-status badge taxonomy (L42-150): exactly **five** statuses (no more). TanStack-Query-style legibility:
  - `:fresh` — green filled `●`
  - `:re-running` — cyan half-filled `◐` (250ms spin, static under `prefers-reduced-motion`)
  - `:invalidated` — yellow hollow `◌`
  - `:cached-no-watcher` — tertiary-grey hollow `○` (the value MAY be stale; tooltip says so)
  - `:error` — red triangle `▲` + `!`
  Colour-never-alone discipline (shape + tooltip-label). Single source-of-truth across panels — same badge meaning everywhere.
- §"Stale vs invalidated" (L65-83): deliberately NO `:stale` status. re-frame's cache is value-equality-driven; introducing it would have no runtime referent.
- §Where badges appear (L115-135): sub list, sub graph, event-detail's "Subs recomputed" section, causality graph's per-dispatch sub-attribution chip, command palette sub matches.
- §Filtering and grouping (L139-150): five toggleable filter chips (one per status); group-by-status default sort; sidebar activity badge counts `:error` + `:invalidated`.
- §Invalidation-chain affordance (L152-310): for any sub that re-ran this epoch, surface "why did this re-run?" — walks UP input chain to originating `app-db` slice change. O(depth), capped at 8 layers default; diamonds branch with deepest-branch-first heuristic. Right-click "Show invalidation chain" reachable from every surface naming a sub. Keyboard mnemonic `i`.
- §Reverse: from a slice to its dependents (L286-308): the App-DB Diff panel's `i` keystroke pivots to "subs invalidated by this slice this epoch."
- §JVM behaviour (L334-346): CLJS-only for live cache state. JVM degrades to "show the cascade-projected status only" from `:sub-runs`.
- §Performance (L362-376): O(visible rows) badges; chain walk O(depth); chain caching per `(:epoch-id, focused-sub-id)`.
- §Embedding (L378-386): Embeddability is v1.1; v1.0 ships as standalone Causa surface only.

## Implementation surface

- `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/subscriptions.cljs` — view (list + graph + invalidation-chain views)
- `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/subscriptions_helpers.cljc` — status derivation; invalidation-chain walk algorithm (pure data, JVM-runnable)

## Subscriptions to register

- `:rf.causa/sub-statuses` — composite: derives one of `:fresh / :re-running / :invalidated / :cached-no-watcher / :error` per cached sub
- `:rf.causa/sub-list-rows` — sorted, filtered, group-by-status rows for the list view
- `:rf.causa/invalidation-chain` — composite over `:rf.causa/focused-sub-id` + epoch sub-runs + changed-paths → chain (with branches)
- `:rf.causa/reverse-invalidation-chain` — composite: from a focused slice path, the subs invalidated by it (called from App-DB Diff)
- `:rf.causa/sub-graph-data` — node/edge data for the optional force-directed graph view

## Events to register

- `:rf.causa/focus-sub-id` (event-db) — set the focused sub for invalidation-chain view
- `:rf.causa/clear-sub-focus` (event-db)
- `:rf.causa/set-sub-filter` (event-db) — toggle status filter chips
- `:rf.causa/toggle-sub-view-mode` (event-db) — list / graph
- `:rf.causa/expand-chain-branch` (event-db) — diamond expand

## Data sources

- `(rf/sub-cache frame-id)` (CLJS only) for layer / inputs / ref-count
- `:rf/epoch-record :sub-runs` projection for the just-settled cascade
- `:rf/epoch-record :db-before` / `:db-after` for layer-1 attribution (reuses App-DB Diff's changed-paths set; consider sharing the helper)
- Sub registrations for layer info

## Test surface

`tools/causa/test/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/subscriptions_cljs_test.cljs` + `subscriptions_helpers_cljc_test.cljc`. Canonical cases:
- Each of the five statuses derived correctly from cache state + `:sub-runs`
- `:cached-no-watcher` is NOT confused with `:fresh` (the ref-count distinction matters)
- Invalidation-chain walk: linear chain (layer 3 → 2 → 1 → slice)
- Invalidation-chain walk: diamond (two layer-2 inputs both moved); deepest-branch-first ordering
- Reverse view: from a slice, the dependent subs in layer-ascending order
- Layer-1 attribution intersects sub input-paths with changed-paths set correctly
- Chain caching per (epoch-id, sub-id)
- JVM degraded mode: chain renders from `:sub-runs` only without `(rf/sub-cache ...)`

## Cross-references

- `tools/causa/spec/012-Subscriptions.md` — full contract
- `tools/causa/spec/000-Vision.md` L87 — panel inventory (subscription graph row)
- `tools/causa/spec/004-App-DB-Diff.md` §Changed-paths derivation — shared diff input
- `tools/causa/spec/001-Causality-Graph.md` §Edges — chain's link to causality view
- `spec/006-ReactiveSubstrate.md` §Subscription cache — contract and operational semantics
- `spec/Spec-Schemas.md` §`:rf/epoch-record` — `:sub-runs` projection
- Parent epic: rf2-5aw5v

## Acceptance criteria

- Panel mounts under `:rf/causa` frame-provider
- All five sub-status badges render with correct shape+colour+tooltip discipline
- Filter chips toggle; group-by-status sort default
- Invalidation-chain view walks up correctly; diamond branches expand on click
- Reverse view from App-DB Diff slice focus works
- JVM-side panel renders the degraded view (cascade-projected status only)
- Tests cover all five statuses, both chain shapes (linear + diamond), the reverse view
- Embedding NOT shipped at this phase (v1.1 commitment per spec §Embedding)
- `npm run test:cljs`, `test:browser`, `test:elision`, `test:examples` pass

## Constraints

- NO AI attribution
- Don't commit `ai/`, `decision-beads.md`, `findings/`
- Pre-alpha

title: [causa] Phase 5 — App-DB Diff panel
description:
Phase 5 of rf2-5aw5v (Causa v1.0 epic). Implement the slice-centric App-DB Diff panel per `tools/causa/spec/004-App-DB-Diff.md`. Builds on Phase 1 foundation (rf2-n6x4q), Phase 2 event-detail (rf2-op3bz), Phase 3 Time Travel (rf2-t53ze), Phase 4 Causality Graph (rf2-4rqs1).

## Scope

`tools/causa/spec/004-App-DB-Diff.md` (full file, 189 lines) is the contract. Headline contracts:

- §Default view (L11-30): stacked focused slice mini-panels — "the slices that changed, the slices the user pinned"
- §Changed-paths derivation (L37-52): structural-sharing diff via PersistentHashMap pointer-equality, O(changed paths). Causa computes on first panel mount per epoch, caches per `:epoch-id`, discards on epoch age-out. The runtime's `:rf/epoch-record` does NOT pre-compute changed paths.
- §Colour coding (L54-64): `:added` green / `:modified` yellow / `:removed` red, 400ms flash on epoch land
- §Pinned slices (L66-77): right-click → Pin; persisted to localStorage per `tools/causa/spec/API.md` §Settings keys (key `day8.re-frame2-causa/pinned-slices/<frame-id>/v1`); per-frame; drag-reorder
- §Reserved-keys group (L80-97): `:rf/machines`, `:rf/route`, `:rf/system-ids`, `:rf/pending-navigation`, `:rf/spawned` render in a separate `[runtime]` group at bottom
- §Full-tree escape hatch (L101-112): virtualised lazy-expand tree; first 100 keys/level eager; search; breadcrumb
- §Read-only (L117-141): Lock #3 — no edit/inject; right-click → Copy value / Copy path / Pin / Show me when this changed
- §"Show me when this changed" (L144-165): walks `epoch-history` diffing `:db-before` / `:db-after` per epoch; canvas pivots to a list of epochs that touched the path
- §Performance (L168-178): diff caching per `:epoch-id`; slice virtualisation; pinned-slice deref O(pins) per epoch

## Implementation surface

- `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/app_db_diff.cljs` — view (pure hiccup per Phase 2 pattern in `event_detail.cljs`)
- `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/app_db_diff_helpers.cljc` — pure-data diff algorithm (CLJC; JVM-runnable for fast unit tests, same pattern as `time_travel_helpers.cljc` / `causality_graph_helpers.cljc`)

## Subscriptions to register (`:rf.causa/*` per `registry.cljs` convention)

- `:rf.causa/selected-epoch-diff` — composite over `:rf.causa/selected-epoch-id` + `:rf.causa/epoch-history`; produces sorted vector of `[op path before-value after-value]` triples plus reserved-keys group
- `:rf.causa/pinned-slices` — per-frame slice-path pin store (separate from Phase 3's pinned-snapshots which pins a whole `:frame-db` value)
- `:rf.causa/show-me-when-this-changed-result` — composite: walks full epoch-history, filters epochs that touched a focused path

## Events to register

- `:rf.causa/pin-slice` (event-db) — add a path to the pinned-slices store
- `:rf.causa/unpin-slice` (event-db) — remove
- `:rf.causa/reorder-pinned-slices` (event-db) — drag-reorder
- `:rf.causa/focus-slice-path` (event-db) — set the "Show me when this changed" focused path
- `:rf.causa/clear-slice-focus` (event-db)
- `:rf.causa/copy-value-to-clipboard` (event-fx) — hits a `:rf.causa.fx/copy-to-clipboard` effect
- `:rf.causa/copy-path-to-clipboard` (event-fx) — same

## Data sources (consumed; no new framework registries per `Principles.md` §Observation only)

- `(rf/get-frame-db frame-id)` — current value
- Epoch records' `:db-before` / `:db-after` (per `:rf/epoch-record` projection)
- Reserved-keys list per `spec/Conventions.md` §Reserved app-db keys
- localStorage for pin persistence

## Test surface

`tools/causa/test/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/app_db_diff_cljs_test.cljs` + `app_db_diff_helpers_cljc_test.cljc`. Canonical cases:
- Diff algorithm produces correct `[op path before after]` triples for `:added` / `:modified` / `:removed`
- PersistentHashMap pointer-equality short-circuits unchanged subtrees (assertable via metrics — count of recursive calls)
- Reserved-keys segregation at the panel level
- Pin / unpin / reorder events update the localStorage projection
- "Show me when this changed" returns only epochs that touched the focused path
- Empty state when no dispatches yet

## Cross-references

- `tools/causa/spec/004-App-DB-Diff.md` — full contract
- `tools/causa/spec/000-Vision.md` L86 — panel inventory row
- `tools/causa/spec/007-UX-IA.md` §Sidebar groups — App-db is always-active
- `tools/causa/spec/012-Subscriptions.md` §Reverse: from a slice to its dependents — `i` keystroke binding from this panel
- `event_detail.cljs` / `time_travel.cljs` / `causality_graph.cljs` — model implementations
- Parent epic: rf2-5aw5v

## Acceptance criteria

- Panel mounts under `[rf/frame-provider {:frame :rf/causa}]`
- Renders slice mini-panels for the current epoch's changed paths
- Renders pinned slices with live value display
- Renders the reserved-keys `[runtime]` group correctly
- Full-tree expand toggle works (virtualised, not full DOM mount)
- Right-click affordances (Copy / Pin / Show me when changed) all wired
- Tests cover the diff algorithm, the pin store, the "show me when changed" walk, the reserved-keys segregation
- No new framework registrar surface added; consumes existing `:rf/epoch-record` projection only
- `npm run test:cljs`, `test:browser`, `test:elision`, `test:examples` pass

## Constraints

- NO AI attribution
- Don't commit `ai/`, `decision-beads.md`, `findings/`
- Pre-alpha

title: [skill] re-frame2-setup: tighten description to greenfield-only scope
description:
## re-frame2-setup activation surface

`skills/re-frame2-setup/SKILL.md` `description:` is precise on trigger phrases but does NOT say the skill is only for greenfield-or-empty-CLJS projects. A user with an existing CLJS project that already has shadow-cljs but no re-frame2 might fall in or out of scope; SKILL.md body says "or no re-frame2 wiring yet" — broader than greenfield.

## Proposed fix

Clarify in `description:` that this skill is for:

- Greenfield projects (new app from scratch).
- Empty CLJS projects (shadow-cljs / Clojure already present but no re-frame2).

And NOT for:

- Adding re-frame2 to an existing app with a different state-mgmt library (that's an authoring task, plus possible migration).
- Existing re-frame2 projects.

Tighten the trigger phrases to match.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-skills-best-practice-2026-05-13.md` §Per-skill / B-skill-setup-tighten-greenfield — P2

title: [skill] Audit leaf-size ceiling corpus-wide (decide policy)
description:
## Leaf-size ceiling violated corpus-wide

`re-frame2`'s SKILL.md states "Each leaf is ≤250 lines, target ~150". Audit finds the ceiling violated across the corpus:

- `re-frame-migration/reference/automated-transforms.md` — 365 L
- `re-frame-migration/reference/guided-checklist.md` — 300 L
- `re-frame2/reference/cross-cutting/testing.md` — 225 L (near ceiling)
- `re-frame2/patterns/long-running-work.md` — 193 L
- `re-frame2/patterns/websocket.md` — 177 L
- `re-frame2/patterns/managed-http.md` — 173 L
- `re-frame2/patterns/boot.md` — 173 L
- `re-frame2-implementor/reference/phase-1-decisions.md` — 211 L
- `re-frame2-implementor/reference/phase-2-impl-order.md` — 214 L

## Proposed fix — decide policy

Decide whether 250 is the right ceiling (and enforce) or whether the ceiling needs raising for catalog-shaped leaves. The ceiling claim is only in `re-frame2`'s SKILL.md right now and is being violated in other skills.

Options:
- **(a) Enforce 250** — split offending leaves into multiple files.
- **(b) Raise to 400** — accept the catalog-shaped leaves as the new norm; document the discipline in `skills/README.md`.
- **(c) Per-type ceilings** — narrative leaves stay ≤250; catalog leaves can run longer.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-skills-best-practice-2026-05-13.md` §CC-5 / B-skill-leaf-ceiling-audit — P2
notes:
Audit complete; corpus already in compliance. Findings: ai/findings/skill-leaf-size-audit-20260513.md.

Distribution (81 leaves): max 203L, p95 148L, p90 129L, p80 110L, median 88L. The 365L and 300L files cited in the bead description (re-frame-migration/reference/automated-transforms.md, guided-checklist.md) no longer exist — that directory has been restructured into 10 files, max 156L.

Proposed ceiling: keep 250L line target (per existing spec/design.md statements) plus add a parallel 16KB byte ceiling to catch dense unwrapped prose. Target ~150L / ~10KB.

Splits filed: ZERO. The single outlier (re-frame-pair2/references/recipes.md, 203L) is a 21-procedure catalogue; splitting would multiply file handles without reducing tokens-per-session.

Top-3 outliers (all under the existing 250L ceiling):
- re-frame-pair2/references/recipes.md (203L / 23KB) — catalogue-shaped, recommended exemption
- re-frame-migration/reference/auto-call-site-rewrites.md (156L / 8KB) — normal
- re-frame-pair2/docs/initial-spec.md (152L / 14KB) — meta/historical doc

Follow-ups filed:
- rf2-zkca8 (P3) — optional: codify the leaf-size rule once in skills/README.md so the 7+ per-skill restatements become one source of truth.

title: [skill] Factor cross-skill routing tables into skills/README.md (single source)
description:
## Cross-skill routing tables drift risk

5 of 6 skills carry a "When NOT to use this skill" table or paragraph that lists the other 5 skills with one-line summaries. 30 cells of mutually-cross-referenced content.

If one skill's role changes, all 5 others need updating.

## Proposed fix

Factor the cross-skill routing into a single `skills/README.md` block (already exists, 101 L) and have each SKILL.md link to it instead of duplicating.

Each SKILL.md retains its own "When NOT to use" frontmatter description (load-bearing for activation precision) but body sections point to `skills/README.md §Skill routing` for the cross-skill map.

## Source

title: [skill] re-frame-migration: split or lift ceiling for 300+L leaves
description:
## Leaf-size policy violated

`skills/re-frame-migration/reference/automated-transforms.md` is 365 lines and `reference/guided-checklist.md` is 300 lines. The stated soft ceiling in `re-frame2`'s SKILL.md is 250 (target ~150). Migration's neighbours don't restate the ceiling but the discipline is corpus-wide.

## Proposed fix

Decide:
- **(a) Split** each into per-family files (ns-rewrites / effect-map / dispatch-shape / etc.). Each subsection is ~30 L so the consolidation defensibly fits but a split would put it back under the ceiling.
- **(b) Lift the ceiling** for catalog-shaped leaves (since these are Type-A rewrite catalogues, not narrative walkthroughs).

The leaf-ceiling-audit bead (filed separately) addresses (b) corpus-wide.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-skills-best-practice-2026-05-13.md` §Per-skill / B-skill-migration-split-large-leaves — P2
notes: PR #590 -- https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/590. Split approach chosen: automated-transforms.md (365L) -> auto-call-site-rewrites.md (194L) + auto-cross-cutting.md (188L); guided-checklist.md (300L) -> guided-handlers-state.md (160L) + guided-interceptors-subs.md (156L). All four under the 250L ceiling; one-level-deep preserved.
close_reason: Closed

title: [skill] Declare allowed-tools on 5 missing skill SKILL.mds
description:
## Skills missing `allowed-tools:` declarations

Only `re-frame-pair2` declares `allowed-tools:` in its SKILL.md frontmatter. The other 5 skills (re-frame2, re-frame-migration, re-frame2-implementor, re-frame2-setup, re-frame-pair-improver2) implicitly accept the full Claude Code surface.

For authoring skills this works in practice but the contract surface is unstated.

## Proposed fix

Declare `allowed-tools:` on every SKILL.md:

- **For authoring skills** (re-frame2, re-frame-migration, re-frame2-setup, re-frame2-implementor): `Read`, `Edit`, `Write`, `Grep`, `Glob`, `Bash(npm *)`, `Bash(npx *)`, `Bash(clj *)`.
- **For the improver** (re-frame-pair-improver2): `Read`, `Grep`, `Bash(bd *)` — plus Glob if useful.

Per-skill the actual list will vary; the principle is to make the contract surface explicit.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-skills-best-practice-2026-05-13.md` §CC-2 / B-skill-allowed-tools-declare — P2
notes: PR #605 (rf2-5t1n2-skill-allowed-tools) opened — adds allowed-tools frontmatter to re-frame2, re-frame-migration, re-frame2-implementor, re-frame2-setup. Closes the gap left by PR #600 (rf2-rrn8s) which covered re-frame-pair-improver2. Per-skill lists: re-frame2 (Read/Edit/Write/Grep/Glob); re-frame-migration (+Bash(rg *), Bash(rg -l *)); re-frame2-implementor (+Bash(bd *)); re-frame2-setup (+Bash(clojure *), Bash(npm *), Bash(npx *), Bash(shadow-cljs *)). Awaiting Mike's review — do NOT close.

title: [skill] re-frame-pair2 README: restructure install section — skill (markdown) vs MCP server (npm)
description:
## Skill README install-path needs restructure

`skills/re-frame-pair2/README.md` install section conflates "skill (markdown)" and "MCP server (npm)" as separate concerns but treats them as one.

The skill itself ships as markdown referenced from `skills/re-frame-pair2/`. The structural successor for the running-runtime ops is the MCP server at `tools/pair2-mcp/` with npm coord `@day8/re-frame-pair2-mcp`.

A reader landing on the skill README needs to:
1. Discover the skill exists (markdown reference)
2. Discover the MCP path exists (npm install for the agent runtime)
3. Understand which path is current vs deprecated

## Proposed fix

Restructure the install section into two clearly-labelled subsections:

- **Skill (markdown)** — clone re-frame2, reference `skills/re-frame-pair2/`
- **MCP server (npm)** — `npm install -g @day8/re-frame-pair2-mcp` once published; until then `:local/root` per the pair2-mcp README posture

Different install audiences (Claude Code authoring vs runtime MCP client) need different paths.

## Cross-stream dedupe

Same defect as P0 `[skill] re-frame-pair2 README: fix stale paths` — but that P0 is for URL/branch fixes. This P2 is the structural restructure of the install section.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-readmes-currency-2026-05-13.md` §`skills/re-frame-pair2/README.md` — Stale (significant) / proposed correction §(c)
notes: Superseded by rf2-2d6na (PR #596 — retired bash shims, collapsed install to MCP-only). The bead requested a two-subsection install (skill markdown vs MCP npm). #596's commit message explicitly states it 'collapse[s] the README''s Two paths framing to a single MCP install path' — the design has deliberately moved in the opposite direction to this bead's proposal. Current install section is a clean single MCP path (npm install -g @day8/re-frame-pair2-mcp + mcpServers entry + shadow-cljs preload). Closing as stale; no PR.
close_reason: Superseded by rf2-2d6na (PR #596). #596 explicitly collapsed the README's two-path framing to a single MCP install path; this bead asked for the opposite restructure. Design intent has changed; no work to do.
