title: [ci] verify-version-lockstep.sh: widen coverage to tools/* deployable jars
description:
## Lockstep enforcement gap

`.github/scripts/verify-version-lockstep.sh` currently iterates `implementation/*` artefacts only. But `tools/causa/deps.edn`, `tools/story/deps.edn`, `tools/story-mcp/deps.edn`, `tools/template/deps.edn` each carry `:clein/build :version "../../VERSION"` and `:local/root` references to `../../implementation/core`.

A hand-edit to any of these is NOT caught by lockstep CI. That is the very drift the script was written to prevent.

## Proposed fix

Either:

- **(a) Widen the script's `ARTEFACT_PATHS` map** to include the deployable `tools/` jars (causa, story, story-mcp, template — leave `pair2-mcp` out as it ships via npm).
- **(b) Generalise the script** to walk both `implementation/` and `tools/` for any `:clein/build` config with `:version "../../VERSION"` lockstep.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-repo-best-practice-2026-05-13.md` §2 / Bead candidate 3 — P2
notes: PR #582 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/582. Widens the verifier to cover tools/causa, tools/story, tools/story-mcp, tools/template. tools/pair2-mcp excluded (npm binary, no :clein/build). Local run passes (16 artefacts = 12 implementation/ + 4 tools/). Drift-injection sanity-check confirms both :version and literal :mvn/version detection fire as expected.
close_reason: Closed

title: [template] shared/README.md: replace re-frame-causa → Causa (downstream impact)
description:
## Downstream impact: every generated user project

`tools/template/src/clj/new/re_frame2/shared/README.md` — the README the template emits into every generated user project — references:

> Stack traces in dev get click-to-source via the `:rf.trace/trigger-handler` preload (auto-wired when the **re-frame-causa** preload is on the classpath) — clicking a frame in the dev console jumps straight to the offending form.

The hyphenated form `re-frame-causa` is not the current name. The Maven coord is `day8/re-frame2-causa`, the namespace is `day8.re-frame2-causa.preload`, the tool is just **Causa**. A generated user app's README inheriting this string would be born stale.

## Proposed fix

Replace `re-frame-causa` (any hyphenated form) with the Maven coord `day8/re-frame2-causa` or the bare name "Causa", depending on context.

Bonus pass through `docs/guide/` for the hyphenated form — `docs/guide/README.md` chapter 15 row uses the same hyphenated string.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-readmes-currency-2026-05-13.md` §`tools/template/src/clj/new/re_frame2/shared/README.md` — Stale (downstream impact)
- Subsumed by the broader Causa-rename umbrella but called out separately because every generated project inherits the staleness — downstream impact warrants P2 not P3.
notes: Pre-flight on dispatch (2026-05-13) found work already landed via rf2-zn0p2 / commit ec699508 ('docs: rename re-frame-10x v2 / re-frame-causa references to Causa'). That commit's diff against tools/template/src/clj/new/re_frame2/shared/README.md changes lines 38-41 exactly as this bead specifies: 're-frame-causa preload' -> 'Causa preload - day8.re-frame2-causa.preload'. Current grep of tools/template/ for any hyphenated re-frame-causa returns zero matches. Verified no other stale tokens (re-frame-10x v2 / tools/10x) remain in the file. No new PR opened; closing eligible.
close_reason: Stale — already shipped in rf2-zn0p2 (PR #556 Causa rename sweep). Pre-flight check confirmed zero remaining renames at target file.

title: [hygiene] tools/causa/LICENSE + CLAUDE.md unfilled sections + tools/story Reagent version drift
description:
## Repo hygiene quick wins

Three small but visible repo-hygiene gaps surfaced by the repo-best-practice sweep:

### 1. `tools/causa/LICENSE` missing

Every other artefact directory has a `LICENSE` file: `implementation/*/LICENSE`, `tools/story/LICENSE`, `tools/story-mcp/LICENSE`, `tools/template/LICENSE`, `tools/pair2-mcp/LICENSE`. `tools/causa/` is the outlier.

Fix: add MIT to match.

### 2. `CLAUDE.md` unfilled scaffold sections

Top-level `CLAUDE.md` lines 75-90 contain placeholder sections:
- `_Add your build and test commands here_`
- `_Add a brief overview of your project architecture_`
- `_Add your project-specific conventions here_`

These weaken the file's authority. Either fill them or remove the empty headings.

### 3. `tools/story/deps.edn` Reagent version drift

`tools/story/deps.edn:25` pins `reagent/reagent {:mvn/version "1.2.0"}` while the rest of the repo pins `2.0.1`:
- `implementation/core/deps.edn:41`
- `tools/causa/deps.edn:42`
- `implementation/shadow-cljs.edn:87`
- `tools/template/src/clj/new/re_frame2/reagent/deps.edn:20`

The consolidated shadow build will resolve to 2.0.1 (since `implementation/shadow-cljs.edn` wins), so this only matters for `clojure -M:test` from `tools/story/`. Bump to 2.0.1 for consistency.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-repo-best-practice-2026-05-13.md` §2 / §10 / Bead candidates 4, 6, 7 — all P2
notes: PR #594 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/594
close_reason: Closed

title: [spec] Stub tools/causa-mcp/spec/000-Vision.md + Principles + DESIGN-RATIONALE
description:
## Stub the causa-mcp spec folder

`tools/causa-mcp/` does not exist on disk. The future shape is described in `tools/causa/spec/010-MCP-Server.md` (transport, 18-tool catalogue, origin tagging, Chrome MCP comparison are all from Causa's side).

Forward references that need a home:
- `tools/causa/spec/010-MCP-Server.md` is the entirety of causa-mcp's contract.
- `tools/causa/README.md:84-94` advertises `npm install -g @day8/re-frame2-causa-mcp` — npm coord is locked even though the package doesn't exist.

The spec is almost ready — it lives inside Causa and needs to be lifted into the dedicated folder when implementation work begins. Stub it now to prevent the lift duplicating edits.

## Proposed scope

Scaffold `tools/causa-mcp/spec/`:
- `000-Vision.md` — what causa-mcp is, the separate-jar rationale, the MCP-over-stdio shape
- `Principles.md` — bundle-isolation, EDN-canonical, origin tagging
- `DESIGN-RATIONALE.md` — locked direction-setting decisions (separate-jar, MCP-over-stdio, origin tagging, 18-tool catalogue scope)
- `API.md` — tool catalogue (pointing at the full enumeration in 010-MCP-Server.md until implementation lands)

Lift content deliberately from `tools/causa/spec/010-MCP-Server.md` rather than duplicating. When implementation lands, the dedicated folder gets fleshed out per the pair2-mcp shape (`001-Wire-Protocol`, `002-nREPL-Transport`, `003-Tool-Catalogue`).

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-tools-spec-completeness-2026-05-13.md` §`tools/causa-mcp/spec/` — P3 (the audit flagged P3 but lifting to P2 because Causa's MCP work depends on this not being a duplication trap when it lands).
notes: PR #576 — scaffold landed (tools/causa-mcp/spec/000-Vision.md + Principles.md + DESIGN-RATIONALE.md + README.md + spec/README.md). API.md deferred to implementation phase per Vision.md §Status — the catalogue prose will migrate from tools/causa/spec/010-MCP-Server.md into a new 003-Tool-Catalogue.md, and the API.md companion lands with it.

title: [cut] fx/unregister-fx alias + one call site (http_machine_wrapper.cljc)
description:
## Transient back-compat alias

`implementation/core/src/re_frame/fx.cljc:106-109`:

```clojure
;; Back-compat alias retained transiently — internal callers may still
;; reach for `unregister-fx`. Pre-alpha: prefer `clear-fx`; the alias
;; can be cut once internal references are migrated.
(def unregister-fx clear-fx)
```

The comment names its own end-state: cut once internal references are migrated.

## Call sites

- `implementation/http/src/re_frame/http_machine_wrapper.cljc:140` — `(fx/unregister-fx stub-fx-id)` → rewrite to `(fx/clear-fx stub-fx-id)`.
- `implementation/core/test/re_frame/fx_test.clj:382` — comment only; the test itself uses `clear-fx`.

## Proposed fix

Rewrite the one call site (`http_machine_wrapper.cljc:140`), delete the alias at `fx.cljc:106-109`, delete the comment reference in the test.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-backcompat-cuts-2026-05-13.md` §S4 / B-4 — P2
notes: PR #573 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/573

title: [cut] epoch.cljc: drop legacy top-level :rf/snapshot-version slot tolerance
description:
## Runtime back-compat tolerance for intra-v2 format change

`implementation/epoch/src/re_frame/epoch.cljc:557-565, 604-627`:

The `snapshot-version` fn tries `[:meta :rf/snapshot-version]` OR the top-level `:rf/snapshot-version`. The docstring says: "Tolerates the legacy top-level `:rf/snapshot-version` slot for snapshots written before the meta-nesting was finalised."

This is an intra-v2 format change — there are no v1 snapshots in the wild. Pre-alpha, the legacy slot serves no users.

## Proposed fix

1. `snapshot-version` → just `(get-in snapshot [:meta :rf/snapshot-version])`.
2. Drop the docstring tolerance clause + the matching line in `machine-version-mismatch`.
3. Delete `restore-version-legacy-snapshot-slot-tolerated` test in `implementation/epoch/test/re_frame/epoch_test.clj:559-591`.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-backcompat-cuts-2026-05-13.md` §S1 / B-1 — P2
notes: PR #571: https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/571
close_reason: Closed

title: [cut] Drop unary-fx-handler back-compat path from do-fx; promote O-5 to M-rule
description:
## Runtime back-compat tax — unary fx handler shim

`spec/002-Frames.md:630-643`:

> "**Backwards compatibility — legacy unary fx handlers**. Existing fx libraries shipping unary handlers `(fn [args] (rf/dispatch ...))` continue to work without update… The dynamic var is a compatibility shim, not the primary mechanism."

`do-fx` detects arity and wraps unary handlers in a `*current-frame*` dynamic-var binding. Per pre-alpha, the migration skill can mechanically rewrite `(fn [args] body)` → `(fn [_ args] body)`. The runtime arity-detection + dynamic-var wrapping is residue.

## Proposed fix

- Drop the unary-handler back-compat path from `do-fx`.
- Drop the `*current-frame*` dynamic-var binding for the unary case.
- Promote O-5 (currently in MIGRATION.md L1724-1769) to a mandatory M-rule. The migration skill makes the rewrite mechanical Type A.
- Reframe `002-Frames.md` lines 15, 331, 339 to drop the "for backwards compat" lead-in language — those grammars are correct designs, not back-compat tax.

## Gate

Verify the migration skill is mature enough to do `(fn [args] body)` → `(fn [_ args] body)` mechanically before cutting the runtime path. If not, gate the runtime cut behind a skill upgrade.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-backcompat-cuts-2026-05-13.md` §S12 / B-11 — P2 (runtime cut)
notes: PR #599 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/599
close_reason: Closed

title: [cut] routing.cljc: pick canonical name for fragment-only-nav trace; drop dual emission
description:
## Runtime dual-emission for fragment-only nav

`implementation/routing/src/re_frame/routing.cljc:752-763`:

```clojure
;; Emitted alongside the legacy :rf.route/url-changed pending its eventual removal.
(trace/emit! :event :rf.route/url-changed {...})
(trace/emit! :event :route.url/fragment-changed {...})
```

The runtime emits BOTH. Spec 012 still names `:rf.route/url-changed` as canonical (`spec/012-Routing.md:81-83, 720, 769-772`). The two surfaces disagree about which is canonical and which is being removed.

## Needs decision

Pick one:

- **(a) Spec 012's `:rf.route/url-changed` is canonical** — drop the second emission (kill `:route.url/fragment-changed`).
- **(b) `:route.url/fragment-changed` is canonical** — update Spec 012, fixture `route-fragment-change.edn`, `docs/guide/17-routing.md` and `17a-routing-reference.md`, then drop the dual emission.

Either way: one name, one emission. The dual-emission state is residue.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-backcompat-cuts-2026-05-13.md` §S2 / B-2 — P2
notes: PR #574 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/574. Canonical name chosen: :rf.route/url-changed (matches Spec 009/012 :rf.route/* reserved-namespace convention; the spec corpus, guide chapters, and conformance fixture already used this form). Dropped: :route.url/fragment-changed (only existed at the emit site + a non-strict trace_test gap-check). Files: routing.cljc, trace_test.clj, skills/re-frame2/reference/tooling/routing.md. Routing tests 28/110 green. Not closed — awaiting review/merge.
close_reason: Closed

title: [docs] MIGRATION.md headline: drop 'maximum backwards compatibility' framing
description:
## MIGRATION.md headline contradicts pre-alpha stance

`spec/MIGRATION.md:1992` (and echoed in `skills/re-frame-migration/reference/sequencing.md:17`):

> "You are migrating a ClojureScript codebase from re-frame v1.x to **re-frame2**. The headline expectation is that **most codebases require no changes at all** — re-frame2 is designed for maximum backwards compatibility."

This phrasing is at odds with:

- `CHANGELOG.md` entry "Removed: The `re-frame/re-frame` 1.x compatibility shim. v1 and v2 cannot coexist on a single classpath".
- 40+ M-rules and O-rules in MIGRATION.md itself, including Type-A mandatory rewrites for namespace renames, effect-map shape changes, removed interceptors, removed surfaces.
- The actual breaking-change set in `18-from-re-frame-v1.md`: "small breaking-change set" plus "fix a handful of compile errors".

The current phrasing actively misleads agents reading MIGRATION.md cold.

## Proposed fix

Replace "designed for maximum backwards compatibility" with the truer framing: "re-frame2 is a small breaking-change set on the v1 API; most call sites compile unchanged but the breaking changes are real and the migration skill handles them mechanically."

The "median migration is M-0 + a handful of compile errors" framing in `18-from-re-frame-v1.md` is the right voice — port it back into MIGRATION.md §Your task.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-backcompat-cuts-2026-05-13.md` §S10 / B-9 — P2 (doc-only but high-visibility)
notes: PR #568 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/568. Reframed §Your task (line 1992) from 'designed for maximum backwards compatibility' to honest voice ('small, well-defined breaking-change set on top of the v1 re-frame.core API — most call sites compile unchanged, but the breakages are real (40+ M-/O-rules) and you are the mechanism that applies them. Median migration is M-0 plus a handful of compile errors; worst case touches a dozen surfaces'). Also tightened step 1 + 2 to bump-then-sweep instead of bump-and-hope. Intro paragraph (line 23) already aligned, left untouched. No other 'drop-in'/'preserve v1'/'backwards compat' wording found in MIGRATION.md.
close_reason: Closed

title: [cut] Cut run-test-sync v1 compatibility shim + add MIGRATION rule
description:
## V1 compatibility shim — pre-alpha cut

`run-test-sync` is explicitly badged as a "v1 compatibility shim" in API.md (L507), the docstring (`test_support.cljc` L53-55, L326, L440-450), and Spec 008 (L32, L428, L464-474, L476-478). It also surfaces in cheatsheet, SKILL reference, and the guide (`docs/guide/13-testing.md` L253-257).

Its ONLY purpose is to let v1 tests port mechanically by snapshot/restoring the registrar around a body that doesn't even need sync-draining (v2 already drains sync).

Per pre-alpha, the migration skill rewrites `re-frame.test/run-test-sync` → idiomatic v2 (`with-fresh-registrar` or inline `dispatch-sync` inside a fixture-wrapped `deftest`). The runtime doesn't need to carry the shim.

## Proposed fix

1. Delete the macro from `implementation/core/src/re_frame/test_support.cljc`.
2. Delete the row in `spec/API.md`, the column in `spec/008-Testing.md`, the cheatsheet row, the SKILL reference, the guide subsection.
3. Add an `M-NN` rule in `spec/MIGRATION.md` if not already covered: rewrite `re-frame-test/run-test-sync` body to use `with-fresh-registrar` (or whichever fixture the test was already inside).

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-backcompat-cuts-2026-05-13.md` §S6 / B-6 — P2 (cleanest "cut" candidate because it's labelled as a shim).
notes: PR #580 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/580. Cut run-test-sync v1 shim from re-frame.test-support; deleted the macro, dropped 3 test cases, and updated spec/008, API.md, MIGRATION.md (§M-25 forwarded, §M-50 added), docs/guide/13-testing.md, skills (re-frame2 testing + cheatsheet + spec/inputs.md; re-frame-migration automated-transforms + breaking-changes). 11 files. Replaced ~9 documentation call sites (no runtime call sites — the macro was only used in its own test file). Tests green: core JVM 350/1952, CLJS node-test 808/2086. Do NOT close.
close_reason: Closed

title: [comments] Sweep cut: Per rf2-* task-tracker references in source (431 occurrences)
description:
## Comment over-density — rf2-* purge

Count: **431 `Per rf2-*` occurrences** in `implementation/` source. **140 in `implementation/core/src/`** alone. `tools/` adds another **65**.

These are internal bd-tracker task IDs. They mean nothing to a future reader: "Per rf2-3nn8" tells you there was a bead, not why the code is this shape.

The surrounding text already states the reason in most cases; the bead id is then redundant. Where the citation IS the substance (no other context), the citation should be replaced with the actual reason.

## Proposed approach

- Cut every `Per rf2-NNNN` reference where the surrounding text already states the reason.
- Where the bead id IS the entire content of the comment, replace with a one-line reason (often the bead title compresses into one sentence).
- Sweep `implementation/core/src/` first (highest density).

## High parallelism

Each file is independent. Agents can dispatch one bead per file once this umbrella bead is split (or sweep can ship as one PR per directory).

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-comments-explanation-2026-05-13.md` §"big cuts" item 1 + first bead candidate — P2

title: [comments] Collapse public-fn docstrings to ≤6 lines on re-frame.core wrappers
description:
## Comment over-density — docstring trim

`core.cljc`'s public `reg-*` macros and other public surfaces carry 8-40 line docstrings each, often containing tutorial-grade examples, cross-references to Spec sections, redundant "See also" lists, and per-feature artefact split lore. The reference docstring belongs in the underlying fn (`events/reg-event-db` etc.); the re-export macro should be a one-liner that points at it.

## Targets

The wrapper macros / re-exports in `re-frame.core` to compress to ≤6 lines:

- `reg-event-db`, `reg-event-fx`, `reg-event-ctx`
- `reg-sub`, `reg-fx`, `reg-cofx`
- `reg-flow`, `reg-route`
- `reg-app-schema`, `reg-app-schemas`
- `reg-machine`, `reg-frame`, `reg-error-projector`
- `dispatch`, `dispatch-sync`, `subscribe`, `inject-cofx`
- `with-frame`, `with-overrides`
- `view`, `reg-view`, `reg-view*`
- `init!`, `configure`
- `current-frame`, `dispatcher`, `subscriber`, `bound-fn`
- `snapshot-of`, `get-frame-db`, `sub-cache`, `sub-topology`

Underlying fn docstrings (in `events.cljc`, `subs.cljc`, `fx.cljc`, etc.) stay full reference docs — they're the canonical home for the per-fn contract.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-comments-explanation-2026-05-13.md` §"big cuts" item 2 + second bead candidate — P2
notes: PR #612 https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/612 — 1428→1056 lines (-372), ~30 wrappers trimmed, JVM 357/1973 + CLJS 1151/3017 tests green.
close_reason: Closed

title: [test] Add :flow/trace to claimed-capabilities; verify flow-lifecycle fixture runs
description:
## Silently skipped fixture

`flow-lifecycle-emits-traces.edn` claims `:flow/trace` capability, which is NOT in `claimed-capabilities` in `conformance_test.clj`. The runner reports it as "skipped" but the test suite still passes green.

This is the rf2-2s1o emit-trace-on-flow-lifecycle contract — required for re-frame-10x v2's flow panel (Causa). It currently has ZERO conformance coverage, only the per-feature `flows_trace_test.clj` exercise.

## Proposed fix

EITHER:
- Add `:flow/trace` to `claimed-capabilities` in `conformance_test.clj` and verify the fixture then runs and passes.
- OR remove the cap from `flow-lifecycle-emits-traces.edn` if `:flow/trace` is genuinely not part of the claimed v1 capability set (unlikely, given Causa's flow panel depends on it).

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-test-coverage-rigour-2026-05-13.md` §Finding 2 — B-2 — P2
notes: PR #579: https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/579 — added :flow/trace to claimed-capabilities in both JVM (conformance_test.clj) and CLJS (conformance_corpus_cljs_test.cljs) runners. JVM: 111/111 passed, 0 skipped. CLJS: 111/111 passed, 0 skipped. flow/lifecycle-emits-traces now executes in both.
close_reason: Closed

title: [test] :actor/timeout — ship a fixture or remove from claim set
description:
## Unverifiable claim

`:actor/timeout` is in `claimed-capabilities` (per `conformance_test.clj`) but NO fixture in `spec/conformance/fixtures/` references it. The claim is unverifiable — there's nothing to PASS.

## Proposed fix

EITHER:
- Ship a fixture exercising `:actor/timeout` (likely covering machine `:after` plus actor invoke patterns).
- OR remove `:actor/timeout` from the claim set and the spec's capability list.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-test-coverage-rigour-2026-05-13.md` §Finding 3 — B-3 — P2

Ship-a-fixture not viable — capability retired per rf2-3y3y; :fsm/delayed-after subsumes it (spec/005 §Capability matrix, MIGRATION §M-44). Dropped from both JVM + CLJS claim sets with retirement note cross-referencing spec. JVM 110/110, CLJS 110/110. Do not close — awaiting merge.

title: [test] Conformance runner: fail on silent fixture skips (distinguish out-of-claim vs typo)
description:
## Conformance runner silently skips fixtures

A fixture targeting a capability not in `claimed-capabilities` is reported via `println` in the test output, but the `(is (zero? (count failed)))` assertion IGNORES skipped fixtures. This is intentional per rf2-3xt7 ("skipped fixtures are explicitly reported but neither claim conformance nor block it") — but combined with the finding that `flow-lifecycle-emits-traces.edn` is silently skipped because `:flow/trace` is missing from `claimed-capabilities`, it creates a coverage-rot risk.

## Proposed fix

Distinguish "intentionally out-of-claim" (a capability the host doesn't implement) from "missing-from-claim-set typo".

One mechanism: require every fixture's caps to be in EITHER `claimed-capabilities` OR an explicit `unsupported-capabilities` set. Fixtures whose caps land in neither set FAIL the suite — they're typos that need explicit decision.

## Related beads in same stream

- `flow-lifecycle-emits-traces.edn` silently skipped — separate P2 bead (B-2).
- `:actor/timeout` claimed but no fixture — separate P2 bead (B-3).

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-test-coverage-rigour-2026-05-13.md` §Finding 10 — B-10 — P2
notes: PR #598 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/598
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] http_interceptors_test.clj: fix bogus poll-loop predicate + 200ms guard
description:
## Latent test flake

`http_interceptors_test.clj` lines 209-218 is a poll loop whose exit condition is structurally broken:

```clojure
(or (nil? @seen-on-default)
    (and (nil? @seen-on-other) ;; ... true))
```

The `(and (nil? @seen-on-other) true)` redundancy means the predicate effectively evaluates as `(or (nil? @default) (nil? @other))` — exits early when EITHER atom is still nil, rather than waiting for BOTH to be non-nil. The comment "we still need to know the request fired — sleep a beat then check again" telegraphs the author's confusion, and the 200ms guard at line 219 papers over it.

## Proposed fix

Rewrite the poll loop to wait for the actual both-flags-set condition:

```clojure
(loop []
  (cond
    (and (some? @seen-on-default)
         (some? @seen-on-other))
    :done

    :else (do (Thread/sleep 5) (recur))))
```

Drop the 200ms fallback guard (or keep it as a max-wait timeout, not a "we don't really know what we're waiting for").

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-test-coverage-rigour-2026-05-13.md` §Finding 6 — B-6 — P2
notes: PR #572 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/572
close_reason: Closed

title: [test] Helix + UIx adapter integration tests — port reagent's high-value specs
description:
## Adapter test asymmetry

Reagent has 23 test files; Helix and UIx each have only 3 (`*_parity_*`, `*_derived_value_baseline_*`, `*_source_coord_dom_elision_prod_*`). These cover hot-reload + render-key + source-coord-DOM contract but leave events / http-managed / websocket / long_running_work / nine_states / routing / cross_spec etc. unexercised at the adapter unit-test level.

The framework-level tests run agnostic of adapter, but adapter-specific integration regressions (e.g. UIx's `useSyncExternalStore` semantics, Helix's hooks-vs-reactive bridge) will only surface in production.

## Why Helix matters more

Helix has a real production user (Vouch). UIx is in pre-alpha. Recommend deeper Helix coverage first.

## Proposed scope

Port Reagent's high-value integration specs to each substrate:

For Helix (priority):
- `events_cljs_test`
- `http_managed_cljs_test`
- `long_running_work_cljs_test`
- `nine_states_cljs_test`
- `routing_cljs_test`
- `cross_spec_cljs_test`

For UIx: same set, post-Helix.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-test-coverage-rigour-2026-05-13.md` §B / Finding 5 — B-5 — P2
notes: PR #583 — adapter-parity test ports for Helix + UIx (rf2-ta4b5-adapter-parity-tests). 12 new test files (6 per adapter), 86 new deftests (43 per adapter): events, runtime, routing, dispatch_frame_capture, http_managed, cross_spec. Browser-only scenarios deliberately excluded (ride through Playwright). 894 tests / 2306 assertions / 0 failures.
close_reason: Closed

title: [spec] Scaffold tools/machines-viz/spec/ — MachineChart contract + share-URL encoding
description:
## Spec scaffolding for machines-viz

`tools/machines-viz/` is referenced from Causa Spec 003 + Story 005 but has no directory on disk. The `MachineChart` component contract is partially specified from Causa's side (`tools/causa/spec/003-Machine-Inspector.md` lists props: `:machine-id`, `:frame-id`, `:on-state-click`, `:on-transition-click`) — but `tools/machines-viz/`'s own surface (the `MachineChart` API, the read-only viewer page, the `#machine=` URL fragment encoding) has no normative home.

Per the per-tool spec convention in `tools/README.md`, every tool ships `000-Vision.md` + `Principles.md` + `DESIGN-RATIONALE.md` + `API.md` + numbered capability docs.

## Cross-stream dedupe

- `sweep-tools-spec-completeness-2026-05-13.md` §`tools/machines-viz/` — P3 stub.
- `sweep-tools-vs-sota-2026-05-13.md` §machines-viz — P2 (the SOTA audit grades it more urgent because there's a strong peer set: Stately Visualizer, XState Inspector, Mermaid, statecharts.dev).

Filed at P2 reflecting the more urgent reading.

## Proposed scope

Scaffold `tools/machines-viz/spec/`:

- `000-Vision.md` — what `MachineChart` is, what the read-only viewer guarantees, share-URL encoding format
- `Principles.md` — bundle-isolation, EDN-first, embedding contract from Causa side
- `DESIGN-RATIONALE.md` — lock decisions migrated from `tools/causa/spec/003-Machine-Inspector.md`
- `API.md` — component prop schema (`:machine-id`, `:frame-id`, `:on-state-click`, `:on-transition-click`), the `MachineChart` exported symbols
- Lift content deliberately from `tools/causa/spec/003-Machine-Inspector.md` rather than duplicating; cross-link

## Source

title: [story] Test Codegen — record canvas interactions as :play body (SB9 killer feature)
description:
## SB9 killer feature: Test Codegen

Storybook 9 records clicks/fills on the canvas and saves them as a `:play` sequence appended to the variant. Story has `:play` and assertions but no recorder. The mode-tabs primitive could host this naturally.

## Proposed shape

- Mode tab in Story (e.g. `:record`) that enters a recording state.
- Click / fill / select interactions on the canvas are captured.
- Stop recording emits a `:play` body that the user can append to the variant (or, via story-mcp `register-variant`, write back to source).
- Adjacent bead: story-mcp `record-as-variant` tool (filed separately as P2).

## Cross-stream dedupe

Same gap as `sweep-tools-vs-sota-2026-05-13.md` §story / bead "Test Codegen" AND §story-mcp / bead "record-as-variant" — both file at P2.

The implementations are coupled (the recorder runs in Story; the codegen surface is exposed through story-mcp). Filed as one combined bead so they ship together.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-tools-vs-sota-2026-05-13.md` §story / first P2 bead + §story-mcp / first P2 bead
notes: PR #603 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/603. Test Codegen recorder: pure-data state machine + trace-bus listener in re-frame.story.recorder (.cljc); chrome-wide REC chip + save-as-variant modal in re-frame.story.ui.recorder (.cljs); listener install at shell mount; toolbar chip lands just before [reset]. Public API: start-recording! / stop-recording! / recording? / recorder-state / clear-recording! / gen-play-snippet. spec/005-SOTA-Features adds the Test Codegen subsection + ship-list entry. 26 JVM tests / 60 assertions; 10 CLJS tests; full Story JVM suite 259/259; full CLJS node-test 1136/1136.
close_reason: Closed

title: [mcp] Token-budget posture across MCP triplet (pair2-mcp + story-mcp + causa-mcp)
description:
## 2026 trend axis: token-budget posture in MCP tools

Microsoft (April 2026) now recommends Playwright CLI **over** Playwright MCP for coding agents because MCP responses are 4× larger in tokens. The re-frame2 MCP triplet (pair2-mcp, story-mcp, causa-mcp) has the same exposure:

- pair2-mcp `snapshot` mega-op is large by design
- pair2-mcp `subscribe` batches can blow up
- story-mcp `run-variant` outputs can be large
- causa-mcp planned read-tools return rich trace/epoch payloads

No spec for any of the three currently addresses token-budget posture.

## Proposed posture per tool

Each tool spec should land:

- **`:compact` flag** or per-tool default that trims response payloads to the minimum
- **Budget hint in `list-tools`** ("this tool typically returns X tokens")
- **Pagination on long lists** (story-mcp's `list-stories` may already do this implicitly; should be explicit)

## Cross-stream dedupe

Same axis flagged for:
- pair2-mcp specifically (`sweep-tools-vs-sota-2026-05-13.md` §pair2-mcp / Gap 1 + per-tool bead "pair2-mcp token-budget guidance").
- story-mcp specifically (same doc §story-mcp / Gap 6).
- causa-mcp (covered by prior `causa-mcp-vs-chrome-mcp-2026-05-13.md` audit).

Filed as one umbrella bead because the axis is shared.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-tools-vs-sota-2026-05-13.md` §Cross-cutting findings §2 — P2
notes: PR #602 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/602

title: [story] Re-run feature-parity matrix against Storybook 9 (deltas: Test Codegen, story-from-UI, tag facets, Vitest reporter)
description:
## Storybook 9 parity drift

The 2026-05-13 `story-sota-audit` benchmarked Story against Storybook 8. Storybook 9 shipped mid-2025 and is the CURRENT SOTA bar. New SB9 features create fresh gaps:

| SB9 feature | Story status today | Gap severity |
|---|---|---|
| Storybook Test (Vitest-powered, chrome-level) | partial | P2 |
| Test Codegen (record-and-save interactions) | missing | P2 |
| Story generation from UI | missing | P3 |
| Tag taxonomies (status / role / team facets) | partial | P3 |
| 48% leaner runtime | unknown | P3 |
| Built-in visual testing (Chromatic-powered) | N/A | — Story punts pixels |

## Proposed scope

Re-run the feature parity matrix against SB9 (not SB8). Update `tools/story/spec/005-SOTA-Features.md` (or create `findings/story-sb9-delta.md`) covering:

- Test Codegen — the SB9 killer feature; gap analysis. (Separate P2 bead for Test Codegen implementation if scope warrants.)
- Story-from-UI — "save current canvas state as a variant" affordance vs SB9.
- Tag taxonomy facets (status/role/team/feature) — Story's canonical-vs-custom split is shallower.
- Vitest-reporter shape for Story's `:test` mode (rf2-qmjo) — mode-tab exists, reporter UI doesn't.
- Bundle-size baseline measurement against SB9's "48% leaner" benchmark.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-tools-vs-sota-2026-05-13.md` §story (extends existing audit) — P2
notes: Findings: ai/findings/story-storybook9-parity-20260513.md. Beads filed (8): rf2-39u9e (P3 assertion-inserter), rf2-one3t (P2 save-variant-from-args / SB9 story-from-UI parity), rf2-frtec (P3 reg-tag :axis + :default-filter), rf2-nwiwr (P3 sidebar tag badges), rf2-q0irb (P2 chrome-level test widget + sidebar status dots / SB9 Vitest-reporter parity), rf2-z1h0f (P3 watch-mode), rf2-xgay8 (P3 bundle-size benchmark), rf2-lc36w (P3 :play step-through scrubber via 10x epoch buffer). Biggest gaps: (1) chrome-level test widget aggregating run-all-tests with per-row sidebar status dots — Story has the runtime but only renders per-variant pane; (2) save-current-canvas-args-as-variant button — Story's recorder covers interaction-recording but not args-tweak recording. NOPEs documented in findings §3: favicon, visual tests (already deferred), React Native, coverage. No spec drift fixes shipped — research-only per the bead contract. Test Codegen (one of the four named gaps) was already shipped via rf2-5fc15.
close_reason: Closed

title: [arch] MCP triplet: factor out re-frame2-mcp-base for shared primitives
description:
## Shared MCP primitives across the triplet

pair2-mcp, story-mcp, and causa-mcp (planned) overlap on:

- `discover-app` (health probe; "is the runtime there, what frames exist, what's the build id?")
- `eval-cljs` / `evaluate-cljs` (escape hatch)
- `subscribe` / `unsubscribe` (streaming trace / epoch)
- `tail-build` (hot-reload await)

Three jars × four shared primitives = 12 implementation sites for four concepts.

## Proposed factoring

A `day8/re-frame2-mcp-base` jar holding the shared runtime + the four primitives, with each tool jar adding its own domain-specific tools on top. Benefits:

- Cut implementation duplication by ~30%.
- Guarantee the shared primitives have identical shapes across the three jars (the agent learns one `:origin` taxonomy, not three).
- Reduce per-consumer wiring: one `re-frame2-mcp-suite` aggregator jar bundles all three. (Optional convenience; jars stay separate.)

This is a v1.1 refactor at most — none of the three is shipped yet, so the cost is purely "decide the factoring before everyone implements separately."

## Decision needed

- Adopt `re-frame2-mcp-base` factoring before causa-mcp / story-mcp / pair2-mcp implementations land their independent primitives.
- Or accept the duplication as the cost of keeping each jar independent.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-tools-vs-sota-2026-05-13.md` §Cross-cutting findings §1 — P2

title: [spec] pair2-mcp: harmonise tool count (7 vs 9) across spec + README
description:
## Drift across pair2-mcp spec + README

The spec corpus has lost agreement on the pair2-mcp tool count:

- `tools/pair2-mcp/spec/000-Vision.md:23` — "the seven canonical pair2 ops"
- `tools/pair2-mcp/spec/Principles.md:23, 88, 117` — variously "seven" / "six" / "seven"
- `tools/pair2-mcp/spec/003-Tool-Catalogue.md:3` — "The nine MCP tools."
- `tools/pair2-mcp/spec/API.md:113, 257` — "the nine tools"
- `tools/pair2-mcp/README.md` table enumerates 7 (discover-app / eval-cljs / dispatch / trace-window / watch-epochs / tail-build / snapshot) — missing `subscribe` and `unsubscribe`

The actual shipped tool count appears to be 9 (the original seven + `snapshot` + `subscribe` + `unsubscribe`, or 8 depending on where `snapshot` and the streaming pair landed). Prose contradicts itself.

## Cross-stream dedupe

Same defect surfaced by:
- `sweep-tools-spec-completeness-2026-05-13.md` §`tools/pair2-mcp/spec/` (bead "harmonise tool count").
- `sweep-readmes-currency-2026-05-13.md` §`tools/pair2-mcp/README.md` (bead "reconcile pair2-mcp tool count").

## Proposed fix

- Pin a single canonical source-of-truth count by counting `003-Tool-Catalogue.md`'s top-level `## <tool-name>` headings.
- Update prose in `000-Vision.md:23`, `Principles.md:23/88/117`, `API.md:113/257` to match.
- Update `tools/pair2-mcp/README.md` "Tool surface" table to enumerate all canonical tools (likely 9 — add `snapshot`, `subscribe`, `unsubscribe` rows).
- Update install snippet posture to match `tools/template/README.md`'s "until published" idiom rather than describing the unshipped npm coord as if shipped.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-tools-spec-completeness-2026-05-13.md`
- `ai/findings/sweep-readmes-currency-2026-05-13.md`
notes: PR #578 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/578 — canonical count = 9 (matches 003-Tool-Catalogue.md headings + tools.cljs registration); 11 prose updates + 2 README table-row additions across 4 files (README.md, spec/000-Vision.md, spec/Principles.md, spec/DESIGN-RATIONALE.md); spec/API.md + spec/README.md already said nine. Out-of-scope follow-ups: same drift exists in tools/pair2-mcp/src/re_frame_pair2_mcp/server.cljs:14 and tools/pair2-mcp/test/stdio-roundtrip.js:6,83 (bead scope hard-locked to spec/ + README.md). slug-check passes.

title: [spec] Story spec 011-* numbering collision + 012-* gap + README index drift
description:
## Story spec numbering collision + README index drift

`tools/story/spec/`:

1. **Numbering collision** — Two files share `011-` prefix: `011-Actions-Panel.md` AND `011-Trace-Scrubber-Cross-Ref.md`. The "ordered, numbered" convention is defeated.

2. **Numbering gap** — No `012-*.md`. The jump from 011 → 013 is unexplained.

3. **README index out-of-date** — `tools/story/spec/README.md` lists files up to `011-Actions-Panel.md` but does NOT list:
   - `011-Trace-Scrubber-Cross-Ref.md` (rf2-sxwvf)
   - `013-Static-Build.md` (the `story:build` static export feature)
   A reader following the README will miss two real capability documents.

4. **README index says "001–007 are normative"** but capability docs go to 013. `README.md:24` reads "the capability docs (001–007) are normative — Stage 2 implements 001, Stage 3 implements 002, and so on." This caps the normative set at 007 even though 008–013 are clearly normative (e.g. `008-Docs-Mode.md` is the entire docs-pane spec).

## Proposed fix

- Decide canonical numbering for the colliding `011-*` files. Options: (a) renumber `011-Trace-Scrubber-Cross-Ref.md` to `012-`; (b) renumber `013-Static-Build.md` down; (c) document the deliberate gap.
- Fan out renumbering edits to any internal cross-references.
- Refresh `tools/story/spec/README.md` index to list ALL files including 011-Trace-Scrubber-Cross-Ref and 013-Static-Build.
- Extend the "001–007 are normative" line to cover the actual normative range (001–013).

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-tools-spec-completeness-2026-05-13.md` §`tools/story/spec/` — first two bead candidates (P2, P2).
notes: PR #577 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/577. Renamed 011-Trace-Scrubber-Cross-Ref.md -> 012-Trace-Scrubber-Cross-Ref.md; 011-Actions-Panel stays as 011; 013-Static-Build stays as 013. README index now enumerates 012 + 013 and the normative range now reads 001-013. Three source files updated to point at the new 012- filename. Not closing per instructions.
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] configure does not handle :strict-subs or :ssr despite API.md documenting them
description:
## Truth-mismatch between spec and code

`API.md §Configure keys` (L570) lists five keys: `:epoch-history`, `:trace-buffer`, `:sub-cache`, `:strict-subs`, `:ssr`. The actual `(rf/configure ...)` fn in `core.cljc:1318-1345` has a CLOSED switch over only THREE keys (`:epoch-history`, `:trace-buffer`, `:sub-cache`), with no `default` clause — `:strict-subs` and `:ssr` fall through to `nil` and silently no-op.

So the public table is ahead of the implementation by two rows. Consumers reading API.md will believe `(rf/configure :strict-subs ...)` does something; it doesn't.

## Proposed fix

EITHER:

- **Land** `:strict-subs` and `:ssr` handlers in the `case` so the API.md table is truthful, OR
- **Document explicitly** in API.md which keys are live (3) vs documented-for-future (2) — mark the deferred rows.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-api-elegance-2026-05-13.md` §Issue 14 — P2 (NEW). The audit calls this a TRUTH-MISMATCH between spec and code, not just an elegance smell.
notes: PR #604 (cut route — :strict-subs and :ssr trimmed from API.md/Conventions/Spec 011 / 006 / 008 / 009 / 010 / Spec-Schemas; configure_test.clj locks closed vocabulary). Awaiting review.
close_reason: Closed

title: [api] Rename with-overrides → with-fx-overrides
description:
## API elegance — three names for one concept

`:fx-overrides` is named three different ways:

| Layer | Name |
|---|---|
| Macro (test ergonomic) | `with-overrides` |
| Per-call opt key | `:fx-overrides` |
| Dynvar | `re-frame.router/*fx-overrides*` |

The macro should be `with-fx-overrides` to match the opt key. A future `with-interceptor-overrides` companion (API.md mentions `:interceptor-overrides` in the same opts map) would compose naturally. The bare `with-overrides` name sits ambiguously — overrides of what?

## Proposed fix

Rename `with-overrides` → `with-fx-overrides`. Sites:

- `implementation/core/src/re_frame/core.cljc:1110` — macro definition
- `spec/API.md` L506 — row
- Spec 002 / 008 cross-references
- test-support test files
- Any rf2-5uwl referencing bead.

Leaves bare `with-overrides` available for a future general-purpose override helper.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-api-elegance-2026-05-13.md` §Issue 4 — P2 (NEW)
notes: PR #595 https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/595 — rename with-overrides → with-fx-overrides + MIGRATION §M-50. JVM 353/1958 + CLJS 808/2086 green.
close_reason: Closed

title: [api] Make -reg-error-projector private at re-export site
description:
## API elegance — leading-dash convention violated

`core.cljc:696`:

```clojure
(def -reg-error-projector rf-ssr/-reg-error-projector)
```

This is exported from `re-frame.core` and reachable as `rf/-reg-error-projector`. The leading dash conventionally signals "private impl, callers should reach the public form" (Clojure's `defn-` discipline). The intent is clear from the comment block above (lines 690-695): the dash form is what the macro `reg-error-projector` delegates to; the macro is the user-facing surface.

The whole point of the `reg-error-projector` macro is the source-coord capture; users should NEVER reach the dash form.

## Proposed fix

- Change `(def -reg-error-projector ...)` to `(def ^:private -reg-error-projector ...)` at core.cljc:696.
- The CLJS branch at line 715 (`(def reg-error-projector -reg-error-projector)`) needs the dash form publicly visible within the compilation unit — `^:private` allows that.
- Verify no API.md row exists for the dash form.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-api-elegance-2026-05-13.md` §Issue 3 — P2 (NEW)
notes: PR #609 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/609. Branch rf2-ubeyt-reg-error-projector-private. Required a small extension beyond the bead description: the bead claimed `^:private` alone would work, but the JVM macro emitted ``(-reg-error-projector ~@args)`` which syntax-quote resolves to `re-frame.core/-reg-error-projector` at user compile site — accessing a private var from outside its ns fails (IllegalStateException, caught by source_coords_test.clj). Fix: macro now emits `rf-ssr/-reg-error-projector` directly (public fn in re-frame.core-ssr). CLJS branch keeps using private local alias (same-ns access OK). Zero external callers found. API.md row at line 43 documents only the public macro form, no edit needed. Tests: JVM core 357/0, JVM ssr 31/0, CLJS 1127/0.
close_reason: Closed

title: [causa] Causality Graph panel impl (Phase 4 of rf2-5aw5v)
description:
Phase 4 of rf2-5aw5v (Causa v1.0 epic). Implement the Causality Graph panel per `tools/causa/spec/001-Causality-Graph.md`. Builds on Phase 1 foundation (rf2-n6x4q), Phase 2 event-detail hero (rf2-op3bz), and Phase 3 Time Travel (rf2-t53ze).

## Why this panel next

The causality graph is the unique-to-Causa differentiator (no peer matches per rf2-76qxg audit). It renders cascade events as a graph node-arrows visualisation, threading `:dispatch-id` and `:parent-dispatch-id` across the cascade. Time Travel scrubber feeds it (selected epoch → highlight that cascade).

## Spec source

`tools/causa/spec/001-Causality-Graph.md` — full contract. Cross-ref `re-frame.trace.projection/group-cascades` (rf2-wvzgd merged) for the projection algebra.

## Scope

1. **Panel ns** `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/causality_graph.cljs`:
   - SVG-based graph rendering (nodes = trace events; arrows = `:parent-dispatch-id` lineage)
   - Layout algorithm: simple top-down or DAG layout
   - Color-coded by `:operation` (event = blue, fx = green, sub = purple, render = pink, error = red, warning = amber)
   - Node click → set `:rf.causa/selected-dispatch-id` (shared with event-detail hero panel)
   - Filter by selected epoch (via Time Travel selection) if active

2. **Pure-data helpers** `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/causality_graph_helpers.cljc`:
   - `project-cascades-to-graph` — turn group-cascades output into nodes + arrows
   - `compute-layout` — node positions (top-down DAG layout)
   - Pure-fn; JVM-runnable for unit tests

3. **Registry** updates:
   - Likely no new subs needed (reuses `:rf.causa/trace-buffer` + `:rf.causa/selected-dispatch-id` from Phase 2)
   - New event `:rf.causa/select-cascade-node` (if needed)

4. **Shell wire-in**: sidebar entry for Causality Graph; mount when selected.

5. **Tests** `tools/causa/test/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/causality_graph_test.cljc`:
   - Pure helpers: cascade → graph nodes/arrows transformation
   - Layout: deterministic positions for fixture cascade
   - Filter behaviour with selected-epoch active

## NOT in scope

- App-DB Diff panel (separate bead)
- Machine Inspector
- Other panels

## Verify

- `clojure -M:test` per artefact passes
- `npm run test:cljs`, `test:browser`, `test:elision`, `test:examples` pass
- Counter app + Causa preload: clicking the counter shows a cascade in the graph
- `mkdocs build --strict` passes

## Constraints

## Cross-refs

- rf2-n6x4q, rf2-op3bz, rf2-t53ze — prior phases

title: [causa] Time Travel panel impl (Phase 3 of rf2-5aw5v)
description:
Phase 3 of rf2-5aw5v (Causa v1.0 epic). Implement the Time Travel scrubber panel per the recently-fleshed-out `tools/causa/spec/002-Time-Travel.md` (PR #525 rewrote §Pinned snapshots with full semantics).

## Why this panel next

Phase 1 foundation landed (rf2-n6x4q). Phase 2 hero (event-detail, rf2-op3bz) landed. The Time Travel panel is the next obvious foundation — it unlocks the "scrub through epochs" affordance that other panels (App-DB Diff, Schema Timeline, Hydration Debugger) will consume.

## Spec source

`tools/causa/spec/002-Time-Travel.md` — full contract including:
- Pinned snapshots: 4-tuple capture `(epoch-id × frame-db × dispatch-id × user-label)`
- Eager-copied at pin time; survives ring-buffer age-out (detached chip)
- `Reset to pinned` uses `reset-frame-db!` (not `restore-epoch`)
- Session-scoped per Lock 4
- 32 pins/frame cap

Also reference Story's `scrubber.cljs` + `scrubber_xref.cljc` (rf2-sxwvf, merged) — Causa can borrow the pure-data scrubber helpers.

## Scope

1. **Panel ns** `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/time_travel.cljs`:
   - Timeline visualisation of epoch history (per `:rf/causa` frame)
   - Scrubber cursor draggable along timeline
   - "Reset to current" + "Reset to pinned" buttons
   - Pin button + pin label input
   - Pin chips (rendered alongside timeline)
   - Click cascade in event-detail panel → scroll-to-that-epoch in scrubber

2. **Registry** updates:
   - `:rf.causa/selected-epoch-id` sub
   - `:rf.causa/pinned-snapshots` sub (returns vector of 4-tuples)
   - `:rf.causa/select-epoch` event
   - `:rf.causa/pin-current` event
   - `:rf.causa/unpin` event
   - `:rf.causa/reset-to-epoch` event (uses `reset-frame-db!`)
   - `:rf.causa/reset-to-pinned` event

3. **Shell wire-in**: sidebar entry for Time Travel; mount panel in canvas when selected.

4. **Helper lift**: if Story's scrubber helpers (`scrubber_xref.cljc`) are general-purpose enough, reference them. Don't reimplement basic timeline algebra.

5. **Tests** `tools/causa/test/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/time_travel_test.cljc`:
   - Pin captures the 4-tuple correctly
   - Unpin removes from set
   - 32-pin cap enforced (per spec)
   - Reset-to-pinned uses `reset-frame-db!` not `restore-epoch`
   - Pins survive ring-buffer eviction (detached chip rendering)

## NOT in scope

- App-DB Diff panel (separate bead)
- Schema Timeline panel
- Other panels

## Verify

- `clojure -M:test` per artefact passes
- `npm run test:cljs`, `test:browser`, `test:elision`, `test:examples` pass
- Counter app with Causa preload + Ctrl+Shift+C → Time Travel panel functional; pin → Reset → state restores

title: [usability] Stamp :doc metadata on framework-registered fx/sub/cofx so handler-meta surfaces it
description:
Per audit rf2-1itgd (U8, D1/D2/D3).

A user calling `(rf/handler-meta :fx :rf.http/managed)` to introspect the canonical HTTP fx gets a metadata map with NO `:doc` key — Spec 014 lives in a separate file, the registration site doesn't stamp documentation.

Same is true for every framework-registered effect, sub, and cofx:

Effects (no :doc today):
- `:dispatch`, `:dispatch-later` (registered by fx.cljc)
- `:rf.machine/spawn`, `:rf.machine/destroy` (machines/lifecycle_fx)
- `:rf.fx/reg-flow`, `:rf.fx/clear-flow` (flows)
- `:rf.http/managed`, `:rf.http/managed-abort`, `:rf.http/managed-canned-success`, `:rf.http/managed-canned-failure` (http_managed)
- `:rf.nav/push-url`, `:rf.nav/replace-url`, `:rf.nav/scroll`, `:rf.route/with-nav-token` (routing)
- `:rf.server/set-status`, `:rf.server/set-header`, `:rf.server/append-header`, `:rf.server/set-cookie`, `:rf.server/delete-cookie`, `:rf.server/redirect` (ssr)

Subs:
- `:rf/machine`, `:rf/machine-has-tag?` (machines)
- `:rf/route`, `:rf.route/{id,params,query,transition,error,fragment,chain}`, `:rf/pending-navigation` (routing)
- `:rf/response`, `:rf/head`, `:rf/public-error` (ssr)

Cofx:
- `:db`, `:event` (cofx.cljc)
- `:rf.server/request` (ssr)

## Scope

For each framework-registered fx/sub/cofx, the call site that registers it should pass a metadata-map with a `:doc` key carrying a one-line description (and ideally a Spec cross-reference).

The :doc string then rides through to `(rf/handler-meta KIND id)` and `(rf/sub-topology)` — tools, IDE introspection, and AI agents reach it for free.

Pre-alpha: stamp freely.
close_reason: merged via PR #526 (API usability batch)

title: [usability] Add docstrings to interceptor primitives (->interceptor, assoc-coeffect, get-coeffect, ...)
description:
Per audit rf2-1itgd (U7).

implementation/core/src/re_frame/interceptor.cljc carries the canonical interceptor-body API but most surfaces have NO docstring or a tiny one:

- `->interceptor` (:14) — 2-line docstring, doesn't explain context map, doesn't show :before/:after signature
- `get-coeffect` (:25) — NO docstring
- `assoc-coeffect` (:30) — NO docstring
- `update-coeffect` (:33) — NO docstring
- `get-effect` (:36) — NO docstring
- `assoc-effect` (:41) — NO docstring

These are the surfaces a user writing a custom interceptor reaches for FIRST. `->interceptor` is in API.md §Standard interceptors as 'the primitive'.

## Scope

1. `->interceptor` — rewrite docstring. Explain ctx shape (`:coeffects`, `:effects` keys), name what :before/:after fns must return, show one example.
2. Five accessor fns — add one-line docstrings each.
3. Sync the canonical-source contract: ctx has `:coeffects {:db :event}` on input, `:effects {:db :fx}` on output; the chain machinery threads them.

Pre-alpha: rewrite freely.
close_reason: merged via PR #526 (API usability batch)

title: [usability] dispatch-sync docstring — lead with in-handler pitfall
description:
Per audit rf2-1itgd (U5).

implementation/core/src/re_frame/core.cljc:732 — `dispatch-sync` docstring says 'Run event-vec end-to-end synchronously, then drain.' No mention of the canonical newcomer footgun: calling dispatch-sync from inside a running event handler raises `:rf.error/dispatch-sync-in-handler` (documented in API.md L422 error contract).

## Scope

Rewrite dispatch-sync docstring (core.cljc:732 macro, dispatch-sync* impl):

1. Lead: 'Run event-vec end-to-end synchronously. **Never call from inside a running event handler** — use `:fx [[:dispatch event]]` instead. Raises `:rf.error/dispatch-sync-in-handler` if you do.'
2. Then mention the contract — drain to fixed point before return.
3. Cross-ref `dispatch` (the production form).
4. Note: `dispatch-sync` is for tests, REPL, and bootstrap.

Same shape applies to dispatch's docstring (less critical — that one is the production default).

Pre-alpha: rewrite freely.
close_reason: merged via PR #526 (API usability batch)

title: [usability] Rewrite reg-cofx + inject-cofx docstrings as a pair
description:
Per audit rf2-1itgd (U4).

implementation/core/src/re_frame/cofx.cljc:53 (`reg-cofx`) and :66 (`inject-cofx`) document themselves in isolation. The cofx mental model needs the pair documented together because:
- `reg-cofx` defines a coeffect (source of input data)
- `inject-cofx` is how an event handler USES the cofx (interceptor-position)

Today a user reading `reg-cofx` sees a handler signature but no consumption path; a user reading `inject-cofx` sees three arities of internal call-site mechanics but no worked example of the canonical usage:

    (reg-cofx :now (fn [ctx] (assoc-in ctx [:coeffects :now] (js/Date.))))
    (reg-event-fx :foo [(inject-cofx :now)] (fn [{:keys [now]} _] ...))

## Scope

1. `reg-cofx` docstring: name handler signature, name the canonical cofx ids `:db`, `:event`, name the consumption surface (`inject-cofx`), example.
2. `inject-cofx` docstring (cofx.cljc:66 + core.cljc:773 macro): LEAD with the use case (interceptor in `reg-event-fx` interceptor-vector); push the call-site mechanism details to a Notes section.
3. Cross-link both ways.

Pre-alpha: rewrite freely.
close_reason: merged via PR #526 (API usability batch)

title: [usability] reg-fx docstring — name handler ctx + args signature
description:
Per audit rf2-1itgd (U3).

implementation/core/src/re_frame/fx.cljc:33 — `reg-fx`'s docstring names the metadata keys (`:doc`, `:spec`, `:platforms`) but never names the handler signature `(fn [ctx args] ...)`.

V1 muscle memory says the handler is `(fn [args] ...)`; v2 changed the signature to a ctx-first form so handlers can address replies back to the originating event (per Spec 014 §Reply addressing). The change is INVISIBLE from the docstring.

## Scope

Rewrite fx.cljc:33 docstring to:
1. Name the handler signature: `(fn [{:keys [frame event]} args] ...)` — `frame` is the active frame-id; `event` is the originating event vector.
2. Show an example registering a custom fx and consuming it via `:fx [[:my-fx args]]`.
3. Cross-ref `:platforms` metadata (#{:client :server} default), the closed effect-map.
4. Note: framework-shipped fx (`:dispatch`, `:dispatch-later`, `:rf.http/managed`, ...) are documented separately — link to API.md §Effect-map shape.

Pre-alpha: rewrite freely.
close_reason: merged via PR #526 (API usability batch)

title: [usability] Rewrite reg-sub docstring — layer-1/-2, :<- form, example, return
description:
Per audit rf2-1itgd (U2).

implementation/core/src/re_frame/subs.cljc:79 — `reg-sub` has a two-line docstring saying 'The only sub-registration form in v2'. The actually-useful documentation is on the private `parse-reg-sub-args` fn above it. Promote.

Should explain:
- Layer-1 form: `(reg-sub :id (fn [db query] ...))` — reads app-db.
- Layer-2 single-input: `(reg-sub :id :<- [:other] (fn [other-val query] ...))`
- Layer-2 multi-input: `(reg-sub :id :<- [:a] :<- [:b] (fn [[a b] query] ...))`
- Return: the id.
- Hot-reload semantics (re-register replaces, cache invalidates).
- Cross-ref `subscribe`, `subscribe-value`, `compute-sub`.

Pre-alpha: rewrite freely.
close_reason: merged via PR #526 (API usability batch)

title: [usability] Rewrite reg-event-db/-fx/-ctx docstrings — signature, return, example, siblings
description:
Per audit rf2-1itgd (ai/findings/implementation-usability-2026-05-13.md U1).

The underlying fns at implementation/core/src/re_frame/events.cljc:206/219/232 carry NO docstrings; the macro layer at implementation/core/src/re_frame/core.cljc:131/140/148 is a single sentence about source-coord stamping.

A user calling `(rf/doc rf/reg-event-fx)` learns nothing about:
- handler signature (`(fn [{:keys [db]} event-vec] {:db ... :fx [...]})`)
- middle slot (metadata-map OR positional interceptor-vector — NOT both)
- return value (the id keyword)
- the closed effect-map shape (`:db` + `:fx` only, per M-8)
- siblings (`reg-event-db`, `reg-event-ctx`)
- an example

## Scope

Rewrite three docstrings in events.cljc and the three matching macro docstrings in core.cljc. Each should:
1. Lead with one-sentence purpose.
2. Name the handler signature.
3. Name the return shape.
4. Mention what may live in the middle slot.
5. Cross-ref siblings.
6. One worked example.

Pre-alpha: no back-compat. Rewrite freely.
close_reason: merged via PR #526 (API usability batch)

title: [causa][copilot] Redaction defaults in 009-AI-CoPilot.md
description: Inspired by Sentry Session Replay's privacy-by-default redaction (all text masked unless sentry-unmask). Causa Lock 12 keeps conversation ephemeral but spec doesn't define WHICH FIELDS go to the LLM by default. Propose defaults in 009-AI-CoPilot.md: event vector args = opt-in (default redact to <redacted>); app-db slice values = opt-in; source coords = always-on; handler IDs = always-on; trace metadata = always-on. Settings panel exposes redaction toggles. Strengthens Lock 12's privacy bet without changing the persistence decision. Cross-ref: ai/findings/causa-sota-alternatives-2026-05-13.md §'What Causa should learn' item 7.
close_reason: PRs #527-#531 merged

title: :sub/run emit was outside *current-trigger-handler* binding
description:
Surfaced by rf2-npm2p test coverage work.

`re-frame.subs/validate-and-trace` was emitting `:sub/run` BEFORE
establishing the `*current-trigger-handler*` binding for the sub's
own coord. Result: `:sub/run` carried the enclosing event handler's
coord (when fired inside a cascade) or nil (top-level subscribe),
never the sub's own coord — contradicting Spec 009 §:rf.trace
/trigger-handler table row "Inside a sub recompute (body fn): the
sub's coord".

Fixed in the same PR (rf2-npm2p) by moving the `:sub/run` emit
inside the `binding [...]` form so the sub's trigger-handler is
established when `emit!` reads `*current-trigger-handler*`.

Verified by `sub-run-trigger-is-sub-not-enclosing-event` in
`success_path_trigger_handler_test.clj` — :sub/run inside a
dispatch cascade now carries `:sub` kind with the sub's id, not
`:event` with the enclosing event's id.

No follow-up work needed; closing on merge.
close_reason: merged in 2026-05-13 wave

title: [audit] Review XState advanced-feature usage — are we missing load-bearing cases or are they rare?
description:
Per Mike 2026-05-13: research how XState users actually use the advanced features in practice. The question: are we missing real use cases by NOT implementing all of XState's surface in `re-frame.machines`, or are the advanced features rarely used in practice?

## Context

re-frame2 has `re-frame.machines` (Spec 005). The design has historically been "ship a tight machine substrate, not a clone of XState." This bead pressure-tests that choice against actual XState user behaviour.

## What re-frame2 ships today (per Spec 005)

- Basic states + transitions
- Guards (`:rf.machine/guard`)
- Actions (entry/exit/transition)
- Parallel regions (per capability matrix)
- 3-arity escape hatch for variadic-fn use
- Source-coord stamping
- Machine inspector (Causa panel)

## XState's "advanced" features to assess

1. **Hierarchical states** (substates / nesting)
2. **History states** (deep + shallow — restore prior state on re-entry)
3. **Final states + done events** (completion signalling)
4. **After (delayed) transitions** (`after: { 5000: 'timeout' }`)
5. **Always transitions** (eventless / automatic)
6. **Internal vs external transitions** (re-entry semantics)
7. **Invoked machines / actors** (XState v5 actor model)
8. **Spawned actors** (dynamic actor creation)
9. **TypeScript-typed events + context** (XState v5 focus)
10. **Visualizer / statechart language** (XState's biggest USP)
11. **Statecharts (full SCXML)** — Harel's original spec

## Research approach

Use **WebFetch / WebSearch** to gather data from:

- **Stack Overflow** — search "xstate hierarchical states", "xstate history", "xstate after", etc. Look at question counts + answer engagement.
- **GitHub XState repo** — issues / discussions / examples / tests showing usage patterns.
- **XState v5 docs** — the maintainers' own framing of which features matter most.
- **Blog posts + tutorials** — what do XState advocates teach? (e.g. David Khourshid's posts, Erik Mogensen, Matt Pocock's content)
- **ReactConf / Reactive.tech / GitNation talks** — what's the canonical demo?
- **Twitter / Bluesky** — XState power users' tips.
- **Statecharts.dev** — the community resource.

Plus assess: which features are documented in the docs but NOT seen in any production tutorial?

## Output

Findings doc at `ai/findings/xstate-advanced-features-2026-05-13.md`:

### Per advanced feature
| Feature | Adoption (high / med / low) | Use cases seen | Re-frame2 status | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|

### Verdicts
- **SHIP** — common use case re-frame2 should add
- **DEFER** — niche; ship later if demand surfaces
- **WON'T SHIP** — anti-pattern or covered by another surface in re-frame2

### Use-case analysis

For each ship/defer/won't-ship decision, cite SPECIFIC examples seen in research (Stack Overflow Q link, GitHub issue, blog post). Don't speculate — cite.

### Re-frame2 Spec 005 gaps

If the audit finds we ARE missing a load-bearing feature, surface it as a candidate spec addition + an impl bead.

### Counter-evidence

If the audit finds XState's "advanced" features ARE rarely used in practice, that's vindication of the current design. Capture that signal too.

## Follow-up beads

`bd create` per actionable finding. Aim 0-5 beads (this audit may produce ZERO actionable items if the verdict is "current scope is right").

## Pre-alpha

If a feature should ship, we add it cleanly. If a feature is rarely used, we leave Spec 005 lean. No back-compat concern either way.

## Cross-refs

- `spec/005-StateMachines.md` — re-frame2's machine spec
- `implementation/machines/src/` — current impl
- `examples/reagent/state_machine_walkthrough/` — current usage demo
- Pattern-NineStates / Pattern-Boot / Pattern-WebSocket / Pattern-LongRunningWork — re-frame2 patterns that use machines
- `docs/guide/08-state-machines.md` — guide chapter

## Discipline

- Read-only audit. WebFetch / WebSearch heavy.
- Don't commit findings doc.
- File follow-up beads via `bd create`.
- No AI attribution.
- Use SPECIFIC citations from real-world usage — opinions don't count without data.
notes: Filed per Mike 2026-05-13. Pressure-tests Spec 005 scope against actual XState user behaviour. Research-shaped audit with WebFetch/WebSearch.
close_reason: merged in 2026-05-13 wave

title: [audit] Review SOTA alternatives to Causa — anything we can learn?
description:
Per Mike 2026-05-13: research SOTA alternatives to Causa and identify what we can learn. Causa is re-frame2's devtools (renamed from re-frame-10x). §10 decisions locked + Phase 1 foundation landed; this audit is "what is the 2026 state-of-the-art outside the re-frame ecosystem, and what should Causa learn from it before we get too far into Phase 2 panel implementations?"

## Reference targets (2026 SOTA)

### State-management devtools peer landscape

- **Redux DevTools** — the gorilla. Time-travel, action log, state diff, payload editor, multi-store, RTK Query integration.
- **Reactime** — React-specific (useState / useReducer / Context tree), time-travel with snapshot graph, session export.
- **Recoil DevTools** — atom/selector dependency graph visualization.
- **Zustand DevTools** — middleware-driven; minimal but composable.
- **Vue DevTools** (Vue 3) — fine-grained component + composable state tree.
- **Svelte DevTools** — reactive-statement granular tracing.
- **TanStack Query DevTools** — query-cache focused (relevant for Causa's `:rf.http/managed` panel).
- **Jotai DevTools** — atom-graph visualisation.

### Adjacent debugging substrates (different category, learn from)

- **Replay.io** — whole-app deterministic replay; record-and-replay debugging. Differs from epoch-based time-travel.
- **Sentry Session Replay** — production session replay tied to error monitoring.
- **Reactotron** (React Native) — broader debugging surface; HTTP, async storage, custom plugins.
- **Flipper / React Native Devtools** — meta-debugging suite.
- **Chrome DevTools Workspaces / Sources / Recorder** — IDE-shaped panel layouts.
- **Tauri DevTools** — desktop-app debugging substrate; relevant for re-frame2 desktop apps.

### CLJS-specific peers

- **re-frame-10x v1** (the parent — we already know it well, but worth re-reading post-§10 to confirm what we're carrying forward).
- **fulcro-inspect** — Fulcro's equivalent.
- **rebl / morse / portal** — generic data-inspection tools used in the broader Clojure ecosystem; Portal especially is widely loved.
- **flow-storm-debugger** — full execution tracing for Clojure(Script); very different model from epoch snapshots.

## Per-tool questions

For each:

1. **Killer feature** — what's the ONE feature that sets it apart? Does Causa have an equivalent (or should it)?
2. **UX choices** — panel layout, navigation, info density, keyboard shortcuts, theme.
3. **Substrate dependencies** — what does it require of the framework? (Redux DevTools needs Redux Enhancer; Reactime needs hook tree access; etc.)
4. **AI integration** — does it ship any AI-pair-debugging affordance? (Many don't yet. This is Causa's differentiator per §10 Lock 2 + 8.)
5. **Production story** — is this dev-only or does it ship to production builds in a stripped form?
6. **Export / share** — does it offer session export? (Causa Lock 4 = NEVER export; verify the reasoning holds against peer rationales for / against.)

## Output

Findings doc at `ai/findings/causa-sota-alternatives-2026-05-13.md`:

- Per-tool inventory (killer feature, UX notes, substrate deps, AI integration, production story, export shape)
- **What Causa should learn** — concrete affordances worth porting (within the §10 lock envelope)
- **Where Causa is unique** — differentiators worth marketing (six-domino trace, frame isolation, source-coord click-to-jump, AI co-pilot, EDN-roundtrip)
- **§10 lock pressure-test** — do any peer rationales challenge our locks? (especially Lock 3 app-db editing: never; Lock 4 session export: never; Lock 10 narrate mode: dropped)
- **Follow-up beads** — sketch one bead per learn-from finding

## Follow-up beads

For each actionable finding: `bd create` with concrete scope. Aim 5-15 beads.

## Pre-alpha

If a peer has a feature that pressure-tests a §10 lock, surface it. Mike can decide whether to revisit the lock. Pre-alpha = no commitments are sacred.

title: [audit] Review /implementation API for usability + understandability
description:
Per Mike 2026-05-13: audit the `implementation/` public API for **usability** and **understandability** — distinct from the elegance/simplicity audit (rf2-6abd1, which focused on shape, asymmetry, duplication).

## Distinction from prior audits

- **rf2-6abd1 (API elegance review, completed)** — shape, asymmetry, cuts/renames/merges. "Is this API surface clean?"
- **rf2-jzs9 / rf2-5uwl / etc.** — ergonomic shortcuts ("can we add X helper?").
- **THIS BEAD** — usability + understandability. "Can a new user figure out how to use this from the docstring + signature alone? Is the mental model coherent? Are error messages helpful?"

## Audit scope

For each public surface in `implementation/` (covering `core`, `schemas`, `machines`, `routing`, `epoch`, `http`, `ssr`, `flows`, `adapters/*`):

1. **Docstring quality** — present? complete? names the contract? gives an example? mentions failure modes?
2. **Signature clarity** — argument names readable? `[event-vec]` vs `[args]` vs `[m]`? required vs optional clear?
3. **Error message helpfulness** — when this surface fails, does the error tell the user what to do? `:rf.error/no-such-handler {:kind :event :id :foo}` ✅ vs `Error: nil` ❌.
4. **Discoverability** — if a user knows what they want to do, can they find the right surface? Naming consistency. Cross-references in docstrings.
5. **Common-workflow ease** — for the canonical patterns (dispatch event, register sub, set up frame, handle error), are the surfaces composed naturally or does the user have to remember a sequence?
6. **Mental model coherence** — does the API present ONE conceptual model, or are there competing models that need reconciling in the user's head?

## Per-surface output

For each finding, classify:
- **Cut** (rare here — usability is more about polish)
- **Improve docstring** (most common)
- **Rename for clarity**
- **Improve error message**
- **Add example in docstring**
- **Reorder argument positions** (rare; back-compat would matter normally but we're pre-alpha)

## Output

Findings doc at `ai/findings/implementation-usability-2026-05-13.md`:
- Per-surface table with verdict + recommended action
- Top 10 high-leverage improvements (where a tiny docstring or rename change unlocks broad usability)
- Common-workflow walkthroughs (e.g. "register a sub" — what does the user need to remember to do, in order?)
- Mental-model gaps (where competing models live in the same surface)

## Follow-up beads

For each actionable finding, `bd create` with concrete scope. Aim 8-20 beads. Most will be small (docstring polish, error message tightening, rename to canonical convention).

## Then ACTION the findings

After filing, dispatch direction-set beads under pre-alpha green-light. Many usability fixes are tiny doc edits; could batch as one cleanup PR.

## Cross-refs

- `ai/findings/api-elegance-review-2026-05-13.md` — shape audit
- `ai/findings/user-ergonomics-review-2026-05-12.md` — earlier ergonomics review
- `spec/API.md` — canonical surface inventory (post-rf2-zrnkm dedupe + rf2-ycesg orphans documented)
- `spec/Conventions.md` — naming bang rule (rf2-wmgp8) + configure-partition (rf2-mqxnc)

## Pre-alpha

Rewrite docstrings + error messages + names freely; no back-compat preservation. Each finding becomes a concrete edit.
notes: Filed per Mike 2026-05-13. Distinct from rf2-6abd1 (shape audit) — focuses on docstrings, error messages, mental-model coherence, discoverability.

title: [causa] Event-detail hero panel — §10 Lock 7 default view (Phase 2)
description:
Phase 2 of rf2-5aw5v (Causa v1.0 epic). Implement the hero panel — event-detail — per §10 Lock 7. Builds on rf2-n6x4q Phase 1 foundation just landed.

## Why this panel first

§10 Lock 7 says the hero panel is event-detail. It's the most-used view; every other panel benefits from being able to navigate INTO an event-detail. Foundation makes mount + trace bus available; this bead makes that visible.

## Scope

1. **Panel ns** at `tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/event_detail.cljs`:
   - Reads from `:rf.causa/trace-buffer` sub.
   - Subscribes to currently-selected `:dispatch-id` (a per-Causa-frame sub).
   - When a dispatch-id is selected, renders the cascade via `re-frame.trace.projection/group-cascades` (rf2-wvzgd, merged).
   - Display: the event vector at top; six-domino rendering below; per-row source-coord + click-to-jump (uses rf2-evgf5 hook once that lands; until then, plain text).

2. **Sidebar entry** for the event-detail panel in the empty shell scaffolded by Phase 1.

3. **Mount-into-canvas** wiring — selecting "Event detail" in the sidebar mounts this panel into the canvas area.

4. **Subs**:
   - `:rf.causa/selected-dispatch-id` — sub returning the currently-selected dispatch-id (or nil if none).
   - `:rf.causa/event-detail` — sub returning the projected cascade for the selected dispatch-id (uses `group-cascades`).

5. **Events**:
   - `:rf.causa/select-dispatch-id` — set the selected dispatch-id.
   - `:rf.causa/clear-selected-dispatch-id` — clear it.

6. **Default panel**: when Causa first opens (Ctrl+Shift+C), event-detail is the active panel. Per Lock 7.

## Tests

- `tools/causa/test/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/event_detail_test.cljs`:
  - Given a trace-buffer snapshot + selected dispatch-id, the panel renders the six-domino cascade.
  - Click a row → `:rf.causa/select-dispatch-id` fires (or jumps to source if rf2-evgf5 hook present).
  - Clear selection → panel renders empty state.

## Spec

- Update `tools/causa/spec/` to describe the event-detail panel's contract (probably extend an existing spec or add a panel-specific spec).

## /examples

The existing counter example with `:devtools/preloads [day8.re-frame2-causa.preload]` should naturally exercise this — clicking the counter button should produce a trace cascade visible in Causa's event-detail panel.

## Out of scope

- AI co-pilot integration on the panel (separate bead).
- Time-travel scrubber on the panel (separate bead; rf2-sxwvf in flight).
- "Pin this cascade" → epoch save (separate bead).
- Other panels (separate beads under rf2-5aw5v).

## Constraints

- NO AI attribution.
- Don't commit `ai/`, `decision-beads.md`, `findings/`.
- Pre-alpha.

## Cross-refs

- rf2-5aw5v — parent epic
- rf2-n6x4q — Phase 1 foundation (landed)
- rf2-wvzgd — group-cascades projection (consumed)
- rf2-g6ih4 — cascade-wide :dispatch-id (consumed)
- rf2-evgf5 — open-in-editor hook (in flight; integrate when lands)
- tools/causa/spec/007-UX-IA.md — shell layout
- §10 Lock 7 — hero panel: event-detail

title: [audit] Test coverage currency review — post 2026-05-13 session (~25 PRs)
description:
Per Mike 2026-05-13: audit test coverage in light of today's ~25-PR session, which made substantial changes across the impl, spec, and tooling surfaces. Beyond "tests pass after each PR", are the tests still asserting the RIGHT contracts, and is anything load-bearing now untested?

## Today's high-impact changes that could affect test currency

**API renames / cuts:**

- `re-frame.views-macros` cut (rf2-4lc9o #482) — 65 files swept; verify no test files still import the deleted ns.

- `trace-buffer` filter vocab extended 9 new keys (rf2-97ah0 #489) — coverage for each new filter axis added in that PR; verify other consumers (Story, pair2 tests if any) exercise the new vocab.
- success-path `:rf.trace/trigger-handler` (rf2-lf84g #505) — 10 new tests for the new surface; verify no existing tests assert the OLD behaviour (no coord on success path).
- `group-cascades` lifted to `re-frame.trace.projection` (rf2-wvzgd #491) — Story's local impl deleted; verify no tests still target the old Story-side fn.

**Tooling:**
