title: [BUG] :on-error per-frame slot elides with trace surface in CLJS production builds
description:
Surfaced by rf2-norar agent (2026-05-13) during the production-debugging guidance write-up at spec/009-Instrumentation.md §Production debugging.

## Gap

The :on-error per-frame slot is currently wired through the trace bus emit path. When goog.DEBUG=false (CLJS production builds), the trace bus is elided — and so is :on-error.

This contradicts the slot's framing as a **runtime error recovery surface**: production users expect a frame's :on-error handler to fire on production errors (the entire point of registering it). With current behaviour, the handler is silent in prod and visible only in dev.

## Impact

Any app that relies on :on-error for production telemetry / user-facing error fallback / Sentry-shaped integration will discover at deploy time that nothing fires. The dev-vs-prod divergence is the worst kind of bug — the integration works in development testing and silently fails when it matters.

## Fix shape (proposed; needs Mike-decision)

The :on-error slot needs a small always-on error-emit substrate that's NOT gated by goog.DEBUG. Specifically:

1. Separate the trace bus (debug-only, elidable) from the error-emit substrate (always-on, never elided).
2. :on-error subscribes to the error-emit substrate.
3. register-trace-cb! at :error severity continues to fire from the same substrate.
4. Other trace severities (:info, :event, :warning) remain on the debug-only bus.

## Cross-references

- spec/009-Instrumentation.md §Production debugging (just-shipped PR #496 documents the gap as a future direction)
- Spec 014 (Errors) — the :on-error slot's normative home
- rf2-norar — surfaced this gap

## Pre-alpha

Two-substrate split is a clean shape; no back-compat concern. Ship the always-on error substrate; document that trace bus is dev-only.

## Validation

CLJS production build (goog.DEBUG=false): register :on-error on a frame, throw inside a handler, assert the :on-error callback fires.
notes: PR: https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/716
close_reason: Closed

title: [causa] Foundation: preload + Ctrl+Shift+C + frame-provider isolation + empty shell + trace bus wiring
description:
Phase 1 foundation work for rf2-5aw5v (Causa v1.0 epic). Establishes the impl architecture so subsequent panel beads can mount cleanly.

## Scope

1. **Causa preload namespace** at `tools/10x/src/day8/re_frame_causa/preload.cljs` (or similar — investigate convention).
   - Registers a global Ctrl+Shift+C key listener.
   - First-press: mounts the Causa shell into the document body (NOT into the host frame's React tree — into its own root via React 18 `createRoot`).
   - Second-press: unmounts / toggles visibility.
   - Idempotent: re-loading the preload (shadow-cljs `:after-load`) doesn't re-attach listeners.

2. **Frame-provider state isolation** per rf2-tijr Option-C hybrid:
   - Internal `(frame-provider :rf/causa)` wrapping all Causa panels.
   - Re-anchors all descendant `with-frame` / subscribe / dispatch to `:rf/causa` so Causa's reads don't touch the host's `:rf/default` frame.
   - All Causa-registered events / subs / fxs / cofxs use `:rf.causa/*` namespace prefix to avoid collision with host registrations (registrar is process-global).

3. **Shell layout** per `tools/10x/spec/007-UX-IA.md`:
   - Empty pane scaffolding: header (with collapse/expand control), main panel area (where the hero event-detail panel will land), side rail (panel switcher), footer (status).
   - Panels themselves are stubs (each renders "Coming soon — rf2-xxx"). The structural shell is what this bead delivers.

4. **Trace bus subscription wiring**:
   - At preload time, `(rf/register-trace-cb! :rf.causa/trace-collector trace-handler-fn)`.
   - `trace-handler-fn` pushes the event into Causa's own ring buffer (subscribed via `:rf.causa/trace-buffer` sub).
   - Hero panel slot reads from this buffer.
   - Filter axes use rf2-97ah0's extended vocabulary (severity, dispatch-id, etc.).

5. **Substrate adapter resolution** per rf2-tijr:
   - `(re-frame.substrate.adapter/current-adapter)` for adapter detection.
   - Pure hiccup for view code (no per-substrate switches).
   - Document the ~5 imperative escape-hatch sites in a code comment for future panel implementors.

## NOT in scope (defer to subsequent beads)

- Actual panel implementations (event-detail hero, causality graph, time-travel, app-db diff, schema timeline, etc.) — each gets its own bead under rf2-5aw5v.
- AI co-pilot (separate bead per Spec 009).
- MCP server (separate bead per Spec 010).
- Clojars publish + lockstep (separate ops bead).
- Production elision verification (separate bead).

## Verify

- Generated app with `(rf/init! ...)` + `:devtools/preloads [day8.re-frame-causa.preload]` boots successfully.
- Ctrl+Shift+C toggles the shell visibility.
- Host app's `:rf/default` frame is unaffected when Causa mounts (sniff for cross-frame writes).
- Trace events from the host fire `:rf.causa/trace-collector` callback.
- Shell renders empty-pane stubs without errors.

## Tests

- JVM: pure-fn portions of the trace routing logic (`group-cascades` is already at `re-frame.trace.projection` per rf2-wvzgd; consume it).
- CLJS node-test: preload registration idempotency (load twice; check single listener attached).
- CLJS browser-test: minimal smoke that mount + Ctrl+Shift+C work in a real DOM. Optional if too costly for foundation bead.

## Cross-refs

- rf2-5aw5v — parent epic
- rf2-tijr — closed; Option-C architecture
- tools/10x/spec/007-UX-IA.md — shell layout reference
- tools/10x/spec/008-Embedding-Contract.md — embedding rules (note: 008 has a spec-gap, separate bead filed; safe to proceed using bead-notes for now)
- tools/10x/spec/011-Launch-Modes.md — in-app overlay
- rf2-97ah0 (PR #489) — extended trace filter vocab; consume it
- rf2-wvzgd (PR #491) — group-cascades projection at re-frame.trace.projection; consume it
- rf2-g6ih4 (merged) — :dispatch-id cascade-wide; consume it

## Pre-alpha

Rewrite freely. This is the impl foundation — establish the shape; subsequent panels follow it.

## Repo placement

Stays at `tools/10x/src/` per rf2-tijr resolution. Will be published as `day8/re-frame2-causa` later (separate publish-ops bead).
notes: Filed 2026-05-13 as Phase 1 of rf2-5aw5v (Causa v1.0 impl epic). Establishes impl architecture so subsequent panel beads land cleanly. Dispatch immediately per Mike's 2026-05-13 keenness.

title: [template] Best-practice demonstrations — error projector + app-db schema + HTTP failure matrix + naming conventions

### 2. App-db schema
- `(rf/reg-app-schema :rf/default schema)` with Malli `:map` typing `:counter/value` as `:int`.
- Demonstrates typed-at-boundaries posture; immediate value (wrong write caught at interceptor boundary).
- Cross-link to Spec 010 + Guide ch.05a (Schemas).
- Note: post rf2-jzs9 SHIP, if `reg-app-schemas` plural is preferred as canonical idiom, use that form.

### 3. HTTP example (commented-out)
- `reg-event-fx` with a `:http` effect including all four `:on-success` / `:on-failure` / `:on-error` / `:on-timeout` cases.
- Comments explain what each case means and when each fires.
- Demonstrates managed-HTTP failure-category matrix from Spec 014 / Guide ch.10.
- Users delete or adapt.

### 4. Naming conventions section in README
- `:domain/action` keyword convention (`:counter/increment`, not `:increment`).
- Namespaced subs and effects same shape.
- Cross-link Spec/Conventions §Naming.

## Tier 2 — README-only content (no code surface)

5. Three event-handler return shapes — `reg-event-db` / `-fx` / `-ctx` mentioned + Guide ch.04 link.
6. Subscription layering — mention when app grows + Guide ch.05 link.
7. Custom effect handler authoring — `reg-fx` mention + Guide ch.04 link.
8. No subscribing-inside-handlers — cofx-wrapping pattern reference (cross-link to rf2-wurbi when its doc work lands).
9. Frame isolation in tests — `with-frame` macro idiom (already demonstrated via rf2-8oi86 test file).

## Out of scope

10. Routing example — would need fourth flag; Q7 capped at three.
11. Multi-frame example — Doc §6.8 locked NO ACTION.

## Verify

- Generated counter app: error projector hookup tested end-to-end.
- Generated app-db schema validates on write.
- HTTP example commented-out cleanly compiles.
- README sections render correctly under mkdocs.

## Cross-refs

- rf2-dolpf — deps-new rebuild epic; this bead extends the v1 emit spec.
- Doc §6 locks cover this bead README sections.
- rf2-5uwl + rf2-jzs9 — example references where appropriate.
- Spec 014 / Spec 010 / Guide ch.05 / ch.05a / ch.10 / ch.14.

## Pre-alpha

Update /examples to reflect any new idioms shown in the template scaffold (per the examples-on-API-change rule).
notes: Filed 2026-05-13 from Mike template best-practice walkthrough. Sits under rf2-dolpf deps-new rebuild epic.

title: docs: fix 2 stale cross-refs missed by #483/#485 post-merge sweep
description:
Two stale cross-references survived the 10-PR merge wave (#483 rf2-22t1, #485 rf2-7dvg). Both fall outside what scripts/check_doc_slugs.py validates (it flags anchor mismatches, not missing target files or stale prose / paths).

2. tools/pair2-mcp/spec/API.md:70 — env-var row for PAIR2_RUNTIME_PATH defaults to skills/re-frame-pair2/scripts/runtime.cljs. After #485 (rf2-7dvg) the runtime ships at skills/re-frame-pair2/preload/re_frame_pair2/runtime.cljs and the inject-runtime tool that consumed this env-var was cut. title: [EPIC] Implement re-frame2-causa v1.0 — devtools per spec corpus
description:
Per Mike walkthrough 2026-05-12: file the Causa v1.0 implementation epic. Architecture is locked Option-C (rf2-tijr): in-app overlay launch (Ctrl+Shift+C), multi-frame isolation, no vendoring. Spec corpus lives at `tools/10x/spec/` (rf2-1lls landed, 18 files / ~5500 LoC, all 13 §10 design decisions locked).

## Scope

Build re-frame2-causa as the devtools surface for re-frame2 apps. Replaces re-frame-10x; consumes the trace bus (Spec 009). Ships as `day8/re-frame2-causa` Clojars artefact (verify coord name; aligns with template Q3 deps-new rebuild rf2-dolpf which needs this published before its preload variant T2 = rf2-y9zqc unblocks).

## Architecture (locked)

- **Launch:** in-app overlay (Option-C per rf2-tijr) — preload + Ctrl+Shift+C hotkey
- **Multi-frame isolation:** Causa runs in its own frame, doesn't bleed into user app state
- **Trace consumption:** via the framework trace bus (spec/009-Instrumentation.md). Recent widening: `:dispatch-id` cascade-wide (rf2-g6ih4 just landed PR #484). Pending: filter vocabulary (rf2-97ah0), success-path source-coord (rf2-lf84g), lift group-cascades (rf2-wvzgd).
- **Persistence:** ephemeral per §10 Lock 12
- **AI co-pilot:** v1.0 per §10 Lock 2; default collapsed (Lock 8)
- **Mobile:** desktop only (Lock 5)
- **App-db editing:** never (Lock 3)
- **MCP server:** v1.0 timing (Lock 6) — Spec 010

## 16 panels (per spec corpus)

Hero panel: event-detail (§10 Lock 7).

Numbered panels from spec corpus:
- 001 Causality Graph
- 002 Time Travel
- 003 Machine Inspector
- 004 App-DB Diff
- 005 Schema Timeline
- 006 Hydration Debugger
- 007 UX & Information Architecture
- 008 Embedding Contract (cross-cutting)
- 009 AI Co-Pilot
- 010 MCP Server (cross-cutting; talks to pair2-MCP server)
- 011 Launch Modes (cross-cutting)

## Decomposition sketch

Will need child beads for at minimum:

1. **Foundation** — repo placement (per rf2-tijr: tools/10x/ stays, becomes re-frame2-causa publishing artefact; ship to Clojars as `day8/re-frame2-causa`); bootstrap; preload entry; Ctrl+Shift+C hotkey; multi-frame allocation; epoch buffer integration; shell layout matching Spec 007 (UX-IA)
2. **Trace consumer foundation** — subscribe to the trace bus; route to per-panel readers; per-frame scoping
3. **Event-detail hero panel** — §10 Lock 7 hero; reads `:dispatch-id` (now cascade-wide), surfaces six-domino cascade with source-coord click-through
4-13. **The 8 numbered panels** (001 through 009 except 008/010/011 cross-cutting) — one bead each
14. **AI co-pilot** — Spec 009 panel; collapsed by default
15. **MCP server** — Spec 010; integrates with pair2-MCP
16. **Embedding contract** — Spec 008; verify the embedding API
17. **Production elision** — Spec 009 production-elide story; ensure Causa code is fully eliminated when `goog.DEBUG=false`
18. **Clojars publish + lockstep** — `day8/re-frame2-causa` deploy chain; pin lockstep with framework version; document for template rf2-y9zqc consumption

## Downstream blockers this unblocks

- **rf2-y9zqc** (template T2: Causa preload) — depends on the Clojars artefact existing
- **rf2-r770** (Story cross-link panel to Causa sub-topology) — depends on Causa shipping
- **rf2-mymg** (guide chapter dedicated to Causa) — depends on Causa v1.0 release
- **rf2-tijr** can be closed once foundation lands

## Spec consumption dependencies (mostly landed)

- ✅ Spec 009 trace bus (`:dispatch-id` cascade-wide — rf2-g6ih4)
- ✅ Spec 007 stories (story tool integration possible via rf2-r770)
- 🟡 Trace surface follow-ups in flight: rf2-97ah0 (filter vocab), rf2-lf84g (source-coord widening), rf2-wvzgd (lift group-cascades)
- 🟡 Production elision verification per Spec 009

## Cross-refs

- `tools/10x/spec/` — full spec corpus
- `tools/10x/spec/DESIGN-RATIONALE.md` — 13 locked decisions
- `tools/10x/spec/README.md` — entry point
- `rf2-tijr` — architecture decision; close once foundation lands
- `rf2-1lls` (closed) — spec corpus landing
- `ai/findings/trace-surface-consumer-audit-2026-05-13.md` — consumer needs survey

## Pre-alpha

Cut/include freely. No back-compat to re-frame-10x. Causa is a fresh artefact.

## Owner: Mike Thompson
notes: Filed per Mike's 'yes' 2026-05-12. Decomposition into child beads happens at epic kickoff.

title: [epic] Rebuild tools/template/ on deps-new — drop clj-new + Clojars, move to git-coord distribution
description:
Per the template walkthrough Q2 lock (2026-05-12): switch the template from clj-new + Clojars publishing to **deps-new + git-coord distribution** (`io.github.day8/re-frame2-template`).

## Scope

This is a substantial rebuild of `tools/template/`. The existing implementation:
- clj-new + Mustache resources under `src/clj/new/re_frame2/`
- Clein/Clojars publish pipeline (`day8/clj-template.re-frame2`)
- Invocation: `clojure -X:project/new :template re-frame2`

The new shape:
- deps-new template with programmatic template body
- Git-tag-based release (no Clojars publish)
- Initial home may stay at `tools/template/` during transition; final home is a dedicated repo `day8/re-frame2-template`
- Invocation: `clojure -Tnew create :template io.github.day8/re-frame2-template :name acme/myapp`

## v1 emit spec (locked per walkthrough)

- **Substrate**: Reagent / UIx / Helix (carry over from existing template)
- **Sample**: counter (Q4 lock)
- **Test harness**: shadow-cljs `:node-test` + `:browser-test` (Q5)
- **CSS default**: plain CSS (Q6)
- **CSS opt-in flag**: `:css :tailwind` — Tailwind v4 (Q6) ⚠ verify Tailwind major per rf2-gthro
- **Build targets**: browser + `:test` (Q7)
- **SSR opt-in flag**: `:include-ssr? true` (Q7) — gated on rf2-0m5ea validation
- **Story opt-in flag**: `:include-story? true` (Q3/Q4 era)
- **Causa preload**: always-on, unconditional (Q9-era; Ctrl+Shift+C per rf2-tijr Option-C)
- **Skill install stub**: `install-skills.sh` placeholder + README mention (Q9 lean A)
- **Layout**: `src/` + `test/` + `dev/scratch.cljs` + `dev/user.clj` (Q8, unconditional)

## Spec updates

`tools/template/spec/000-Vision.md §Non-goals` and `001-Substrate-Variants.md` need rewording:
- Remove "no branching toggles" language; replace with "exactly three flags permitted: `:include-story?`, `:css`, `:include-ssr?`. Resist further proliferation."
- Remove SSR deferral language
- Document the deps-new switch in `DESIGN-RATIONALE.md`

## Dependencies

- `rf2-0m5ea` — SSR validation bead (gates `:include-ssr?` flag work)
- `rf2-gthro` — verify Tailwind major version before locking `:css :tailwind` impl
- `rf2-y9zqc` — Causa preload (blocked on re-frame2-causa Clojars artefact)

## Existing template follow-ups (subject to rebuild scope)

T1-T6 follow-up beads (rf2-8oi86, rf2-y9zqc, rf2-t009p, rf2-r2jqo, rf2-htvqk, rf2-8437x) were filed against the **clj-new** template. Their content carries over conceptually but the implementation surface changes. Re-evaluate each after the deps-new rebuild scaffold is in place — some may merge into this epic; some may stand as separate post-rebuild beads.

## Repo move

Eventual home: dedicated `day8/re-frame2-template` repo (rather than `tools/template/` inside the re-frame2 monorepo). Initial transition: keep at `tools/template/` while content ports over; split to its own repo when ready. Org choice deferred (`day8/` initially; rename later if/when `re-frame/` org per rf2-vf8m is established).

## Decomposition (sketch — not yet broken into child beads)

1. **deps-new scaffold port** — programmatic template body replacing Mustache + clj-new entry
2. **Mustache → deps-new file emission** — port `shared/` + per-substrate file sets
3. **Drop clein/Clojars publish** — remove `:clein/build`, update DESIGN-RATIONALE
4. **Spec rewrite** — 000-Vision §Non-goals, 001-Substrate-Variants, DESIGN-RATIONALE
5. **Validate against existing end-to-end test** — `core_test.clj` adapts to deps-new invocation
6. **Repo split** — extract to `day8/re-frame2-template` git repo (when ready)
7. **Resume v1 flag work** — `:include-story?`, `:css`, `:include-ssr?` opt-in flags

## Pre-alpha

Cut freely. No back-compat to the clj-new invocation; the old `clojure -X:project/new :template re-frame2` simply stops working. Users move to the new invocation.
notes: Epic from template walkthrough Q2 lock. Child beads to be decomposed once epic owner takes it up. T1-T6 may need re-evaluation post-rebuild.

title: [template] Validate Spec 011 SSR reference impl as a scaffolded template variant end-to-end
description:
**Context**: Template v1 ships an `:include-ssr? true` opt-in flag (per Q7 walkthrough decision 2026-05-12, lifting the 000-Vision §Non-goals SSR deferral). The `implementation/ssr/` impl and Spec 011 contract are in place; this bead validates that they survive being scaffolded into a fresh user app and running end-to-end.

## Scope

End-to-end validation of the SSR variant of the generated counter app. The variant must:

1. **Boot the server** — `clojure -M:server` (or whatever the canonical invocation is) starts a ring handler that returns hydrated HTML.
2. **Hydrate on client** — generated client entry attaches to the server-rendered DOM without re-mounting.
3. **Counter works post-hydration** — clicking buttons advances state with no re-render glitch.
4. **Dev reload coordinates** — shadow-cljs hot-reload on the client side, plus ring-server `reset!` on the JVM side, both work in the dev loop without conflicting.
5. **Production build** — `clojure -X:release` produces a deployable artefact set (compiled JS + JVM ring uberjar or similar).

## Investigation

- Read `implementation/ssr/src/re_frame/` for the SSR API surface (render-to-string, hydration entry, request-handler shape per Spec 011 §HTTP response contract).
- Read `spec/011-SSR.md` for the normative contract.
- Read `examples/` for any SSR example app already shipped — if one exists, model the template after it.

## Output

1. **Findings doc** (local-only) noting:
   - Any spec gaps surfaced (file separate Spec 011 beads against impl/spec).
   - The validated boot + hydrate + reload procedure (becomes README content in the eventual SSR template variant).
   - Whether the existing `implementation/ssr/` impl is ready or needs work.
2. **One or more follow-up beads** for:
   - Adding `:include-ssr?` flag to the (deps-new) template — this is the work bead.
   - Any Spec 011 impl gaps surfaced.
   - Any documentation gaps (Guide ch.11 server-side, Spec 011 itself).

## Gates this unblocks

- `:include-ssr?` flag becomes part of the template v1 emission (per Q7 + Q3 walkthrough decisions).
- Removes the "validate first" caveat from Mike's Q7 lock.

## Pre-alpha

Cut/include freely. If `implementation/ssr/` has shortcomings, file beads to fix them rather than working around in the template.

## Cross-refs

- `tools/template/spec/000-Vision.md §Non-goals` — SSR deferral language being lifted
- `tools/template/spec/001-Substrate-Variants.md` L114-115 — the same deferral
- `spec/011-SSR.md` — the contract this validates against
- `implementation/ssr/` — the impl being validated
- `ai/findings/re-frame2-template-design-2026-05-13.md` Q7 — walkthrough decision
notes: Direction-set. Required before :include-ssr? flag work bead can dispatch.

title: [flake] counter-with-stories Playwright: 'expected text 6 but got 5'
description:
**Symptom**: The `Examples (browser, headless Chromium, rf2-lyj0)` CI job intermittently fails on `counter-with-stories` with `expected text "6" but got "5" within 10000ms`. Confirmed reproducing on `origin/main` (multiple PRs this session reported it: rf2-rlpo, rf2-zrnkm, rf2-8oi86, rf2-5otf0, rf2-4lc9o, rf2-9n1qd, rf2-p0mv, rf2-22t1).

**Suspicion**: One agent (rf2-4lc9o report) flagged the root cause: "`createRoot()` warning from the recent rf2-qmjo test-mode change." That change added the `:test` mode-tab view which calls `createRoot()` outside the variant's controlled mount; if the test page asserts the counter value before the test-mode rehydration completes, it reads a stale value.

**Action**: Investigate `tools/story/src/re_frame/story/ui/test_mode.cljc` for the `createRoot()` call site. Either (a) defer the createRoot until after the variant mounts, (b) gate it on a `:test` mode being active, or (c) widen the Playwright wait to a stable selector instead of text content.

**Pre-existing**: not caused by any specific PR — they all inherit it. Worth fixing soon to stop the noise.
notes: P2 because it'\''s blocking confident merges. Will retry-merge through it until fixed; this bead is the durable fix.
close_reason: merged in 2026-05-13 batch landing wave

title: [story] Implement chrome-level toolbar surface consuming reg-mode tuples
description:
Follow-up impl bead for the rf2-p0mv spec ([tools/story/spec/010-Toolbar.md](../tools/story/spec/010-Toolbar.md)).

## Scope

Implement the chrome-level toolbar specified in 010-Toolbar.md:

1. New ns re-frame.story.ui.toolbar — (toolbar-strip) Reagent component + pure helpers (toggle-mode, active-args, hydrate-modes-from-storage!, save-modes-to-storage!).
2. Add the strip to re-frame.story.ui.shell/shell above the three-pane flex-row.
3. Extend :rf/mode schema with the optional :axis slot (additive — existing bodies remain valid).
4. Generalise/replace cofx: :story/mode → :story/active-modes + :story/active-args.
5. Register :story/active-modes and :story/active-args subscriptions backed by reactions off shell-state-atom.
6. localStorage hydrate at shell mount + URL deep-link hydrate (?modes=... already encoded by share.cljc).
7. Delete controls/mode-picker — modes are chrome-wide now, not a per-variant controls section.
8. Playwright section 5: toolbar block in counter_with_stories spec.
9. Add one :axis :theme-tagged reg-mode to counter_with_stories.stories to exercise single-select.

## Verify

- Existing Story tests pass.
- Playwright section 5 exercises chip render, click, persistence, URL deep-link.
- canvas, workspace, docs, test-mode, multi-substrate, controls.args-editor, share all read the same :active-modes slot — no regressions.

## Constraints

- No new framework registries (per Principles.md).
- EDN-first preserved (:axis is data, no fn-slot).
- DCE under :advanced.

## Blocked

Blocks rf2-wk41 (Backgrounds + Viewport addon UX) — the canonical reg-mode registrations land there once this toolbar surface ships.
notes: Audit re-run 2026-05-13 (ai/findings/story-sota-audit-2026-05-13.md F1): bumped from P3 to P2. The toolbar spec is merged (rf2-p0mv → 010-Toolbar.md) but the impl is the gating gap for Storybook-8 chrome parity — Story's mode-picker currently lives only inside the right-pane controls panel, not at the chrome level. Until this lands, Story diverges from Storybook on the most visible chrome element.
close_reason: PRs #527-#531 merged

title: Lift Story group-cascades / domino-bucket into framework-side re-frame.trace.projection (shared by Story, Causa, pair2)
description:
Per ai/findings/trace-surface-consumer-audit-2026-05-13.md Finding 8 + Recommendation 'Consumer refactors'.

Story already extracted the six-domino projection as a JVM-runnable pure fn at tools/story/src/re_frame/story/ui/trace.cljs:144-178 (group-cascades + domino-bucket). Causa's planned event-detail panel and causality-graph node renderer need the same projection. pair2's cascade-of reimplements a slimmer version.

Three implementations of the same shape is the smell. Causa is spec-only — lift before Causa ships impl.

Fix: move group-cascades and domino-bucket into re-frame.trace.projection (or a separate re-frame2-trace-tools artefact if we want to keep core slim). Signature: `[trace-events] -> [{:dispatch-id … :event … :handler … :fx … :effects [] :subs [] :renders []} …]`. Pure data; JVM-runnable for unit tests.

Touch:
- New: implementation/core/src/re_frame/trace/projection.cljc (or new artefact)
- Migrate: tools/story/src/re_frame/story/ui/trace.cljs to consume it
- Spec: cross-link from spec/009-Instrumentation.md §Subscription / consumption
- Future: Causa consumes it when 001-Causality-Graph + 002-Time-Travel land impl; pair2 cascade-of migrates.

Depends-on: rf2-g6ih4 (widen :dispatch-id) for the projection to be robust against non-dispatched events.

Cross-ref: ai/findings/trace-surface-consumer-audit-2026-05-13.md, parent rf2-usa3

title: Source-coord on success-path :rf.fx/handled and :rf.machine/transition (mirror :rf.trace/trigger-handler)
description:
Per ai/findings/trace-surface-consumer-audit-2026-05-13.md Finding 4.

Post-rf2-3nn8 the error path (:rf.trace/trigger-handler slot) carries handler source-coord. But the success path doesn't:

- :rf.fx/handled (every successfully-dispatched fx) — Story/Causa want the fx-handler's registration coord for jump-to-source.
- :rf.machine/transition — Causa's machine-inspector wants 'jump to the action that just ran' from a transition trace. Machine actions/guards have registration coords on their registrar slots; no current emit hoists them.

Fix: either widen :rf.trace/trigger-handler to ride on success-path traces too (the Var is already bound by the runtime boundary), or add a parallel :tags :source-coord field on the success-path emits.

Recommendation: ride the existing :rf.trace/trigger-handler slot on success-path fx — same shape, same elision behaviour, no new fields to wire through tools.

Cross-ref: ai/findings/trace-surface-consumer-audit-2026-05-13.md, parent rf2-usa3. Also adjacent to rf2-ts1a (call-site coord macro) — but distinct: rf2-ts1a is the call-site, this is the registration-site.
close_reason: PRs merged in batch landing wave 2026-05-13

title: Spec 009 + impl — extend trace-buffer filter vocabulary (severity, event-id, source, origin, dispatch-id, since-ms, pred)
description:
Per ai/findings/trace-surface-consumer-audit-2026-05-13.md Finding 3.

trace-buffer currently filters by :operation / :op-type / :since / :frame. Consumers all post-filter for:

| Axis | Where wanted |
|---|---|
| :severity (:error/:warning/:info) | Causa issues feed, pair2 error scan |
| :event-id | Story scrub-by-event, Causa filter-graph-to-event |
| :handler-id | Causa event-detail 'all handlers that ran' |
| :source (:ui / :timer / :http / :repl / :machine / :ssr-hydration) | Causa cascade-by-trigger axis |
| :origin (:app/:pair/:story/:test) | Story/pair2 'show only my dispatches' |
| :dispatch-id | Causa 'show this cascade's traces' |
| :since-ms / :between [t0 t1] | Causa drag-time-range, pair2 trace-window |
| :pred (fn [ev] …) | pair2 epoch-matches?-equivalent for raw traces |

Fix: implementation/core/src/re_frame/trace.cljc trace-buffer already runs filterv over a composed pred; adding axes is one clause each. Spec 009 needs a new §Filter vocabulary section locking the recognised keys (today's set is in the impl docstring but not the spec).

Pair-flow: this also lets pair2's epoch-matches? predicate-map become the canonical predicate vocabulary for raw traces too — extend trace-buffer to accept the same map shape.

Cross-ref: ai/findings/trace-surface-consumer-audit-2026-05-13.md, parent rf2-usa3

title: Spec 009 + impl — widen :dispatch-id to every cascade event, not just :event/dispatched
description:
Per ai/findings/trace-surface-consumer-audit-2026-05-13.md Finding 2.

Problem: Spec 009 §Dispatch correlation defines :dispatch-id / :parent-dispatch-id only on :event/dispatched. But Story group-cascades, Causa causality-graph, Causa schema-timeline pivot, and pair2 cascade-of all need :dispatch-id on every event in the cascade. Causa 001-Causality-Graph spec lines 26-27 already document this as intent.

Fix: introduce *current-dispatch-id* dynamic Var (mirror of *current-trigger-handler*), bound by router.cljc around the drain. emit! reads it and merges into :tags. Spec 009 §Dispatch correlation widens wording: every event inside a cascade carries :dispatch-id under :tags; :parent-dispatch-id stays :event/dispatched-only.

Forward-compat: additive.

Cross-ref: ai/findings/trace-surface-consumer-audit-2026-05-13.md, parent rf2-usa3
close_reason: merged in 2026-05-13 batch landing wave

title: API.md orphan exports: row 11 surfaces present in core.cljc but absent from spec/API.md
description:
Eleven surfaces are exported from re-frame.core but have no row in spec/API.md (the canonical reference). Either document each in the appropriate section or cut from core.

## Orphans

| Surface | Source line | Suggested section |
|---|---|---|
| bound-subscriber | core.cljc:620 | §View ergonomics (pair with bound-dispatcher; or cut — see other bead about dispatcher/bound-dispatcher alias collapse) |
| machine-by-system-id | core.cljc:650 | §Machines |
| dispatch-to-system | core.cljc:651 | §Machines |
| has-tag? | core.cljc:653 | §Machines (per Spec 005 §State tags / rf2-ee0d) |
| render-tree-hash | core.cljc:291 | §SSR |
| project-error | core.cljc:292 | §SSR |
| install-managed-request-stubs! | core.cljc:763 | §HTTP requests |
| uninstall-managed-request-stubs! | core.cljc:764 | §HTTP requests |
| with-managed-request-stubs* | core.cljc:765 | §HTTP requests (fn-form of the with-managed-request-stubs macro per Conventions * shape) |
| set-schema-validator! | core.cljc:484 | §Schemas (and consider configure-surface fragmentation note) |
| set-schema-explainer! | core.cljc:485 | §Schemas |

## Discipline

- Read-only fact-check first: re-run grep against core.cljc + each core_X.cljc to confirm each surface is genuinely exported.

title: API review: surface has grown organically — simplify + elegance pass
description:
The re-frame2 public API surface has grown organically across many landings. Pre-alpha is the right moment to step back and do an **elegance + simplification** pass before any back-compat constraint kicks in.

## Scope

Audit the **`re-frame.core`** public surface (per `spec/API.md`) plus the wider published-namespace surface (`re-frame.core-flows`, `re-frame.core-machines`, etc per rf2-2vbm dash-form). Ask:

1. **What's the smallest API that delivers re-frame2's pitch?** If we redesigned from the locked decisions today, what would we ship vs what's grown ad-hoc?
2. **Are there redundant surfaces?** Two ways to do the same thing where one would suffice.
3. **Are there asymmetries** that should be symmetric? Examples:
   - `register-trace-cb!` (bang) vs `register-epoch-cb` (no bang) — per rf2-1pqc decision already open
   - `dispatch` (fn) vs `dispatch-sync!` (bang) — when does a bang ride?
   - `reg-event-db` / `-fx` / `-ctx` triple — natural or could collapse?
4. **Are there confusing names?** Where would a v1 user (or fresh re-frame2 user) trip on naming?
5. **Are there missing convenience helpers** that would crystallise a recurring pattern? Or conversely — are there helpers that should be cut because they encourage a bad pattern?
6. **Configuration surfaces**: `rf/configure :sub-cache`, etc — is `configure` a sensible umbrella or a place where unrelated knobs are pooled?
7. **Macro vs fn shape**: which surfaces are macros (compile-time stamping for source-coord) vs fns? Is the distinction visible / explicable to users?
8. **Per-frame vs per-process surfaces**: `with-frame`, `init!`, `reg-frame`, etc — are the per-frame surfaces clearly distinguished from process-level ones in the API surface?
9. **Re-exports**: `re-frame.core` re-exports from many sub-namespaces (`core-flows`, etc). Is the re-export set complete + sensible? Any orphans?

## Output

Findings doc at `ai/findings/api-elegance-review-<date>.md`:

1. **Per-surface table** — every documented public surface, with verdict:
   - **Keep as-is** (canonical, well-shaped)
   - **Rename** (with proposed name + reasoning)
   - **Cut** (redundant with another surface)
   - **Merge** (collapse with another surface)
   - **Split** (one surface doing two things)
   - **Hold open** (Mike-decision-territory)

2. **Symmetry audit** — pairs/triples that should match shape; flag asymmetries.

3. **Naming audit** — names that read confusingly + proposed rewrites.

4. **Convenience-helper proposals** — recurring patterns that deserve a one-liner helper.

5. **Cut list** — surfaces with no consumers or worse than their alternative.

6. **Follow-up beads** — sketches per finding; one bead per coherent change (some will be BREAKING; pre-alpha allows it).

## Inputs to read first

- `spec/API.md` — canonical API surface table
- `implementation/core/src/re_frame/core.cljc` (and `core-*` sub-namespaces) — what's actually exported
- `spec/Conventions.md` — naming conventions
- `skills/re-frame2/SKILL.md` + reference leaves — surface from the user's POV
- Recent landings: rf2-4far (reg-* return-value contract), rf2-hzos (reg-view return), rf2-3nn8 (trigger-handler), rf2-gn80 (final-states), rf2-ngdp (fx-overrides fn-branch) — surface that grew today

## Discipline

- **Read-only audit**. Don't edit API surface.
- Findings doc local-only.
- File follow-up beads via `bd create`.
- **Pre-alpha**: cut + rename + simplify freely. No back-compat constraint.
- NO AI attribution in bead bodies.

## Cross-references

- `feedback_pre_alpha_no_backcompat.md` (memory) — cut not deprecate
- `feedback_downstream_eps_consume_foundation.md` — don't grow framework registries / dispatch types
- The user-ergonomics-review (older, but covers some of the same ground): `ai/findings/user-ergonomics-review-2026-05-12.md` — 5 open questions for Mike still pending; this audit should subsume or cross-reference them.
notes: Audit complete. Findings at ai/findings/api-elegance-review-2026-05-13.md (local-only). 12 follow-up beads filed: rf2-zrnkm, rf2-suv7s, rf2-fva00, rf2-ycesg, rf2-8p4v5, rf2-knz3l, rf2-3ubmv, rf2-wmgp8, rf2-6vgwv, rf2-hyh5w, rf2-a6ske, rf2-18185. Headline: API surface is coherent; leverage sits in API.md hygiene (duplicates/ghosts/orphans), not impl renames.
close_reason: audit complete; 12 follow-ups filed

title: Decide: :re-frame/* keyword alias — implement coercion or strip the spec promise
description:
Spec/Conventions.md L43 and spec/MIGRATION.md L776 both promise that the runtime accepts `:re-frame/<x>` as an alias for `:rf/<x>` during v1→v2 migration. Grep across `implementation/**/src/` finds **no coercion code** — there is no alias-acceptance in `registrar`, `events`, `subs`, or `fx`. The promise dangles.

## Two outcomes

**Outcome A — Implement as warn-once shim.**

1. Add alias-coercion at registrar-lookup sites (a small set: `events`, `subs`, `fx`, `cofx`, `frame` lookups), keyed on the small list of v1 ids that survive into v2 per M-20 (notably `:re-frame/default` → `:rf/default`).
2. Each first-encounter per id calls `deprecated-warn-once!` directing the user at the canonical `:rf/*` form.
3. Add a small unit-test corpus driving each aliased id through registration + lookup, asserting both the canonical form and the alias resolve.

**Outcome B — Strip the promise.**

1. Remove the alias mention from Conventions.md L43 and MIGRATION.md L776.
2. Reword to: "Direct authoring of `:re-frame/*` ids does not resolve in v2; the migration agent rewrites mechanically per M-20." This makes M-20 a clean pre-v1 break.

## Decision criteria

Outcome A is justified iff there is a non-trivial population of in-flight v1 codebases or examples that *automatically* author `:re-frame/default` etc. — i.e. the migration agent rewrite alone isn't enough. Otherwise Outcome B is cleaner: less coercion-at-lookup overhead, and one fewer warn-once cache to maintain.

## Reference

- `ai/findings/back-compat-deprecation-audit-2026-05-13.md` §Per-surface table row 4
- Spec/MIGRATION.md §M-20 (the mechanical rename rule)
- Spec/Conventions.md §Reserved namespaces (where the alias appears)
close_reason: merged in 2026-05-13 batch landing wave

title: Add :rf.warning/deprecated-surface-used to Spec-Schemas + 009-Instrumentation
description:
Add the schema entry + paragraph describing the warn-once-per-id-per-session semantic so pair tools (Causa, 10x, re-frame-pair, Story trace panel) can surface deprecation warnings programmatically.

## Schema (spec/Spec-Schemas.md)

```clojure
;; :rf.warning/deprecated-surface-used — A v1→v2 back-compat shim is in use.
;; Emitted exactly once per surface-id per session. Pair tools surface the
;; warning; production builds elide per Spec 009 §Production builds.
[:map
 [:op           [:= :warning]]
 [:operation    [:= :rf.warning/deprecated-surface-used]]
 [:surface-id   :keyword]
 [:replacement  [:or :keyword :symbol :string]]
 [:doc-url      :string]
 [:reason       :string]
 [:recovery     [:= :warned-and-shipped]]]
```

## Paragraph (spec/009-Instrumentation.md §`:rf.warning/*`)

Document:
- Warn-once-per-surface-id-per-session semantic.
- `:doc-url` convention (MIGRATION.md anchor or canonical spec section).
- Test-fixture cache clear via `:adapter/clear-warn-once-caches!`.
- Production-elision contract via `interop/debug-enabled?`.

## Reference

- `ai/findings/back-compat-deprecation-audit-2026-05-13.md` §`:rf.warning/deprecated-surface-used`
- Existing `:rf.warning/*` schema entries in `Spec-Schemas.md`
close_reason: Withdrawn (pre-alpha): :rf.warning/deprecated-surface-used event unneeded. The shims will be cut, not warned about.

title: Add re-frame.warnings ns with deprecated-warn-once! helper
description:
Foundation for the back-compat deprecation surface (bead rf2-3k2w7). The helper sits alongside the existing `re-frame.views.warn-once` cache pattern (`implementation/core/src/re_frame/views/warn_once.cljs` L96-107) and rides the chained `:adapter/clear-warn-once-caches!` late-bind hook (per rf2-4edk) so test-fixture resets clear the cache between cases.

## API shape

```clojure
(re-frame.warnings/deprecated-warn-once!
  surface-id     ;; :keyword — keys the warn-once cache
  replacement    ;; :keyword / :symbol / :string — v2-canonical replacement
  doc-url)       ;; :string — MIGRATION.md anchor
```

Behaviour:

- One emit per `surface-id` per session (`defonce` atom set).
- Emits `:rf.warning/deprecated-surface-used` (per the companion Spec-Schemas + Spec 009 bead).
- CLJS path also `console.warn`s for browser users without a trace consumer attached.
- Entire body gated on `interop/debug-enabled?` — production builds DCE the literal under `:advanced` + `goog.DEBUG=false`, including the string fragments (surface-id, replacement, doc-url).
- Test-fixture isolation via the chained `:adapter/clear-warn-once-caches!` late-bind hook (rf2-4edk pattern).

## Task

1. Create `implementation/core/src/re_frame/warnings.cljc` with the defonce cache, `clear-warned-deprecated-surfaces!`, and `deprecated-warn-once!`.
2. Register the cache-clear under the chained `:adapter/clear-warn-once-caches!` hook so `reset-runtime-fixture` clears it.
3. Add `re-frame.warnings` re-exports under `re-frame.core` if user-facing surface area is desired.
4. Tests: warn-once-per-id semantics; production-elision smoke check; test-fixture isolation between cases.

## Reference

- `ai/findings/back-compat-deprecation-audit-2026-05-13.md` §Recommended deprecation mechanism
- `implementation/core/src/re_frame/views/warn_once.cljs` (pattern to follow)
- Spec 009 §Production builds (elision contract)
close_reason: Withdrawn (pre-alpha): deprecation warn-once mechanism unneeded. The shims will be cut, not deprecated.

title: Audit: v1 back-compat shims to deprecate — strip from spec, console.warn on use
description:
re-frame2 ships some v1-era surfaces purely for backwards-compatibility (principle of least disruption for v1→v2 migration). Examples:
- `:http` effect (superseded by `:rf.http/managed` — far better)
- possibly: legacy `dispatch` shapes, v1-style interceptors, etc.

These exist to ease migration. They should NOT appear in spec/ (which is normative — AI-facing). They should be tagged **deprecated** and emit **console.warn** on use, guiding users to the v2 idiom.

## Audit scope

Sweep the codebase for v1 back-compat surfaces. For each, determine:

1. **What is it?** — name, location, what it does.
2. **Why does it exist?** — v1 mental-model carryover, ergonomic, missing v2 equivalent, …
3. **Modern v2 replacement** — is there one? If not, this surface is **not** a back-compat shim — it's the v2 idiom.
4. **Disposition**:
   - **Keep + deprecate** — ships, but logs `console.warn` on first-use per session, with link to migration guide.
   - **Strip from spec** — already shipping but the spec mentions it; remove from spec/.
   - **Remove entirely** — no users depend on it; cut.
   - **Document as v2-idiom** — was misclassified; it's not back-compat, it's canonical.

## Where to look

- `implementation/core/src/re_frame/` — base namespaces (events, fx, cofx, subs)
- `implementation/http/src/` — `:http` effect (canonical example)
- `implementation/routing/src/` — possibly legacy routing fns
- `implementation/flows/src/` — possibly legacy reg-flow shapes
- `spec/` — find back-compat surfaces currently documented as if normative
- `spec/MIGRATION.md` — already lists what changed v1→v2; the inverse (what stayed for compat) is what we want

## Deliverable

**Findings doc** at `ai/findings/back-compat-deprecation-audit-<date>.md`:
1. Per-surface table (name | location | v2 replacement | disposition | reasoning)
2. Recommended deprecation mechanism — `console.warn` on first-use (once-per-id-per-session); message shape; cross-link target.
3. Recommended spec scrub — list of spec/ files needing removal of back-compat mentions.
4. **Recommended `:rf.warning/*` event for deprecated-surface-use** — adds to `spec/Spec-Schemas.md` + `spec/009-Instrumentation.md`. Tools (Causa, Story trace panel) surface deprecation warnings automatically; user code doesn't need to know about console.

## Mechanism proposal (audit should refine)

- Add a `deprecated-warn-once!` helper to `re-frame.warnings` (or wherever warn-once-cache lives — per rf2-4edk #347 cleanup).
- Each deprecated surface's emit-site calls it on first invocation per session.
- Helper emits `:rf.warning/deprecated-surface-used` trace event with `{:surface-id :http :replacement :rf.http/managed :doc-url ...}`.
- Trace bus consumers (Causa, Story) surface it; production builds elide per Spec 009 production-elision rules.

## File follow-up beads for each surface

After audit lands, each "deprecate" finding becomes a bead:
- (a) Add deprecation warn-once to the impl
- (b) Strip the spec mention
- (c) Update MIGRATION.md to confirm the v2 replacement

Aim ~5-10 follow-up beads.

## Discipline

- **Read-only audit**. Don't edit impl/spec.
- Findings doc is local-only (ai/ gitignored).
- File follow-up beads via `bd create`.
- NO AI attribution in bead bodies.
- All shell commands `cd C:/Users/miket/code/re-frame2` first.

## Cross-references

- `spec/MIGRATION.md` — the canonical v1→v2 diff
- `spec/009-Instrumentation.md` — warning-trace schema
- `spec/Spec-Schemas.md` — error/warning event shapes
- rf2-4edk #347 — `:adapter/clear-warn-once-caches!` late-bind hook (the mechanism already exists for warn-once discipline)
notes:
**Reframing 2026-05-13 (Mike): re-frame2 is pre-alpha. Back-compat is NOT a concern.** The audit's disposition vocabulary should change:

- ~~Keep + deprecate~~ → **Remove the shim entirely.** v2 idiom is the surface; v1 shims should be cut, not preserved.
- **Strip from spec** → still valid (anything documented as if normative but really back-compat).
- **Remove entirely** → primary disposition for v1 shims.
- **Verify v2 idiom is complete** → if a v1 shim was patching over a deficiency, file an improvement bead for the v2 path. Don't ship both.

The console.warn / trace-warning mechanism proposed in the original brief is **withdrawn** — there's nothing to warn about because the shim won't exist. The 'least-disruption migration' principle doesn't apply until beta.

Once re-frame2 enters beta / 1.0, back-compat becomes a real concern. Until then: cut freely.

When the in-flight audit returns, the follow-up beads should focus on:
1. **Inventory**: what back-compat shims exist?
2. **Cut list**: which can be removed right now?
3. **v2-idiom gaps**: which removals require the v2 idiom to be enriched first? File improvement beads.

NO deprecate-with-warn dispositions. NO console.warn schemes. Just clean removal.
close_reason: Audit complete; 9 follow-up beads filed. Notable: no :http shim exists (the brief's canonical example was wrong); real shims are re-frame.views-macros/{reg-view, with-frame, bound-fn}. PRE-ALPHA REFRAMING (Mike 2026-05-13): the deprecate-with-warn-once approach is withdrawn. Children should be re-scoped to CUT the shims (not deprecate), plus strip the spec mentions. The warn-once mechanism (rf2-0d67x, rf2-6dq0y) is unneeded — there's nothing to warn about once shims are removed. Children rf2-w4f3o, rf2-pepfb, rf2-81dzu need reframing from 'deprecate' to 'cut + sweep callers to canonical imports'.

title: Review guide → spec cross-references — most should be removed (spec is for AIs)
description:
The /guide is a **human-facing tutorial**. The /spec is **AI-facing normative contract**. The two have different audiences and the guide should rarely reference spec.

Currently many guide chapters cross-link to spec docs for various reasons (rationale, schemas, edge cases, normative claims). Most of those should NOT exist — they leak spec-shaped detail into a tutorial.

## Permitted spec references

Only a small set of cross-links from guide → spec should survive:

- **`spec/Principles.md`** — the load-bearing philosophy (Spec-ulation, etc.); deserves human reference too.
- **`spec/MIGRATION.md`** — Mike already directs migrants here from ch.18 (From re-frame v1).
- **A handful of others** to be determined by the audit — e.g. maybe `spec/Conventions.md` for reserved namespaces? Maybe `spec/Pattern-*.md` for Pattern walkthroughs? The audit should propose the canonical short list.

Everything else: **remove the link** + either delete the reference outright OR replace with paraphrased explanation if the spec content was load-bearing for the guide reader.

## Audit scope

For every `[…](../spec/*)` or `[…](spec/*)` or `[…](../../spec/*)` reference in `docs/guide/*.md`, ask:
- Does a human-facing tutorial reader actually need this?
- Is the spec content paraphrased in the guide already? If so, drop the link.
- Is the spec content load-bearing? If so, paraphrase it into the guide.
- Is the reference to one of the "permitted" docs? If so, keep.

## Output

A findings doc at `ai/findings/guide-spec-refs-audit-2026-05-13.md` listing:
1. **Per-link disposition** — `[file:line target → KEEP / DROP / PARAPHRASE]`
2. **Per-chapter summary** — total refs, count of each disposition
3. **Recommended permitted-targets list** — the canonical short list of spec docs the guide may keep linking to (with reasoning)
4. **Follow-up bead sketches** — group by chapter file (1 bead per chapter that needs surgery)

Then file `bd create` for each per-chapter follow-up.

## Discipline

- Read-only audit. Don't edit guide chapters.
- Findings doc is local-only (ai/ gitignored).
- File follow-up beads via `bd create`.
- All shell commands `cd C:/Users/miket/code/re-frame2` first.
- No AI attribution in bead bodies.

## Cross-references

- `docs/guide/01-why-re-frame2.md` through `docs/guide/20-stories.md` — the corpus
- `spec/Principles.md`, `spec/MIGRATION.md`, `spec/Conventions.md`, `spec/Pattern-*.md`, etc. — the corpus to audit links to
- Recent landings that may have added new refs: rf2-bbbc (coeffects), rf2-fgvi (interceptors), rf2-r82g (performance), rf2-9uec (app-db), rf2-fk8s children, etc.
notes: **Add 2026-05-13 (Mike)**: the guide reader only cares about the CLJS reference implementation. Any spec content the guide currently links out for that's only meaningful at the cross-port / substrate-agnostic level should DROP. Examples to watch for: rf2-osxa spec/010 substrate-agnostic schemas wording → CLJS users just want Malli; spec/006-ReactiveSubstrate cross-port detail → CLJS users just want Reagent/UIx/Helix; per-port carve-outs in MIGRATION → not relevant. The audit's KEEP set is tighter than originally framed: only Principles.md (universal) + MIGRATION.md (v1→v2 utility); paraphrase the rest into CLJS-shaped tutorial prose.
close_reason: Audit complete. 100 refs catalogued. 9 follow-up chapter-surgery beads filed (rf2-1foi5 + 8 chapter beads). Permitted-set proposal: Mike's Principles + MIGRATION, plus 3 Pattern docs (AsyncEffect, RemoteData, Forms) per their genuine tutorial value. Conflict flagged: rf2-09ew3 needs Mike review re: rf2-q2yv intentional spec/004 cross-links.

title: Research: new re-frame2 template — modern shape + name + design walkthrough
description:
Research + design a successor to re-frame-template targeting re-frame2. Output: \`ai/findings/re-frame2-template-design-<date>.md\` capturing the design + open questions for Mike walkthrough.

## What the new template is for

A scaffolding tool that gives a developer a runnable re-frame2 app in seconds, with the right framework conventions in place — modern Reagent/UIx/Helix toolchain, Causa wired, optional Story, unit testing harness, no v1-era cruft.

## Mike's locked / preferred decisions

### Cut from re-frame-template

These v1 dependencies are dropped:

- re-frame-10x (replaced by Causa, added unconditionally — see below)
- garden / spade (CSS-in-CLJS — modern apps use CSS / Tailwind / vanilla)
- re-frisk
- cljs.test (replaced by a modern test runner — research recommendation)
- git-inject
- re-pressed
- breaking-point
- github-actions option (or the boilerplate workflows from v1 — the user adds their own)
- the routing options (the user picks routing as a separate concern; re-frame2's spec/012 routing is built in)

### Always-add

- **re-frame-causa** — Causa wired by default (in-app overlay launch per rf2-tijr Option-C; Ctrl+Shift+C). The Causa-MCP server stays optional install.
- **Unit testing harness** — modern Clojure(Script) test runner. Research: kaocha? cognitect-test-runner? shadow's built-in :node-test? Pick a 2026 best practice.

### Optional

- **Adapter pick** — user chooses Reagent / Reagent-slim / UIx / Helix at scaffold time.
- **Story** — opt-in via flag. If enabled, scaffolds with `examples/`-style stories file + workspace + variant + `tools/story/` integration.
- **(Eventually) skills install** — when the re-frame2 skills are published to the Claude marketplace, scaffold should optionally install the appropriate skills for the user's adapter + tools.

## Open research questions for the findings doc

1. **Should the new template be lein-based?** Lein is legacy; deps.edn + clojure CLI is the modern Clojure path. Babashka clj-new / deps-new are the modern scaffolding tools (cf. `tools/template/` which is already deps-new-based). Probably **no lein**; confirm.

2. **What's the modern way to ship a scaffolding tool?**
   - clj-new template (lein dependency, but lein is just the harness — output uses deps.edn). 
   - deps-new template (more modern, deps.edn-native).
   - Standalone CLI (Babashka script or compiled native).
   - `tools/template/` already does some of this — extend it or replace?

3. **What name?**
   - `re-frame-template` (v1 name; replace?)
   - `re-frame2-new` (matches `clojure -X:project/new` convention)
   - `re-frame2-template` (continuity, but tied to "template" verb)
   - `create-re-frame2-app` (npm-style — could work cross-toolchain)
   - Something distinctive?

4. **Should the template ship a sample app or a blank slate?**
   - Sample (counter app, like rf-v1 template) — shows the user the shape they're aiming for.
   - Blank — minimal mount + `:rf/default` frame + one event + one view; no semantics.
   - Lean: sample, because newcomers learn from the example.

5. **What's the test-harness recommendation for 2026 CLJS?**
   - kaocha + kaocha-cljs2
   - shadow-cljs `:node-test` (lightweight, single-tool)
   - cognitect-test-runner + cljs.test
   - Recommend best practice given re-frame2's idioms (per-test `with-frame`, etc.).

6. **What about CSS strategy?**
   - Plain CSS file (simplest)
   - Tailwind (most popular 2026)
   - CSS Modules
   - No opinion — let the user pick by leaving CSS plumbing minimal

7. **Build target — single-target or multi-target?**
   - Browser + Node (testing) + JVM (SSR) — comprehensive
   - Browser-only — simpler; user adds Node/JVM later
   - Recommend re-frame2's intended deployment shapes

8. **What goes in `dev/` vs `src/` vs `test/`?**
   - Match the existing `tools/template/` shape?
   - Document any deviation.

9. **MCP-skills integration** — when re-frame-causa, re-frame-pair2, re-frame2-implementor skills are published, the template should offer to install them via Claude's `/skill` mechanism. Spec the hook for now; impl when the skills publish.

10. **What's the upgrade path?** A user with an existing v1 app + the template — does the template help them migrate (cross-link the re-frame-migration skill)?

## Output

Findings doc:
- One-paragraph executive summary (the lean: name, scaffold-tool choice, test harness, default-on items, optional items)
- Per-question section with options + recommendation
- "What I'm forgetting" section — surface gaps Mike's brief might have missed
- Suggested next-steps (likely: 1-2 follow-up beads to scaffold the template based on the findings)

## Discipline

- **Read-only research**. No commits to src/template/ or anywhere. Just the findings doc.
- Findings doc lives at `ai/findings/re-frame2-template-design-<date>.md` (local-only, gitignored).
- File any follow-up beads via `bd create`.
- NO AI attribution.
- Cross-reference: `tools/template/` (existing scaffolding tool) — does the new template extend it or replace it? Mike's brief doesn't say; surface the question.

## Inputs to read first

- `tools/template/` — existing scaffolding tool (deps-new-based per spec/000-Vision)
- `tools/template/spec/` — what it already does
- `skills/re-frame2-setup/` — current setup-skill, references what a working re-frame2 app needs
- `examples/reagent/counter/` and other examples — what shape the template should produce
- re-frame-template (v1) — the original at github.com/day8/re-frame-template (WebFetch if you can; otherwise reason from the cut-list above)
- 2026 Clojure ecosystem state — deps.edn defaults, popular tooling
notes: Research complete. Findings doc at ai/findings/re-frame2-template-design-2026-05-13.md (local-only). 6 follow-up beads filed: T1=rf2-8oi86, T2=rf2-y9zqc, T3=rf2-t009p, T4=rf2-r2jqo, T5=rf2-htvqk, T6=rf2-8437x. Open items for Mike: name choice; 000-Vision §Non-goals tweak to permit :include-story? flag (T3); confirm Clojars coord name for re-frame2-causa (T2 blocker).
close_reason: research complete; 6 follow-ups filed

title: Review trace surface design for Story / Causa / re-frame-pair consumer needs
description:
The trace bus is the load-bearing observability substrate consumed by every tool surface in the re-frame2 ecosystem: Story's trace panel, Causa's 16 panels, re-frame-pair2's MCP operations. **Does the current trace surface satisfy these consumers elegantly? How can it be improved?**

## Scope

Audit `spec/009-Instrumentation.md` (the trace surface contract) + `implementation/core/src/re_frame/trace.cljc` (the impl) against the three consumer surfaces:

1. **Story** (`tools/story/`) — trace panel surfaces the six-domino cascade per event; needs filterable, scrollable, time-correlated trace events tagged by variant.
2. **Causa** (`tools/10x/` design — spec corpus at `tools/10x/spec/`) — the 16 panels each consume different slices: causality graph keys on `:dispatch-id` / `:parent-dispatch-id`; event-detail (hero) needs the full per-event trace; epoch panel needs commit boundaries; sub-topology needs sub-evaluation events; etc.
3. **re-frame-pair2** (`skills/re-frame-pair2/`, `tools/pair2-mcp/`) — MCP ops query the running app via nREPL; needs structured trace events that can be filtered/summarised by AI without ambiguous payloads.

## Questions to answer

1. **Event-shape elegance**: are `:rf/op` / `:op-type` / tags consistent? Does the schema support the consumer queries naturally, or do consumers do post-hoc reshaping?
2. **Correlation**: `:dispatch-id` + `:parent-dispatch-id` exist for the causality graph; what about per-event correlation across the trace/epoch/sub-evaluation surfaces? Are there missing correlation keys?
3. **Filtering vocabulary**: can Story / Causa / pair2 filter by event-id, frame-id, handler-id, source-coord, time window, severity? What's missing?
4. **Source-coord coverage**: rf2-3nn8 (`:rf.trace/trigger-handler`) landed today; rf2-ts1a (call-site coords) is queued. Are there remaining source-coord gaps tools would benefit from?
5. **Severity discipline**: per Spec 009, ops fall into `:event` / `:warning` / `:error` / `:assert` / etc. Are consumers tagging severity correctly? Any drift between spec and impl emit sites?
6. **Elision behaviour**: production elision (Spec 009 §Production elision) — does it preserve enough of the trace for production debugging via Causa MCP? Or strip too much?
7. **Per-frame routing**: traces emit per-frame; can a consumer subscribe to all frames vs filter to one? Is `:rf.trace/frame-id` always populated?
8. **Cross-tool re-use**: Story's trace panel and Causa's event-detail panel render the same data; are they sharing rendering code or duplicating? Should they share a slim renderer library?

## Output

Findings doc at `ai/findings/trace-surface-consumer-audit-<date>.md` (local-only). Recommendations grouped by:
- **Spec edits** — wording / schema additions to `spec/009-Instrumentation.md` and `spec/Spec-Schemas.md`.
- **Impl additions** — new emit sites, missing correlation keys, refactor opportunities.
- **Consumer refactors** — places where Story / Causa / pair2 should share code.

File `bd create` follow-ups for each actionable finding. Aim ~5-15 beads.

## Discipline

- Read-only audit. Don't edit spec or impl or docs in this bead.
- Don't commit findings doc.
- No AI attribution in bead bodies.
- All shell commands `cd C:/Users/miket/code/re-frame2` first.

## Cross-references

- `spec/009-Instrumentation.md` — the trace contract
- `spec/Spec-Schemas.md` — trace event schemas
- `spec/Tool-Pair.md` — the cross-tool consumer surface
- `tools/10x/spec/` — Causa design corpus (16 panels per `tools/10x/spec/007-UX-IA.md`)
- `tools/story/spec/` — Story trace panel docs
- `skills/re-frame-pair2/SKILL.md` + reference leaves — pair2's trace consumption
- `implementation/core/src/re_frame/trace.cljc` — the runtime
notes: Audit complete. Findings at ai/findings/trace-surface-consumer-audit-2026-05-13.md (local-only). 8 follow-ups filed: rf2-g6ih4, rf2-97ah0, rf2-norar, rf2-fv8dy, rf2-tj2fn, rf2-lf84g, rf2-wvzgd, rf2-shaa1. Headline: widen :dispatch-id to every cascade event (the big one) — Story/Causa/pair2 all assume cascade-wide and the spec already promises it; runtime only stamps :event/dispatched.
close_reason: audit complete; 8 follow-ups filed

title: Normalise tools/pair2-mcp shape with other tools/ artefacts
description:
tools/pair2-mcp is structurally inconsistent with story/, story-mcp/, template/:

1. No deps.edn (other tools/ artefacts all have one with :clein/build).
2. No LICENSE file at the artefact root. The package.json declares `"files": ["out/server.js", "README.md", "LICENSE"]` — that LICENSE entry references a file that doesn't exist; npm publish will silently drop it.
3. shadow-cljs version is ^3.4.10 while implementation/ is 2.28.20 and the rest of tools/ uses neither pin (they don't run shadow-cljs).

Actions:
- Add tools/pair2-mcp/LICENSE (copy of root license.txt).
- Decide whether shadow-cljs version aligns with implementation/ (after rf2-9cek shadow-cljs bump) or stays independent — document choice in tools/README.md.
- Consider adding a deps.edn if any JVM-side surface lands later.
close_reason: Closed

title: [spec-drift] Document :rf/interceptor-error / :rf/interceptor-errors ctx keys (rf2-mm2a follow-on)
description:
rf2-mm2a (commit b53a3f7) introduced a two-key contract on the post-chain interceptor context:

  :rf/interceptor-error  — the FIRST error only (the original cause; what tracing code reads to fire :rf.error/handler-exception).
  :rf/interceptor-errors — a vector of ALL errors in occurrence order (preserves later :after-phase failures that would otherwise be overwritten).

Impl ref:
  implementation/core/src/re_frame/interceptor.cljc:46-55 (record-error doc-string explains the contract).
  implementation/core/src/re_frame/router.cljc:362-364 (singleton drives :rf.error/handler-exception emit).
  implementation/core/test/re_frame/interceptor_test.clj:370-385 + 426-428 (test contract).

Gap: neither key appears in any spec file. spec/002-Frames.md §Drain-loop pseudocode (line 875) glosses interceptor handling at high level. spec/001-Registration.md and spec/009-Instrumentation.md make no mention. Spec-Schemas has no schema for the chain context post-execution.

Why it matters: pair-tools and 10x v2 reading post-drain ctx snapshots see a richer error surface than the trace stream alone provides. Ports re-implementing the interceptor chain may legitimately ship the singleton only without realising the vector is contract.

Direction: spec→impl. Document in spec/002-Frames.md §Drain-loop pseudocode (or spec/001-Registration.md §Interceptor contract — 002 is the drain-loop owner so the natural home). Pin:
  - singleton-FIRST / vector-ALL semantics
  - :before failures short-circuit subsequent :before stages
  - :after pass runs in full (for teardown) regardless
  - trace stream emits ONLY for the singleton (paired with finding F-5)

Source finding: ai/findings/spec-impl-audit-2026-05-12-v2.md §F-4.
close_reason: Closed

title: [spec-drift] Spec-Schemas HandlerExceptionTags: add :phase / :exception / :frame keys to mirror impl emit (rf2-mm2a follow-on)
description:
spec/Spec-Schemas.md:333-341 HandlerExceptionTags currently lists:
  :category :failing-id :reason :event :handler-id :exception-message :exception-data

Impl emit at implementation/core/src/re_frame/router.cljc:224-234 carries:
  :event-id :event :frame :failing-id :handler-id :phase :exception :exception-message :reason :recovery

Gap: schema lacks :phase, :exception, :frame, :event-id. :phase is the rf2-mm2a-introduced discriminator (commit b53a3f7) — values :before / :after / :handler — that tooling reads to surface WHERE in the interceptor chain the throw came from. Open-map convention permits the additional keys at the schema level, but the canonical contracted tag-set should advertise :phase explicitly so ports know to populate it (FxHandlerExceptionTags at line 343 sets a precedent for contracting the full payload).

Direction: spec→impl. Update HandlerExceptionTags to mirror impl:

  (def HandlerExceptionTags
    [:map
     [:category          [:= :rf.error/handler-exception]]
     [:event-id          {:optional true} :keyword]
     [:event             [:vector :any]]
     [:frame             {:optional true} :keyword]
     [:failing-id        :keyword]
     [:handler-id        :keyword]
     [:phase             {:optional true} [:enum :before :after :handler]]
     [:exception         {:optional true} :any]
     [:exception-message :string]
     [:exception-data    {:optional true} :any]
     [:reason            :string]])

Source finding: ai/findings/spec-impl-audit-2026-05-12-v2.md §F-3.
close_reason: Closed

title: [BUG] drain! peek+pop in router.cljc not atomic across threads — production race
description:
Surfaced during rf2-lmkk JVM flake stabilisation (#442). The test-only `with-redefs` fix is in place; the underlying production race remains.

## Root cause

`router.cljc` `drain!` reads the queue via `peek` then mutates via `pop` (or equivalent). The pair is not atomic. When the single-thread executor (`interop/next-tick`) wakes up mid-sync-drain, both threads can:

1. `peek` the same envelope
2. process it (double-process one event)
3. `pop` — dropping a different envelope from the queue

Failure shape under stress: `[:sync-only :sync-only]` where `[:sync-only :outside-async]` was expected, because the async event got `peek`'d twice (one of them with the wrong drain state) and the next event was popped without being processed.

## Repro before fix

- Tight reset-runtime-per-iter loop on `async-dispatch-resolves-after-current-drain`: **4 fails / 1000 iters**, matching the CI flake shape exactly.

## After rf2-lmkk test fix

- 0/1000 with `with-redefs [interop/next-tick ...]` — but only because the executor is bypassed in the test. Production code still has the race.

## Likely fix shapes

1. **Atomic peek+pop** — replace the queue with a structure that gives atomic dequeue (e.g. `ConcurrentLinkedQueue`'s `poll` on JVM, or guard the queue access with a lock).
2. **Single-drainer invariant** — enforce that only one thread can be in `drain!` at a time. The executor wake-up should be a no-op if a drain is already in progress; the active drainer picks up any newly queued envelopes before returning.
3. **Recursive-drain shape** — the current code may be assuming drain is recursive; if so, document and enforce that the executor's wake-up only fires when the queue's state machine is in a known-stable phase.

## Scope

`implementation/core/src/re_frame/router.cljc` `drain!` + the executor wake-up path (`process-event*` or wherever the `next-tick` callback dispatches into).

## Cross-references

- rf2-lmkk #442 — test-only stabilisation (this bead is the prod fix).
- rf2-iosc #327 — sister flake, same intervention shape (also test-only).
- spec/002-Frames.md §run-to-completion — verify the drain semantics this fix must preserve.

## Discipline

- Affects all builds (no `goog.DEBUG` gate to elide behind).
- JVM-specific surface (CLJS is single-threaded so the race can't manifest there).
- May affect performance — pick the cheapest fix that preserves correctness.
notes: Decision 2026-05-13: B = single-drainer invariant. Reasoning: spec/002 run-to-completion implies single-drainer logically; encoding it explicitly makes the contract self-documenting. Future-proofs Causa/pair2 hooks from non-drain threads (clear answer: enqueue + wake-up, active drainer picks up). Bounded cost (~5 lines for AtomicBoolean + try/finally). Pre-alpha green-light dispatch.
close_reason: PRs merged in batch landing wave 2026-05-13

title: [diagnostic] Warn on dispatch from async callback fell through to :rf/default
description:
Currently, dispatch from a setTimeout / addEventListener / requestAnimationFrame callback attached in a view body silently routes to :rf/default (per Spec 002 line 373 — fundamental property of dynamic scope). A 2048-game implementor hit this in production; the bug was silent because the handler was registered against the user's intended frame (:game), not :rf/default.

**Proposal:** add a warning op-type **:rf.warning/dispatch-from-async-callback-fell-through-to-default** emitted by dispatch when ALL of these hold:
- No *current-frame* dynamic binding active
- No React context value resolvable (we're outside a render pass)
- AT LEAST ONE non-default frame is registered in the runtime (single-frame apps don't have the footgun)
- The dispatch hit :rf/default by fallback

Suppression policy mirrors :rf.warning/plain-fn-under-non-default-frame-once: suppress repeats per (event-id, non-default-frames-registered) pair. First occurrence emits :warning to trace bus + console.warn in dev.

Message body should name the canonical fixes (in priority order): (a) use :dispatch-later or a reg-fx (carries frame in closure); (b) capture (rf/bound-dispatcher) inside render and call from the callback; (c) for true lifecycle escape, use Form-3 :component-did-mount / use-effect to attach listeners (the lifecycle hook runs after commit but the dispatcher is captured during render).

Detection mechanism is the load-bearing design question: can we cheaply know 'we're not in a render pass and not in a handler drain'? At minimum we can detect 'no *current-frame* binding AND no resolved React context' — the same null-binding fall-through case the current :rf/default fallback already handles.

**Edits:**
- spec/009-Instrumentation.md — register the new warning op-type in the standard warning catalogue
- spec/Conventions.md — add to the reserved warning-id list if applicable
- implementation/core/src/re_frame/router.cljs (or wherever dispatch fall-through lives) — emit the warning
- production elision per Spec 009 §Production builds

**Findings:** ai/findings/animation-pattern-audit-2026-05-12.md §5 row 6.

**Discipline:** detection must be ZERO-cost in single-frame apps (the warning gate's first check is 'are there non-default frames?' — if not, the entire branch DCEs away). False-positive rate matters more than coverage — only warn when we're certain the call is async-stack-from-view.
close_reason: Closed

title: [guide] Add view-antipattern callout + animation sidebar to chapter 06
description:
Mirror the spec-side antipattern callout in the views guide chapter, in guide voice.

**Edits to docs/guide/06-views-and-frames.md:**

1. Add §Views don't attach native listeners (sibling to existing §Views compute hiccup only). Worked example showing the wrong shape:

```clojure
;; WRONG — dispatch fires outside render; loses frame; silently routes to :rf/default
(rf/reg-view tile [props]
  [:div {:ref (fn [el]
                (when el
                  (.addEventListener el "animationend"
                    #(dispatch [:tile/finished]))))}])
```

and the right shape (Form-3 via reg-view* with create-class, or use-effect for UIx/Helix). Cross-reference Spec 002 §Dispatches issued from inside a handler body (the canonical "async callbacks escape the binding" line).

2. Add §Animations sidebar — the three regimes (CSS transitions / RAF as reg-fx / library bridges via outer/inner). Cross-references: AsyncEffect for regime B, rf2-i5n9 for regime C. Headline: 'state is the truth; the view layer animates transitions; animation completion is silent unless explicitly modelled in state.'

**Findings:** ai/findings/animation-pattern-audit-2026-05-12.md §5 rows 4-5.

**Discipline:** stay in guide voice — no spec citations in the body, only in cross-reference links. Worked examples should compile.
close_reason: Closed

title: [spec] Add view-antipattern callout: views don't attach native DOM listeners
description:
Views are pure (state, props) -> render-tree. They MUST NOT attach native DOM listeners (addEventListener, setTimeout, requestAnimationFrame in render bodies) — callbacks fire outside render and lose frame context, silently routing dispatch to :rf/default (per Spec 002 line 373).

The footgun was hit in production by a re-frame2 implementor building a 2048 game who attached animationend/transitionend listeners inside view bodies. The user's manual fix was to thread :frame :game explicitly — symptom-level, not root cause.

**Edits:**
1. spec/004-Views.md — add a new §View antipatterns section. Include the dispatch-from-native-listener footgun, the silent fall-back-to-:rf/default behaviour, and the lifecycle escape hatch (Form-3 / use-effect) as the right shape when imperative attach is genuinely required. Cross-reference 002 §Dispatches issued from inside a handler body line 373 area.
2. spec/004-Views.md — add §Animations subsection (transitions / RAF-as-reg-fx / library-bridges three regimes table). Cross-reference Pattern-AsyncEffect §Concrete instances for regime B and rf2-i5n9 for regime C.

**Findings:** ai/findings/animation-pattern-audit-2026-05-12.md §5 row 1 + row 2.

**Discipline:** read-only audit decided NOT to ship Pattern-Animation as a standalone Pattern doc — three regimes don't share one shape; content folds into Spec 004 + existing Patterns. Don't create Pattern-Animation.md.
close_reason: Closed

title: docs: sweep skills + SKILL-REDIRECT.md — verify (and restructure if needed) per-skill terseness
description:
Follow-up to rf2-kydy (which restructured SKILL-REDIRECT.md into two audience-shaped sections). Mike's directive 2026-05-12: don't just verify audience-fit — verify **per-skill** that SKILL-REDIRECT fulfills its purpose **tersely** for each skill that points at it.

## What "from each skill's perspective" means

For every skill under `/skills/`, identify:

1. **Which URLs from `SKILL-REDIRECT.md` does this skill actually depend on?** (Search the skill's `SKILL.md` + `reference/*` for cross-references.)
2. **What does this skill need that's NOT in `SKILL-REDIRECT.md`?** (e.g. a Pattern doc, a Conventions section, an example link)
3. **What does `SKILL-REDIRECT.md` currently expose that no skill needs?** (dead weight to trim)
4. **Does the current 2-section structure (rf2-kydy: "Building with the reference implementation" + "Implementing the spec" + "Other") read tersely from each skill's vantage?** Or is the agent loading the redirect file forced to scan past entries irrelevant to their skill before finding what they need?

## Skills currently shipping

```
skills/re-frame-migration
skills/re-frame-pair-improver2
skills/re-frame-pair2
skills/re-frame2
skills/re-frame2-setup
skills/re-frame2-implementor   ← landing via rf2-5xje (in flight)
```

## Output

Audit each skill against SKILL-REDIRECT, then **restructure or refine** SKILL-REDIRECT so that:

- Each skill can route through it quickly without scanning irrelevant entries.
- Per-skill terseness — no skill-specific entry should be longer than one line.
- The audience-shaped sections from rf2-kydy stay (or get refined) — Mike confirmed those have value. But verify from the per-skill angle whether sub-headings or skill-prefixed groupings make the file land harder.
- Dead entries get trimmed.
- Missing entries get added.

Possible restructures (the agent picks):
- **Audience-shaped + skill-tagged**: keep the 2 sections, but tag each entry with which skill(s) reach it (e.g. `[re-frame2, re-frame-migration]`).
- **Skill-shaped sections**: one section per skill, listing the pointers that skill needs.
- **Hybrid**: audience-shaped sections at the top + a small "per-skill jump table" at the bottom listing each skill with its anchor-link bullets.

## Discipline

- **Single PR**.
- **NO AI attribution** in commit or PR.
- Don't commit `ai/`, `decision-beads.md`, `findings/`.
- All URLs from the current `SKILL-REDIRECT.md` should remain reachable unless verified as unused by every skill.
- **Tersely** is Mike's emphasis — don't bloat the file with explanations. The redirect's job is fast routing, not narration.
- Touches: `SKILL-REDIRECT.md` (definitely) + possibly minor tweaks to skill files to clean up references (only if a skill's reference is genuinely broken).

## Depends on

- **rf2-5xje** — the `re-frame2-implementor` skill is in flight. Wait for it to land before dispatching this bead so the implementor skill's URL needs are part of the audit.

## Coordination

- Don't touch the skill content itself unless a cross-reference is broken.
- If you find a skill is missing a SKILL-REDIRECT pointer for something it needs, **add the pointer**.
- If you find a skill is reaching outside SKILL-REDIRECT to fetch a URL it should be redirecting through, **flag it** in the PR body (don't auto-fix — could be intentional).

## Output structure of PR body

- Per-skill audit table (skill | pointers needed | pointers missing | dead-weight identified).
- The chosen restructure (which approach + why).
- Verification: re-read SKILL-REDIRECT from each skill's perspective post-rewrite + confirm 5-second routing works.
