title: Suggestion: review stale nil cache path when subscribing before a sub is first registered
description:
Suggestion for further agent review on suitability and possible action.

Review finding:
A subscribe against an unregistered sub appears to cache a reaction with  ody-fn nil. If the sub is then registered for the first time, the registrar takes the first-registration path rather than the replacement path, so the existing cached entry is not invalidated. An early subscriber can therefore remain stuck on 
il until the cache slot is cleared or disposed.

Observed surface:
- implementation/core/src/re_frame/subs.cljc: compute-and-cache! emits :rf.error/no-such-sub when lookup fails but still stores a cache entry.
- implementation/core/src/re_frame/registrar.cljc: first-time egister! emits :rf.registry/handler-registered but does not notify replacement hooks.
- implementation/core/src/re_frame/subs.cljc: subscribe prefers cache hits, so the stale entry can survive the later registration.

Why this may merit action:
This looks like a boot-order / lazy-load correctness edge case. It could surface if code subscribes before optional namespaces have loaded their registrations.

Suggested review questions:
- Should missing-subscribe attempts avoid caching altogether?
- Alternatively, should first registration of a :sub invalidate any pre-existing cache entries keyed by that sub id?
- What regression test best captures the sequence: subscribe missing sub -> register sub -> subscribe again?

Possible action, if the review agrees:
Prevent caching on missing-sub lookup or add an explicit invalidation path on first registration, then cover the sequence with a focused regression test.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #161 — sub-cache stale nil regression fixed via skip-cache-write for no-such-sub miss + tests + spec invariants (commits 745e9e3, 18c4256, aa4f425).

title: Suggestion: review whether clear-sub should invalidate materialized subscription cache entries
description:
Suggestion for further agent review on suitability and possible action.

Review finding:
f/clear-sub currently unregisters the registrar entry but does not invalidate already-materialized per-frame subscription cache entries. Because subscribe serves cache hits before consulting the registrar, a cleared sub can continue returning stale values until the cache is explicitly cleared or naturally disposed.

Observed surface:
- implementation/core/src/re_frame/subs.cljc: clear-sub delegates directly to registrar removal.
- implementation/core/src/re_frame/subs.cljc: subscribe returns cached :reaction on hit before re-checking the registrar.
- implementation/core/src/re_frame/registrar.cljc: unregister/clear-kind do not trigger the sub replacement hook used by re-registration invalidation.

Why this may merit action:
This looks like a correctness gap for a public API surface re-exported from e-frame.core, and it can also affect tooling or hot-reload flows that rely on clearing subs.

Suggested review questions:
- Is clear-sub intended to guarantee that subsequent reads behave as though the sub is gone immediately?
- Should unregister / clear-kind for :sub also evict matching cache entries across frames, analogous to the existing re-registration hook?
- Are dedicated tests needed for clear-sub id and clear-sub with no args against pinned cache entries?

Possible action, if the review agrees:
Invalidate matching sub-cache entries across frames when subs are cleared, and add regression coverage for stale-cache reads after clear.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #161 — clear-sub pin-behaviour pinned via tests + spec invariants alongside rf2-l9u5 (commits 18c4256, aa4f425).

title: Conventions :rf/route vs corpus-wide :route for the route slice — Conventions is the outlier
description:
Audit rf2-smpt finding F4, ranked top-1 most load-bearing.

Quote (audit-conventions table F4 line 56): 'Conventions says the route slice lives at :rf/route. 012-Routing canonically says :route (no rf/ prefix), API.md's framework-sub list shows :route, Runtime-Architecture.md:124 says ":rf/machines, :route". Conventions is the outlier; the rest of the corpus uses :route. This is the single highest-impact cross-spec inconsistency in the file.'

Audit §4 Top-1 (line 79): 'Conventions.md is alone in saying the route slice lives at :rf/route. Every other relevant spec (012, API, Runtime-Architecture) says :route. Conventions is supposed to be the canonical home for reserved app-db keys; if Conventions disagrees with 012, then either 012 is wrong about the runtime contract, or the migration agent is going to flag legitimate user code as a collision against a key that doesn't actually exist.'

Cross-spec inconsistency per audit-criterion #2: Conventions §Reserved app-db keys table (line 99) lists :rf/route; 012-Routing.md:225,933 + API.md:112 + Runtime-Architecture.md:124 use :route. A migration agent following Conventions would flag user code that writes to :route as legitimate, and ignore it as collision against :rf/route — exactly backwards.

Resolution per audit R-S3: 'Decide :route vs :rf/route. Spec corpus consensus is :route; Conventions is the outlier. Two-character edit (s/:rf\/route/:route/g in the Reserved app-db keys table cell). Then re-check the §Reserved namespaces :rf.route/* row and the §Reserved sub-ids list — [:rf/route] appears as a sub-id alongside [:rf.route/id] etc., which itself is muddled (sub-id is :route, family-prefix is :rf.route/*).'
notes:
2026-05-09 — investigation by rf2-ljw6 (halt-and-report)

CORPUS DISAGREEMENT confirmed: the audit (rf2-smpt F4) claims "the rest of the corpus uses :route" but discovery shows the corpus is split, NOT consistent.

Route slice in app-db (the :rf/route vs :route question):
  Uses :route (no rf/ prefix):
    - 012-Routing.md:225,933 (canonical: "The runtime maintains a single slice in app-db under the :route key")
    - Spec-Schemas.md:549 ("The shape of app-db's :route slice")
    - Runtime-Architecture.md:124 (":rf/machines, :route")
    - API.md:134 (":route" framework sub returns "the full :route slice")
    - Cross-Spec-Interactions.md:81 ("the resolved :route slice")
  Uses :rf/route (with prefix):
    - Conventions.md:108 (the table cell flagged by audit)
    - 002-Frames.md:69 ("[:rf/route] — the route slice for url-bound frames")
    - 011-SSR.md:67 (":rf/route (carried inside :rf/app-db under the :route slice)" — internally contradicts itself)
    - MIGRATION.md:771-772 — explicit v1 → v2 rename rows: "[:route] (framework sub) → [:rf/route]" and "app-db [:route] (slice key) → app-db [:rf/route]"
    - Conventions.md:286 (substrate-adapter table — :rf/route reg-sub family)
    - Conventions.md:315 (rationale paragraph — :rf/route reg-sub family)

MIGRATION.md is particularly load-bearing: it asserts the v2 target IS :rf/route. So either MIGRATION is wrong (and Conventions+002+MIGRATION need updating) OR 012/Spec-Schemas/Runtime-Architecture/API.md are wrong (and they need updating).

Per the rf2-ljw6 brief's "if the corpus disagrees, HALT" rule, no spec edits made. Branch rf2-ljw6-conventions-route-fix created (clean), worktree torn down.

Sibling discrepancies (F22, F15) — corpus IS consistent, but withheld pending Mike's D1 call to keep the §Reserved namespaces table change as one logical sweep:

D2 (F22): Conventions:22 says "[:rf.machine <id>]" for the framework sub-id; corpus consensus is :rf/machine (single-segment kw): 005-StateMachines.md:1546 "(rf/reg-sub :rf/machine ...)"; API.md "(rf/sub-machine ...)" sugar over [:rf/machine ...]; 002-Frames.md:1105.

D3 (F15): Conventions:24 lists ":rf.route/url-changed" in the routing-events list; corpus consensus is :rf/url-changed: 012-Routing.md:32,301,531,595,712,847,926; API.md:124; Construction-Prompts.md:745,830,834,841; Conventions.md:286 (substrate-adapter row, internally contradicts the line-24 cell).

Recommendation: settle D1 first (Mike's call: prefix or no prefix on the route slice key), then a single sweep can fix D1+D2+D3 plus the spec(s) that are actually wrong.
close_reason: Stale in_progress cleanup — fix commits on origin/main per git log sweep 2026-05-13. Bead status was never flipped after PR merge.

title: Tool-Pair surface behaviour against destroyed frames is unspecified (epoch-history, restore-epoch, get-frame-db, register-epoch-cb)
description:
Audit rf2-ehpu finding F6, ranked top-2 most load-bearing.

Quote (audit-tool-pair line 28): 'Tool-Pair commits consumers to the pattern: "walks the epoch history of a frame, snapshot state at each point, restore an earlier state." It does NOT say what (rf/epoch-history frame-id), (rf/restore-epoch frame-id epoch-id), (rf/register-epoch-cb), or (rf/get-frame-db frame-id) do when called against a destroyed frame. 002 §destroy-frame defines :reason :frame-destroyed for dispatch/subscribe but does not extend the contract to the Tool-Pair query surface. A pair tool watching epochs while the user calls (rf/destroy-frame …) has no spec-defined behaviour to handle: nil? throw? empty vec? :rf.error/no-such-handler (kind :frame)? Tool-Pair must close this — it's the most likely "registration race" failure mode.'

Why load-bearing (audit-tool-pair §3 Top 2): re-frame2's multi-frame architecture makes frame churn ordinary — story tools mount and unmount frames freely; pair tools probe ephemeral test frames. A pair tool consuming epoch-history is guaranteed to encounter destroyed-frame races over enough wall-clock. The spec's silence forces consumers to discover behaviour empirically and likely diverge across implementations.

Pair2 corroboration (audit §5): runtime.cljs pair-dispatch-sync! (line 443+) has explicit failure-mode branching for 'no epoch recorded after dispatch' — depth=0, frame destroyed, interop/debug-enabled? false. Pair2 inferred them from the surface and built defensive logic that should have been specified.

Resolution per audit R-M (medium): add a §Tool-Pair surface against destroyed frames subsection; recommended shape — read APIs return nil (or empty vec for collection-shaped returns), mutating APIs throw :rf.error/no-such-handler (kind :frame), listeners stop receiving epoch records from destroyed frame on next emit boundary.
close_reason: Stale in_progress cleanup — fix commits on origin/main per git log sweep 2026-05-13. Bead status was never flipped after PR merge.

title: Tool-Pair source-coord attribute: "opaque + parseable" + missing schema + 3-spec format drift
description:
Audit rf2-ehpu finding F1, ranked top-1 most load-bearing.

Quote (audit-tool-pair line 23): 'Tool-Pair §Source-mapping says the attribute value is "the contract (an opaque string the tool parses to recover :ns / :line / :file — schema in [Spec-Schemas])". Three faults in one sentence: (a) "contract" + "opaque" is self-cancelling — either it's parseable (contract) or it's not (opaque); pair2's STATUS.md surfaces the same tension and resolves it pragmatically against the consumer's interest; (b) it lists :file as recoverable from the attribute, but Spec 006 §Attribute value format defines the format as <ns>:<sym>:<line>:<col> with no :file component (the <sym> is the registry handler-id, NOT the file); (c) the cited Spec-Schemas schema does not exist (grep source-coord against Spec-Schemas.md returns zero matches).'

Why load-bearing (audit-tool-pair §3): pair2's STATUS.md explicitly resolves the tension by parsing pragmatically and warning consumers it might break; intersects multiple specs (Tool-Pair, 006, Spec-Schemas) with three different shapes; every pair-shaped consumer that wants click-back-to-source — which is most of them — WILL parse the attribute. Cross-spec inconsistency report (audit-tool-pair §4 row 1): Tool-Pair line 116 says :ns/:line/:file recoverable; Spec 006 §Attribute value format says <ns>:<sym>:<line>:<col> (no :file). Severity: HIGH.

F9 also bundled (audit-tool-pair line 31): 'Tool-Pair line 54 promises :ns/:line/:file/:column on every registration's metadata. Spec 001 §Source-coordinate capture lists only :ns/:line/:file (no :column). Spec 006 §Capture mechanism captures :line/:column from (meta &form). Consumer reading 001's table writes a click-back resolver against (handler-meta) without expecting :column.' Same root cause: source-coord shape inconsistent across 001/006/Tool-Pair/Spec-Schemas.

Resolution per audit R-M (medium): pick (a) commit the format as part of the public contract — declare 4-segment shape in Tool-Pair, add Spec-Schemas entry for :rf/source-coord-attr, kill 'opaque' framing; OR (b) commit a parsing helper (rf/parse-source-coord attr-string). Either way, sync recovery list with Spec 006's actual format, and reconcile :column claim.
close_reason: Stale in_progress cleanup — fix commits on origin/main per git log sweep 2026-05-13. Bead status was never flipped after PR merge.

title: Spec 009 claims "each error category has a registered Malli :tags schema" but Spec-Schemas registers only 2 of ~25
description:
Audit rf2-ra02 finding F-9.

Quote (audit-009 line 53): 'Per-category :tags schemas are described as "registered as a Malli schema" (line 482) but only TWO are shown — HandlerExceptionTags and SchemaValidationTags (line 491–509) — under a "Conceptual; the actual registration mechanism is implementation-specific" caveat. Spec-Schemas does NOT register schemas for the other ~25 categories. Either (a) every category needs its :tags schema in Spec-Schemas (the natural home for them), or (b) 009 should drop the "each category has a registered Malli schema" claim and just say the example shape is illustrative. The spec promises "consumers can validate / branch on the payload safely" (line 430) but a consumer trying to validate, say, :rf.error/no-such-sub against a registered schema today would not find one.'

Audit confidence note (audit-009 line 208): 'I confirmed Spec-Schemas does not register them by searching for the category names directly. I did NOT check the implementation tree to see if register-app-schema! is called for each category at boot. If it is, the spec is just under-documented; if it isn't, this is a real coverage gap.'

Schema-coverage gap per audit-criterion #4: documented payload (the per-category :tags shape) has no entry in Spec-Schemas.

Resolution per audit R-M3: decide if 'every category has a registered Malli schema' is pattern requirement, CLJS-reference requirement, or aspirational. If pattern-level, Spec-Schemas needs the ~25 schemas. If CLJS-only, narrow 009 line 482's claim.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #154 — ~65 missing :tags schemas registered in Spec-Schemas (commit a6b8ea8).

title: Spec 005 has two precedence lists for transition resolution that disagree
description:
Audit rf2-goxn finding #9.

Quote (audit-005 line 24): 'Two precedence lists for transition resolution disagree. L211 (§Wildcard transitions): "state-local explicit → state-local :* → top-level explicit → top-level :*". L760 (§Transition resolution): "leaf up to root… explicit, :*, parent explicit, parent :*…". Real disagreement on whether a parent's :* fires before walking up to grandparent's explicit.'

Internal contradiction per audit-criterion: spec A says X, spec B says Y, both can't be right; an AI implementor following one would build something that diverges from the other.

Resolution per audit §5 medium: 'Reconcile the two precedence lists at L211 and L760. One canonical order (deepest explicit → deepest wildcard → walk up → root explicit → root wildcard) — remove the other.'
close_reason: Stale in_progress cleanup — fix commits on origin/main per git log sweep 2026-05-13. Bead status was never flipped after PR merge.

title: Spec 009 trace category coverage gap: ~25 emitted categories absent from §Error categories / §op-type vocabulary tables
description:
Audit rf2-ra02 findings F-2, F-3, F-5, F-6, F-7 plus rf2-8syd F13 and rf2-goxn #7, #8 — bundled because they all describe the same root cause: 009's §Error categories table and §op-type vocabulary section are incomplete relative to §Where trace emission lives and to actual runtime emit sites. 009 §Error contract opens (line 407) by claiming 'This section is the authoritative model for re-frame2's error taxonomy … the single source of truth for category names, payload shapes, and recovery defaults.' That claim is materially false today.

ra02 F-2 (most load-bearing): ~25 missing categories the runtime emits but 009 does not tabulate — :rf.error/no-such-fx (fx.cljc), :rf.error/frame-destroyed (router.cljc), :rf.error/flow-eval-exception (router.cljc), :rf.warning/route-shadowed-by-equal-score (routing.cljc), all :rf.http/* and :rf.warning/decode-defaulted from http_managed.cljc, plus EVERY :rf.error/machine-* category enumerated in spec/005 (machine-action-wrote-db, machine-grammar-not-in-v1, machine-unresolved-guard, machine-unresolved-action, machine-unhandled-event, machine-state-not-in-definition, machine-snapshot-version-mismatch, machine-raise-depth-exceeded, machine-always-depth-exceeded, machine-always-self-loop, machine-compound-state-missing-initial). The 'five distinct namespace prefixes' claim at line 434 is genuinely six once 014 is folded in (:rf.http/ is unmentioned).

ra02 F-3: Six :rf.epoch/restore-* categories (Tool-Pair lines 79–87) are not registered in 009's table. Tool-Pair canonicalises them; 009 also claims taxonomy authority. The two specs disagree on which is canonical.

ra02 F-5: :rf.machine/spawned and :rf.machine/destroyed (009 line 222 emit) are not in 009's §op-type vocabulary section, which lists only :rf.machine.lifecycle/created / destroyed. The same destroy event fires from two emit sites under two different operation names.

ra02 F-6: :rf.machine/system-id-bound and :rf.machine/system-id-released (Spec 005 §1203–1204) absent from 009. rf2-suue added the :rf.error/system-id-collision row but missed the symmetric -bound/-released events.

ra02 F-7: :rf.machine.microstep/transition (documented in Spec 005 §887–892, registered in Spec-Schemas §195/§202) absent from 009 §op-type AND §Where trace emission lives.

8syd F13: :rf.frame/drain-aborted mentioned only in 002 line 935 + 009 line 224, but not in 009 §Error categories table or Spec-Schemas. An AI implementor cannot tell op-type, :tags, or :recovery.

goxn #7: 005 lists :rf.machine/raised (L1960) — never emitted. 009 lists :rf.machine.lifecycle/destroyed — never emitted. Drop or wire.

goxn #8: machines.cljc:501 emits :rf.machine.timer/skipped-on-server but no spec lists it.

Resolution per ra02 R-M1: audit every emit site in §Where trace emission lives against the §Error categories table and §op-type vocabulary; file the missing rows. Mechanical work, not a design decision. Note: rf2-2s1o landed :rf.flow/* rows but did not address the broader gap.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #154 — Spec 009 trace category coverage sweep (~50 missing categories added) (commit 013c48f).

title: Spec 002 reg-frame metadata grammar drifts from Spec-Schemas / Spec 009 (ghost keys + enum drift)
description:
Audit rf2-8syd findings F1, F4, plus rf2-ra02 F-10 — three independent inline drifts from Spec-Schemas as canonical source.

F1 (audit-002 line 23): The reg-frame canonical grammar list does NOT include :on-error or :platform. Both are referenced as preset expansion keys (002 line 232–233) and :on-error is referenced again at 002 line 927. The :rf/frame-meta schema (Spec-Schemas line 896–897) carries both. 009 §Error-handler policy says :on-error is registered per-frame via reg-frame metadata (per [002-Frames §:on-error]) — but 002 has no §:on-error section. Quote: 'The reg-frame canonical grammar lists :doc :on-create :on-destroy :fx-overrides :interceptors :drain-depth :ns :line :file but does NOT list :on-error or :platform.' Resolution: 002 is incomplete; both other specs are correct. Add the keys to the canonical grammar.

F4 (audit-002 line 26): :source enum values disagree across three locations. 002 line 330 lists 5 values (:ui :timer :http :repl :machine); 002 line 366 lists 6 (adds :ssr-hydration); Spec-Schemas :rf/dispatch-envelope (line 94) ships 8 (:ui :timer :http :machine :repl :ssr-hydration :test :other). Resolution: replace 002's inline enumerations with a pointer to :rf/dispatch-envelope.

ra02 F-10 (audit-009): :operation is <kw-or-vec> per 009 §Core fields line 25, but :keyword per Spec-Schemas §:rf/trace-event line 183. Pick one (Spec-Schemas matches every consumer pattern).

Common root cause: 002 and 009 inline grammars/enums for fields whose canonical schemas live in Spec-Schemas, and the duplications drift. Fix: replace inline lists with pointers to Spec-Schemas, or lock the order and widen.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #155 — Spec 002 reg-frame metadata grammar aligned via cross-reference fixes (commit 567ba79).

title: Add :flow op-type + :rf.flow/* trace vocabulary to spec 009 + flows instrumentation (toolpair audit #3)
description: Decision (Mike 2026-05-09): yes — file and implement. Required for re-frame-10x v2 flow panel. Spec edits: 009 add :flow op-type; define :rf.flow/registered, :rf.flow/computed, :rf.flow/skip (value-equal recompute suppression per rf2-719e), :rf.flow/cleared, :rf.flow/failed. spec 013 (verify filename) new §Flow tracing cross-reference. Implementation: flows.cljc emits via (re-frame.trace/emit! :flow :rf.flow/<event> payload) — same pattern as machines.cljc:896. Tool-Pair.md: consumers register-trace-cb! filtering by :flow op-type. Tests: each lifecycle event fires; :rf.flow/skip semantics; conformance fixture flow-lifecycle-emits-traces (host-agnostic EDN). Sequencing: spec 009 also edited by rf2-suue and Performance API impl — different sections, merges cleanly.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #138 — :flow op-type + :rf.flow/* trace vocabulary shipped (commits 1770068, e2f0c2d, 653739c).

title: Performance API instrumentation for prod timing — behind a compile-time flag (toolpair audit #2)
description: Decision (Mike 2026-05-09): replace v1 span-shape (spec 009 already drops it) with browser Performance API. Debug builds: skip (browsers too noisy). Prod builds: behind a goog-define flag, default OFF, Closure DCE elides when off. No 10x v1 back-compat (v2 is rewrite). Documented in: spec/009-Instrumentation.md (new §Performance instrumentation), spec/Tool-Pair.md (consumption surface), spec/API.md (configure flag). Mechanism: (goog-define ^boolean re-frame.performance/enabled? false) + closure-defines toggle. Initial scope marks/measures: event dispatch, sub recompute, fx execution, render. Naming rf:event:<id>, rf:sub:<id>, rf:fx:<key>, rf:render:<frame-id>. Tests: helper round-trip; bundle-grep counter 0 calls under :advanced+flag=false; presence under flag=true; browser smoke for measure entries. Sequencing: spec 009 also edited by rf2-suue (small additive row) and the :flow op-type bead — different sections, merges cleanly.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #139 — Performance API instrumentation behind compile-time flag (commits 97bd915, 8ec6950, 263a0ff, 9227143).

title: Source-coord instrumentation for state machines (guards / actions / entry-exit / on-spawn / reg-machine site)
description: Gap (per Mike verification 2026-05-09): reg-machine is a defn, not a macro (machines.cljc:878). No source-coord stamping. Mirror rf2-z7f7 view pattern: turn reg-machine into a macro, capture &form metadata, walk machine spec at expansion to stamp :ns/:line/:column on each guard/action/entry/exit/on-spawn reference. Production-elision gated on goog.DEBUG. Spec edits: 005 §reg-machine + new §Source-coord stamping, 006 cross-ref, Tool-Pair.md, API.md. Tests: stamped meta returns correct coords; bundle-grep counter 0 under :advanced+DEBUG=false. Sequencing: rf2-suue is in flight editing machines.cljc; this bead may rebase against it (different sections, should merge cleanly).
close_reason: Resolved via PR #140 — source-coord stamping for guards/actions/entry-exit/on-spawn/reg-machine site (commits 7da95f6, d1db82d, 2794781, 6a1ca75).

title: Audit spec vs implementation for actionable drift
description: Audit the current /spec against /implementation and record each actionable difference as a possible necessary change. This bead is the audit container; once the comparison is complete, its description/notes will be updated with the concrete drift items, phrased as implementor-facing possible changes rather than mandatory fixes.
notes:
Audit result: filed child beads rf2-4o1c.1 through rf2-4o1c.5 for concrete spec/runtime drift found during the comparison.

New child beads:
- rf2-4o1c.1 - public machine lifecycle fx ids (`:rf.machine/spawn` / `:rf.machine/destroy`) vs implementation's registered ids (`:spawn` / `:destroy-machine`)
- rf2-4o1c.2 - dynamic machine spawn/destroy semantics promised by Spec 005 vs current trace-only runtime behaviour
- rf2-4o1c.3 - fragment-only routing trace op name drift (`:route.url/fragment-changed` vs `:rf.route/url-changed`)
- rf2-4o1c.4 - head-mismatch trace category drift (`:rf.warning/head-mismatch` vs `:rf.ssr/hydration-mismatch` + `:failing-id`)
- rf2-4o1c.5 - head hydration payload keys (`:rf/head`, `:rf/head-hash`) ignored by current `:rf/hydrate` runtime

Already tracked elsewhere; not duplicated here:
- rf2-0gfo - SSR nav-on-server prose vs `:rf.fx/skipped-on-platform`
- rf2-y509 - route-not-found / error-projector contradiction
close_reason: Audit completed; actionable drift filed as child beads.

title: Audit recent feature work for sufficient test coverage (pre-beta verification)
description:
## Scope

Walk PRs merged over the last couple of days and verify each added behaviour has sufficient test coverage. Pre-beta integrity check — gaps surface as follow-up beads to file before v0.0.1.beta release.

## What "sufficient" means here

- New behaviour has a happy-path test.
- Edge cases the spec calls out are tested (boundary conditions, error paths, lifecycle).
- Production-elision contracts are sentinel-tested if the surface is dev-only.
- Host-agnostic surfaces have conformance fixtures (EDN under `spec/conformance/` or wherever the project pattern lives).
- The test isn't just a smoke test of "doesn't crash" — it asserts the documented contract.

## Recent work to audit (chronological, last ~2 days)

### Per-feature artefact splits (rf2-5vjj Strategy B)
- **rf2-p7va schemas split** — first per-feature template.
- **rf2-xbtj machines split** (PR #129) — out-of-core machinery; bundle-isolation grep was 0 (with the strict pattern). Fixed a latent `:spawn` shadowing bug.
- **rf2-k682 routing split** (PR #130) — biggest single-PR bundle drop (−15.6 KB).
- **rf2-tfw3 flows split** (PR #132) — 2 unavoidable consumer-side keyword strings remained.
- **rf2-5kpd HTTP split** (PR #133) — bundle dropped −14.8 KB on counter; managed-http-counter has 12 distinct `:rf.http/*` keywords (full failure taxonomy).

For each split: are there permanent tests verifying:
1. Bundle-isolation grep stays clean (no regression if a future change accidentally re-imports the split-out namespace into core)?
2. Late-bind hooks raise the documented error when the artefact is absent from the classpath?
3. Late-bind hooks resolve correctly when the artefact IS on the classpath?

### Functional features
- **rf2-d0pi auto-inject** (Form-1 views auto-receive `dispatch` / `subscribe` arg). Tests covering: Form-1 happy path, Form-2 wrapping behaviour (does Form-2 still get auto-inject? per Reagent v2 — see feedback memory), Form-3 / not-supported error path?
- **rf2-sixo frame-provider Path 1** (React context for frame propagation). Tests covering: nested component frame inheritance, default-frame fallback, missing-context error path?
- **rf2-yl9n Form 2 canonical** + **rf2-heo2 Form 2 + view canonical, no get-view**. Tests covering the canonical pattern; non-canonical patterns rejected at expansion?
- **rf2-hbt1 data-direct contract** (qppo). Tests covering the contract surface?
- **rf2-piag render-key tuple** (t5tx). Tests covering the tuple shape and rendering?
- **rf2-een2 on-spawn data-relative** (smba). Tests covering `:on-spawn` callback receiving `(data, spawned-id)`, mutating data correctly, parent's data updated atomically?
- **rf2-xb5o event-arg split** (pz9f). Tests for the new arg shape?
- **rf2-z7f7 source-coord DOM annotation** (PR #135). Agent reported: 5 new SSR source-coord tests + 9 new CLJS DOM-injection tests + 18 sentinels in elision probe. Coverage: Form-1 capture, Form-2 wrapping, Fragment/interop fallback warning, production-elision, server-side rendering. Does the test suite cover all of these? Does the warning fire-once-per-id semantics have a test?

### Infra / process
- **rf2-0hxm substrate artefact split**. Bundle-isolation tests for the substrate artefact?
- **rf2-ace2 CI/CD restructure** (PR #134). Lockstep verifier has positive + negative tests. The topological deploy DAG itself is not exercisable without running an actual release — accept that and move on.
- **rf2-gqm7 examples restructure** (PR #131). Paths sweep, examples passing in CI. No new behaviour to test beyond the existing examples suite.
- **rf2-fc8i Information for AIs** (README). Documentation only, no tests needed.
- **rf2-7j0q namespace re-frame.core**. Test coverage assumed via existing core tests, but verify.
- **rf2-tbli alpha disposition**. Documentation/decision; verify no behaviour change snuck in.

## Process

For each item above:
1. Read the PR diff (`gh pr view <N> --json files,body`).
2. Identify the new behaviour added.
3. Check: is there a test in the diff (or a follow-up commit) that exercises this behaviour with assertions on the documented contract?
4. If yes — note "covered, [count] tests".
5. If no or partial — flag as a gap with sized recommendation:
   - Trivial gap (one missing case) → fold into a single follow-up bead "test gaps from rf2-XXXX..rf2-YYYY sweep".
   - Substantial gap (whole feature lacks coverage) → file a per-feature gap bead.

## Deliverable

Write findings to: `C:\Users\miket\code\re-frame2\findings\test-coverage-audit-2026-05-09.md`.

Findings doc format (~1500-2500 words):
- §1 Methodology — what was checked, what was excluded.
- §2 Coverage matrix — table mapping each PR → new behaviour → test status (covered / partial / missing).
- §3 Gap list — each gap sized + recommended bead title.
- §4 Top recommendations — which 3 gaps are most load-bearing.

Then post-back report (under 250 words):
1. Path to findings file.
2. Total gaps count + load-bearing count.
3. Top 3 gap-bead titles.
4. Approximate word count.

Do NOT file the gap-beads yet — Mike reviews findings and decides which to file (mirror the rf2-nfyf pattern).
Do NOT modify code. Do NOT branch. Do NOT open a PR.

## Disposition

P2 — pre-beta verification. Ideally completes before any further per-feature splits land so the test-coverage convention catches up to the impl velocity.
notes: 2026-05-09 bookkeeping: top-3 + sweep beads filed and merged: rf2-51x5 bundle-isolation CI gate (PR #149), rf2-5b6x late-bind missing-artefact coverage (PR #148), rf2-1e0n 3-arity guard coverage (PR #156), rf2-d4v7 reg-view test gaps sweep (PR #153), rf2-22ds frame-provider runtime React-context coverage (deferred). Audit doc itself remains open as reference material; close when Mike confirms all derived beads have been dispatched.
close_reason: Audit complete. Top-5 recommendations all actioned: rf2-51x5 (bundle-isolation CI gate, PR #149), rf2-5b6x (late-bind missing-artefact coverage, PR #148), rf2-1e0n (3-arity guard coverage, PR #156), rf2-d4v7 (reg-view test gaps sweep, PR #153), rf2-22ds (deferred 30d, blocked-by rf2-kdwc). Findings doc remains as reference at findings/test-coverage-audit-2026-05-09.md.

title: XState parity: final states / onDone / actor output / termination contract
description:
## XState concept

What it is in XState:
- `type: 'final'` states
- Parent `onDone` transitions when a child machine reaches a final state
- Top-level termination semantics — actor stops accepting events when done
- Final `output` value carried from completed child to parent

Sources:
- https://stately.ai/docs/final-states
- https://stately.ai/docs/output

## What it buys

- A standard completion story for child workflows.
- A built-in "this actor is done; stop sending it events" contract.
- A canonical result channel from child to parent.
- Cleaner async-child orchestration than ad-hoc success/failure event conventions.

## What re-frame2 already has

- Explicit terminal-state conventions via per-state `:meta`.
- Explicit `:raise` / `:dispatch` from a terminal state's `:entry`.
- Machine `:data` for result storage.

## Local references

- `spec/005-StateMachines.md` documents per-state `:meta` and notes final-state `onDone` as post-v1.

## Gap

- No runtime-level notion of "done".
- No standardised parent completion hook.
- No standardised output / result surface.

## Judgment

**High-value nice-to-have.** More broadly useful than parallel / history because many workflows naturally have completion (form submission, multi-step wizards, async fetch flows, modal task workflows).

## Decisions to surface (when this is picked up)

1. **Mark final state via `:meta` flag or a dedicated `:final?` key on the state spec?**
   - `:meta {:rf.machine/final? true}` — keeps spec 005's existing pattern for state metadata.
   - First-class `:final?` key — louder, easier to lint / verify, easier for AI-implementation to spot.
2. **Parent `:on-done` hook syntax.** Mirrors `:on-spawn`?
   ```clojure
   {:invoke {:machine-id :child/flow
             :on-done    (fn [data result] (assoc data :child-result result))}}
   ```
3. **Output value source.** Pulled from child's `:data` at termination time (entire `:data` or a designated key)? Computed by an `:output` fn? Both?
   ```clojure
   ;; option a — designated key
   {:meta {:rf.machine/final? true
           :rf.machine/output-key :result}}
   ;; option b — output fn
   {:meta {:rf.machine/final? true
           :rf.machine/output-fn (fn [data] (:result data))}}
   ```
4. **Termination semantics.** When a final state is entered:
   - Auto-destroy the spawned actor (current snapshot cleared, handler unregistered)?
   - Or just mark "done" and require explicit `[:rf.machine/destroy id]`?
   - Auto-destroy is more xstate-flavoured and avoids leaks; explicit-destroy is more re-frame-conservative.
5. **Stop-accepting-events contract.** Once done, dispatches to that machine should be silently dropped (with a trace) or raise an error?
6. **Trace events.** New: `:rf.machine/done` (with the output value), `:rf.machine/output-published`. Or fold into the existing `:rf.machine/destroyed`?
7. **Top-level (non-spawned, non-invoked) machine termination.** Is the contract symmetric — a singleton machine reaching a final state behaves the same way? Or is `:on-done` only meaningful for spawned/invoked actors with parents?
8. **Interaction with `:system-id` (rf2-suue / Option B).** When a system-id-bound actor reaches done and gets auto-destroyed, the `[:rf/system-ids]` reverse-index entry should clear too. Mechanical but should be specced.
9. **Schema impact.** `spec/Spec-Schemas.md` § machine-state spec gets a `:rf.machine/final?` row.
10. **Capability matrix axis.** spec 005 §Capability matrix actor-model axis — add `:actor/final-output` row.

## Disposition

P3 / post-v1 per current spec 005 note. But: if rf2-suue (Option B :system-id) lands first, and rf2-tijr / rf2-nfyf surface 10x v2 needing the trace events, this could promote earlier. Track here; revisit when those land.

## Recommendation (placeholder for Mike)

Adopt at the spec level. Implementation can wait for v1.1 unless 10x v2 / a real user flushes it forward. When implemented:
- Use a first-class `:final?` key on the state spec, not `:meta` (loud > silent for AI-implementation).
- Mirror `:on-spawn` with `:on-done` for parent hook (consistent with rf2-een2 / data-relative pattern).
- Provide both `:output-key` and `:output-fn` for the output source.
- Auto-destroy the spawned actor on done (xstate-flavoured, avoids leaks).
- Dispatches to a done actor → silently dropped + warn-level trace (matches re-frame's permissive defaults).

Open for redirect.
notes:
Locked design 2026-05-12 (Mike walked the 10 sub-decisions):

D1 — Final-state marker: first-class :final? key on the state map (NOT :meta — loud > silent).
D2 — Parent hook: :on-done on the parent's :invoke map, mirroring :on-spawn shape. Signature (fn [data result] ...) returning new data.
D3 — Output source: :output-key only on child's :final? state (designated key in :data). No :output-fn escape hatch.
D4 — Termination: auto-destroy actor on entering :final? state (synchronous, same tick as the :on-done fire).
D5 — Dispatches to done-then-destroyed actor: reuse existing destroyed-frame trace path. No new :rf.machine/dispatched-while-done half-state.
D6 — Trace events: NEW :rf.machine/done event (tags :machine-id, :output, :parent-id) + ENRICH existing :rf.machine/destroyed with :reason tag (:rf.machine/finished | :explicit | :parent-unmount-cascade etc).
D7 — Singleton symmetry: singleton (non-spawned, non-invoked) machine reaching :final? also auto-destroys. 'Final means final.' Footgun warning in skill leaf + spec/005: 'if you want a persistent terminal state, omit :final?'.
D8 — :system-id interaction: runtime auto-clears [:rf/system-ids <system-id>] reverse-index entry on done. Clears AFTER :on-done fires so the hook can still read it.
D9 — IMPLEMENT NOW (not deferred per spec/005's current post-v1 note). Spec AND impl in this delivery.
D10 — Capability matrix: ONE axis :fsm/final-states (naming matches existing :fsm/parallel-regions, :fsm/tags). Conformance fixture exercises the contract.

Scope of work this bead now covers:
1. spec/005-StateMachines.md — new §Final states section documenting D1-D10
2. spec/Spec-Schemas.md — :final? + :output-key on state-spec; :rf.machine/done event schema; :rf.machine/destroyed tags enriched with :reason
3. spec/009-Instrumentation.md — :rf.machine/done emit row
4. spec/API.md capability-matrix row — :fsm/final-states
5. implementation/machines/src/re_frame/machines.cljc — runtime support (final-state recognition, auto-destroy on :entry of :final? state, :on-done hook invocation with output, :system-ids reverse-index clear)
6. Conformance fixtures under spec/conformance/fixtures/ — one or more :fsm/final-states cases
7. implementation/.../test/ — JVM + CLJS regression tests
8. Conformance runner claims :fsm/final-states
9. skills/re-frame2/reference/state-machines/invoke.md updated to document :on-done; possibly new leaf or section on :final?

HOT-ZONE: The clarity batch rf2-5hnn + rf2-g1s1 + rf2-f9tu is currently in flight on machines.cljc (PR pending). rf2-gn80 must DISPATCH AFTER that PR lands to avoid merge conflict. Hold until #5hnn-batch-PR merges.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement Option B: :system-id named-machine addressing + finish spec 005 spawn-fx live-handler wiring (resolves rf2-ecv4)
description:
## Decision (from rf2-ecv4)

Adopt Option B: opt-in `:system-id` on `:invoke` / `[:spawn ...]` plus `(rf/machine-by-system-id sid)` lens. Decline Option C (hierarchical system primitive).

## Scope

### Surface additions

| Surface | Owner |
|---|---|
| `:system-id` key on `:invoke` / `[:spawn ...]` spec | spec 005 |
| `[:rf/system-ids]` reserved app-db slot | spec 002 (alongside `[:rf/machines]`) |
| `(rf/machine-by-system-id sid)` lens | spec 005 §Querying machines |
| Optional `(rf/dispatch-to-system sid event)` sugar | spec 005 |
| Trace event `:rf.machine/system-id-bound` (and `-released`) | spec 009 |
| Error category `:rf.error/system-id-collision` | spec 009 |
| Late-bind hook `:machines/machine-by-system-id` | core late-bind |

### Spec changes

- `spec/005-StateMachines.md`:
  - §Spawn-spec keys → add `:system-id` row.
  - New §Named addressing via :system-id (cross-linked from §Spawning).
  - §Querying machines → add `(rf/machine-by-system-id sid)`.
  - New §Cross-machine messaging by name.
  - §Capability matrix actor-model axis → add `:actor/system-id` row, claimed by v1 CLJS reference.
- `spec/002-Frames.md` §What lives in a frame → add `[:rf/system-ids]` slot.
- `spec/Spec-Schemas.md` §`:rf.fx/spawn-args` → add `:system-id` to schema.
- `spec/009-Instrumentation.md` §Error contract → add `:rf.error/system-id-collision`.
- `spec/Conventions.md` reserved app-db slots → list `[:rf/system-ids]`.
- `spec/MIGRATION.md` → additive O-N entry (next O-number).

### Implementation (in `implementation/machines/`)

- Extend `spawn-fx` (machines.cljc:962) to:
  1. Update `[:rf/system-ids]` reverse-index when `:system-id` present.
  2. Emit trace event `:rf.machine/system-id-bound` if bound.
  3. Warn-on-rebind if collision: emit `:rf.error/system-id-collision`, last-write-wins for the index update (matches re-frame's existing re-registration semantics).
- Extend `destroy-machine-fx` (machines.cljc:975) to clear the `[:rf/system-ids]` entry and emit `:rf.machine/system-id-released`.
- Add `machine-by-system-id` fn in `re-frame.machines`.
- Late-bind hook `:machines/machine-by-system-id` in `late_bind.cljc`.
- Re-export `re-frame.core/machine-by-system-id` (and optional `dispatch-to-system` sugar) via the late-bind shim.

### IMPORTANT: Discovered precondition (from rf2-ecv4 §2 footnote)

`spawn-fx` and `destroy-machine-fx` (`machines.cljc:962, 975`) currently emit traces but **do not register a handler or initialise a snapshot**. The full lifecycle described in spec 005 §Spawning is partially-realised: id allocation runs, `:on-spawn` writes the id into parent's `:data`, traces fire — but the spawned actor as a *live event handler* isn't actually wired up yet.

**Bundle this work.** Per the rf2-ecv4 finding: "Adding `:system-id` *while* the live-handler wiring is being built is one sweep; later is two." Implement the live-handler wiring as part of this bead so end-to-end testing of `:system-id` resolves to a real running actor.

If the wiring gap is too large to bundle, halt and file a separate bead — but the expectation is bundle.

### Conformance fixtures

- `spawn-with-system-id-then-lookup-resolves` (Spec/conformance pattern — host-agnostic EDN).
- `spawn-without-system-id-leaves-index-empty`.
- `destroy-machine-clears-system-id-index`.
- `system-id-collision-warns-and-rebinds`.

### Tests

- JVM unit: index population, lookup resolution, collision warn, destroy clear.
- CLJS unit: same as JVM (host-agnostic).
- Integration / browser-test: spawn-with-system-id → dispatch-by-system-id reaches the live actor (this exercises the bundled wiring fix).

## Verification

- All artefact test suites green.
- Bundle-isolation grep: examples/counter (no machines used) MUST NOT contain `system-id` strings.
- Production-elision probe: the optional `dispatch-to-system` sugar should DCE-fold cleanly under `:advanced` if unused.

## Sizing

Per rf2-ecv4 §5: 1–2 days for the system-id surface alone. The live-handler wiring bundle adds undetermined scope — the agent reports back if it needs to halt.

## Disposition

Pre-1.0. Direction set; ready to dispatch.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #136 — :system-id named-machine addressing + spawn-fx live-handler wiring shipped (commits 7fc9dfa, bd12c1c, ab92359).

title: Implement multi-artefact CI/CD: topological deploy + lockstep version verification + release-process doc (resolves rf2-w05l)
description:
## Decision (from rf2-w05l)

- Lockstep versions through 1.0 (single root VERSION file).
- Topological-order deploy in release.yml.
- realworld as canonical integration test.
- Reagent canonical substrate; UIx/Helix smoke-tested when landing.
- MIGRATION.md stays flat (M-1..M-NN) through 1.0.

## Implementation tasks

### .github/workflows/release.yml

1. Order deploy jobs: core → schemas → reagent → machines → routing → flows → http → (ssr, epoch when split).
2. Each per-artefact deploy `needs: [previous-artefacts]` so a mid-release failure halts before publishing dependent artefacts to Clojars.
3. Pre-deploy verification job: assert all artefact deps.edn files reference a single VERSION (lockstep contract). Fail if any artefact has drifted.
4. Add a clear failure-recovery comment block at the top: \"If a partial release lands on Clojars, re-tag with vX.Y.Z+1 and re-run; do not attempt to yank — Clojars doesn't support it.\"

### .github/workflows/test.yml

5. Sanity check: verify per-artefact test jobs run in parallel (no false `needs:` dependencies that serialise unnecessarily).
6. Optional: add a 'lockstep version verification' job that runs on every PR (cheap, catches drift before release).

### Documentation

7. Create docs/release-process.md (or extend spec/Conventions.md §Packaging conventions) covering:
   - Tag format: vX.Y.Z (or v0.0.1.beta.N for beta).
   - Trigger: tag push triggers release.yml.
   - Order of deploys (topological DAG diagram).
   - Pre-flight checklist: all tests green on main, VERSION file updated, MIGRATION.md entry added.
   - Recovery: if Clojars publish fails mid-deploy, what to do.
8. Update spec/Conventions.md §Packaging conventions: add 'Lockstep versioning through 1.0' as an explicit row.

### Cross-artefact integration test note

9. Add a comment to examples/realworld/README.md noting it serves as the canonical multi-artefact integration test. This isn't a code change — it's making the canonical-test status legible to future contributors.

### Substrate matrix note

10. Add a note to spec/Conventions.md §Substrate-adapter shipping convention (or wherever substrate-adapter strategy lives): 'Reagent is the canonical substrate; full test suite runs against Reagent. UIx and Helix adapters are smoke-tested only.'

## Verification

- Trigger a dry-run release (push a vX.Y.Z-rc.1 tag to a fork, watch the deploy DAG fire in correct order).
- Verify lockstep version check fails when artefact deps.edn is intentionally desynced (negative test).
- Inspect rendered release-process.md.

## Dependencies

- Wait for rf2-5kpd (HTTP split, in flight) to land — same release.yml file.
- Ideally wait for rf2-uo7v (SSR split) and rf2-lt4e (epoch split) too, so the topological DAG is complete in one pass — but those aren't in flight yet.

## Disposition

Pre-beta blocker. Schedule after rf2-5kpd merges; possibly delay further to bundle with ssr+epoch splits if those are imminent.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #134 — topological deploy DAG, lockstep verification, release-process.md, substrate-matrix all merged (commits 436baf6, f5fa6ec).

title: CI/CD strategy for multi-artefact release coordination
description:
## Context

The Strategy B per-feature artefact split (rf2-5vjj) has produced multiple Maven artefacts that need coordinated CI/CD:
- day8/re-frame-2 (core)
- day8/re-frame-2-schemas (rf2-p7va)
- day8/re-frame-2-machines (rf2-xbtj)
- day8/re-frame-2-routing (rf2-k682)
- day8/re-frame-2-flows (rf2-tfw3)
- day8/re-frame-2-http (rf2-5kpd, in flight)
- ...with ssr (rf2-uo7v), epoch (rf2-lt4e), reagent (already split via rf2-0hxm) still to come

Each per-feature split PR has incrementally added a per-artefact test job to .github/workflows/test.yml and a per-artefact deploy entry to .github/workflows/release.yml. The bigger picture has not been thought through.

## Questions to answer

### Versioning strategy

1. **Lockstep vs independent versions.** Do all artefacts share a single VERSION (current pattern — each per-feature deps.edn reads ../../VERSION), or can each version independently?
   - Lockstep: simpler for users, simpler for changelog, simpler for compatibility matrix. Cost: every artefact ships even when only one changed.
   - Independent: each artefact moves at its own cadence. Cost: compatibility matrix to maintain (re-frame-2-routing 1.4.0 with re-frame-2 1.5.0 — does it work?), bigger user mental model.
2. **Pre-1.0 vs 1.0+ semantics.** Maybe lockstep through the v0.0.1.beta → v1.0 stretch, then independent post-1.0? Or commit to one model now?

### Release coordination

3. **Order of publish.** core → schemas → machines → reagent → routing → flows → http? Some have inter-dependencies (machines depends on core; reagent depends on core). The release workflow needs a topological deploy or it'll break Clojars resolution mid-release.
4. **Atomic-ish releases.** If publish to Clojars succeeds for core but fails for schemas mid-release, what's the rollback story? Clojars doesn't support yank. Options: tag-then-publish (re-tag if a publish fails), publish-from-snapshot-then-promote, or accept that partial-release-recovery is manual.
5. **Beta vs stable channel.** v0.0.1.beta releases are fine to ship loose. Once we're 1.0, the release workflow needs to gate on something stronger — all-tests-green plus reviewer-approval, presumably.

### Testing strategy

6. **Cross-artefact integration tests.** Currently per-artefact tests run via `clojure -M:test` from each artefact's own dir, plus the top-level CLJS browser/elision/examples suites. Where do cross-artefact integration tests live? E.g. \"machines + routing + http used together in a realistic app\" — that's the realworld example today, but is it the canonical integration test or just a demo?
7. **Per-substrate matrix.** When UIx (rf2-3yij) and Helix (rf2-2qit) substrate adapters land, the test matrix multiplies. Do we run the full suite per substrate? Or pick one canonical (Reagent) and smoke-test the others?

### Consumer upgrade story

8. **Migration guide structure.** spec/MIGRATION.md is currently flat (M-1, M-2, ... M-31). For multi-artefact consumers, do we group by artefact (M-routing-1, M-machines-3) or keep flat? Once independent versions land, flat doesn't work.
9. **What does \"upgrade re-frame-2\" mean for a consumer with 4 of our artefacts?** Single bundle update or 4 separate steps?

### Tooling

10. **deps.edn template for new consumers.** A consumer wanting routing + flows + http + schemas + machines reads /implementation/core/deps.edn or just adds them all by hand. Is the README's \"Adding to your project\" section keeping pace with each split?
11. **lein support.** Does any of this work for lein consumers, or is this deps.edn-only?

## Disposition

This is a P2 decision bead — needs input before v0.0.1.beta release. Each split PR has been adding to CI/CD piecemeal; the meta-strategy needs to be set before the release workflow runs in anger.

## Recommendation (placeholder for Mike)

My initial leaning:
- **Lockstep versions through 1.0**, revisit independent for 1.1+. Simpler users-facing story for the early adopter window.
- **Topological-order deploy** with explicit dependency DAG in the release workflow. Hard-code for now, automate later.
- **realworld example as canonical integration test**, augment with a few cross-artefact unit tests as gaps surface.
- **Reagent as the canonical substrate**, smoke-test UIx/Helix when they land.

Open for Mike to redirect.
notes:
---

## Decision (2026-05-09, Mike "agree")

Adopt the recommended disposition:

1. **Lockstep versions through 1.0.** Revisit independent versioning post-1.0. All per-feature artefacts continue reading from the single root VERSION file via `:local/root "../core"`.
2. **Topological-order deploy.** core → schemas → reagent → machines → routing → flows → http (and ssr, epoch when split). Hard-code the DAG in .github/workflows/release.yml using `needs:` linkages.
3. **realworld example as canonical integration test.** Augment with a few cross-artefact unit tests when gaps surface.
4. **Reagent as the canonical substrate.** UIx (rf2-3yij) and Helix (rf2-2qit) adapters smoke-tested when they land; full suite is Reagent.
5. **MIGRATION.md stays flat (M-1..M-NN) through 1.0.** Restructure when independent versioning lands post-1.0.

## Implementation

Filed under separate impl bead — see linked.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #134 — multi-artefact CI/CD strategy implemented (topological deploy DAG, lockstep version verification, release-process doc). Companion bead rf2-ace2 covers the impl side.

title: Bundle: split schemas (Malli) into day8/re-frame-2-schemas (rf2-5vjj feature 1)
description:

title: Pre-document packaging conventions in spec corpus (rf2-5vjj prerequisite)
description:
Lock in the multi-artefact packaging architecture as normative in the spec corpus, BEFORE any per-feature split lands.

## Edits

### spec/Conventions.md — new §Packaging conventions

Document:
- re-frame2 ships as multiple Maven artefacts.
- Core artefact (day8/re-frame-2): registry, drain, fx, dispatch, subscribe, frame-provider, trace.
- Per-feature artefacts (day8/re-frame-2-<feature-id>): optional capabilities. feature-id matches the spec topic (machines, flows, routing, http, ssr, schemas, epoch).

Cross-links to Conventions §Packaging.

Each surface row gains the artefact that ships it. For features not yet split (everything currently in core except substrate adapters), mark as 'core (split pending: rf2-NN)'.

### README.md Project Layout

Update the directory tree to show the per-artefact structure (currently shows implementation/core, implementation/reagent post-rf2-0hxm).

## Out of scope

- DON'T do any code splits in this bead. Doc-only.
- DON'T touch the README narrative.
- The packaging convention is documented as the norm; per-feature splits land in their own beads.

## Dispatch ordering

Land BEFORE any per-feature split bead. Establishes the rule before code lands.

## Closes / related

- Pre-work for rf2-5vjj.
- Each per-feature split bead inherits this convention as its target.
