title: Implement reg-view as defn-shaped macro with auto-inject + reg-view* escape hatch (resolves rf2-3442)
description:
Replace the current reg-view shape with a defn-shaped macro that auto-defs the symbol, auto-injects dispatch/subscribe, and compile-errors on non-literal-fn bodies. Add reg-view* as the public underlying fn for metaprogramming.

## Final API shape

### re-frame.core/reg-view (macro)

  (reg-view sym [args] body)
  (reg-view sym docstring [args] body)
  (reg-view ^{:rf/id ...} sym [args] body)   ;; metadata id override

- Auto-derives id from (keyword (str *ns*) (str sym)).
- Optional ^{:rf/id :explicit/id} metadata on the symbol overrides.
- Auto-injects 'dispatch' and 'subscribe' as lexical bindings in the body.
- Compile-time error if body is not a literal (fn ...) / (fn* ...) form.
  Form-1 (literal fn returning hiccup) and Form-2 (literal fn returning a fn)
  are supported; Form-3 (create-class) is out of scope — error message points
  the user to reg-view*.
- Defs the symbol to a frame-aware fn AND registers in the views registry under the id.

### re-frame.core/reg-view* (fn)

  (reg-view* id render-fn)

- Plain fn. Takes an id keyword and a render fn of any shape.
- No auto-def, no auto-inject, no compile-check. Users who want frame-bound
  dispatch/subscribe inside their render fn capture explicitly with
  (rf/dispatcher) / (rf/subscriber).
- Public surface for metaprogramming: computed ids, runtime composition, library
  generation, registration without a Var.

## Macro sketch

  (defmacro reg-view
    {:arglists '([sym args body] [sym docstring args body])}
    [sym & more]
    (let [{:keys [docstring args body]} (parse-args sym more)
          id-meta (-> sym meta :rf/id)
          id      (or id-meta (keyword (str *ns*) (str sym)))]
      (when-not (and (seq? body) (#{'fn 'fn*} (first body)))
        (throw (ex-info "reg-view body must be a literal fn (Form-1 or Form-2). Use reg-view* for non-literal-fn registrations."
                        {:id id :sym sym :body body})))
      `(do
         (reg-view* ~id
           (fn ~sym ~args
             (let [~'dispatch  ((resolve 'rf/dispatcher))
                   ~'subscribe ((resolve 'rf/subscriber))]
               ~body)))
         (def ~sym ~@(when docstring [docstring])
           (views/lookup-view ~id)))))

(Sketch — actual macro must use proper namespaced unquoting, not (resolve ...) — and place dispatch/subscribe in the lexical scope of the body, not at outer call time. Reference the lexical-scoping argument from the rf2-3442 discussion.)

## Compile-time check details

The check fires when (first body) is not 'fn or 'fn*. Detected cases the error message must address:

- Var-ref body: (reg-view foo my-render) — body is a symbol.
- create-class body: (reg-view foo (reagent.core/create-class {...})) — body is a call form whose head is not 'fn.
- Computed body: (reg-view foo (some-fn-returning-a-fn)) — body is a non-fn call.

Error message template: "reg-view body must be a literal fn (Form-1 or Form-2). Got: <type>. For runtime registration, use (re-frame.core/reg-view* :id render-fn)."

## Example sweep

Convert all view definitions in examples/ to defn-shaped reg-view, drop explicit (rf/dispatcher) / (rf/subscriber) captures, drop pass-through dispatch/subscribe args, drop explicit registry-id keywords:

- examples/counter/core.cljs
- examples/flight-booker/core.cljs
- examples/nine-states/core.cljs
- examples/routing/core.cljs
- examples/temperature/core.cljs
- examples/timer/core.cljs
- examples/todomvc/views.cljs (root-view, plus convert task-entry / task-list / todo-item / footer-controls if appropriate — they were sub-views without their own reg-view in PR #51; under the new model they could become reg-view'd or stay plain Reagent fns)

For TodoMVC specifically: the recent PR #51 used capture-and-pass to thread dispatch/subscribe through sub-views. With auto-inject available, that thread-through becomes unnecessary — sub-views can be plain Reagent fns again calling the auto-injected names directly, OR can become their own reg-view's. Either pattern works post-this-bead; pick whichever reads cleaner.

## Guide-prose sweep

- docs/guide/02 §The view — update example shape; drop "use (rf/dispatcher) to capture" caveat.
- docs/guide/04 §The Var-reference idiom — update; flag that reg-view body must be a literal fn.
- docs/guide/* — sweep for any 'reg-view :keyword (fn [] ...)' shape and convert.
- One new short subsection: "reg-view vs reg-view*" explaining when to use each.

## Tests

- implementation/test/re_frame/views_macros_test.* (or wherever reg-view is tested):
  - reg-view auto-defs the symbol
  - id auto-derived from *ns* + symbol
  - ^{:rf/id} metadata override works
  - dispatch and subscribe are lexically bound and resolve to current frame
  - Form-2 closure case works (inner fn closes over the lexical bindings)
  - Compile-time error on Var-ref body, create-class body, computed body
  - Compile error message matches the documented template
- Conformance: add a fixture for the auto-id derivation and the literal-fn requirement.

## Breaking-change migration

This is a breaking change to the existing reg-view shape. Pre-1.0; acceptable.

Migration note in CHANGELOG / MIGRATION.md:

- (def my-view (reg-view :ns/my-view (fn [] body))) becomes (reg-view my-view [] body).
- (def my-view (reg-view :explicit/id (fn [] body))) becomes (reg-view ^{:rf/id :explicit/id} my-view [] body).
- For computed ids or library generation, switch to (reg-view* id render-fn).

## Closes / related

- Resolves rf2-3442 (decision: auto-inject + defn-shape).
- Removes the substrate of rf2-562e (silent auto-def from :*/root keyword no longer happens; defs are now explicit user-written symbols).
- Touches every file already touched by PR #51 (TodoMVC capture-and-pass becomes unnecessary). Coordinate sequencing: this bead lands AFTER PR #51 merges, then sweeps PR #51's threading away.
- The Reagent v2 directive (rf2-25aq) constrains scope to Form-1 + Form-2; Form-3 is explicitly out and points users at reg-view*.
notes:
## Updated scope (2026-05-08)

### Example sweep — full enumeration

Verification step (mandatory after sweep): grep -rn 'reg-view' examples/ — every match must be defn-shaped or a deliberately-flagged metaprogramming case using reg-view*.

- DO NOT change reg-view* to be macro-shaped. It's a plain fn by design — the metaprogramming surface for users with computed ids or non-literal-fn bodies.

title: TodoMVC: decide persistence demo — keep localStorage or graft onto :rf.http/managed (Spec 014)
description:
TodoMVC currently persists via reg-fx :todo.storage/save / reg-cofx :todo.storage/todos against localStorage. Spec 014 just landed and is the canonical request shape for re-frame2. The TodoMVC example as the smallest 'real app' is a natural surface to demo Spec 014 — but it's also natural to keep localStorage for offline-first simplicity.

Options:
- A: Keep localStorage. Add a one-line comment explaining the v1 TodoMVC tradeoff. realworld becomes the canonical Spec-014 demo.
- B: Replace localStorage with :rf.http/managed against a stub server (or :rf.http/managed-canned-success in tests). TodoMVC becomes the canonical Spec-014 demo.
- C: Both. Add a parallel examples/todomvc-http/ that uses Spec 014, leave examples/todomvc/ as-is.

Mike's decision needed before any implementation work. None of the options are spec-compliance gaps.
notes: Decision (Mike, 2026-05-08): Option A — keep localStorage. realworld (rf2-w3vn phase 3) becomes the canonical Spec-014 demo. Added a one-line note to examples/todomvc/README.md (and a brief comment in db.cljs near the localStorage code) explaining the choice; folded into PR for rf2-94s1/rf2-pu9q/rf2-24or via SendMessage to background agent.

title: TodoMVC: replace hand-rolled hashchange router with Spec 012 routing
description:
core.cljs:install-router! does (.addEventListener js/window 'hashchange' ...) and dispatches :todo/url-changed manually. Spec 012 (Routing) is the canonical re-frame2 surface for exactly this — :rf.route/handle-url-change, registered routes, :rf.route/id in app-db.

Action: remove the install-router! / current-hash bookkeeping from core.cljs and the :todo/url-changed event from events.cljs. Replace with Spec 012 route registrations:
- #/         -> :showing :all
- #/active   -> :showing :active
- #/completed -> :showing :completed
- #!/active / #!/completed accepted

The router-installed? defonce becomes unnecessary.

Spec compliance: TodoMVC requirements 34-41 still satisfied via Spec 012's hash-route mode.

Affected files:
- examples/todomvc/core.cljs (drop install-router! + current-hash + :todo/url-changed dispatch)
- examples/todomvc/events.cljs (drop :todo/url-changed; replace with :rf.route route handlers)
- examples/todomvc/db.cljs (no change)

Reference Spec 012 for exact API surface.
notes: PR #51: commit 19a0d38 on todomvc-review-fixes. Awaiting merge. Note: Spec 012 is path-based, but TodoMVC's canonical URLs are hash-based (Playwright contract: href=#/active). Wired up via three reg-route entries (/, /active, /completed) plus a tiny host-adapter in core.cljs that strips '#' (and optional '!') from window.location.hash before dispatching :rf.route/handle-url-change. Pattern parallels examples/routing/ which uses popstate. Adapter is 4 lines.

title: TodoMVC: convert sub-views to frame-aware (rf/dispatcher)/(rf/subscriber) capture
description:
TodoMVC's only reg-view'd component is root-view; task-entry, task-list, todo-item, footer-controls are plain Reagent fns calling rf/dispatch and rf/subscribe directly. This works for a single-frame app but doesn't model the canonical multi-frame-aware shape.

Action: convert sub-views to capture-based subscribers/dispatchers. Two options:
- Option A: keep the views as plain Reagent fns, but capture (rf/dispatcher) and (rf/subscriber) at the call site and close over them.
- Option B: convert each sub-view to a reg-view (Form-1) and rely on whatever rf2-3442 lands as.

Prefer A unless rf2-3442 has resolved to auto-inject (then B). Either way, eliminate bare rf/dispatch / rf/subscribe calls inside view bodies.

Spec compliance: not affected. Idiomatic example quality: improved.

Affected files:
- examples/todomvc/views.cljs (task-entry, task-list, todo-item, footer-controls)
notes: PR #51: commit f28f3a3 on todomvc-review-fixes. Awaiting merge.
close_reason: Stale in_progress cleanup — fix commits on origin/main per git log sweep 2026-05-13. Bead status was never flipped after PR merge.

title: Add TodoMVC example under examples/
description: Add a TodoMVC example application under examples/, aligned with the current official TodoMVC behavior/markup and informed by the original re-frame v1 TodoMVC implementation. Include a shadow-cljs example build, a hand-written host page, any local assets needed for stable rendering, and a Playwright headless-browser smoke/spec that verifies core TodoMVC interactions (add, complete, edit, filter, clear completed, remaining count). Audit the example against the current re-frame2 runtime and file follow-up beads for any unrelated implementation gaps uncovered during the work.

title: Phase 3 examples: 7guis 05-07 + realworld + login (rf2-lyj0 follow-up)
description:
Follow-up to rf2-lyj0 Phase 1+2.

Phase 1+2 covers: counter, temperature (7guis #2), flight-booker (7guis #3),
timer (7guis #4), nine-states, routing.

Phase 3 — remaining examples to run + smoke test:

7GUIs:

Other:

   pointing at the example's run fn.
2. Add a hand-written index.html host page next to the source.

Known blockers / dependencies:

  Apply the same `react-root` rename used in nine-states and routing.

  same workarounds Phase 1+2 used).

When this is done, all examples under examples/ have a runnable
browser surface and a Playwright smoke covering their headline
behaviour.

title: Examples colliding defonce root with reg-view :*/root — name collision overwrites React root
description:
Discovered while implementing rf2-lyj0.

When an example registers a view with id ending in `:root` — e.g.
`(reg-view :ui.view/root ...)` or `(reg-view :app/root ...)` — the
reg-view macro auto-defs a local Var with `(name id)` as its symbol,
i.e. `(def root ...)`. If the same namespace also has
`(defonce root (rdc/create-root ...))` for the React mount point,
the LATER def silently overwrites the React root with the wrapped view
fn (the macro expands to a top-level def that runs after the defonce).

At run time, `(rdc/render root [view])` then attempts to call
`view-fn.render(...)` and fails with TypeError: root.render is not
a function.

Affected examples in this repo (with namespace-load-time `(def
root ...)` from a `:*/root` view):
  - examples/nine_states/core.cljs    (:ui.view/root)
  - examples/routing/core.cljs        (:app/root)
  - examples/login/core.cljs          (:auth.login/root)
  - examples/realworld/core.cljs      (:app/root)
  - examples/ssr/core.cljc            (:app/root)  — JVM-only, may be unaffected

The rf2-lyj0 patch renames the React mount in nine_states + routing to
`react-root`. Login + realworld are deferred to follow-ups.

Possible global fix candidates:
1. Document the collision in the reg-view macro's docstring; recommend
   not naming root views with `:*/root`.
2. Have reg-view's auto-def emit a guard (e.g. throw if the symbol is
   already bound to something that isn't a view fn).
3. Rename React mount points to `react-root` everywhere (bigger blast
   radius; cleaner long-term).
notes:
Substrate change: under the rf2-3442 resolution (reg-view becomes defn-shaped, auto-defs from explicit user-written symbol, id auto-derived from *ns* + symbol), the silent auto-def from :*/root keyword no longer happens. The collision shape (defonce root + reg-view :ns/root) becomes user-explicit (defonce root + reg-view root [] ...) — a normal Clojure double-def visible at the call site. Verify after the reg-view implementation lands; close if the substrate is genuinely gone.
Verified after rf2-d0pi merged. Grep across examples/: no file has both (defonce root ...) AND (reg-view root ...) with the colliding 'root' symbol. Examples that have a :*/root view use 'root-view' as the symbol; React mounts use either 'root' or 'react-root'. The silent auto-def-from-keyword substrate is gone (defn-shape macro requires user-explicit symbol). Closed as fixed: substrate-removed and no residual user-collision shape exists in-tree.

title: Examples: make all runnable in browser + Playwright smoke tests for each
description:
Mike (2026-05-08): every example under examples/ should be runnable in a browser AND smoke-tested via Playwright that the user-visible behaviour works.

## Examples to cover

Simple:
- counter — click +/- buttons; number changes; isolated frame works.
- login — auth state machine drives form transitions.
- nine_states — page progresses through the nine documented UI states.
- routing — URL navigation updates the active route.

7GUIs (six):
- 02_temperature — typing C updates F (and vice versa).
- 03_flight_booker — flight-type selector + dates produce a valid booking.
- 04_timer — gauge advances; reset works.
- 05_crud — create / update / delete a name.
- 06_circle_drawer — click to add a circle; undo/redo restores prior state.
- 07_cells — typing a formula updates dependent cells.

Composite (largest, may be a follow-up):
- realworld — full Realworld SPA. Login, article view, comment post.

JVM-side (skip — no browser surface):
- ssr — runs against the JVM, not browser. Already covered by ssr_end_to_end_test.clj.

## Deliverable

Per example:
1. shadow-cljs :browser build target in implementation/shadow-cljs.edn (or a separate examples shadow-cljs.edn). Output to a dedicated dir; serve from a static-server in CI.
2. An HTML host page (examples/NAME/index.html or similar) with a #app mount point div.
3. The example's source itself fixed if it's currently broken (temperature is known to be missing :on-create wiring per the earlier audit; check each).
4. A Playwright spec at examples/NAME/playwright.spec.cjs (or one consolidated suite) that drives the example's user-visible behaviour and asserts the result.
5. An npm run test:examples script that compiles + serves + runs Playwright across all examples.
6. A GitHub Actions job examples-browser parallel to the existing cljs-browser / cljs-elision jobs.

## Constraints

- Examples target Reagent v2 per Mike's directive (rf2-25aq); use the v2 idioms.
- No Claude attribution on commits or PRs.
- If an example has a real impl bug, file a bd create follow-up rather than masking.
- Playwright Chrome only (Mike: Just chrome is fine).

## Scope guidance

This is a substantial deliverable. If it doesn't fit one PR, split:
- Phase 1: infrastructure + counter (bootstrap the pattern).
- Phase 2: simple examples (login, routing, nine_states, 7guis 02-04).
- Phase 3: harder 7guis (05, 06, 07) and realworld.

Use bd create to file follow-up beads if you split.

## Verification

- npm run test:examples runs locally and reports pass/fail per example.
- The new CI job is green.
- Each example's user-visible behaviour is asserted (not just it compiles).
close_reason: PR #50 merged. Phases 1+2 (6 examples). Phase 3 deferred to rf2-w3vn.

title: Spec: define :render-key identity (Reagent component instance vs reg-view id)
description:
Tool-Pair audit (Tool-Pair §Time-travel epoch-record) added :renders [{:render-key <id> :triggered-by <sub-id-or-nil> :elapsed-ms <num>}] to :rf/epoch-record. The audit punted on what :render-key actually identifies. Two candidates:

(a) The reg-view registration id (:counter, :cart.item/row, etc.) — coarse-grained; multiple instances of the same view share a key. Easy to relate back to source via (handler-meta :view id).

(b) A per-Reagent-component-instance opaque token — fine-grained; lets the pair tool distinguish 'instance 3 of :cart.item/row re-rendered' from 'instance 7 did'. Requires the runtime to mint a stable id per mount.

Decision needs to land before Tool-Pair impl beads (rf2-shjf, rf2-smee) can populate :renders. Document the choice in Spec 004 §Render-tree primitives and Spec-Schemas §:rf/epoch-record.

Plain Reagent fns (not registered via reg-view) further complicate (a); the audit punt is currently captured as 'TBD pending rf2-XXXX' in Tool-Pair.md and Spec-Schemas.md.
notes:
Resolved (Mike, 2026-05-08): Option C — hybrid tuple.

:render-key is [<view-id> <instance-token>] where:
- <view-id> is the reg-view registry id (or :rf.view/anonymous for plain Reagent fns; if cheap, derive from (.-displayName fn) for tooling-friendliness)
- <instance-token> is an integer minted at mount time from a runtime counter atom

Tooling can use either half: registry id for aggregation, instance-token for fine-grained per-mount tracking. Plain Reagent fns work uniformly.

Replay/restore semantics: instance-tokens regenerate per mount, so cross-run correlation needs an alternative key (positional path, parent context). Out of scope for this bead — file follow-up if Tool-Pair replay actually needs cross-run correlation.

Implementation bead filed.

title: Tool-Pair / Spec 009 §Production builds: cross-cutting elision audit + verification
description:
Mike's mandate (2026-05-08): all pair / debug / trace / epoch infrastructure is dev-only. Production builds elide entirely. Spec 009 §Production builds: zero-overhead, zero-code via goog-define dead-code elimination.

Deliverable: an audit + tests confirming each piece elides:

1. Identify the goog-define flag(s) used to gate trace machinery. Likely ^:export'd as something like re-frame.trace/debug-enabled? per the existing goog-define pattern.
2. Verify trace.cljc's emit! / register-trace-cb! / trace-buffer all check the flag at compile time so :advanced compilation drops them.
3. Verify the new epoch-history machinery (post-rf2-???-epoch-history) is gated on the same flag.
4. Verify schema validation (PR #27) is gated on a similar flag if Spec 010 mandates production elision.
5. Verify the source-coord capture (post-rf2-???-source-coord) is dev-only (the data is captured at compile time but the *runtime-queryable* surface should not be in production builds).
6. Add a test that compiles a representative app under :advanced + the production-mode flag and verifies the dead-code elimination — i.e., grep the resulting JS for the trace ops / epoch-history fn names; they should not appear.
7. Document the elision contract in Spec 009 §Production builds and Tool-Pair §Production elision (already mentions it; ensure the pointers all line up).

This bead is the cross-cutting safety net for the rest of the Tool-Pair work. Should land alongside or after the other Tool-Pair beads, not before — there's nothing to elide until the other surfaces exist.
close_reason: PR #44 merged. Found and fixed two real elision bugs (schema validators, registrar trace emits).

title: Tool-Pair: (rf/machines) + (rf/machine-meta id) public API per Spec 005 §Querying machines
description:
Tool-Pair §How AI tools attach lists (rf/machines) and (rf/machine-meta id) as required surfaces. Currently neither is exported in re-frame.core. The data is reachable via app-db at [:rf/machines] and via (handler-meta :event machine-id), but pair tools shouldn't have to know that.

Deliverable:
1. (rf/machines) — returns a vector / set of registered machine ids (i.e., event-handler ids whose handler-fn was created via create-machine-handler). Implementation: the meta map stamped at registration time (per rf2-nzi3) carries a flag like :rf/machine? true; query it.
2. (rf/machine-meta id) — returns the registered machine's spec map (:initial, :data, :guards, :actions, :states, :doc, plus source coords).
3. Both must be queryable across all frames (machine snapshots are per-frame; the registration is global).
4. Tests pinning each.

Per Tool-Pair this is dev-only; elision via the trace goog-define isn't needed for these (they're query APIs, not emission), but the machinery should still be no-cost when trace is off.
close_reason: PR #40 merged.

title: Tool-Pair: epoch history + restore-epoch (Tool-Pair §Time-travel)
description:
The big one. Tool-Pair §Time-travel mandates per-frame epoch recording + restore. Largest of the new commitments.

Deliverable:
1. Per-frame ring buffer of :rf/epoch-record values (default depth 50, configurable). Each record: :epoch-id, :frame, :committed-at, :event-id, :trigger-event, :db-before, :db-after, optional :trace-events.
2. Hook into router/drain — every drain-settle (queue empty) appends an epoch-record. Atomic; a partial drain doesn't record.
3. (rf/epoch-history frame-id) — returns the vector oldest-first.
4. (rf/restore-epoch frame-id epoch-id) — sets the frame's app-db to the named epoch's :db-after, emits :rf.epoch/restored. Six documented failure modes (Tool-Pair table); each fires a structured trace under :rf.epoch/* and is a no-op on the frame's app-db.
5. (rf/configure :epoch-history {:depth N}) — runtime configuration; default 50.
6. **Production elision** — entire epoch-history machinery gated on the same goog-define as the trace surface; production builds elide.
7. Trace events: :rf.epoch/snapshotted (on each record), :rf.epoch/restored (on successful restore), plus the 6 failure-mode ops.
8. Tests: ring depth, restore happy path, all 6 failure modes, elision, per-frame isolation, schema-mismatch detection.

This is substantial — possibly split across two PRs (epoch recording + query first; restore + failure modes second). Agent should use judgement.
notes:
Spec updated in PR #39 (tool-pair-spec-gaps). New requirements baked into the bead's scope:
- :rf/epoch-record now includes :sub-runs / :renders / :effects substructure (per Spec-Schemas).
- (rf/register-epoch-cb key callback) — assembled-epoch listener mirroring register-trace-cb!.
- :render-key identity TBD pending rf2-t5tx; agent should treat it as opaque and document the open question.
Correction: actually landed via PR #48 (recovered after my mismerge of PR #43). 596 LOC of epoch.cljc + 527 LOC of epoch_test.clj.
close_reason: PR #43 merged. :renders projection empty until view layer is wired (tracked by rf2-t5tx).

title: Tool-Pair: trace-buffer ring (Spec 009 §Retain-N) + (rf/configure :trace-buffer {:depth N})
description:
Tool-Pair §How AI tools attach requires (rf/trace-buffer) — a retained ring buffer of recent trace events that pair tools query for 'what just happened' context. Spec 009 §Retain-N trace ring buffer specifies the contract: dev-only, configurable depth (default 100 per Spec 009 — verify in spec), filterable.

Deliverable:
1. Ring buffer impl in trace.cljc — circular collection, append on each emit!.
2. (rf/trace-buffer) public API — returns the buffer's events, optionally filtered by :operation / :op-type / :since.
3. (rf/configure :trace-buffer {:depth N}) config knob.
4. **Production elision** — gated on the same goog-define as the trace machinery; production builds drop the buffer entirely (no append, no storage).
5. JVM smoke + CLJS smoke covering append, filter, depth, elision behaviour.

Don't change the existing register-trace-cb! API — the ring is additive.
notes:
Spec updated in PR #39. New scope:
- :dispatch-id / :parent-dispatch-id correlation tags on :event/dispatched traces (Spec 009 §Dispatch correlation).
- :origin opt on (rf/dispatch event opts) lands on :tags :origin per Spec 002 §Dispatch origin tagging.
Plus the original retain-N ring + (rf/configure :trace-buffer ...) + production elision.
Implemented and merged via PR #41. Ring buffer in trace.cljc, :dispatch-id/:parent-dispatch-id in router.cljc, :origin opt in build-envelope. All three pieces gated on interop/debug-enabled? for production elision. 11 new JVM tests / 39 assertions; all CLJS Node tests still green.
close_reason: PR #41 merged.

title: Tool-Pair: source-coord (:ns/:line/:file) capture across all reg-* macros + verification
description:
Tool-Pair §Source-mapping requires every registration's metadata to carry :ns/:line/:file. Spec 001 §Source-coordinate capture mandates this for the CLJS reference. Audit + verify + backfill across reg-event-db, reg-event-fx, reg-event-ctx, reg-sub, reg-fx, reg-cofx, reg-frame, reg-view, reg-machine, reg-flow, reg-route, reg-app-schema, reg-error-projector, reg-head. Each macro captures the call site at expansion time and threads it into the metadata map.

Verification:
1. JVM smoke test that registers a handler then asserts (rf/handler-meta :event :foo) returns metadata with non-nil :ns, :line, :file.
2. Cover all kinds.
3. Compile-time only (no runtime cost in production via goog-define elision).
notes: Implemented in PR #42: https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/42. Source coordinates (:ns/:line/:file) auto-captured by reg-* macros at the re-frame.core boundary on JVM; underlying fns merge via re-frame.source-coords/merge-coords. CLJS remains on fn-alias form pending re-frame.core-macros companion ns. 15 new deftests / 99 assertions. Tests: 157/825/0.
close_reason: PR #42 merged. CLJS capture path queued for follow-up.

title: Browser-test runner still hangs after runtime_cljs_test/reg-view-macro-double-def-investigation (post rf2-9tx3)
description:
rf2-9tx3 fixed the bare-assert hang in nine_states.core (the
nine-states-runs-end-to-end test now completes cleanly under
:browser-test). However the browser-test runner still hangs at the
SAME point the bead's hypothesis 2 predicted: after
runtime_cljs_test/reg-view-macro-double-def-investigation, before
runtime_cljs_test/h-rewrites-namespaced-view-keys.

Browser console output from `npm run test:browser` after rf2-9tx3:

  [browser:log] :cljs-canonical [:div {:class "x"} [:p "hi"]]
  [browser:log] :cljs-hash 9d7457ef
  [browser:log] :outer-def-result-type cljs.core/MetaFn
  Timed out after 120000ms waiting for cljs.test summary.

Test order under cljs.test (alphabetical by ns then file order):
hash_check → machines → nine_states → routing_cljs (passed silently)
→ runtime_cljs (reg-view-macro-double-def-investigation prints, then
runner stalls before/inside h-rewrites-namespaced-view-keys).

Under :node-test the same test passes (53 tests, 175 assertions, 23
failures, 0 errors, exits cleanly), so this is browser-only — most
likely the h-macro view-key rewriting interacts with Reagent's async
render scheduler in a way that schedules a frame that never settles.

Hypothesis (carried over from rf2-9tx3 hypothesis 2): the h macro /
reg-view interaction in h-rewrites-namespaced-view-keys does
something async under Reagent in a real browser (where reactive
scheduling is async). Under :node-test, *animation-frame is
synchronous and the same code returns immediately.

Repro:

  cd implementation
  npx shadow-cljs compile browser-test
  node scripts/serve-and-run-browser-tests.cjs
  # → Timed out after 120000ms

Filed from rf2-9tx3 PR.
close_reason: PR #31 merged — root cause was cljs-test-display printing flag, not h-macro/reg-view.

title: nine-states test fixtures fail when state isn't actually driven (revealed by rf2-9tx3)
description:
After rf2-9tx3 converted bare clojure.core/assert to cljs.test/is in
examples/nine_states/core.cljs, the previously-masked test failures are
now visible.

`run-all-tests` previously aborted at the first failed bare assert
inside test-state-1-nothing (Assert failed: (in-state? f :ui.state/nothing?)),
so subsequent fixtures never ran. With `is` they all run, and 12
assertions across all 9 test-state-* fixtures fail in roughly the same
shape:

  expected: (in-state? f :ui.state/nothing?)
  actual:   (not (in-state? :rf.frame/2 :ui.state/nothing?))

  expected: (pos? (:attempt (rf/compute-sub [:todos] (rf/get-frame-db f))))
  actual:   (not (pos? nil))

  expected: (= :archived (:state snap))
  actual:   (not (= :archived nil))

The pattern is clear: `(rf/make-frame {:on-create [:app/initialise]})`
is creating a frame, but the `:on-create` event handler is not running
against it (or not running synchronously) — every read of the frame's
db comes back as nil/empty. The test-state-1 nothing? predicate is
false because :todos/status is nil instead of :idle (defaults from
:app/initialise haven't been seeded). Likewise for state-9, the
machine snapshot isn't created.

This is independent of rf2-9tx3 (which only fixed the runner-hang
caused by the bare assert exception path). Either:
- `:on-create` is not honoured by the current make-frame
  implementation, or
- the example needs to call `dispatch-sync [:app/initialise] {:frame f}`
  explicitly inside each `(with-frame ...)` body, or
- nine-states.core's :app/initialise registration doesn't actually
  reach the new frame's handler registry (frame-vs-shared-registry
  mismatch).

Repro:

  cd implementation
  npx shadow-cljs compile node-test
  node out/node-test.js
  # → 23 failures (12 in nine-states-runs-end-to-end)

Filed from rf2-9tx3 PR.
close_reason: PR #32 merged — actual root cause was (resolve ...) being a no-op in CLJS for cross-ns forward refs. Fixed via late_bind.cljc hook registry across 13 sites.

title: Effect-map shape policing (M-8) not enforced in fx interpreter
description:
Spec migration M-8 (docs/specification/MIGRATION.md §M-8) requires the runtime to police effect-map shape: only :db and :fx are allowed at the top level. Today re-frame.events/fx-handler->interceptor silently merges other top-level keys into the :effects map without raising. A handler returning {:dispatch [:foo]} (legacy v1 form) is silently dropped — no fx fires and no error trace is emitted.

Reproducer:
  (rf/reg-event-fx :legacy (fn [_ _] {:dispatch [:bar]}))
  (rf/dispatch-sync [:legacy])
  ;; nothing happens, no :rf.error/* emitted

Expected: a structured trace event (e.g. :rf.error/invalid-effect-map or :rf.error/legacy-effect-key) flagging the offending key with :recovery :ignored / :no-recovery per Spec 009.

Surfaced by rf2-mpug fx test suite. The test asserts a :rf.error/* trace is emitted and currently uses (is (some? trace-or-nil)) so the suite passes while the gap is tracked here.
close_reason: PR #34 merged.

title: flow fixtures fail when conformance runs them: app-db not updated by flow output
description:
After rf2-y3bo claimed :flow/* capabilities so the flow-*.edn fixtures actually run, all 5 fail.

Symptoms:
- :flow/recompute-on-input-change: expected {:width 3, :height 4, :area 12}, actual {:width 3, :height 4}; subscribe-value [:area/value] returns nil instead of 12.
- :flow/multi-input-topo: expected {:width 5, :height 4, :area 20, :area*2 40}, actual {:width 5, :height 4}.
- :flow/noop-on-value-equal-input: expected {:n 7, :doubled 14}, actual {:n 7}.
- :flow/hot-reload-preserves-output: errors with :rf.error/flow-bad-output.
- :flow/toggle-via-fx: errors with :rf.error/flow-bad-output.

In every case the flow's output is not being assoc-in'd at the flow's :path. Either the conformance harness isn't realising flow handlers from :fixture/registry / :fixture/handlers, or the runtime drain isn't running flows after the dispatch (or both).

The hot-reload + toggle ones erroring with :rf.error/flow-bad-output suggests the harness IS reaching the flow but the output fn is returning the wrong shape — perhaps because the DSL body is not being realised into a clojure fn (it's running with a vector as the output fn?).

Discovered while implementing rf2-y3bo. The smoke_test.clj flow tests pass (they construct flows with literal Clojure fns), so the runtime itself works for the basic case — the gap is in the conformance harness's flow-realisation path.
close_reason: PR #28 merged.

title: Tests: SSR end-to-end (Spec 011) — full request lifecycle with hydration handshake
description:
Existing ssr-* fixtures cover render-to-string, hydrate, hydration-mismatch, head-emits, head-hydration, error-known-mapping, error-sanitisation, cookie, redirect, set-status. Plus ssr-with-fx-override and ssr-end-to-end smoke tests. Solid base; need a comprehensive request-lifecycle test that exercises the whole flow in one go:

- A request arrives.
- A per-request frame is created via make-frame {:on-create [:rf/server-init request]}.
- :rf/server-init dispatches data-load fx (stubbed via :fx-overrides).
- The drain settles synchronously.
- get-frame-db reflects the post-drain state.
- render-to-string against the registered root view produces stable HTML with the data-rf-render-hash attribute.
- The build-payload fn produces a serialisable payload (frame-id, app-db, render-hash).
- Client-side: dispatch-sync :rf/hydrate replaces app-db; subsequent renders match the SSR hash; mismatch case verified by mutating the hydrated state and confirming the trace event.

Deliverable: NEW file implementation/test/re_frame/ssr_end_to_end_test.clj that drives the full flow in one deftest, plus targeted deftests for set-status precedence, multi-cookie, redirect short-circuit, and head-hash mismatch detection.
close_reason: Stale in_progress cleanup — fix commits on origin/main per git log sweep 2026-05-13. Bead status was never flipped after PR merge.

title: Tests: interceptor chain + std interceptors (Spec 002, std_interceptors.cljc)
description:
The retained interceptor surface in v2 is small (path, unwrap, inject-cofx, ->interceptor primitive) but the contract is load-bearing. Today nothing dedicated exercises:

- path: scopes a handler to a sub-tree of app-db; both reading and writing.
- unwrap: extracts the event-arg map from [:event {...}] envelope shape.
- inject-cofx: registered cofx are injected into handler invocation; cofx that raise propagate as handler errors.
- ->interceptor primitive: custom interceptors compose with the standard chain; before / after invocation order.
- Chain composition: multiple interceptors in metadata vector; each runs before / after; exceptions interrupt.

Deliverable: NEW file implementation/test/re_frame/interceptor_test.clj with one deftest per interceptor + a chain-composition deftest.
close_reason: PR #18 merged.

title: Tests: fx subsystem comprehensive (Spec 002 §Effects, Spec 009 §Errors)
description:
Effects today are partially covered via per-feature tests (http stubs in login-machine-flow, ssr-* fixtures, fx/db-first / fx/ordering-source-order / fx/override-by-id / fx/platforms fixtures). Still missing:

- Source-order ordering across mixed effect types within one handler return.
- :fx-overrides per-call vs per-frame precedence.
- fx-handler exception → :rf.error/fx-handler-exception trace, recovery contract.
- Missing fx-id (no registration) → :rf.error/no-such-fx trace.
- :platforms gating: skipped fx emits :rf.fx/skipped-on-platform; filtered fx don't fire in headless mode.
- Effect map shape policing: only :db and :fx at top level (Spec migration M-8); raise on legacy keys.

Deliverable: NEW file implementation/test/re_frame/fx_test.clj with ~6 deftests. Existing fixtures already cover the basics; this is the consolidated edge-case suite.

title: Tests: frame lifecycle (Spec 002) — create, surgical re-register, destroy with active machines / pending events / live subs
description:
Frame lifecycle has frame-lifecycle and frame-multi-instance fixtures plus destroy-frame-signals-active-machines smoke test. Need broader coverage:

- reg-frame surgical update: re-register frame metadata while machines, subs, app-db all live; verify state preserved.
- destroy-frame with pending events in queue: events are drained or discarded per Spec 002 §Destroy.
- destroy-frame cascade: :rf.machine/destroyed-on-frame-exit emitted per active machine.
- destroy-frame with live subscribers: subs return nil with :replaced-with-default; on-dispose fires.
- ensure-default-frame idempotence.
- make-frame returns gensym'd id; per-frame fx-overrides; isolation between frames.
- :on-create runs synchronously per Spec 002 §reg-frame is atomic.

Deliverable: NEW file implementation/test/re_frame/frame_lifecycle_test.clj.
close_reason: Stale in_progress cleanup — fix commits on origin/main per git log sweep 2026-05-13. Bead status was never flipped after PR merge.

title: Tests: drain semantics (Spec 002) — run-to-completion, depth limit, async vs sync, dispatch-during-handler
description:
Spec 002 §Drain semantics is load-bearing for re-frame2's behavioural contract. Today only one or two paths are tested transitively via login-machine-flow and dispatch-sync-in-handler-errors. Need targeted coverage:

- run-to-completion: events queued during a handler run before the handler returns; verify the handler completes before the next event drains.
- drain depth limit: deep dispatch chain, verify :rf.error/drain-depth-exceeded raises with atomic rollback.
- dispatch-sync inside a handler: verify :rf.error/dispatch-sync-in-handler raises (already partially covered).
- dispatch async vs sync interleaving: events queued via dispatch resolve after the current drain; sync side-events resolve before.
- per-frame drain isolation: events on frame A do not interleave with frame B's drain.

Deliverable: NEW file implementation/test/re_frame/drain_test.clj with ~5 deftests covering each path. Each deftest's docstring references the relevant Spec 002 section anchor.
notes: Implemented drain_test.clj with 5 deftests covering run-to-completion, drain-depth limit, dispatch-sync-in-handler (JVM + transitive), async vs sync interleaving, and per-frame isolation. PR https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/22. Filed rf2-8hi7 for the spec/impl gap on 'atomic rollback of failing handler :db' — implementation does not roll back; test asserts what drain! actually does.
close_reason: Closed

title: Browser-test runner hangs after runtime_cljs_test/reg-view-macro-double-def-investigation
description:
After landing rf2-zoem (PR #15), the new shadow-cljs :browser-test +
Playwright runner times out locally (and likely in CI too) waiting for
the cljs.test summary. The browser console shows tests running in this
order:

  1. hash_check_cljs_test/jvm-cljs-hash-parity (prints :cljs-canonical, :cljs-hash) — passes
  2. (machines_cljs_test runs silently)
  3. nine_states_cljs_test/nine-states-runs-end-to-end — ERRORS with
     'Assert failed: (in-state? f :ui.state/nothing?)' and the error
     also surfaces as a browser pageerror (uncaught)
  4. runtime_cljs_test/reg-view-macro-double-def-investigation
     (prints :outer-def-result-type cljs.core/MetaFn) — runs
  5. (silence — no further test output, no summary)

Hypotheses:

- The 'Assert failed' from nine_states.core/test-state-1-nothing (a
  bare clojure.core/assert wrapped in (with-frame ...)) escapes
  cljs.test.run_block's try/catch via Reagent's batched render
  scheduler and pollutes a microtask queue, eventually deadlocking the
  block driver in run-block.
- OR: the next test after :outer-def-result-type, which is
  runtime_cljs_test/h-rewrites-namespaced-view-keys, hits something in
  the h macro / reg-view interaction that blocks indefinitely under
  Reagent in a real browser (where reactive scheduling is async).
  Under :node-test, *animation-frame is sync and the same code returns
  immediately.

Repro:

  cd implementation
  npm install
  npx playwright install chromium
  npm run test:browser

The runner times out after BROWSER_TEST_TIMEOUT_MS (default 120s).
Compare with `npm run test:cljs` (Node), which completes and reports
'Ran 52 tests containing 149 assertions. 11 failures, 1 errors.' with
exit code 1.

The fix is one of:
- Refactor nine_states.core's test-state-* fixtures to NOT use bare
  clojure.core/assert (which throws JS Error in a way the
  Reagent-on-real-browser scheduler can't reabsorb). Use cljs.test
  `is` directly, or wrap dispatches and reads in a try/catch.
- Or, gate the nine_states.core mount-point block (defonce root ...)
  to only fire when window.parent != window (i.e. not in test runner
  pages).
- Or, refactor h / reg-view to avoid whatever async work blocks
  h-rewrites-namespaced-view-keys in a real browser.

Diagnostic value: this bead is exactly the kind of thing the new
:browser-test target exists to surface. The infrastructure works as
designed; the surfaced failure is real and worth fixing.
close_reason: PR #26 merged; nine-states pollution actually fixed by rf2-coks.

title: Bug: reg-machine first dispatch doesn't cascade :initial through compound states
description:
Discovered by the rf2-pc82 agent. When reg-machine registers a machine with a compound initial state — e.g. {:initial :foo :states {:foo {:initial :bar :states {:bar {} :baz {}}}}} — the runtime's first dispatch leaves the snapshot at {:state :foo} (single keyword) rather than descending through the compound's :initial chain to {:state [:foo :bar]}.

Spec 005 §Initial-state cascading mandates the descent: 'a target landing on a possibly-compound node descends through :initial chain until a leaf.' initial-cascade is implemented in machines.cljc but appears to only run inside transitions, not at first-dispatch / on-create snapshot synthesis.

Workaround in PR #9 (rf2-pc82 tests): a seed-snapshot! helper positions machines at a known compound-leaf state via direct app-db assoc-in, bypassing :on-create.

Fix: when reg-machine creates the initial snapshot (or :on-create runs), apply initial-cascade to the declared :initial. The snapshot's :state should be the leaf path, not the parent keyword.

Discovered-from: rf2-pc82.
close_reason: PR #23 merged.

title: Bug: sub-machine + :rf/machine reg-sub never landed on main (PR #2 reverted)
description:
Discovered by the rf2-pc82 CLJS machine-tests agent. Spec 005 §Subscribing to machines mandates a framework-shipped :rf/machine reg-sub plus a sub-machine convenience wrapper in re-frame.core. PR #2 (rf2-hbt1 — ch.05 + machines rewrite) introduced both, but the working tree was reverted before merge and these never made it into main.

Current state on main:
- (rf/sub-machine ...) does not exist in re-frame.core.
- The :rf/machine sub is not registered.

Tests in machines_cljs_test.cljs (PR #9) work around this by reading the snapshot path directly: (get-in (rf/get-frame-db frame-id) [:rf/machines machine-id]).

Re-land scope:
1. Add (defn sub-machine [machine-id] (subscribe [:rf/machine machine-id])) to implementation/src/re_frame/core.cljc, alongside create-machine-handler / machine-transition.
2. Add (subs/reg-sub :rf/machine (fn [db [_ id]] (get-in db [:rf/machines id]))) to implementation/src/re_frame/machines.cljc as a top-level form (so it survives the smoke-test fixture's clear-all! through the require :reload of machines.cljc).
3. Update smoke_test.clj reset-runtime fixture to add (require 're-frame.machines :reload).
4. Smoke test exercising reg-machine + dispatch-sync + compute-sub against :rf/machine.

This was originally part of rf2-hbt1; re-extracting because the broader PR was reverted.
close_reason: Landed via PR #14 (commit 44777e5).

title: CI: shadow-cljs :browser-test + Playwright runner (replaces Karma path in rf2-443l)
description:
Concrete implementation for the browser-test runner Mike wants per rf2-443l. Karma is deprecated since 2023; Playwright is the modern actively-maintained answer and the working tree already contains Playwright tooling (examples/playwright-inspect.cjs, .edge-cdp/).

Deliverable:

1. **shadow-cljs config** — add a :browser-test target to implementation/shadow-cljs.edn:

   :browser-test
   {:target    :browser-test
    :ns-regexp "-cljs-test$"
    :test-dir  "out/browser-test"
    :runner-ns shadow.test.browser
    :devtools  {:http-port 8021
                :http-root "out/browser-test"}}

2. **Playwright runner script** — implementation/scripts/run-browser-tests.cjs (Node, ~30 lines): launches headless Chromium, navigates to http://localhost:8021, waits for the shadow-cljs test runner page to render the cljs.test summary, parses pass/fail counts from the DOM (or hooks shadow.test.browser's reporter), exits with status 0 on green / 1 on red.

3. **GitHub Actions job** — append to .github/workflows/test.yml a 'cljs-browser' job alongside the existing 'cljs' (Node) job:
   - npm install (with playwright)
   - npx shadow-cljs compile browser-test (and serve from out/browser-test)
   - node implementation/scripts/run-browser-tests.cjs
   - Pin actions to commit SHAs per the org policy (already enforced in the workflow).

4. **Update implementation/README.md** with the local browser-test command.

5. **Update the ruleset on main** (optional) to require the new 'cljs-browser' check alongside 'jvm' and 'cljs'. Mike's call.

Verification: all existing CLJS tests pass under :browser-test (real Chromium) AND under :node-test. Diamond/chain glitch tests in particular validated under realistic browser timing.

Coordinate with rf2-443l (parent): this is the concrete implementation; rf2-443l can close once this lands. The trace-stream / diamond tests / hot-reload tests / cross-spec tests written by other agents become browser-runnable as a side-effect once the runner is wired.
close_reason: PR #15 merged.

title: Conformance runner: realise cofx handler bodies from fixture DSL
description: Today the conformance runner does not realise cofx handlers from :fixture/handlers; only events, subs, fx, routes, views, and app-schemas are realised. The Spec 010 cofx-validation fixture (schema-cofx-validates.edn) requires a cofx body that injects a value, then triggers schema validation. Add cofx body realisation alongside the event/sub/fx realisation in conformance_test.clj. Surfaced while authoring rf2-dznb.
close_reason: PR #35 merged.

title: Spec 010/011 wiring: reg-error-projector + default-error-projector
description: Spec 011 §Server error projection — implement reg-error-projector (registry kind :error-projector exists) plus the default :rf.ssr/default-error-projector mapping per Spec 011 §Default projector table (covers :rf.error/no-such-handler 404, :rf.error/schema-validation-failure 400, plus the 500-default catch-all). The :code/:status/:message/:retryable? public-error shape is locked. Surfaced while authoring schemas_test.clj for rf2-dznb.
close_reason: PR #37 merged.

title: Spec 010 wiring: sub-return :spec validation post-compute
description: Spec 010 §Validation order step 6 — after each sub recompute, validate the return value against the sub's :spec. Default recovery on failure: :replaced-with-default (sub yields nil). Wire into re-frame.subs/compute-sub and the reaction body in subscribe. Surfaced while authoring schema-sub-return-validates.edn for rf2-dznb.
close_reason: Wired in PR #27 — validate-sub-return! fires in subs/compute-and-cache! reaction body and subs/compute-sub. :replaced-with-default recovery yields nil. Conformance fixture schema-sub-return-validates.edn passes.

title: Spec 010 wiring: cofx :spec validation post-injection
description: Spec 010 §Validation order step 2 — after each cofx injects, validate the merged value against the cofx's :spec. Add validate-cofx! to schemas.cljc and call it from re-frame.cofx/inject-cofx (or in the interceptor chain). Surfaced while authoring schema-cofx-validates.edn for rf2-dznb.
close_reason: Wired in PR #27 — validate-cofx! fires in cofx/inject-cofx post-injection; sets :rf/skip-handler? on the context for the handler-as-interceptor short-circuit. Conformance fixture schema-cofx-validates.edn passes via the runner's first-pass cofx body realisation (rf2-g25p remains open for richer cofx body DSL coverage).

title: Spec 010 wiring: validate-event! pre-handler
description: Spec 010 §Validation order step 1 — the runtime must call re-frame.schemas/validate-event! before invoking the handler. Today the fn exists in schemas.cljc but nothing calls it. Wire into router/process-event! (or interceptor chain). Surfaced while authoring schema-event-payload-validates.edn for rf2-dznb.
close_reason: Wired in PR #27 — validate-event! fires in router/process-event! pre-handler; skip-handler on failure; downstream queue continues. Conformance fixture schema-event-payload-validates.edn passes.

title: Routing impl: emit :rf.nav/scroll fx on navigation per Spec 012
description: routing.cljc currently does not emit a :rf.nav/scroll effect on :rf.route/navigate or :rf/url-changed. Spec 012 Scroll restoration requires the runtime to dispatch :rf.nav/scroll with {:strategy :from :to :saved-pos :fragment}, resolved from the route's :scroll metadata or the navigate opts. Surfaced while writing rf2-hh37 conformance fixtures.
close_reason: Implemented :rf.nav/scroll fx emission in routing.cljc (PR #25). Added JVM smoke test, registered fx with :platforms #{:client}, added conformance fixture routing-scroll-restoration.edn.

title: Migration: target Reagent v2 across impl + examples + CLAUDE-facing code
description:
Mike's directive (2026-05-08): the CLJS reference targets Reagent v2. Stop investing in Reagent 1.2 back-compat.

Audit + update:
1. implementation/deps.edn — currently pins reagent 1.2.0; bump to the 2.x line.
2. implementation/shadow-cljs.edn — currently pins reagent 1.2.0 in the dependencies vector; bump.
3. examples/deps.edn (if present in working tree) — same.
4. implementation/src/re_frame/substrate/reagent.cljs — re-validate the 1.2-specific affordances under 2.x: ratom/make-reaction, r/current-component for React-context resolution, r/flush behaviour. The current code path via :context-type metadata may need a hooks-flavoured replacement (useContext) on functional components; class-based contextType is legacy.
5. implementation/src/re_frame/views.cljs — same. PR #4's fix (keyword? check on (.-context cmp)) was correct for Reagent 1.2; verify under 2.x. May need a use-context-shaped replacement for functional components.
6. implementation/src/re_frame/views_macros.clj — the reg-view macro and the :context-type metadata on the wrapper fn may not be the right idiom in Reagent 2.

Verification: all existing tests green under the new dep; the counter / login / nine-states examples render correctly.

Related: rf2-3442 (reg-view auto-inject decision) — Form-1-only recommendation strengthens under Reagent 2 because Form-3 is even less idiomatic with hooks-based components.
close_reason: Stale in_progress cleanup — fix commits on origin/main per git log sweep 2026-05-13. Bead status was never flipped after PR merge.

title: Tests: Cross-Spec-Interactions edge cases
description: docs/specification/Cross-Spec-Interactions.md enumerates ~10 boundary cases at Spec interfaces — plain Reagent fn under non-default frame, frame destruction with active machines, hot-reload during in-flight drain, dispatch-sync during render, schema validation under SSR, etc. Each case is one targeted test. Most are CLJS-side because they involve Reagent/React boundaries; a few are JVM-suitable. New file implementation/test/re_frame/cross_spec_test.cljs (or .clj as appropriate); one deftest per documented case, with the case's section anchor in the docstring.
close_reason: PR #7 merged.

title: Tests: schema integration (Spec 010) — events, subs, cofx, app-db slices, dev/prod elision
description: Today only error/schema-failure exists. Spec 010 covers: event-payload schemas (validated at dispatch, raise :rf.error/schema-validation-failure), sub-return-shape schemas (validated post-compute), cofx shape schemas, app-db slice schemas (validated after :db write per app-schema-at), dev/prod elision via goog-define flag, and the error projector mapping schema failures to :rf/public-error in SSR. Need: one fixture per validation point; JVM smoke testing the elision toggle; CLJS smoke under Reagent to confirm schema validation runs for live dispatches.
close_reason: PR #12 merged.

title: Tests: hot-reload semantics (Spec 001) — re-registration, sub-cache eviction, view re-render
description: Spec 001 §Hot-reload guarantees three properties: (1) re-registering a handler is non-destructive to in-flight work (the running drain keeps its captured handler body); (2) re-registering a sub evicts cached reactions across all frames; (3) under Reagent, mounted views re-render after a re-register. sub-hot-reload-cljs in runtime_cljs_test.cljs covers (2) partially. Need: JVM smoke for (1) and (2) (replace handler mid-drain via a synthetic timing fixture; replace sub mid-subscription and verify cache invalidation across two frames). CLJS smoke for (3): re-register a view's body and see the next render's output change. Plus a fixture exercising re-registration of frame metadata while a machine snapshot is live (existing app-db preserved per surgical-update contract).
notes: PR #11 opened: https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/11
close_reason: PR #11 merged.

title: Tests: Flow coverage (Spec 013) — reg-flow, dirty-check, topo-sort, register/clear via fx
description: Flows are claimed Done in the impl README but have only transitive coverage. Need: reg-flow registration, the dirty-check / topo-sort path, computed-state writes, hot-reload preservation, register-via-:rf.fx/reg-flow + clear-via-:rf.fx/clear-flow round-trip. Conformance fixtures in docs/specification/conformance/fixtures/ + JVM smoke in implementation/test/re_frame/smoke_test.clj. Ideally a CLJS smoke test too, but secondary.
close_reason: PR #10 merged.

title: Tests: comprehensive routing test coverage (Spec 012 audit + gap-fill)
description:
**Why:** Routing today has only six conformance fixtures (`routing-match-url`, `routing-navigate`, `route-fragment-change`, `route-navigation-blocked`, `route-ranking-precedence`, `route-stale-nav-token-suppression`) and no JVM/CLJS smoke tests beyond what those fixtures exercise. Spec 012 defines a much wider surface — per-route data loading, scroll restoration, query strings, redirects, multi-frame routing, SSR integration. Coverage is shallow.

**Deliverable:** comprehensive routing tests at three levels.

**Conformance fixtures (in `docs/specification/conformance/fixtures/`):**

Per Spec 012's canonical surface, gap-fill fixtures for at least:

- `routing-on-match-fires` — `:on-match` events fire on a successful match.
- `routing-not-found` — unmatched URL routes to `:rf.route/not-found` and emits the canonical trace.
- `routing-redirect-on-match` — `:redirect-on-match` short-circuits and routes to the destination.
- `routing-can-leave-blocks` — `:can-leave` guard pauses navigation through `:rf/pending-navigation`; resume / cancel both work.
- `routing-query-string-coercion` — query-string params coerce to declared shapes (`:int`, `:keyword`, etc.).
- `routing-fragment-as-slice` — fragment changes update `:rf/route :fragment` without firing `:on-match`.
- `routing-scroll-restoration` — declared scroll behavior fires the correct fx on navigation (`:rf.scroll/top` etc.).
- `routing-nested-layout` — nested route segments resolve their parent layout.
- `routing-multi-frame` — same route registered in two frames navigates each frame independently.

**JVM smoke tests (in `implementation/test/re_frame/smoke_test.clj`):**

- `routing-bootstrap` — `(rf/reg-route ... )` + `(rf/dispatch [:rf.route/navigate ...])` updates `:rf/route` slice.
- `routing-pending-nav-protocol` — drive a `:can-leave` guard through pause / continue / cancel, verify slice transitions.
- `routing-nav-token-staleness` — issue two navigations in flight; the older one's resolution is suppressed.

**CLJS tests (in `implementation/test/re_frame/runtime_cljs_test.cljs`):**

- `routing-handle-url-change-cljs` — exercise the URL-change path under the Reagent adapter.
- `routing-frame-provider-routing-cljs` — multi-frame routing under React context, ensuring `:rf/route` resolves per-frame.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- All new fixtures pass `clojure -M:test` alongside the existing 43+.
- New JVM smoke tests pass.
- New CLJS tests pass under `:node-test` and (once rf2-443l lands) the browser runner.
- Each test references the relevant Spec 012 section in a comment so the contract anchor is explicit.

**Related:**
- rf2-443l (browser runner) — gates the CLJS tests' browser run.
- rf2-3nqu (machine fixtures) — same shape of work, different domain.

**Suggested workflow:** start with conformance fixtures (highest leverage; mirror the existing ones); then JVM smoke; then CLJS. Don't try to be exhaustive — pick the high-value gaps from Spec 012's surface inventory.
close_reason: PR #13 merged.

title: Tests: missing state-machine conformance fixtures (cross-actor-send, spawn-from-action, destroy-clears-snapshot, spawn-on-spawn-callback, machine-actor-isolation)
description:
**Why:** Spec 005 §Capability matrix references fixtures that should exist for the v1 CLJS reference's claimed capabilities, but several are missing on disk.

**Missing fixtures:**

- `cross-actor-send.edn` — claimed under §Cross-actor send via `:fx`. Two registered machines; one's action dispatches an event addressed to the other; verify the receiver's snapshot transitions.
- `spawn-from-action.edn` — claimed under §Imperative spawn / destroy. An action returns `[:spawn machine-id]`; verify a child machine is created and its snapshot lives under `[:rf/machines child-id]`.
- `destroy-clears-snapshot.edn` — claimed under §Imperative spawn / destroy. After `[:destroy-machine actor-id]`, verify `[:rf/machines actor-id]` is removed from app-db.
- `spawn-on-spawn-callback.edn` — claimed under §Imperative spawn / destroy. The `:on-spawn` callback fires after spawn, with the new actor-id available.
- `machine-actor-isolation.edn` — claimed under §Own state + message ports. Two machines coexist; mutations to one's snapshot do not bleed into the other's.

**Deliverable:** five fixture EDN files plus their integration into `conformance_test.clj` (which auto-loads everything in `fixtures/`). Each fixture follows the shape of the existing `machine-transition.edn` and `invoke-spawn-on-entry-destroy-on-exit.edn`.

**Verification:** all 43+5 = 48 fixtures pass JVM conformance.

**Discovered-from:** rf2-hbt1.
close_reason: PR #5 merged.

title: Tests: JVM equivalents for sub-topology glitch-freedom (diamond, chain, value-equal)
description:
**Why:** the new sub-topology tests (`sub-topology-glitch-free-diamond`, `-chain`, `sub-correctness-on-value-equal-input`) are CLJS-only because they exercise Reagent's reaction propagation. The JVM reference uses the plain-atom adapter, which has different semantics (no caching, recompute every deref) but should satisfy the same glitch-free contract.

**Deliverable:** equivalent JVM tests in `implementation/test/re_frame/smoke_test.clj` exercising:
1. Diamond: app-db → {a,b} → c via compute-sub. Single mutation, c is correct, no opportunity for glitch (plain-atom recomputes each deref).
2. Chain: app-db → a → b → c via compute-sub. Same shape; verify final value.
3. Value-equal mutation: a no-op `(assoc db :unrelated "z")` keeps downstream computed values correct.

**Note:** plain-atom is glitch-impossible by construction (no caching, no propagation queue). The JVM tests are a contract-shape pin: they verify compute-sub on diamond and chain shapes returns the right algebraic result. They should NOT use Reagent.

**Discovered-from:** rf2-hbt1.
close_reason: PR #3 merged.

title: Test infrastructure: browser-environment runner for CLJS tests
description:
**Why:** CLJS tests currently run only via `shadow-cljs :node-test` build under Node. Real browser semantics — DOM, React's batched rendering, requestAnimationFrame, real Reagent reaction propagation timing — are not exercised.

**Deliverable:** a `shadow-cljs :browser-test` build (or equivalent Playwright / Karma harness) that runs every existing CLJS test in a headless browser and reports pass/fail.

**Scope:**
- Add the build target.
- Add a CI job that invokes it (alongside the existing `cljs` job in `.github/workflows/test.yml`).
- Verify the existing CLJS smoke + topology + nine-states tests pass in-browser.
- Document the new run command in `implementation/README.md`.

**Note:** the user already has working-tree drafts (`examples/playwright-inspect.cjs`, `examples/.edge-cdp/`, `examples/counter-dom.html`) — coordinate with them before adding new tooling, may be partial overlap.
close_reason: Subsumed by rf2-zoem (PR #15) — :browser-test + Playwright runner now in CI.

title: State-machine API surface: docs/guide/05 references symbols not exported by re-frame-2.core
description:
docs/guide/05-state-machines.md uses these symbols, none of which are exported by `re-frame-2.core`:

- `(rf/sub-machine :auth.login/event-handler)` — sugar over a framework-shipped `:rf/machine` sub. Not in core.
- `(rf/machine-handler [:auth :login] login-flow)` — claimed helper that returns a `reg-event-fx`-shaped handler. Core exposes `create-machine-handler` instead, with a different signature (no `:machine-path`).
- `(rf/reg-machine-guard :auth.login/under-retry-limit ...)` — guard registry. Not in core.
- `(rf/reg-machine-action :auth.login/issue-request ...)` — action registry. Not in core.
- xstate-flavoured `:guards`/`:actions`/`:meta` slots inside a transition table — Spec 005's transition-table grammar may not include all of these.

Plus prose claims that may or may not match Spec 005:
- `:machine-path` as a registration metadata key for the snapshot location.
- "Transition tables are pure data, runtime owns the snapshot, headless test via `machine-transition`" — the impl matches this for the basic case, but the rich grammar in the chapter (`:cond`, `:entry`, multi-target arrays) needs verification against the transition spec.

Two questions to resolve:
1. Which of these symbols are aspirational (post-v1) vs missed exports?
2. For each one we keep aspirational, the guide chapter needs a "the API is sketched here; not all of it ships in v1" caveat — currently it reads as fully implemented.

This is the largest gap in the guide-vs-impl audit. Lower urgency than ch.02/04 because users hitting state machines will have already read enough of the spec to spot the gap.
notes:
## Decisions taken (2026-05-08)

**:data is canonical** (Spec 005 §Naming). The extended-state slot is `:data`; snapshot is `{:state :data}`. `:context` is FORBIDDEN — overloaded with re-frame's interceptor-context and React-context idioms.

**Guards/actions/sub-machine sub fns receive the whole snapshot.** They never receive `:data` directly; they receive `{:state :data :meta?}` and pull what they need.

**`sub-machine` is approved as a core convenience.** Thin wrapper over `(get-in db [:rf/machines machine-id])`. Adds one symbol to core; saves users from spelling the reserved path themselves and reinforces "the snapshot is the state".

## Open question added to scope: parameter-style standardisation

Today the corpus uses two forms inconsistently for guard/action/sub fns:

  ;; Form A (destructured) — Spec 005 §Registration L386
  (fn [{:keys [data]} _] ...)

  ;; Form B (whole-arg) — Pattern-LongRunningWork, machines.cljc impl
  (fn [snapshot _] (... (:data snapshot)))

Three plausible policies:
1. Canonical Form A everywhere.
2. Canonical Form B everywhere.
3. Both first-class — spec adds one paragraph saying either is fine.

Decision needed before ch.05 rewrite lands; otherwise the new examples will themselves drift from whatever the rest of the corpus does.

## Resulting work scope

1. ch.05 rewrite per the original points (drop reg-machine-{guard,action}, move guards/actions inside the spec map, replace machine-handler with create-machine-handler, drop :machine-path, replace :context with :data).
2. Audit and harmonise param style across Spec 005, Pattern-* docs, machines.cljc per the policy chosen above.
3. Add sub-machine to re-frame.core (one-line thin wrapper).
4. Verify the rewritten ch.05 example actually loads (smoke test or compile check).
Decision on parameter style: canonical Form A (destructure {:keys [data]}). Spec 005 examples already use this; Pattern-LongRunningWork drift to be cleaned up in the harmonisation pass. Form B (whole-arg snapshot) is the deeper contract question — filed as rf2-qppo and parked.

## rf2-qppo resolved (2026-05-08): TIGHTEN

Canonical guard/action/sub-machine sub-fn signature is now:

  (fn [data event] ...)              ; 2-arity — the 99% case
  (fn [data event ctx] ...)          ; 3-arity — opt-in introspection (ctx is the snapshot {:state :meta?})

machines.cljc arity-detects and dispatches accordingly: passes :data directly for 2-arity; passes :data + event + snapshot's introspection slot for 3-arity. Effect-map return shape unchanged ({:data ... :fx [...]}).

## Updated rf2-hbt1 work scope (folding rf2-qppo)

1. Spec 005 contract update:
   - §Registration: signature examples become (fn [data event] ...)
   - §Guards: same
   - §Actions: same; effect-map return shape stays
   - §Naming: re-emphasise — :data is the parameter, not a destructure key
   - Add 3-arity escape hatch documentation

2. machines.cljc impl change:
   - Arity-detect on guard/action fns
   - 2-arity: (guard-fn data event)
   - 3-arity: (guard-fn data event {:state state :meta meta})
   - Default to 2-arity; 3-arity is opt-in by declaring the third arg

3. Corpus sweep (mechanical — every (fn [{:keys [data]} event] ...) becomes (fn [data event] ...)):
   - implementation/src/re_frame/machines.cljc — internal call sites + tests
   - implementation/test/re_frame/machines_test.cljc / .cljs
   - docs/specification/Pattern-LongRunningWork.md
   - docs/specification/Pattern-NineStates.md
   - docs/specification/Pattern-Forms.md (verify)
   - Any other Pattern-*.md showing machine code (grep first)
   - docs/specification/conformance/fixtures/machine-*.edn — sweep fn forms
   - docs/specification/CP-5-MachineGuide.md (likely)

4. ch.05 rewrite (the original rf2-hbt1 work):
   - Drop reg-machine-guard / reg-machine-action — guards/actions live inline in spec map
   - Drop machine-handler — use create-machine-handler
   - Drop :machine-path registration metadata
   - Replace :context with :data
   - Use new tightened (fn [data event] ...) signature throughout
   - Verify rich-grammar slots (:cond, :entry, multi-target arrays) against Spec 005; file follow-up beads for any gap

5. Add sub-machine to re-frame.core:
   - Thin wrapper: returns the sub vector for [:rf/machines machine-id]
   - Plus reg-sub if not already present

6. Smoke-test rewritten ch.05:
   - Either paste-and-load each code block, OR extract the chapter into a runnable example app

## Sequencing

This bead lands AFTER rf2-d0pi merges. File conflicts:
- docs/specification/Pattern-NineStates.md (rf2-d0pi sweeps reg-view shape)
- implementation/src/re_frame/core.cljc (rf2-d0pi adds reg-view; rf2-hbt1 adds sub-machine)

Both manageable but cleaner serialised.

## Type promotion

Was filed as decision; all decisions now taken (rf2-qppo resolved, rf2-hbt1's API drops confirmed). Promoting to task on Mike's confirmation.
close_reason: Stale in_progress cleanup — fix commits on origin/main per git log sweep 2026-05-13. Bead status was never flipped after PR merge.

title: Decide frame-provider public API: rf/frame-provider Reagent component vs views/build-frame-provider factory
description:
The guide (docs/guide/04 §frame-provider, §A small example: split counter) writes:

    [rf/frame-provider {:frame :left}  [counter-view]]
    [rf/frame-provider {:frame :right} [counter-view]]

The implementation has no `rf/frame-provider` symbol. The real counter example uses a factory + frame-id positional:

    [(v/build-frame-provider :counter)
     :counter
     [counter-buttons]]

Two paths:
1. **Add `rf/frame-provider` as the documented API.** It would be a Reagent component accepting a props map `{:frame frame-id}` and children. Build it on top of `build-frame-provider` so the existing impl substrate is preserved. Wins: the prose-shape (`{:frame :left}`) reads idiomatically and matches React-context conventions; one symbol to import.
2. **Update the guide to use `views/build-frame-provider`.** Cost: clunkier idiom (`[(v/build-frame-provider :left) :left [counter]]`); duplicates the frame id; requires an extra `:as v` import.

Path 1 looks like the natural shipping API; the guide may have been the source of truth and the impl drifted. Decision drives whether the guide gets edited or the impl grows a thin wrapper.
notes: Resolved (Mike, 2026-05-08): Path 1 — add rf/frame-provider as a Reagent component to re-frame.core. Props-map shape ({:frame frame-id} + children). Thin wrapper over build-frame-provider; the factory becomes lower-level substrate. Implementation bead filed.

title: Decide reg-view body: auto-inject dispatch/subscribe vs require dispatcher/subscriber capture
description:
The guide (docs/guide/02 §The view, docs/guide/04 §The Var-reference idiom) states that inside a `reg-view` body, `dispatch` and `subscribe` are *injected* — bound to the surrounding frame — so view bodies can be frame-blind:

    (rf/reg-view :counter
      (fn []
        [:div
         [:button {:on-click #(dispatch [:counter/inc])} "+"]
         [:span @(subscribe [:count])]]))

The implementation does NOT inject them. The real example in `examples/counter/core.cljs` requires explicit capture:

    (reg-view :counter-buttons
      (fn []
        (let [d (rf/dispatcher)
              s (rf/subscriber)]
          [:div
           [:button {:on-click #(d [:counter/dec])} "-"]
           [:span @(s [:count])]])))

Two paths:
1. **Make the impl match the guide** — the macro `reg-view` rewrites the body to bind locals `dispatch`/`subscribe` to frame-bound fns captured at render time. Cost: macro complexity + potential aliasing surprises if the user already has those names in scope. Win: the prose claim becomes true and view bodies are visibly frame-blind.
2. **Make the guide match the impl** — document the `(rf/dispatcher)` / `(rf/subscriber)` capture pattern as canonical. Cost: every view body now opens with two `let` bindings; less elegant. Win: zero macro magic.

Decision needed before mechanical doc fixes can complete the namespace sweep — without a direction, every view example in the guide either over-promises or under-explains.
notes:
Reagent v2 directive (2026-05-08): re-frame2 targets Reagent 2, not 1.2. This strengthens the Form-1-only recommendation: Reagent 2's hooks-based functional components make Form-3 (class-based lifecycle methods) even less idiomatic. The macro-injection-into-Form-3 problems (data-shaped body, var-bound bodies invisible to the macro, lifecycle methods running outside render context) become 'don't bother' rather than 'too hard'. Form-1 + Form-2 (runtime-detected) is the pragmatic scope; Form-3 users continue to use explicit (rf/dispatcher) / (rf/subscriber) capture if they need it.
Resolved (Mike, 2026-05-08): auto-inject + defn-shape. Final surface: reg-view (macro, defn-shaped, auto-defs symbol, auto-injects dispatch/subscribe, compile-errors on non-literal-fn bodies — Form-1 + Form-2 only) + reg-view* (public fn, no magic, for metaprogramming). Both in re-frame.core. Implementation bead filed (see linked task). Form-3 explicitly out of scope per rf2-25aq Reagent v2 directive.

title: Spec: should normative requirements be rewritten in RFC 2119 phrasing (MUST / SHOULD / MAY)?
description:
Question raised during the implementation work: would the spec corpus (docs/specification/*.md) read more cleanly — and produce fewer 'is this mandatory or just descriptive?' beads — if the normative language followed RFC 2119 conventions (MUST / SHOULD / MAY etc.)?

Motivation:
- Several beads filed this session (rf2-r044, rf2-smba, rf2-sklf, rf2-pz9f) exist because the current spec phrasing doesn't separate 'this is the contract' from 'this is what the reference impl does'. Normative keywords force that separation.
- AI implementers (the spec's stated audience per Goal 2) parse MUST / SHOULD reliably; conformance fixtures could be mechanically cross-referenced, since each fixture pins a specific MUST.
- Cross-runtime portability — host language ports targeting the same spec need the same MUST / MAY split.

Counter-arguments:
- The current spec has a distinctive narrative voice. Wholesale RFC-2119-ification would flatten it. The 'spec-as-argument' function (000-Vision arguing why re-frame2 exists) doesn't suit normative phrasing.
- Cost: ~14 spec docs to rewrite, all cross-references break.

Suggested middle path (cheapest experiment first):
- Pick ONE doc — 002-Frames.md is the most heavily-referenced contract.
- Add a 'Contract' / 'Normative requirements' block near the top with MUST / SHOULD / MAY bullets, each cross-referenced to its conformance fixture(s).
- Leave the rest of the doc in narrative voice for rationale / examples.
- See if it earns its keep — easier to evolve, mechanically grep-able for fixture mapping, doesn't lose readability.
- If yes, scale to other docs; if it clutters more than helps, abandon.

Either way, this is a Mike-judgement call. Filed for explicit consideration rather than as an action item.
notes:
Resolved (Mike, 2026-05-09): Option B — pilot in 002-Frames.md.

Add a Normative Requirements (Contract) block to 002-Frames.md, placed at the END of the doc (or as a sister file 002-Frames-Contract.md). MUST/SHOULD/MAY bullets, each cross-linked to its conformance fixture(s). Leave the rest of 002-Frames.md in narrative voice.

Implementation bead filed. After the pilot lands, observe for a few weeks: if no spec-clarity beads land against 002-Frames AND the format earns its keep with conformance-fixture cross-referencing, scale to other heavily-referenced specs (004-Views, 005-StateMachines, 006-ReactiveSubstrate, 009-Instrumentation, 010-Schemas, 014-HTTPRequests). If it clutters more than it helps, abandon.

title: reg-view macro: outer (def x ...) wrapper double-defs against the macro's own def
description:
Across the v2-API-aligned examples (login/, realworld/*, routing/, 7guis/, ssr/) every reg-view call follows the pattern:

  (def login-form
    (reg-view :auth.login/form
      {:doc "..."}
      (fn render-auth-login-form [] ...)))

The reg-view macro (re-frame-2.views-macros) expands to
  (def <kw-name> (reg-view* <kw> <meta> <fn>))
i.e. (def form (reg-view* :auth.login/form ...)) — which evaluates to the var #'form.

The outer (def login-form ...) then binds login-form to that var, producing a var-of-var. Reagent's auto-deref MAY rescue this in practice but it's brittle and Spec 004 §reg-view defs the Var by default already commits to the macro defining the Var with the keyword's name.

Pick one resolution:
(a) Drop the outer (def some-name ...) wrappers across the examples and reference [form] / [banner] / [root] / [preview] / [home] / etc. directly — the macro already binds them.
(b) Change the macro so reg-view does NOT auto-def — let users wrap in their own (def some-name (reg-view* ...)). Spec 004 §reg-view defs the Var would need to relax.
(c) Extend the macro to accept an optional :as some-name so users pick the local name.

Implementation today follows (a) per Spec 004; the recently-landed example commit landed (b)-style wrappers without removing the macro's auto-def. Pick a direction and align spec, macro, and examples in one pass.
notes:
Empirically: in CLJS the double-def works because (def x y) at the CLJS level evaluates y and returns the SAME JS value (no Var-objects like JVM Clojure). So (def login-form (reg-view :auth/form ...)) results in BOTH login-form AND form bound to the same MetaFn — two names, same value. Verified via reg-view-macro-double-def-investigation in runtime-cljs-test (commit pending).

The pattern works at runtime but is still:
- Two names for the same view (semantic duplication / drift risk).
- Spec 004 says reg-view defs the Var by-keyword-name (so 'login-form' shouldn't exist; only 'form' should).
- Reliant on CLJS-specific def-returns-value semantics; the same pattern in JVM-side code would create var-of-var.

Reduces severity from 'breaks at runtime' to 'spec-vs-example mismatch worth aligning when convenient'. Pick a resolution per the bead body.
Verified after rf2-d0pi merged. Grep across examples/ + implementation/ shows zero (def x (reg-view ...)) wrappers in source code. The defn-shape macro now emits the def itself; the old wrapper shape no longer compiles (compile-error contract pinned by reg-view-rejects-* tests). Doc drift in docs/guide/ + docs/specification/Construction-Prompts.md is tracked separately by rf2-xv7e. Added regression guard test 'rf2-atsv: expansion contains exactly one def form' in implementation/test/re_frame/views_macros_test.clj that asserts vm/expand-reg-view emits exactly one def for plain / docstring / metadata-override input shapes. All 10 views-macros tests green.

title: Spec: 013-Flows must reflect frame-scoped registration / clear semantics
description:
Spec 013 currently treats flows as a process-global registry — reg-flow and clear-flow take only a flow id; the description in §Drain integration / §Reference doesn't say which frame they belong to. The implementation has now moved to frame-scoped flows (commit 86ade3a):

  (rf/reg-flow flow)              ; defaults frame to (current-frame)
  (rf/reg-flow flow {:frame :f})  ; explicit
  (rf/clear-flow id)
  (rf/clear-flow id {:frame :f})

Update Spec 013 to:
- State explicitly that flows are frame-scoped (per the rf2-jflj rationale: undo / time-travel boundaries are frame-local).
- Document the :frame opt on reg-flow / clear-flow.
- Update §Drain integration to clarify run-flows! evaluates only the called frame's flows.
- Update §Reference to show the registry shape: {frame-id {flow-id flow-map}}.
- Re-affirm that the :rf.fx/reg-flow / :rf.fx/clear-flow runtime fx receive {:frame frame-id} from the cofx and thread it through.
close_reason: Stale in_progress cleanup — fix commits on origin/main per git log sweep 2026-05-13. Bead status was never flipped after PR merge.

title: Spec: h macro view-vs-DOM-tag heuristic is undocumented
description:
