title: Worktree hygiene cron: skip 'permission-denied' worktrees instead of nuking their git pointers
description:
From rf2-fn5rk PR #671's process note: my hygiene-sweep cron destroyed rf2-fn5rk's worktree mid-flight. The git pointer was removed even though the filesystem dir was permission-denied (Windows file lock from the running agent). The agent's process kept going, lost its worktree-tracking, and had to recreate as agent-rf2-fn5rk-redo and reapply 4 edits.

Self-healed cleanly, but it shouldn't have happened.

Fix the hygiene-sweep loop prompt to:
1. Check if 'git worktree remove -f -f <path>' returns a permission-denied error
2. If yes: SKIP that worktree (its agent process is still running)
3. Don't proceed with the git pointer removal — leave it intact so the agent's process doesn't see its tracking disappear

Current loop wording (in cron 93fea78b at :23 hourly):
'for each worktree under .claude/worktrees/agent-*, check whether its branch has any commits unique to it via git log <branch> --not origin/main. If empty: the work has landed; run git worktree remove -f -f <path>. Skip any worktree whose branch carries unique commits — those are mid-flight or unpushed work.'

Amendment needed: also skip worktrees that produce permission-denied errors on removal.

Source: rf2-fn5rk PR #671 process note.
notes: PR: https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/723. Cron prompt is in Mike's personal cron registration (not in-repo). Documented the corrected skip-logic prose in docs/the-mayor-method.md (new 'Worktree hygiene' section). Follow-on bead rf2-q20wq filed for Mike to update his live cron registration.
close_reason: Closed

title: Bundle-comparison verifier: filter to top-level main.js or clean output dir before release
description:
scripts/check-counter-slim-and-fast.cjs uses readAllJs which walks recursively under out/examples/<build>/. If a previous `shadow-cljs compile` leaves a cljs-runtime/ debug source dir, the next `release` run won't clean it and the script greps both the release main.js AND the dev sources, producing false FAILs.

Repro: `npx shadow-cljs compile examples/counter-slim-and-fast` (creates cljs-runtime/), then `npm run test:bundle-comparison` — false FAILs on cljsLegacyRender (1 hit from leftover dev output) and react-dom/server (4 hits).

Fix options:
  (a) restrict readAllJs to top-level *.js only (filter out cljs-runtime/)
  (b) clean out/examples/<build>/ before running shadow-cljs release in the npm script
  (c) require shadow-cljs release to write to a separate dir

Spotted during rf2-tba5e smoke-test of reagent-slim. The CI is unaffected (clean dir per run); the trap is local-dev only.

Surfaces touched: implementation/scripts/check-counter-slim-and-fast.cjs (the verifier). Possibly implementation/package.json scripts.
notes: PR: https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/719
close_reason: Closed

title: spec/009 §Size elision: add DESIGN-RATIONALE paragraph for why markers stay separate (sensitive vs large)
description:
Follow-on from rf2-vbk6j (kept rf2-hmmx7's separate-markers lock-in). The unified-marker intuition is structurally beautiful — a future agent will likely re-ask 'why aren't these the same?'. Add a single explainer paragraph in spec/009 §Size elision composition (or a parallel DESIGN-RATIONALE Lock in the appropriate spec doc) capturing the three reasons:

1. Path-leak risk is STRUCTURALLY WORSE with unified marker — hoisting :path to its own field advertises 'this slot is worth redacting' (stronger breadcrumb than today's per-key :rf/redacted sentinel)
2. Handle is REDUNDANT for sensitive — when :include-sensitive? true, value is inline (no marker); when false, event vanishes (nothing to fetch from). No useful in-between state.
3. Defense-in-depth would be LOST — today: trace stream is read-only metadata (one boundary) + get-path auth-gates retrieval (second boundary). A handle-bearing sensitive marker collapses these into one policy decision.

Plus: the size axis genuinely NEEDS the handle (5MB value can't ride the wire even when wanted); sensitive has no size pressure (a redacted string fits inline). The handle's value is asymmetric, not symmetric.

Source: rf2-vbk6j PR-not-filed findings doc ai/findings/sensitive-vs-large-technique-20260513-1823.md
notes: PR: https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/736
close_reason: Closed

title: Tool-Pair.md §Wire-protocol mechanisms: consolidate canonical home or update findings cross-refs
description:
Follow-on from rf2-hmmx7 (PR #664). The rf2-vnmt6 + rf2-ok47g findings docs reference 'Tool-Pair.md §Wire-protocol mechanisms' as if a section by that name exists in spec/Tool-Pair.md. It does not. The actual catalogue lives in tools/pair2-mcp/spec/Principles.md (§Token budget cap + sub-sections) and is reflected in DESIGN-RATIONALE.md.

Two options to resolve the cross-ref inconsistency:

(a) Consolidate: add a §Wire-protocol mechanisms section to spec/Tool-Pair.md that catalogues the 6 mechanisms (cap, path slicing, pagination, lazy summary, dedup, size elision) as the cross-MCP canonical home. Each tool's Principles.md then points up to Tool-Pair.md.

(b) Update findings cross-refs: amend the 2 findings docs to point at tools/pair2-mcp/spec/Principles.md instead of Tool-Pair.md.

(a) is more consistent with the spec-is-the-artefact thesis — the cross-MCP shared vocabulary should live in the canonical spec dir, not in a per-tool subdirectory. But (a) is the larger scope (requires Tool-Pair.md amendment + per-tool back-reference updates).

Source: rf2-hmmx7 PR #664 dropped a broken Tool-Pair.md anchor reference; agent left this question for Mike's call.

title: SSR: register :rf.ssr/check-version + :rf.ssr/check-schema-digest fxs — spec dispatches them, no handler
description:
Per ssr-audit-20260513-1757 findings (rf2-ul137) gap #12. Spec 011 §272–276 names two fxs the :rf/hydrate handler dispatches:

- :rf.ssr/check-version — compares the payload's :rf/version against the runtime's; mismatch emits :rf.ssr/version-mismatch trace.
- :rf.ssr/check-schema-digest — when payload includes a digest, hashes the client's registered app-schema set and compares to the server's digest; mismatch emits :rf.ssr/schema-digest-mismatch.

NEITHER is registered in re-frame.ssr.

The reference :rf/hydrate handler at ssr.cljc:462 doesn't dispatch them either (it only stashes server-hash + version in app-db).

The worked example's hand-rolled :rf/hydrate (examples/reagent/ssr/core.cljc:112) dispatches both of them:

  {:db app-db
   :fx (cond-> [[:rf.ssr/check-version version]]
         schema-digest (conj [:rf.ssr/check-schema-digest schema-digest]))}

These dispatches will silently surface :rf.error/no-such-handler traces at hydration time.

## Recommendation
Three options:

A) **Land both fxs** in re-frame.ssr. They're cheap:
   - :rf.ssr/check-version: compare payload version against (resolve 'rf2-version) or equivalent; emit warning trace on mismatch.
   - :rf.ssr/check-schema-digest: hash (rf/handlers :schema) ids; emit warning trace on mismatch.
   AND wire them into the reference :rf/hydrate handler so the example's pattern works out-of-the-box.

B) **Drop them from spec**. The :rf/version slot is already stashed in [:rf/hydration :version]; the schema-digest case is rare. Apps can register their own check handlers if they care.

C) **Mark as deferred** with a banner like reg-head's.

Recommendation: A (implement). Schema-digest mismatches are a real production concern when deploy-drift puts mismatched schemas on server vs client.

## Surfaces touched
- implementation/ssr/src/re_frame/ssr.cljc (register both fxs; update :rf/hydrate to dispatch them).
- spec/011-SSR.md §272–276 (no spec change if going with A).

## Acceptance
- (rf/reg-fx :rf.ssr/check-version ...) and :rf.ssr/check-schema-digest are registered with :platforms #{:client}.
- Reference :rf/hydrate handler dispatches both when payload carries the keys.
- Mismatch traces fire as documented.

## Severity rationale (P3)
Not blocking — :rf.error/no-such-handler is recoverable; default projector maps it to 404 server-side but client-side it's just a trace. But it's a clear spec/impl drift; should resolve.
notes: PR: https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/731
close_reason: Closed

title: Meta: file a cross-cutting designs inventory (wire elision + retro protocol + token budgets + naming + origin)
description:
Source: rf2-zf7zd investigation (ai/findings/improver-architecture-20260513-1752.md §The unified-improver-surface proposal §Resonance).

## The cross-cutting-design pattern

Multiple session-13 investigations converged on cross-cutting design surfaces — patterns that span multiple artefacts and that each artefact would otherwise re-derive locally. Examples emerged independently:

1. **Wire elision** (rf2-vnmt6 large-path elision + rf2-ok47g sensitive-audit elision) — unified elision surface for "don't put X on wire"
2. **Retro protocol** (rf2-dhe9v from this bead) — unified retro surface for "critique a body of work"
3. **Token-budget posture** (rf2-ll0yq umbrella) — unified budget surface for "cap MCP response size"
4. **Cross-MCP naming conventions** (rf2-fvy7o finding #5) — unified naming surface for "tool name → behaviour mapping"
5. **Origin tagging** (pair2-mcp + story-mcp + causa-mcp + causa overlay) — unified origin surface for "where did this event come from?"

## The meta-pattern

Pre-alpha is the time when cross-cutting designs emerge. The codebase is small enough that one pattern reused in 3 places is visible to a single investigator. Once the codebase grows, each instance evolves independently and the shared pattern is lost.

Filing a cross-cutting designs inventory NOW captures the shape before the divergence happens.

## Proposed deliverable

A new spec or docs artefact enumerating each cross-cutting design surface:

- Location candidates: `spec/CrossCutting.md`, `docs/development/cross-cutting-designs.md`, or extend `spec/Conventions.md` with a §Cross-cutting designs section
- Per entry: name, the pattern, the consumers (skills/MCP servers/tool artefacts that implement it), the canonical specification location, the open evolution direction

## Process

Future investigations check the inventory before designing a new local implementation of a cross-cutting concern. When a new cross-cutting concern is identified, file a bead to add it to the inventory.

## Why P3

Meta-bead; quality-of-design improvement, not a blocking feature. Mike-decision on whether to spawn an inventory artefact now or defer until v0.1.

## Cross-refs

- ai/findings/improver-architecture-20260513-1752.md §The unified-improver-surface proposal + §Resonance with rf2-vnmt6 + rf2-ok47g
- ai/findings/skills-mcp-interaction-20260513-1519.md §Finding 4 (cross-MCP naming)
- bead rf2-vnmt6 (large-path elision investigation, sibling-running)
- bead rf2-ok47g (sensitive-audit elision investigation, sibling-running)
- bead rf2-ll0yq (token-budget umbrella)
- bead rf2-dhe9v (retro protocol extraction from this bead's findings)
notes: PR: https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/741

title: Populate re-frame2-improver anti-pattern catalogue with 6 launch patterns
description:
Source: rf2-zf7zd investigation (ai/findings/improver-architecture-20260513-1752.md §Angle 2 §Pattern catalogue).

Depends on: rf2-sqw80 (skill must exist before catalogue is populated).

## Six launch anti-patterns

| # | Anti-pattern | Symptom in code | Canonical re-frame2 idiom | Detect-from |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Manual HTTP retry loops | cljs-http calls inside reg-event-fx with manual retry counters or setTimeout retries | Managed HTTP (Spec 014 / Pattern-RemoteData) | Repeated setTimeout + counter + same URL across 2+ events |
| 2 | Boolean discriminator subs for FSM states | reg-sub :screen/loading?, :screen/error?, :screen/empty?, :screen/loaded? all on same key | Tags query layer (Spec 005 §Tags + :rf/in-tag sub) | 3+ boolean subs on same path; FSM-shaped key set |
| 3 | Manual loading flags | (assoc db :foo/loading? true) + (dissoc ... :foo/loading?) | Nine States pattern (patterns/nine-states.md) | :*/loading? keys + manual flip events |
| 4 | Schemaless events at HTTP boundary | reg-event-fx :api/loaded with no :rf/event-schema and no reg-app-schema for the destination path | Spec 010 schemas | New event whose :effects include :http and whose :db writes to a previously-unschemed path |
| 5 | Imperative effects | (.setItem js/localStorage ...) in event handler body | Data-only fx via reg-fx (Spec 003 fundamentals) | Direct DOM/JS API calls inside reg-event-db/fx body |
| 6 | View-side state via React hooks for non-render-local state | reagent/atom inside view body for app-wide-visible state; useState for non-render-local concerns | Move to app-db + reg-sub | Reagent atom declared at component top + read by sibling component |

## Leaf shape per pattern

Option A — one leaf per pattern (~50-80 lines each, 6 leaves total):
- `patterns/manual-retry-loop.md`
- `patterns/boolean-discriminator-subs.md`
- `patterns/manual-loading-flags.md`
- `patterns/schemaless-boundary.md`
- `patterns/imperative-effects.md`
- `patterns/view-side-state.md`

Each leaf carries: detection signal / symptom shape / canonical idiom / migration recipe / cross-link to skills/re-frame2/patterns/<canonical>.md.

Option B — condensed: one `references/anti-patterns.md` (~250-300 lines) with all six in a table-and-detail layout.

Option B is preferred for launch (single file, easier to grow incrementally); refactor to Option A once the catalogue grows past 10 patterns.

## Cross-link policy

Each pattern entry must cross-link to the canonical pattern leaf it suggests adopting:

- Pattern #1 → skills/re-frame2/patterns/managed-http.md
- Pattern #2 → skills/re-frame2/reference/state-machines/tags.md
- Pattern #3 → skills/re-frame2/patterns/nine-states.md
- Pattern #4 → skills/re-frame2/reference/fundamentals/schemas.md
- Pattern #5 → skills/re-frame2/reference/fundamentals/fx.md
- Pattern #6 → skills/re-frame2/reference/fundamentals/subs.md

## Growth procedure

When a new anti-pattern surfaces across 3+ retro sessions, add it to the catalogue. Mirrors how `re-frame-pair-improver2/references/known-frictions.md` grows organically.

## Out of scope at launch (defer to later beads)

- Anti-pattern #7: view renders only happy state (no clauses for loading/error/empty)
- Anti-pattern #8: effect handlers writing to a foreign frame's app-db (Spec 002 frame isolation violation)
- Plus: anti-patterns involving stories, routing, decorators, state-machine misuse

## Cross-refs

- ai/findings/improver-architecture-20260513-1752.md §Angle 2
- skills/re-frame-pair-improver2/references/known-frictions.md (template for catalogue shape)
- spec/Pattern-RemoteData.md / spec/005-StateMachines.md / spec/010-Schemas.md / spec/014-HTTPRequests.md (canonical idiom sources)
notes:
PR #688 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/688

Populated all 6 launch anti-pattern leaves + restructured references/README.md catalogue index.

Leaves (453 LoC, 68-83 lines per leaf):
- manual-retry-loops.md           → Managed HTTP (Spec 014)
- boolean-discriminator-subs.md   → Tags layer (Spec 005)
- manual-loading-flags.md         → Nine States (Pattern-NineStates)
- schemaless-events.md            → Spec 010 schemas
- imperative-effects.md           → data-only fx (reg-fx)
- view-side-hook-state.md         → app-db + reg-sub

Per-leaf format: detection rules / why-it's-anti-pattern / canonical fix (cross-linked) / before-after worked example / edge cases. All cross-links to skills/re-frame2/patterns/, skills/re-frame2/reference/, and spec/ resolve. The 2 pre-existing broken-link entries (SKILL.md -> retro-protocol.md) are rf2-dhe9v territory and predate this PR.

Bonus anti-patterns (view-only-renders-happy-state, foreign-frame-app-db-writes) deferred per ai/findings/improver-architecture-20260513-1752.md §Angle 2.

Leaving open per dispatch instructions.
close_reason: Closed

title: [docs+spec] Document runtime auto-detect path + :rf.warning/runtime-large-elision
description:
**Source**: ai/findings/declare-large-path-20260513-1748.md rec 6 (rf2-vnmt6).

The runtime auto-detect path (option (d) in the findings survey) needs UX scaffolding so authors notice when the heuristic fires and either confirm (add a declared :large? entry) or override (declare :large? false).

**Spec work**:
- Add `:rf.warning/runtime-large-elision` to the §Error event catalogue in spec/009-Instrumentation.md. Trigger: walker auto-flags a value at a path with no existing declaration. Default :recovery: :informational (the trace event is the only output; behaviour proceeds).
- Add the per-category :tags schema entry in spec/Spec-Schemas.md §Per-category :tags schemas. Fields: :path (vector) :bytes (int) :frame (keyword) :max-bytes-cap (int — the threshold that tripped).
- One-shot per path within a frame (don't spam every emit) — implementation note for the walker.

**Skill docs**:
- skills/re-frame-pair2/ — add §"Large-value elision" reference doc covering the marker shape, the :elision-policy arg, the handle vocabulary, and the fetch pattern (get-path with the handle).
- skills/re-frame2/ — add a §"Declaring large paths" section to the existing setup or registrations guide, covering schema-driven (the recommended path) and fx-driven (the fallback for non-schema apps).

**Depends on**: rf2-v9tw2 (the walker exists), rf2-hmmx7 (the contract is specced).

**Acceptance**:
- New warning category in the catalogue + schema entry.
- Skill docs for both surfaces landed.
- A worked-example walkthrough in re-frame-pair2 showing the auto-detect → warning → declare-large → suppression flow.

**Note**: this is the discoverability layer; without it, auto-detect feels like "random elision" to authors. With it, the heuristic becomes self-documenting.

title: [causa-mcp] Spec :rf.size/large-elided as 6th wire-protocol mechanism
description:
**Source**: ai/findings/declare-large-path-20260513-1748.md rec 5 (rf2-vnmt6).

causa-mcp's `tools/causa-mcp/spec/Principles.md` already enumerates five wire mechanisms (rf2-lwgg8): cap, slicing, pagination, lazy-summary, dedup. Add size-elision as mechanism #6.

**Scope**:
- New §"6. Large-value elision" subsection in §Wire-protocol mechanisms.
- Marker shape: `{:rf.size/large-elided {...}}` — same as pair2-mcp's (rf2-urjnc).
- Per-tool opt-out via `:include-large? true` arg (default false).
- Default `:max-bytes` aligned with pair2-mcp.

**Catalogue contract**: when `tools/causa-mcp/spec/003-Tool-Catalogue.md` lands, every tool that emits tree-typed payloads MUST declare its default `:elision-policy`. Add this to the catalogue-entry contract pinned in §Tight token budget per response.

**DESIGN-RATIONALE lock**: add "Lock #10 — size-elision marker shape is normative across MCP servers" to `tools/causa-mcp/spec/DESIGN-RATIONALE.md` (same pattern as Lock #9 for the existing five mechanisms).

**Depends on**: rf2-hmmx7 (the spec contract for the marker shape).

**Hot zone**: causa-mcp Principles.md, DESIGN-RATIONALE.md.

**Acceptance**:
- §6 added to Principles.md with the marker shape.
- Catalogue contract amended.
- DESIGN-RATIONALE Lock #10 added.
- Cross-link to spec/009 §Size elision and pair2-mcp Principles §Size-elision wire markers.
notes: PR: https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/715

title: [spec/Conventions] Cross-MCP indicator vocabulary alignment for elision counters
description:
Audit rf2-ok47g §G12 — the four implemented consumers surface suppression indicators in three different shapes:
- Causa panel: '[● REDACTED N]' in the bottom rail
- Story panel: '[● REDACTED]' with N suppressed in the trace panel
- pair2-mcp / story-mcp: ':dropped-sensitive N' field on every MCP response

Same concept; defensible variation (panel chrome vs JSON field) but unspec'd. Same problem will recur for rf2-vnmt6's :large? mechanism (':elided-large' analog).

Required:
- Specify the field name as ':dropped-sensitive' for MCP-shaped returns (currently the de-facto standard).
- Recommend '[● REDACTED N]' for panel chrome with N suppressed count.
- Add a §Suppression-indicator subsection to spec/Conventions.md.
- Forward-compat: name the related :large? field consistently (':elided-large' or similar).

Source: ai/findings/sensitive-elision-audit-20260513-1748.md §G12

title: [spec/010 + spec/009] Path-level :sensitive? declaration via :rf/schema metadata
description:
Audit rf2-ok47g §G11 — sibling to rf2-vnmt6 (path-level :large? declaration). A schema-driven approach where :rf/schema declares :sensitive? true on a sub-tree would be ergonomic for apps that already have schemas. Cross-cuts the registration-time metadata approach (spec/009 line 1155+); choose one home or compose both.

Required:
- Design how :sensitive? on a Malli subtree propagates to trace events that include data at that path.
- Decide composition with registration-time :sensitive? (overrides? widens? path-scoped subset?).
- Joint design with rf2-vnmt6 — both want path-level declaration; should share form even if semantics differ.
- Per the audit recommendation: same shape, two flags, one helper. Schema-driven path declaration is the natural shared home.

Source: ai/findings/sensitive-elision-audit-20260513-1748.md §G11 + rf2-vnmt6 cross-pollination

title: [spec/009] Investigate richer path forms for with-redacted (wildcards, predicate-paths)
description:
Audit rf2-ok47g §Elegance assessment noted that with-redacted paths vocabulary is spartan in v1 — literal-keys vectors only. Spec 009 line 1224 explicitly says 'Implementations are free to extend the vocabulary to accept richer path forms (e.g. wildcards, predicate-paths)'. The v1 contract is intentional but creates usability friction: a form with 30 PII fields needs 30 paths.

Required:
- Survey the path-vocabulary axes: wildcard segments (':*'), predicate-paths ('(fn [k v] (sensitive-key? k))'), Malli-driven (':sensitive? true' on a schema subtree applied transitively).
- Design recommendation with rationale.
- Likely depends on G11 (schema-driven :sensitive? declaration) — investigate jointly.

Source: ai/findings/sensitive-elision-audit-20260513-1748.md §G10

title: [Tool-Pair] Document direct-read privacy posture for sub-cache and get-path
description:
Audit rf2-ok47g §Composition tests + §G9 found that direct-read surfaces (sub-cache via rf/sub-cache, app-db via get-path) bypass :sensitive? entirely. :sensitive? is a trace-event flag; direct reads against app-db return the live value. with-redacted only scrubs the trace emit, not the live db.

This is architecturally correct (the trust boundary is the trace surface plus MCP egress) but creates a privacy hole that needs documentation. pair2-mcp's scrub-snapshot-sensitive explicitly passes sub-cache through unchanged with the comment 'redaction of those payloads is the with-redacted interceptor job, not the forwarder job'.

Required:
- Add a §Direct-read privacy posture subsection to spec/Tool-Pair.md (or DESIGN-RATIONALE in pair2-mcp / causa-mcp).
- Document: apps that need path-level privacy use with-redacted on the writing handler, OR do not put sensitive values in app-db at all, OR use a future path-level privacy mechanism (G11).
- Note get-path bypasses :sensitive? by design.

Source: ai/findings/sensitive-elision-audit-20260513-1748.md §G9

title: scripts/check_skill_mcp_drift.py: trim stale _BASELINE entries for subscribe/unsubscribe
description:
Follow-on from rf2-nkygg (PR #650). The drift-check script emits a warning that the _BASELINE entries silencing subscribe and unsubscribe drift are now stale (those tools have been added to allowed-tools via rf2-aks1t PR #633). The baseline silencing succeeded; now it should be removed so the script returns to true-state.

Scope: just scripts/check_skill_mcp_drift.py — delete the subscribe and unsubscribe entries from the _BASELINE collection. Run --ci to confirm 0-exit AND no warning.

title: test(causa): extend causality_graph view test — pan/zoom, hover, click-to-select-dispatch
description:
Per rf2-otcbz findings doc, recommendation #9.

`tools/causa/test/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/causality_graph_view_cljs_test.cljs` is thin (9 t / 23 a) given the panel's algorithmic weight (`panels/causality_graph.cljs` 332 LoC + `causality_graph_helpers.cljc` 498 LoC = 830 LoC across view + helpers).

Helpers are well-covered by `causality_graph_cljs_test.cljc` (23 t / 70 a covering project-cascades-to-graph, compute-layout, filter-to-cascade, enrich-cascades). The **view wiring** is light. The panel is the structural centrepiece of Causa per spec/001-Causality-Graph.md.

Add view-test coverage for the SVG hiccup tree's interactive layer:

  - Click on a dispatch node → fires `:rf.causa/select-dispatch-id` with the right id.
  - Hover on a dispatch node → fires the highlight-cascade event (or pure CSS hover if that's the v1 path; assert testid sets the correct hover-class).
  - Pan/zoom state (if v1 ships interactive — confirm with the panel source first; if v1 is static, leave a comment).
  - Empty graph (no cascades in buffer) — assert empty-state SVG renders rather than a zero-dimension viewbox.
  - Cap at 200 dispatches — assert when buffer has >200 cascades, the panel renders only the most-recent 200 (per spec/001-Causality-Graph.md L92).

Source: rf2-otcbz findings doc at `ai/findings/causa-test-coverage-20260513-1706.md`, recommendation #9.

title: test(causa): extend event-detail view test — empty buffer, missing dispatch-id, sensitive-redacted states
description:
Per rf2-otcbz findings doc, recommendation #8.

`tools/causa/test/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/event_detail_cljs_test.cljs` is the lightest panel test (9 t / 19 a) for the heaviest user-facing surface (`panels/event_detail.cljs` at 380 LoC — the hero panel of Causa per spec/007-UX-IA.md). The panel has no helpers file; algebra is inlined in the view.

Existing tests cover the happy path. Missing edge cases:

  - Empty buffer — selected-dispatch-id present but no events exist; assert panel renders an explicit empty state rather than crashing on a nil event.
  - Dispatch-id not in buffer — selected-dispatch-id refers to a cascade that's been evicted; assert the panel renders a 'cascade unavailable' state.
  - Sensitive-redacted cascade — the selected dispatch-id's cascade has been suppressed by the privacy gate (the trace events never landed); assert the panel surfaces the redaction state distinctly from 'cascade unavailable'.
  - No selected-dispatch-id (initial state) — assert the panel renders the spec/007-UX-IA.md §Default landing view guidance.

Source: rf2-otcbz findings doc at `ai/findings/causa-test-coverage-20260513-1706.md`, recommendation #8.

title: Causa spec: :rf.causa/epoch-collector epoch-pump callback is unspec'd
description: Investigation rf2-j41ya found tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/preload.cljs L74–99 registers a rf/register-epoch-cb! callback that dispatches :rf.causa/epoch-recorded into the :rf/causa frame on every settle — this is how Causa stays reactive to host state without polling. tools/causa/spec/API.md §Trace surfaces only mentions register-epoch-cb! as something Causa 'reads'; the *pump* (Causa fires its own dispatch on every host settle) isn't documented. Recommendation: brief paragraph in the new 013-Trace-Bus.md (rf2-lc3hy) or 011-Launch-Modes.md §Install describing the per-settle pump. Findings: ai/findings/causa-impl-spec-gap-20260513-1705.md Gap #7.

title: pair2-mcp DESIGN-RATIONALE: add Lock entry for wire-cap design (egress-centralised + pluggable strategy + truncate-with-marker)
description:
Follow-on from rf2-rvyzy (PR #641). The cap enforcement landed in pair2-mcp's Principles.md (MUST-level), but the comparative analysis — WHY this design — wasn't captured in tools/pair2-mcp/spec/DESIGN-RATIONALE.md.

Follow the rf2-lwgg8 pattern (PR #642 added Lock #9 to causa-mcp's DESIGN-RATIONALE.md). For pair2-mcp's DESIGN-RATIONALE.md, add a Lock entry that captures:

**Decision**: enforce token cap at the invoke/egress boundary (centralised), not per-tool.
**Options considered**:
- (a) Per-tool: each tool function knows its cap. Rejected: cap-logic scattered across many functions, hard to compose new strategies.
- (b) Egress-centralised + pluggable strategy (CHOSEN): wrapper at invoke boundary, :strategy keyword lets future beads (path slicing, lazy summary, diff encoding) plug in without rebuilding the wrapper.
- (c) Caller-side: agent enforces its own budget. Rejected: agent context already corrupted by then.
**Overflow shape decision**: truncate + {:rf.mcp/overflow {...}} marker, NOT silent truncation or refuse-with-error.
**Token rule**: (quot (count s) 4) on serialised string; sum across multi-part :content slots.

Source: rf2-rvyzy PR #641 'Key design decisions'; investigation context in ai/findings/wire-protocol-bigapp-20260513-1541.md.

When this lands, the findings doc loses one more reason to exist — once all 12 wire beads have their corresponding spec + rationale entries, the findings doc can be deleted.

title: [skills/re-frame-pair2] Document de-dupe expand decoder + size-conscious tool args
description:
Source: rf2-jlq5j findings doc (ai/findings/wire-protocol-bigapp-20260513-1541.md), recommendation #12

Once rf2-obpa9 (de-dupe integration) lands, tool responses carry the deduped wire form. Agents need to know how to expand. Once rf2-tygdv (path slicing) + rf2-u2029 (lazy summary) + rf2-1wdzp (diff encoding) land, agents need to know when to use each.

## Scope

Update `skills/re-frame-pair2/`:

1. New reference: `skills/re-frame-pair2/references/wire-size-budget.md` covering:
   - The 5K-token cap and how to recognise `:budget-exceeded` responses.
   - Path slicing (`:path` arg on snapshot + get-path tool).
   - Lazy summary (`:mode :sample` default) — when to ask for `:full`.
   - Diff-encoded epochs default — when to opt in with `:full-epoch-snapshots? true`.
   - de-dupe wire form — agent-side expand if not handled by host.
   - Cache responses (`:unchanged-since`).

2. Update SKILL.md trigger list to mention "investigating a large app-db".

3. Update allowed-tools front-matter if new tools land (get-path).

## When

After rf2-rvyzy, rf2-tygdv, rf2-1wdzp, rf2-obpa9 land — i.e. once the dependent code surfaces exist.

## Acceptance

- New reference doc exists and is linked from SKILL.md.
- Tested via a smoke story: agent asked "what's the auth state" on a 5MB app-db; agent uses get-path / `:path`, not snapshot-everything.

title: [pair2-mcp + story-mcp] Add typical-tokens hint to every tool descriptor
description:
Source: rf2-jlq5j findings doc (ai/findings/wire-protocol-bigapp-20260513-1541.md), recommendation #11

Per `tools/pair2-mcp/spec/Principles.md:174`: 'The hints surface in `list-tools` so the agent can plan ahead'. Today no tool descriptor in pair2-mcp's `tools.cljs:702 tool-descriptors` carries a token hint; same for `tools/story-mcp/src/re_frame/story_mcp/tools.cljc:551 tool-registry`. Helps the agent prefer `:include [:app-db]` + `:path` over `:include [:app-db :epochs :traces]` based on budget.

## Scope

Each entry in `tool-descriptors` (pair2-mcp) and `tool-registry` (story-mcp) gets a `:typical-tokens` slot in the descriptor, e.g.

```clojure
{:name "snapshot"
 :description "..."
 :typical-tokens "~3k under :sample mode; ~50k+ under :full"
 :inputSchema {...}}
```

Hint appears in the `tools/list` response.

## Dependencies

- For pair2-mcp, the hint values become real numbers AFTER rf2-rvyzy (cap enforcement) + rf2-1wdzp (diff encoding) + rf2-u2029 (lazy summary) land. Until then they're estimates per the worked example in the findings doc.
- For story-mcp, the hint values can be set now.

## Acceptance

- `tools/list` JSON response carries `typicalTokens` on every tool entry.
- Spec/003-Tool-Catalogue documents the hint contract.
- Hints are calibrated against real-world snapshots (track in the findings doc tests).

title: [causa-mcp] Bake cap + slicing + pagination + lazy-summary into spec before implementation
description:
Source: rf2-jlq5j findings doc (ai/findings/wire-protocol-bigapp-20260513-1541.md), recommendation #10

causa-mcp is spec-only today (`tools/causa-mcp/spec/`). pair2-mcp's normative cap (`tools/pair2-mcp/spec/Principles.md:127`) exists but is unenforced in code — that drift is expensive to retire (rf2-rvyzy / rf2-tygdv / etc). For causa-mcp, baking the budget posture into the spec BEFORE implementation begins is much cheaper than retrofitting.

## Scope

When the causa-mcp `001-Wire-Protocol`, `002-nREPL-Transport`, `003-Tool-Catalogue` specs land (currently the spec folder has 000-Vision, Principles, DESIGN-RATIONALE, README), each tool entry MUST carry:

- `typical-tokens` hint (e.g. ~1.2k, ~3k under :sample).
- Cap-reached behaviour (truncate-with-cursor / `:budget-exceeded`).
- Slicing surface where applicable (path-based for any app-db read).
- Pagination surface for unbounded lists (cursor + default :limit fitting the cap).
- Lazy-summary mode for rich payloads (default :sample, opt-in :full).

Mirror the pair2-mcp Principles §"Tight token budget per response" verbatim.

## When

Before causa-mcp `tools.cljs` (or equivalent) lands.

## Acceptance

- causa-mcp/spec/003-Tool-Catalogue (when authored) lists all tools with token hints and cap behaviour.
- causa-mcp/spec/001-Wire-Protocol (when authored) declares the same 5K cap as pair2-mcp.
- No tool in causa-mcp ships without these slots.
notes: PR #642 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/642. Five mechanisms baked into tools/causa-mcp/spec/Principles.md §'Tight token budget per response' with MUST wording + reserved :rf.mcp/overflow / :rf.mcp/summary / :rf.mcp/dedup-table payload shapes. DESIGN-RATIONALE Lock #9 added. Catalogue-entry contract binds 003-Tool-Catalogue.md when it lands.
close_reason: Closed

title: stories.md schema-derivation: add variant-side :rf/schema row when Spec 010 lands
description:
Follow-on from rf2-7iks3 (PR #634). The schema-derivation pipeline section in skills/re-frame2/reference/tooling/stories.md documents how the Malli walker generates controls from a component's :rf/schema. Spec 010 (referenced in tools/story-mcp/spec/001-Authoring.md) introduces variant-side :rf/schema with a forward-compat path for 'resolve-argtypes' — letting a variant declare its own schema override.

When Spec 010 lands and the variant-side :rf/schema is implementable, the stories.md schema-derivation table needs a new row covering variant-side schema (precedence relative to component schema, what happens when both are declared, fallback behaviour).

This is a doc-only follow-up gated on Spec 010 landing. File this so the doc update isn't forgotten when the spec ships.

Source: rf2-7iks3 PR #634 'Source-material gaps noticed'.

title: pair2-mcp: expose subscription-info as MCP tool (avoid eval-cljs round-trip)
description:
Follow-on from rf2-aks1t (PR #633). The streaming-subscriptions leaf documents using the public 'subscription-info' fn for the 'what's currently subscribed?' diagnostic, invoked via eval-cljs. This works but forces an eval-cljs round-trip for an introspection that's natural to expose as an MCP tool.

Fix: add a 'subscription-info' (or 'list-subscriptions') tool to tools/pair2-mcp/src/re_frame_pair2_mcp/tools.cljs that wraps the runtime fn. Update skills/re-frame-pair2/references/streaming-subscriptions.md to use the MCP tool form instead of eval-cljs.

When this lands, update the skill's allowed-tools to include the new MCP tool (per the rf2-aks1t pattern).

Source: rf2-aks1t PR #633 'Out-of-scope observations'.

title: pair2: add :timing-ms predicate to epoch-matches? for server-side timing filter
description:
Follow-on from rf2-aks1t (PR #633). The pair2 'subscribe' MCP tool's epoch-matching vocab (epoch-matches? in runtime.cljs ~L1148-1186) has no :timing-ms predicate. The 'Alert me on slow events' recipe in skills/re-frame-pair2/references/recipes.md works around this by subscribing without a server-side timing filter and applying the threshold caller-side on each progress tick. Slight ergonomic regression vs the bash-shim --timing-ms flag.

Fix: add :timing-ms (or equivalent) to epoch-matches?'s filter vocab so the filter rides server-side and the wire format stays narrow. Update streaming-subscriptions.md leaf to use it and remove the caller-side workaround.

Source: rf2-aks1t PR #633 'Key decisions' note.

title: re-frame-pair-improver2: allow discover-app for live tool-catalogue introspection
description:
**Source**: rf2-fvy7o findings doc, finding #7 + recommendation #6.

The improver skill is retrospective: it analyses a just-finished pair2 session for friction. Its `allowed-tools` correctly excludes the live-runtime tools (it doesn't operate on the runtime). But it can't introspect the pair2-mcp tool catalogue either — when reasoning "did the user reach for the right tool?" the agent has no way to list what tools the server exposes.

## What to do

Add `mcp__re-frame-pair2__discover-app` to `skills/re-frame-pair-improver2/SKILL.md` front-matter `allowed-tools`. This tool is a read-only marker probe (no runtime mutation, no side effects beyond a bencode round-trip). It returns the build id and health summary; together with the tool catalogue (declared in pair2-mcp's `tools/list`) the improver can sanity-check "was tool X actually available in the user's session?"

## Alternative: docs-only fix

Add a "pair2 tool catalogue (frozen)" subsection to `references/known-frictions.md` so the improver can reason about which tools exist without needing the live probe. This is lower-leverage because it drifts (see rf2-flzdp on the same drift problem for the canonical consumer).

## Why P3

Lower-priority because the canonical lists are in `re-frame-pair2/references/ops.md` which the improver can `Read`. The improvement is small — surfaces the live build's id to the retrospective. File and revisit if a retrospective session shows the improver guessing wrong about tool availability.
close_reason: Closed

title: re-frame-pair-improver2: add :on-error policy analysis lens
description: Follow-on from rf2-bf409 (PR #629). The pair2 SKILL now documents the :on-error closed return shape and :recovery enum (skills/re-frame-pair2/references/on-error.md). The sibling improver2 skill's analysis lenses (skills/re-frame-pair-improver2/references/analysis-lenses.md) should gain a corresponding lens that surfaces :on-error policy issues observed during pair sessions — bad return shapes, exceptions thrown from the policy fn, missing :replacement when :recovery is :replaced-with-default, etc. The improver2 lens is the mechanism by which trace observations get fed back into improvements to pair2 itself, so this completes the loop.

title: [skill] re-frame-pair-improver2: clarify recorder-session retros out of scope
description: Per ai/findings/re-frame-pair-story-skill-investigation-2026-05-13.md candidate #3. Add explicit Do-not-use guard for Story-recorder-session retros (rf2-5fc15 captures). Those belong in re-frame-pair2's refinement workflow, not improver2's pair-session retrospective lens. Source: rf2-ncp5n.

title: [skill] Investigate need for re-frame-pair-story skill — or is it covered by existing skills?
description:
Mike's investigation question: do we need a dedicated 're-frame-pair-story' skill that combines pair-programming with Story workspaces? Or is the combination of skills/re-frame-pair2 (live runtime inspection via nREPL + MCP) and skills/re-frame2 (authoring) plus the Story tool itself (tools/story/) already sufficient for the workflow?

## Lens for the investigation

Identify the SPECIFIC workflows that might justify a dedicated skill:
1. Authoring stories with AI pairing (where does Story content design + re-frame2 authoring overlap?)
2. Using Stories AS test fixtures for pair2 sessions (replay :play sequences while pair2 watches)
3. Story Test Codegen + pair2 (the just-landed PR #603 lets users record canvas interactions; could a skill help refine those recordings?)
4. Live-coding components in a Story workspace with pair2 attached
5. Anything else surfacing from the existing skill audit (ai/findings/sweep-skills-best-practice was deleted but referenced 'cross-skill coupling' as a concern)

## Output

Findings doc at ai/findings/re-frame-pair-story-skill-investigation-2026-05-13.md. Structure:
- Executive summary: yes/no + rationale
- Workflows currently underserved by existing skills
- Coverage matrix: workflow x (current skill / proposed new skill)
- If yes: scope sketch (description: frontmatter shape, references/ structure, allowed-tools, trigger phrases)
- If no: which existing skill should grow to cover the gap + how
- Bead candidates list if scope is well-defined

## Constraints

- READ-ONLY research (no source edits)
- File local-only (ai/ gitignored)
- Do NOT call bd create from the agent — mayor decides on bead-creation after reading findings

## Source

Mike asked 2026-05-13 — wants investigation results in ai/findings/.
close_reason: Investigation complete: findings at ai/findings/re-frame-pair-story-skill-investigation-2026-05-13.md. HYBRID recommendation — no new skill needed; ~30% gap is leaf-content drift inside already-the-right-skill. 4 follow-on beads filed (skill extensions for re-frame2 stories + re-frame-pair2 variant-as-frame + re-frame-pair-improver2 scope clarification + decision marker for future re-frame-story consideration).

title: [skill] re-frame-migration: document the new wfbn3 error event catalogue (95 categories) as the single source
description:
rf2-wfbn3 (PR #575, merged this session) consolidated all error/warning categories into spec/009 §Error event catalogue — a single normative listing of 95 categories with :operation, :op-type, trigger, default :recovery, :tags columns. Previously these were scattered across three separate sections.

The re-frame-migration skill teaches agents to recognise error traces during a migration but doesn't currently point them at the catalogue as the single source of truth. Agents auditing migrated apps for 'is this old or new error category?' need to know:

1. The catalogue exists at spec/009-Instrumentation.md §Error event catalogue (slug #error-event-catalogue)
2. Old prose / blog-post / Stack Overflow advice naming specific categories may be stale; the catalogue wins.
3. The reg-event-error-handler → :on-error rewrite (M-13) implies cross-referencing the catalogue for which categories the policy intercepts.

## Proposed fix
- Add a §Error vocabulary section to skills/re-frame-migration/reference/breaking-changes.md (or guided-cross-cutting.md) pointing at the catalogue.
- Mention in M-13 prose that the new :on-error policy receives any :rf.error/* / :rf.warning/* / :rf.fx/* / :rf.cofx/* / :rf.ssr/* / :rf.epoch/* / :rf.http/* / :rf.http.interceptor/* / :rf.frame/* / :route.nav-token/* event — the closed catalogue.

## Why P3
Doc-only; the migration mechanics work without the cross-reference. Adding it makes the skill more authoritative.

## Source
Sweep of tools-and-skills drift after rf2-wfbn3 merge (2026-05-13 14:58 AUSEST).
notes: PR #639 (in CI, one check still running)
close_reason: Closed

title: [causa] issues_ribbon_helpers: update 'five prefixes' comment to match rf2-wfbn3 9+ prefix catalogue
description:
rf2-wfbn3 (PR #575, merged this session) consolidated the error-event vocabulary into a single normative catalogue spanning 95 categories across :rf.error/*, :rf.warning/*, :rf.fx/*, :rf.cofx/*, :rf.ssr/*, :rf.epoch/*, :rf.http/*, :rf.http.interceptor/*, :rf.frame/*, and :route.nav-token/* (10 distinct namespaces).

tools/causa/src/day8/re_frame2_causa/panels/issues_ribbon_helpers.cljc L29-39 enumerates only 'five normative error-event prefixes' (:rf.error/, :rf.fx/, :rf.ssr/, :rf.warning/, :rf.epoch/) then handwaves the rest ('Plus the :rf.cofx/, :rf.frame/, :rf.http/, :rf.http.interceptor/ and :route.nav-token/ subgroups that appear in the catalogue').

The category-prefix projection (L141-152) is dynamic (reads namespace) so this is doc/comment-only drift, not a logic bug. But the docstring is the contract a panel author reads when modifying the projection — the rewrite should treat all 10 prefixes uniformly per the catalogue.

## Proposed fix
Rewrite the docstring's prefix list to enumerate all 10 catalogue prefixes uniformly (or drop the enumeration and refer to spec/009 §Error event catalogue as the single source).

## Why P3
Cosmetic comment drift; runtime behaviour unchanged.

## Source
Sweep of tools-and-skills drift after rf2-wfbn3 merge (2026-05-13 14:58 AUSEST).

title: [skill] re-frame-pair2 SKILL.md + ops.md: note rf2-ciy :on-error closed return shape
description:
rf2-ciy (PR #618, merged this session) pinned the :on-error per-frame policy return shape to a closed {:recovery :replacement :notes} map and added two new error categories: :rf.error/bad-on-error-return and :rf.error/on-error-policy-exception. :retry-count was explicitly struck.

The pair2 skill teaches agents to wire / inspect frame-level error policy via Tool-Pair surfaces. If an agent dispatches against a frame using the old-style {:recovery :retried :retry-count 3} return, the runtime now emits :rf.error/bad-on-error-return and falls back to the category default.

## Sites
- skills/re-frame-pair2/references/errors.md (if present) — add the two new categories to the catalogue rows agents are taught to recognise
- skills/re-frame-pair2/SKILL.md — when the skill describes policy semantics, ensure it says "closed shape, :retry-count not honoured"
- skills/re-frame-pair-improver2/references/analysis-lenses.md — adding 'review :on-error policy returns' as a lens that catches the now-rejected shapes

## Why P3
Doc-only — the runtime traces correctly and the bd-trail surfaces it; agents reading old skill prose won't hit silent failure, just a confusing trace.

title: [causa] Fix stale :op-type values per rf2-6o6 trace-event enum reshape
description:
rf2-6o6 (merged this session) restructured :rf/trace-event :op-type into three groups — severity (:error/:warning/:info), cascade-body (:event/:event/do-fx/:sub/create/...), family (:frame/:machine/:registry/:flow/:rf.epoch/:rf.epoch.cb/:ssr). Per-operation keywords (e.g. :rf.machine/transition, :rf.ssr/hydration-mismatch) are now :operation values; their :op-type is :machine or :error respectively.

Three Causa spec files still say :op-type :rf.machine/transition or treat :rf.ssr/hydration-mismatch as an :op-type:

## Sites
- tools/causa/spec/001-Causality-Graph.md L20 — `| **Machine transition** | `:op-type :rf.machine/transition` |` → should be `:operation :rf.machine/transition` (:op-type :machine)
- tools/causa/spec/000-Vision.md L94 — `All `:op-type :error` / `:warning` / `:rf.ssr/hydration-mismatch` traces` → :rf.ssr/hydration-mismatch is :operation (:op-type :error)
- tools/causa/spec/findings/re-frame-10x-v2-design.md L304 — same wording as 000-Vision L94

## Why it matters
Spec 001 + Vision are the canonical references panels read against. A panel author wiring (rf/trace-buffer {:op-type :rf.machine/transition}) will get an empty set because :rf.machine/transition is no longer a valid :op-type filter value — it's an :operation under :op-type :machine.

## Proposed fix
Mechanical rewrite per the rf2-6o6 enum: drop the wrong family-level claims and use {:operation :rf.machine/transition} or {:op-type :machine} where appropriate.

title: [causa] Extend editor_uri.cljc with :windsurf + :zed scheme builders (follows rf2-mqm2d)
description:
## Context

rf2-mqm2d (PR #593) added `:windsurf` + `:zed` rows to the editor-protocol matrix in `tools/causa/spec/007-UX-IA.md`. The implementation helper at `implementation/core/src/re_frame/source_coords/editor_uri.cljc` still recognises only `:vscode` / `:cursor` / `:idea` / `{:custom <template>}` — so a user who sets `:rf.causa/editor :windsurf` today falls through to the `:vscode` default per the helper's unknown-keyword posture.

## Scope

- `implementation/core/src/re_frame/source_coords/editor_uri.cljc`:
  - Extend `known-editors` to `#{:vscode :cursor :windsurf :zed :idea}`.
  - Add `windsurf-uri` (mirrors `cursor-uri` shape — colon-suffix grammar) and `zed-uri` (same shape).
  - Add the two cond branches in `editor-uri` keyed off `(= :windsurf editor)` and `(= :zed editor)`.
  - Update the namespace docstring + ditto vocabulary section.
- Add unit tests in `implementation/core/test/re_frame/source_coords/editor_uri_test.cljc` for both new schemes (file present, line/column defaults, missing-file -> nil URI).

## Out of scope

- Settings-modal UI changes (Causa's editor dropdown lives in a separate surface; that bead will pick up the new keywords organically once `known-editors` lifts).

## Cross-refs

- rf2-mqm2d (matrix spec) — merged via PR #593.
- rf2-evgf5 (the chip implementation bead — original `editor_uri.cljc` author).
notes: PR #601 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/601. Branch rf2-achw1-editor-uri-windsurf-zed. Adds :windsurf + :zed to known-editors and the editor-uri cond. JVM 16/36 green; CLJS 1066/2771 green.

- `tools/pair2-mcp/src/re_frame_pair2_mcp/server.cljs:14` — docstring says "`tools/list`: returns the seven tool descriptors (mirror of the …)". Actual list now has 9 descriptors.
- `tools/pair2-mcp/test/stdio-roundtrip.js:6` — comment says "tools/list (expects all seven tools, including the snapshot mega-op)".
- `tools/pair2-mcp/test/stdio-roundtrip.js:83` — comment says "expect all nine tools (six original + snapshot…)" — internally inconsistent with line 6.

## Proposed fix

Sync all three to "nine" with the canonical breakdown (seven per-op + snapshot + subscribe/unsubscribe). Sanity-check that the test actually asserts nine entries on the `tools/list` response.

`spec/Construction-Prompts.md:39, 116`:
> "Multi-positional `[<id> a b c]` is tolerated for v1 compatibility but not what new code should emit."

Pre-alpha decision:
- If v2 runtime tolerates multi-positional vectors, say "tolerated for ergonomic flexibility" — drop the v1-compat framing.
- If v2 runtime should reject (or warn on) multi-positional, file a separate bead.

The doc currently splits the difference and uses "v1 compatibility" as the reason — incorrect framing pre-alpha.

## Proposed fix

Reframe both lines. Likely just: "Multi-positional `[<id> a b c]` is accepted by the runtime; the linter nudges to map shape for new code."

## Source

title: [spec] Resolve OQ 005 §Stately.ai compatibility scope (paste-and-render)
description:
## SA-4 — resolve OQ Stately.ai compatibility scope

Spec 005 §Open questions L2892: "Stately.ai compatibility — exact or approximate?" carries "Recommendation: paste-and-render" but no commitment row.

## Proposed fix

Land the paste-and-render posture explicitly as the v1 contract. State which Stately features ARE supported in round-trip (states, transitions, guards, actions, hierarchical, parallel, history) vs intentionally NOT (specific Stately Studio metadata, design-system tokens, layout-position hints).

Document the export shape (`machine->xstate-json`) precisely.

title: [docs] Spec 002: rewrite ':dispatch-n is deprecated' → 'is gone in v2'
description:
## Rewrite `:dispatch-n is deprecated` comment

`spec/002-Frames.md:593`:
> ";; multiple dispatches are expressed via :fx (nested pairs) — :dispatch-n is deprecated"

`:dispatch-n` is REMOVED in v2 (per M-8). Calling it "deprecated" in a v2 spec is misleading — it's not a thing in v2.

## Proposed fix

Rewrite as "multiple dispatches are expressed via `:fx` (nested pairs); the v1 `:dispatch-n` top-level key is gone."

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-backcompat-cuts-2026-05-13.md` §S17 / B-15 — P3

title: [cut] Generalise or drop v1-key suggestion strings in events.cljc legacy-key-suggestion
description:
## Cut v1-key suggestion strings in events.cljc

`implementation/core/src/re_frame/events.cljc:69-79, 88-105`: the `legacy-key-suggestion` fn maps v1 top-level keys (`:dispatch`, `:dispatch-later`, `:dispatch-n`, `:http`) to migration hints inline in the `:reason` string of the `:rf.error/effect-map-shape` trace.

This is a RUNTIME carrying v1 migration documentation. The policing itself (drop non-`:db`/`:fx` keys, emit a structured error) is correct. The migration suggestion string baked into the trace is back-compat tax — the migration skill (M-8) already handles the rewrite.

## Proposed fix

Option A (aggressive): Drop the suggestion strings entirely; the error keyword + `:offending-key` is enough.

Option B (lighter): Keep the policing but generalise the message — don't enumerate v1 keys; just say "only `:db` and `:fx` are allowed at the top level".

Either way, the `legacy-key-suggestion` fn and its case table is residue.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-backcompat-cuts-2026-05-13.md` §S16 / B-14 — P3

title: [docs] MIGRATION.md: drop :legacy/<event-id> placeholder for on-changes→flow
description:
## Drop `:legacy/*` placeholder in MIGRATION.md

`spec/MIGRATION.md:812`:

> "The agent rewrites `(rf/on-changes f out-path & in-paths)` to a `(rf/reg-flow {:id ... :inputs in-paths :output f :path out-path})` registration. The id has to be picked — agent uses `:legacy/<event-id>` or asks."

This pushes a `:legacy/*` keyword into user codebases at migration time — defeating the cut-the-legacy-namespace stance.

## Proposed fix

Have the agent ask the user for the flow id rather than mint `:legacy/...`. Or use the user's own ns prefix (e.g. `:<user-ns>/<event-id>-flow`).

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-backcompat-cuts-2026-05-13.md` §S15 / B-13 — P3

title: [docs] Conventions.md: drop re-frame.core.legacy row (phantom namespace)
description:
## Drop `re-frame.core.legacy` example from Conventions table

`spec/Conventions.md:512` lists `re-frame.core.legacy` → `re-frame.core-legacy` as a naming-convention example. There is NO such namespace (verified via Grep). The example invents a hypothetical "legacy" submodule that should not exist post-alpha.

## Proposed fix

Drop the `re-frame.core.legacy` row from the table; replace with an actual shipped sub-namespace example, or remove the row.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-backcompat-cuts-2026-05-13.md` §S14 / B-12 — P3

title: [docs] Spec 001 §middle slot: drop 'for back-compat with v1' lead-in
description:
## Spec 001 prose reframe — drop back-compat lead-in

`spec/001-Registration.md:57`:

> "For backwards-compatibility with re-frame v1, the middle slot of `reg-event-*` is either:
> 1. A vector of interceptors…
> 2. A metadata map…
> 3. Both…"

The positional, optional-meta-map grammar is the RIGHT grammar. It is not retained because of v1 back-compat; it is retained because it is the correct grammar. Pre-alpha framing should be "the middle slot is either a vector (interceptors) or a map (metadata) or both" — full stop. Drop the "for back-compat with v1" lead-in.

title: [test] Add conformance fixtures for epoch / view / http-interceptors / trace-buffer
description:
## Conformance fixtures for under-covered surfaces

Epoch, view, http-interceptors, trace-buffer have rich per-feature tests but zero cross-host portable contract pinning in the conformance corpus. A second-host re-frame2 implementation that nailed every conformance fixture would have NO signal that its epoch ring, trace filter vocab, view-render trigger-handler, or http-interceptor pipeline work.

## Proposed scope

Add conformance fixtures for:

- **Epoch** — record / restore / `reset-frame-db!`
- **View** — `reg-view` + render
- **HTTP-interceptors** — reg / clear / before / after pipeline
- **Trace-buffer** — filter vocab (consume across categories)

Second-host implementations (e.g. rf-clr) need these as portable contracts.

## Proposed scope

## Replicate prod-elision runner pattern

Today only `schemas_boundary_prod_test.cljs` runs under `:optimizations :advanced` + `:goog-define` flips. Other elision contracts (trace, epoch, frame-provider) are tested with `set! enabled?` flips but never under real `:advanced`.

## Proposed scope

Replicate the `schemas_boundary_prod_test.cljs` pattern for:
- Trace (`trace_boundary_prod_test.cljs`) — confirms `re-frame.trace/emit!` and the trace bus DCE under :advanced
- Epoch (`epoch_boundary_prod_test.cljs`) — confirms epoch recording DCE under :advanced
- Frame-provider (`frame_provider_prod_test.cljs`) — confirms the dev-only frame-provider mechanics elide

Each new runner is a small shadow-cljs build with `:goog-define` flips + a runner test that asserts the elided symbols are absent from the production bundle.

title: [hygiene] Repo P3 polish batch (.editorconfig + .clj-kondo/ + .gitignore + COC/CONTRIBUTING/SECURITY + version-pin)
description:
## Repo hygiene quick wins

Batch of P3 polish items from the repo-best-practice sweep:

### 1. Repo-root `.editorconfig` (missing)

Minimal — `indent_style = space`, `indent_size = 2` for `.clj{,c,s}`, `end_of_line = lf`, `insert_final_newline = true`, `trim_trailing_whitespace = true`. Reduces friction for external contributors.

### 2. Repo-root `.clj-kondo/config.edn` (empty `{}`)

Activates project-aware analysis in Calva/Cursive/CIDER for any contributor. The template ALREADY ships this pattern for generated apps; the repo itself should follow suit. Add `/.clj-kondo/.cache/` and `/.clj-kondo/.cache-version` to `.gitignore`.

### 3. Repo-root orphan `package-lock.json`

Empty lockfile, no corresponding `package.json`. Delete locally (it's not in the repo); add an explicit `/package-lock.json` to `.gitignore` if a future stray npm invocation at the repo root is likely.

### 4. Explicit `.gitignore` entries for `examples/.edge-cdp/` and `.claude/worktrees/`

Both are currently ignored by git without an explicit rule (working but accidental). Adding them documents intent.

### 5. CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md

Pre-alpha-public-publish OSS hygiene. None block release; all are easy adds before the first Clojars publish.

### 6. tools/pair2-mcp/package.json shadow-cljs version pin

`^3.4.10` (caret) vs `implementation/package.json` exact `3.4.10`. Align to one policy (pre-1.0, exact pin is the safer convention).

### 7. release.yml DAG comment block doesn't mention tools/* deploys

Once tools/causa, tools/story, tools/template, tools/story-mcp join the release pipeline (presumably as separate leaves), the comment block at the top of `release.yml` (lines 4-17) will need to grow.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-repo-best-practice-2026-05-13.md` §Bead candidates 8-13 — all P3

title: [test] CLJS integration test for setTimeout/addEventListener/rAF async-escape fallthrough warn
description:
## CLJS-side async-escape integration

`dispatch_fallthrough_warn_test.clj` covers the JVM proxy of the JS async-callback frame-fallthrough trigger but does NOT have a CLJS counterpart that exercises the actual setTimeout/addEventListener/requestAnimationFrame async-escape paths. The framework warn fires (the JVM test proves it), but the JS-side trigger never gets integration coverage.

## Proposed scope

Add a CLJS integration test that:
- Wraps an `addEventListener` callback that calls `dispatch` without `:frame` opt
- Wraps a `setTimeout` callback similarly
- Wraps a `requestAnimationFrame` callback similarly

Asserts the `:rf.warning/dispatch-fallthrough` fires from each — and that the user is shown the right message.

## Source

Scaffold `tools/deps.edn` + `tools/shadow-cljs.edn` build coordinators (matching the pattern in `implementation/`). Make `clojure -X:tools:test` or `shadow-cljs compile tools-all` invocations possible.

title: [api] destroy-frame / reset-frame public re-export strips bang — document or align
description:
## Convention asymmetry

`core.cljc:471-472`:
```clojure
(def reset-frame     frame/reset-frame!)
(def destroy-frame   frame/destroy-frame!)
```

Internal names have bangs (they mutate the frames table). Public re-export drops the bang. By the convention (registrar-shaped registrations are not bang-shaped), this is defensible — but the internal/public split is a footgun for contributors reading core.cljc cold.

## Proposed fix

Either:
- Add a comment block at line 470 explaining the strip.
- Or align internal: `frame/reset-frame!` → `frame/reset-frame`, `frame/destroy-frame!` → `frame/destroy-frame`. The latter is cleaner — by the documented convention, frames-as-registrar-entries don't carry bangs anywhere. DECISION needed.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-api-elegance-2026-05-13.md` §NEW finding 17 — P3

title: [api] Move or rename clear-subscription-cache! (outlier in clearing block)
description:
## API elegance — outlier in clearing block

`core.cljc:727-732`:
```clojure
(def clear-event events/clear-event)
(def clear-sub   subs/clear-sub)
(def clear-fx    fx/clear-fx)
(def clear-subscription-cache! subs/clear-subscription-cache!)
```

First three are bang-less ("registry clear"); fourth is bang-suffixed ("runtime cache clear").

## Proposed fix

Either:
- Move `clear-subscription-cache!` out of this section into the "runtime introspection / cache" cluster (`core.cljc:1219+`).
- Or rename to `clear-sub-cache!` for terseness (matches `sub-cache` reader two lines away).

Sites: `core.cljc:727-732`, `spec/API.md` L55.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-api-elegance-2026-05-13.md` §Issue 10 — P3 (NEW; mild)

title: [api] Clarify validate-at-boundary is an interceptor val, not a fn
description:
## API elegance — value, looks like fn

`core.cljc:1270`: `(def validate-at-boundary spec/validate-at-boundary)`.

API.md L358 documents this as the only "interceptor val" in the public surface. Every other `re-frame.core` export with the same shape (lower-case symbol, no suffix) is a fn or a macro. Users will reach for `(rf/validate-at-boundary ...)` and get an `ArityException`.

## Proposed fix

Either:
- Add `^{:rf/interceptor-val true}` meta + clarifying docstring ("pre-built interceptor value; use it in a `reg-event-*`'s interceptor vector — do not call it as a fn").
- Or rename to `boundary-validator` (noun-shaped).

Sites: `core.cljc:1270`, `spec/API.md` L358.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-api-elegance-2026-05-13.md` §Issue 8 — P3 (NEW)

title: [api] Rename subscribe-value → subscribe-once OR read-sub (naming convention drift)
description:
## API elegance — naming convention drift

`core.cljc:760`: `(def subscribe-value subs/subscribe-value)`.

API.md L69 distinguishes `subscribe` (returns reaction) and `subscribe-value` (one-shot read, no caching). The naming convention elsewhere in re-frame for "one-shot" is NOT `-value` (cf. `dispatch` / `dispatch-sync` — qualifier names the INVOCATION MODE, not the RETURN SHAPE).

## Proposed fix

Consider `subscribe-once` or `read-sub`. The current `-value` suffix leads readers to think `subscribe` returns "not a value" (it returns a reaction; colloquially also a "value"). DECISION needed.

Sites: `core.cljc:760`, `subs.cljc` impl, `spec/API.md` L69.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-api-elegance-2026-05-13.md` §Issue 9 — P3 (NEW; mild)

title: [api] Rename view → view-fn OR sharpen docstring (fn returns a fn)
description:
## API elegance — fn that returns a fn

`(rf/view :my-view)` returns a function, not a hiccup tree. From the name "view", the natural reading is "a view" (something renderable). Sibling `reg-view` / `reg-view*` already implies "register-view".

## Proposed fix

Either:
- **(a)** Rename `view` → `view-fn`. Closes the trio (`reg-view` register / `reg-view*` register-fn / `view-fn` lookup-fn). Sites: `core.cljc:444`, `spec/API.md` L86, Spec 004 references.
- **(b)** Sharpen the docstring (3-line fix): "returns the render-fn for use in hiccup as `[(rf/view :id) args...]`".

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-api-elegance-2026-05-13.md` §Issue 7 — P3 (NEW; mild)

title: [api] Rename emit-trace! → emit-trace-event! (name asymmetry)
description:
## API elegance — name asymmetry

`core.cljc:1276`: `(def emit-trace! trace/emit!)`.

Internal name is `trace/emit!` — one word. Public name `emit-trace!` puts "trace" as awkward noun-adjective. Pair with siblings `trace-buffer` / `clear-trace-buffer!` (both compound with `trace` as noun) and the asymmetry sharpens.

## Proposed fix

Either:
- Rename public to `emit-trace-event!` (most accurate).
- Or move internal `re-frame.trace/emit!` → `re-frame.trace/emit-trace-event!` and re-export under same name.

Sites: `core.cljc:1276`, `spec/API.md` L372, any direct callers (story panels likely).

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-api-elegance-2026-05-13.md` §Issue 6 — P3 (NEW)

title: [api] Rename has-tag? → machine-has-tag? OR cut entirely
description:
## API elegance — too-bare name

`core.cljc:1180`:
```clojure
(def has-tag? rf-machines/has-tag?)
```

Reading `(rf/has-tag? machine-id tag)`, "tag" is one of the most overloaded words in software. re-frame already has `:tags` on routes, machine state nodes, trace events, stories.

## Proposed fix

Sibling machine surfaces all carry `machine-` prefix (`machine-meta`, `machine-by-system-id`, `machine-transition`, `machine->xstate-json`). Rename to `machine-has-tag?` for consistency.

Alternative: cut entirely (per API.md L611 it's pure sugar over `(subscribe [:rf/machine-has-tag? machine-id tag])`).

DECISION needed.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-api-elegance-2026-05-13.md` §Issue 5 — P3 (NEW)

title: [api] Cut handler-ids OR document partition with handlers
description:
## Surface bloat

`handler-ids` is `(-> (handlers kind) keys set)` per registrar.cljc:191-194 — five chars of inlining. User code that wants ids almost certainly wants the meta-map too.

## Proposed fix

Either:
- Cut `handler-ids` from public surface; inline.
- Or document the partition explicitly: "use `handlers` when you want metadata; use `handler-ids` when you only need to enumerate."

Sites: `core.cljc:1186`, `spec/API.md` L329.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-api-elegance-2026-05-13.md` §Issue 2 — P3 (NEW)

title: [api] Cut or rename registry-summary (one-liner over handlers)
description:
## Surface bloat

`(registry-summary)` is a one-liner over `handlers`: `(into {} (map (juxt key (comp count val))) @kind->id->metadata)`. Six characters of inlining over the full meta map.

## Proposed fix

Either:
- Cut `registry-summary` from the public surface; inline at the ~3 dev-tooling call sites that use it.
- Or rename to `handler-counts` so the return shape is in the name.

Hold-open if `:strategy = preserve dev-tooling helpers`.

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-api-elegance-2026-05-13.md` §Issue 2 — P3 (NEW)

title: [api] Rename handlers → registrations (misleading name; returns map not seq)
description:
## API elegance — misleading name

`(handlers kind)` returns a MAP `{:my/event {<meta>}, :other/event {<meta>}, ...}` keyed by id — not a sequence of handlers. The plural-noun `handlers` shape is misleading; a reader expects a seq of handler fns.

Pre-alpha rename window is open. The registrar internally uses `registrar/register!` for the operation; `registrations` is the more accurate name.

## Proposed fix

Rename `handlers` → `registrations`. Sites:
- `implementation/core/src/re_frame/registrar.cljc`
- `implementation/core/src/re_frame/core.cljc:1184` — the def re-export
- `spec/API.md` L328 — row
- ~12 internal call sites (mostly story / causa)

## Source

- `ai/findings/sweep-api-elegance-2026-05-13.md` §Issue 1 — P3 (NEW / DIFF — prior audit kept as-is; this read argues the name is actively misleading)

Add a section to tools/causa/spec/010-MCP-Server.md framing Causa-MCP as 'load alongside Chrome MCP' with a quick-guide table ('what did the app do?' -> Causa; 'what did the browser do?' -> Chrome).
