title: [examples/managed-http-counter] assert :loading transition — cancel sub-test currently vacuous against fastpath stubs
description:
Per ai/findings/examples-playwright-audit-2026-05-13.md §Per-spec (managed_http_counter).

examples/reagent/managed_http_counter/managed_http_counter.spec.cjs lines 72-77:

  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Start long' }).click();
  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Cancel' }).click();
  await expectTextEquals(status, 'idle');

If the canned-stub for 'Start long' resolves synchronously (fastpath) before the Cancel dispatch, the status lands at :idle naturally and the Cancel sub-test passes vacuously — without exercising :rf.http/managed-abort.

## Action

Assert the status transitions through an intermediate :loading (or whatever the example surfaces) BEFORE the Cancel click. Either:

- (a) Slow the 'Start long' stub enough that the :loading state is observable, OR
- (b) Use `expectTextContains` to verify status reads :loading immediately after Start-long, then proceed with Cancel + idle assertion.

Bead context: rf2-lxd3 already changed reply behaviour on supersede; this spec needs to align with the current contract.
close_reason: PRs #527-#531 merged

title: [examples/websocket] expose reconnect-attempts counter — RECONNECTING window currently invisible to spec
description:
Per ai/findings/examples-playwright-audit-2026-05-13.md §Per-spec (websocket).

examples/reagent/websocket/websocket.spec.cjs asserts that after a Drop click the status returns to CONNECTED, but it does NOT verify the intermediate RECONNECTING phase actually rendered. The spec comment acknowledges: "Either we catch the brief RECONNECTING window, or the cascade resolved already and we're already back to CONNECTED. Both are valid evidence the reconnect machinery worked" — but it isn't, because if the reconnect path were silently bypassed the spec would still pass.

## Action

1. Add a `data-testid="ws-reconnect-attempts"` counter to the websocket view that reads from the :ws/connection machine's :retries slot.
2. Update the spec to assert it advances after Drop (`>= 1` after a click that previously read 0).

This makes the reconnect machinery a load-bearing assertion, not a vacuous fallback.
close_reason: PRs #527-#531 merged

title: [examples/flight-booker] pin seeded date — spec asserts hardcoded 2026-05-06 baseline
description:
Per ai/findings/examples-playwright-audit-2026-05-13.md §Per-spec (flight_booker).

examples/reagent/7Guis/flight_booker/flight_booker.spec.cjs line 31:

  await expectInputValue(start, '2026-05-06', 10000);

The seeded start-date drifts with calendar "today" — once the system clock passes 2026-05-06 the example's :flight/initialise computes a different default and this assertion fails.

## Action

Either:

- (a) Update flight-booker's :flight/initialise to read the seed from a frame config slot the spec can dispatch-sync before mount, OR
- (b) Update the spec to read the start input's current value and use it as the baseline for the subsequent return-date comparisons.

(b) is the safer fix — keeps the example's user-visible "today as default" behaviour intact.

Today's date: 2026-05-13. The seed has already drifted past the literal — if the spec is run as-is right now it will fail.
close_reason: PRs #527-#531 merged

title: [examples/realworld] expand Playwright spec coverage — auth navigation + article detail
description:
Per ai/findings/examples-playwright-audit-2026-05-13.md §Per-spec (realworld) + §Cross-cutting C.

The bead rf2-h9qqb hypothesised realworld may carry the same latent rf2-jymd4 brittleness shape (multi-route SPA + auth transitions). The audit found realworld.spec.cjs DOESN'T exercise that depth — it only asserts initial load, global feed (2 articles), and the tag list (4 tags). The auth transitions and article-detail navigation the example DEMONSTRATES are not under test.

## Action

Expand examples/reagent/realworld/realworld.spec.cjs:

1. Click an article preview, assert the detail page renders with the article body + comments slice.
2. Click "Sign in" → navigate to /login, sign in with the canned-stub demo credentials, assert the navbar updates to authed shape (username link, no Sign in link).
3. Submit a comment via the canned-stub seam, assert it appears in the comments list.

Each new step should anchor on a stable testid where possible — pairs with rf2-0gdsb (data-testid sweep).
close_reason: PRs #527-#531 merged

title: [examples] data-testid sweep across example views — kill first()/.nth() selector fragility
description:
Per ai/findings/examples-playwright-audit-2026-05-13.md §Cross-cutting B + Top fix 2.

Multiple spec files anchor on classnames or DOM-order positional selectors (`inputs.nth(N)`, `page.getByText(...).first()`, `page.locator('p.error')`). Every such site silently breaks when a sibling node lands in the view.

## Surfaces missing data-testid

- examples/reagent/login + examples/uix/login_uix + examples/helix/login_helix: `p.error`, `.banner` — three substrate variants of the same surface; coordinate the view changes.
- examples/reagent/nine_states: Archive button (regex name match is over-broad).
- examples/reagent/routing: nav links (Home / Back / See the articles).
- examples/reagent/7Guis/crud: filter / name / surname inputs (currently `inputs.nth(0..2)`).
- examples/reagent/7Guis/temperature: celsius / fahrenheit inputs.
- examples/reagent/7Guis/flight_booker: start / return inputs.
- examples/reagent/7Guis/cells: cells (add `data-cell="A1"` etc.).

## Action

1. Add `data-testid` (or `data-cell` for the cells grid) to each surface listed.
2. Update the matching spec selectors.
3. Confirm specs still pass via the canonical `npm run test:examples` driver.

Pre-alpha — no back-compat concerns.
close_reason: PRs #527-#531 merged

title: [examples] waitForStableValue helper + replace fixed-duration sleeps in Playwright specs
description:
Per ai/findings/examples-playwright-audit-2026-05-13.md §Cross-cutting D + Top fix 1.

Four Playwright spec sites use fixed-duration sleeps with no stable observable trigger, so they're load-bearing on wall-clock budget rather than on a settled state:

- examples/reagent/7Guis/timer/timer.spec.cjs line ~34: `await page.waitForTimeout(700)` before asserting elapsed > 0.
- examples/reagent/long_running_work/long_running_work.spec.cjs line ~102: `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 500))` after cancel to assert progress froze.
- examples/reagent/long_running_work/long_running_work.spec.cjs line ~125-131: a polling block that gates on a snapshot rather than a settled signal.
- examples/reagent/counter_with_stories/counter_with_stories.spec.cjs line ~840: `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 250))` after an inc click before reading slider max.

## Action

1. Add a `waitForStableValue(readFn, {intervalMs=100, samples=2, timeoutMs=5000})` helper to examples/scripts/spec-helpers.cjs. Polls readFn until `samples` consecutive reads agree, then returns the stable value. Throws on timeout.

2. Replace each of the four sites with a steady-state poll keyed to the actual observable (progress.done, slider max, elapsed reading).
close_reason: PRs #527-#531 merged

title: [usability] Stamp canonical phrasing across Reagent/UIx/Helix frame-provider docstrings
description:
Per audit rf2-1itgd (per-surface table — Reagent/UIx/Helix frame-provider).

Three React adapters each ship their own `frame-provider` component. UIx and Helix have full docstrings (phrasings diverge); Reagent's component is in views.cljs and re-exported through core.cljc:953 with NO local docstring.

Three substrates, three slightly-different presentations of the same conceptual surface — and core.cljc's user-facing re-export shows no docstring at all.

## Scope

1. Pick canonical phrasing (start from UIx's docstring at adapters/uix/src/re_frame/adapter/uix.cljs:183 — it reads cleanly):

   'User-facing component scoping frame-kw to its subtree. Wraps children in the shared frame Context Provider. Per Spec 002 §What frame-provider is.'

2. Stamp it on:
   - implementation/adapters/reagent/src/re_frame/adapter/reagent.cljs (currently in views.cljs / no docstring on the re-export)
   - implementation/adapters/uix/src/re_frame/adapter/uix.cljs:183
   - implementation/adapters/helix/src/re_frame/adapter/helix.cljs:188
   - implementation/core/src/re_frame/core.cljc:953 (CLJS-only re-export)

3. Per-substrate examples can differ (Reagent's `[frame-provider {:frame :session} ...]`, UIx/Helix's `($ frame-provider {:frame :session :children [...]})`) — that's an honest difference. Body of docstring is uniform.

Pre-alpha: edit freely.
close_reason: merged via PR #526 (API usability batch)

title: [usability] Hoist :path pattern syntax into reg-route docstring (named params, splats, optional groups)
description:
Per audit rf2-1itgd (W7).

The reg-route macro at implementation/core/src/re_frame/core.cljc:459 documents the late-bind / artefact split but says NOTHING about the :path pattern syntax. The pattern grammar is documented in:

- routing.cljc:231 — `compile-pattern` (a private fn) docstring
- Spec 012-Routing.md

…neither of which is reachable from `(rf/doc rf/reg-route)`.

The grammar is:

  /literal       — literal segment
  /:name         — named param ([^/]+ capture)
  /*name         — splat (greedy across /)
  /{...}?        — optional group; inside, /:name normal

## Scope

Add a 'Path pattern syntax' section to the reg-route macro docstring at core.cljc:459. Show the four forms with one example each. Cross-ref Spec 012 §Pattern syntax for the full grammar (escapes, conflict resolution).

Same audit applies — but lower-leverage — to:
- reg-flow's docstring (hoist required keys :id :inputs :output :path)
- reg-app-schema (link to Malli + the path-vector contract)
- reg-machine (link to spec keys :initial, :data, transition-table shape)

Pre-alpha: edit freely.
close_reason: merged via PR #526 (API usability batch)

title: [usability] Cross-ref pass across 11 docstring deserts in re-frame.core
description:
Per audit rf2-1itgd (Cross-reference deserts §).

Eleven public surfaces in implementation/core/src/re_frame/core.cljc have NO sibling cross-references in their docstrings. Adding them is high-leverage — a user reading reg-event-db's docstring should naturally discover reg-event-fx, dispatch, etc.

## Surfaces + recommended cross-refs

| Surface | Should cross-ref |
|---|---|
| reg-event-db | reg-event-fx, reg-event-ctx, dispatch |
| reg-event-fx | reg-event-db, reg-fx, the closed :fx shape |
| reg-event-ctx | reg-event-fx, ->interceptor |
| reg-sub | subscribe, subscribe-value, :<- form |
| reg-fx | reg-cofx, :platforms metadata |
| reg-cofx | inject-cofx, reg-fx |
| inject-cofx | reg-cofx, interceptor-vector position |
| dispatch | dispatch-sync, :fx [[:dispatch event]] |
| dispatch-sync | dispatch, :rf.error/dispatch-sync-in-handler |
| subscribe | subscribe-value, unsubscribe, current-frame |
| init! | adapter ns selection — reagent/adapter, uix-adapter/adapter, etc. |

## Scope

In each docstring, append a 'See also:' line naming the siblings. Inline references (markdown-style backticks) are preferred over prose links.

Pre-alpha: edit freely.
close_reason: merged via PR #526 (API usability batch)

title: [usability] Factor artefact-split boilerplate out of seven core_*.cljc ns-docs
description:
Per audit rf2-1itgd (U10, D6).

The seven optional-artefact wrappers each open with 20-25 lines of near-identical prose:

  Public-API wrappers for the optional X artefact (Spec Y).

  Per rf2-Z the X implementation ships in the day8/re-frame2-X Maven artefact. The core artefact MUST NOT statically :require [re-frame.X] — that would pull X and its dep onto every consumer's classpath even when no X is registered.

  The fns in this namespace look the X API up through the late-bind hook table at call time, which the X artefact populates from its own ns-load.

  Per rf2-hoiu these wrappers live here (and re-frame.core re-exports them) so core.cljc is not cluttered with optional-artefact glue. The single-import contract is preserved: users continue to write rf/reg-X after (:require [re-frame.core :as rf]).

A reader of any one file sees 20+ lines of TRUE-but-line-noise prose; trained to skim, they miss the per-file specifics that ARE worth reading.

## Scope

1. Add a paragraph to spec/Conventions.md §Artefact split (or a new section §Optional-artefact wrapper convention) describing the late-bind contract in one place.
2. Trim each core_*.cljc ns-doc to: one-sentence purpose + cross-ref to the Conventions section + any artefact-specific notes.
3. Verify the trimmed ns-doc still names the artefact name (day8/re-frame2-X) and the require ns — those are per-file specifics worth keeping.

Pre-alpha: trim freely.
close_reason: merged via PR #526 (API usability batch)

title: [usability] Fix :where symbols in :rf.error/*-artefact-missing throws to rf/<surface>
description:
Per audit rf2-1itgd (U9, M4).

Seven optional-artefact wrappers (`re-frame.core-{flows,routing,schemas,machines,ssr,epoch,http}`) raise `:rf.error/*-artefact-missing` ex-info when the late-bind hook is absent. Each carries `:where 'unqualified-fn-name`:

- core_routing.cljc:30 — `:where 'match-url` → should be `'rf/match-url`
- core_routing.cljc:44 — `:where 'route-url` → `'rf/route-url`
- core_routing.cljc:59 — `:where 'reg-route` → `'rf/reg-route`
- core_routing.cljc:87 — `:where 'route-link` → `'rf/route-link`
- core_machines.cljc:29 — `:where 'create-machine-handler` → `'rf/create-machine-handler`
- (...and ~20 others across the 7 wrapper files)

User effect: error sees `:where 'match-url` and greps their codebase for `match-url`, doesn't find anything obvious (because they call `rf/match-url`). Tighten by namespace-qualifying.

Reason strings ALREADY name `rf/match-url requires day8/re-frame2-routing` etc. — good. Just the `:where` slot needs qualifying.

## Scope

Sweep through all seven `implementation/core/src/re_frame/core_*.cljc` files. Every throw with `:where 'name` → `:where 'rf/name`.

Pre-alpha: refactor freely.
close_reason: merged via PR #526 (API usability batch)

title: [usability] Add docstrings to clear-event/-sub/-fx, clear-subscription-cache!
description:
Per audit rf2-1itgd (U6).

Four clear-* surfaces are exported from rf/ but most carry NO docstring:

- `events/clear-event` (events.cljc:245) — NO docstring
- `subs/clear-sub` (subs.cljc:96) — NO docstring
- `fx/unregister-fx` (fx.cljc:49) — NO docstring (and is renamed to `clear-fx` in core.cljc:623 — the surface-vs-underlying name divergence is itself confusing)
- `subs/clear-subscription-cache!` (subs.cljc:583) — has a docstring but doesn't say when a USER should call it

## Scope

1. Add one-paragraph docstring to each. Name: what it does, when to use, return.
2. Decide: rename `fx/unregister-fx` to `fx/clear-fx` so the impl name matches the user surface (or document the divergence explicitly).
3. Pair `clear-subscription-cache!` docstring with the canonical alternative `reset-runtime-fixture` (for test code).

Pre-alpha: rewrite freely.
close_reason: merged via PR #526 (API usability batch)

title: [causa][editor] Editor-protocol matrix for handler-coord jumps
description:
Inspired by Redux DevTools' 'Trace in Editor'. Lock 11 specifies handler-coord fallback; 007-UX-IA.md mentions 'o opens source in editor'. But there's no normative spec for which editor URI schemes Causa supports. Propose normative entry in 007-UX-IA.md or new mini-spec: vscode://file/:: (VS Code / Cursor / Windsurf forks); jetbrains://idea/navigate/reference?project=

&path=:: (IntelliJ Cursive); emacs://: (Org-Protocol); configurable per-user in Settings. Lock 11 unchanged; this fills the surface gap. Cross-ref: ai/findings/causa-sota-alternatives-2026-05-13.md §'What Causa should learn' item 1.

title: [causa][copilot] Render co-pilot citations as interactive Causa chips
description: Inspired by Portal's 'any value can be inspected by clicking'. Lock 2 ships co-pilot pull-only Q&A. When the co-pilot output includes structured data (dispatch-ids, paths, handler-ids, epoch numbers), render them as interactive Causa chips — clickable, not text. A dispatch-id in the answer opens that epoch in the event-detail panel; a path opens that slice in the app-db inspector; an epoch number jumps the scrubber. Strengthens Lock 2's 'navigator pointing at evidence' framing. Cross-ref: ai/findings/causa-sota-alternatives-2026-05-13.md §'What Causa should learn' item 10.

Clarify in 002-Time-Travel.md (or new mini-spec): pin captures (epoch × frame-db × cascade-id × user-label); pins are session-scoped (Lock 4 holds — pins do NOT survive reload); pins show as marks on the scrubber; 'Reset to pinned' rewinds. Honours Lock 4 while delivering the snapshot-graph affordance peers ship. Cross-ref: ai/findings/causa-sota-alternatives-2026-05-13.md §'What Causa should learn' item 3.

The Causa vision's question #4 ('Why is this view re-rendering?') deserves a one-click affordance. title: [test-add] template emission: deps.edn parse + scaffold sanity under all 3 substrates' generated test harness
description:
Per ai/findings/test-currency-audit-2026-05-13.md.

rf2-8oi86 #478 added a default test harness (events_test.cljs) to the generated app. rf2-r2jqo added the dev-ergonomics bundle (.editorconfig, .clj-kondo, dev/). The template test (tools/template/test/clj/new/re_frame2_test.clj) asserts:

- The expected file set is emitted.
- deps.edn references substrate-adapter coords.
- shadow-cljs.edn :test build targets :node-test and :source-paths includes "test".
- The artefact-not-on-Clojars path is gracefully skipped.

Coverage gap: the test does NOT actually RUN the emitted events_test.cljs against the generated app — it only verifies the file exists and shadow-cljs.edn is wired. A regression where:

- The test file emits with a stale ns require (e.g. references a renamed re-frame ns),
- The events_test fixture pattern is incompatible with the substrate adapter (e.g. UIx vs Reagent's reset story),
- The :node-test build fails to compile due to missing optional dep,

ships green from the static-shape assertions.

## Scope

Extend tools/template/test/clj/new/re_frame2_test.clj OR add a sibling test that — best-effort, gated on shadow-cljs being available on the host — does ONE OF:

(a) Runs  on the generated app and asserts exit 0 + the events_test deftest passes. Gated similarly to the existing  skip pattern.

(b) Statically parses the emitted events_test.cljs and asserts it requires re-frame.core (the bang-rename + views-macros cut would surface if the template's test file still imports the renamed surface — currently it's emitted from the template's own scaffold but no test guards drift between scaffold + framework).

(b) is cheaper and catches the most likely regression. (a) is fuller fidelity but slow + brittle until alpha publish.

## Constraints

- NO AI attribution.
- Pre-alpha.
notes: PR #547: static-parse test for emitted cljs scaffold across all 3 substrates — events_test.cljs ns-requires + per-symbol surface drift check (option (b))

title: [test-add] sub-cache disposal: layer-2+ disposal under deferred grace-period + hot-reload race
description:
Per ai/findings/test-currency-audit-2026-05-13.md.

rf2-f3rd #501 added layer-2+ input ref-count decrement on disposal (sub_cache_test.clj covers it). The new tests use grace-period-ms 0 (sync dispose path) for assertion clarity.

Coverage gap: the deferred-dispose path (grace > 0) interacting with the new cascade is NOT directly asserted. Specifically:

1. **Layer-2 disposal cascade under non-zero grace**: parent and inputs may all enter their grace-period windows simultaneously; cancellation semantics across the chain are nontrivial. Pre-fix this path could have been wrong without any test catching it because grace=0 short-circuited the cancellation logic.

2. **Hot-reload during pending layer-2 cascade**: reg-sub re-registration on an input mid-cascade-dispose. The hot_reload_test covers single-sub re-reg, sub_cache_test covers single-sub grace cancellation, but the COMBINATION (cascade-pending-dispose + re-reg-mid-cascade) is not covered.

3. **Disposal symmetry with on-create**: layer-2 sub's on-create incrs N input ref-counts; disposal MUST decr exactly N. Test asserts the matched-pair invariant directly (e.g. via a recording wrapper around subscribe/unsubscribe) rather than the indirect cache-state-after-dispose assertion the current tests use.

## Scope

Add 2-3 deftests to sub_cache_test.clj (or a new sub_cache_grace_test.clj) covering 1-3 above. JVM-only is sufficient — the substrate work is platform-agnostic.

## Constraints

- NO AI attribution.
- Pre-alpha.
close_reason: PRs #533 #534 #535 merged

title: [test-add] examples Playwright: mount-isolation parity across the brittle-shape set
description:
Per ai/findings/test-currency-audit-2026-05-13.md.

rf2-jymd4 #494 fixed counter-with-stories Playwright after page.reload() invalidated state-comparison assertions. rf2-gkf9 #513 landed test-harness server ownership + example mount isolation.

Other examples that exercise SIMILAR shapes (page.reload mid-spec, multi-mount state assertions, frame-scoped state) may have the same latent brittleness:

- examples/reagent/realworld/realworld.spec.cjs — multi-route SPA with auth state transitions.
- examples/reagent/long_running_work/long_running_work.spec.cjs — async cancellation timing.
- examples/reagent/managed_http_counter/managed_http_counter.spec.cjs — supersede + abort + reply flow (rf2-lxd3 changed reply behaviour).
- examples/reagent/state_machine_walkthrough/state_machine_walkthrough.spec.cjs — multi-machine state transitions.
- examples/reagent/nine_states/nine_states.spec.cjs — flow + state shape.

## Scope

Audit each spec for: (a) page.reload() patterns, (b) assertions on cross-mount state survival, (c) old supersede behaviour assumptions (managed_http_counter specifically — rf2-lxd3 cut :on-failure on supersede). Refactor brittle spots to match the counter-with-stories fix template.

Likely some specs are already clean; the audit produces either a green tick per spec or a fix per spec.

## Constraints

- NO AI attribution.
- Pre-alpha.
close_reason: PRs #527-#531 merged

title: [test-add] concurrency stress beyond single-drainer: nested dispatch + cross-frame interleave + executor jitter
description:
Per ai/findings/test-currency-audit-2026-05-13.md.

rf2-ynk7 landed a single-drainer stress test (router_drain_race_test.clj, 5000 iters @ 0 fails) for the JVM peek/pop race. The framework has OTHER concurrency-shaped surfaces that don't have stress coverage:

1. **Nested dispatch under executor jitter** — an fx handler firing :dispatch while the executor is mid-drain on a different frame. Per Spec 002 frames are independent, but the drain-lock CAS interaction across frames isn't stress-tested.

2. **Cross-frame :dispatch-sync during sibling drain** — rf2-fp97 added the warning. The deterministic single-shot path is tested (cross_frame_dispatch_sync_warn_test.clj). A burst of cross-frame :dispatch-sync while the sibling is mid-drain is not.

3. **Hot-reload race during drain** — hot_reload_test.clj covers mid-drain re-registration of handlers (single-shot). Under burst hot-reload + dispatch contention (10x dev session pattern), no stress signal exists.

4. **Mid-handler :dispatch-later resolve** — :dispatch-later with very short delays could resolve back into the queue mid-drain. Not stressed.

## Scope

Add JVM stress tests (5000 iter style, RF2_STRESS_ITERS env-gated) for at minimum 1, 2, 3 above. Mirror the router_drain_race_test.clj fixture pattern. 4 is lower-prio — file as a follow-up if 1-3 land clean.

These pin invariants pre-alpha while the runtime is settling, so future executor work doesn't silently regress.

## Constraints

- NO AI attribution.
- Pre-alpha.
close_reason: PR #532 merged

title: [test-add] Causa trace-bus consumer: extend filter vocab assertions to match rf2-97ah0
description:
Per ai/findings/test-currency-audit-2026-05-13.md.

rf2-97ah0 extended trace-buffer filter vocabulary with 9 new axes (severity, event-id, source, origin, dispatch-id, since-ms, handler-id, pred, frame). The framework-side test (implementation/core/test/re_frame/trace_buffer_test.clj) covers each axis end-to-end.

Causa is the new heaviest consumer (rf2-n6x4q Phase 1 just landed; rf2-op3bz event-detail panel pending). Its pure-fn trace-bus_test only exercises push/eviction algebra; preload_cljs_test smoke-checks the register-trace-cb! handshake. Neither asserts the new filter vocab works against Causa's buffer.

Risk: when Causa's panels (event-detail, sub-detail, fx-detail per rf2-5aw5v) begin slicing the buffer by :dispatch-id / :event-id / :severity, a bug in filter-vocab consumption ships without a unit-test catching it.

## Scope

Add JVM/CLJS tests under tools/causa/test exercising each of the 9 filter axes against Causa's trace-bus contents. Reuse the framework-side test fixture style (record fake events with the right tag shape and filter through Causa's reader fn).

This is preparatory work for rf2-op3bz event-detail panel (Phase 2) — that panel will slice by :dispatch-id, so verifying filter behaviour now reduces test-debt later.

## Constraints

- NO AI attribution.
- Pre-alpha.
close_reason: PRs #527-#531 merged

title: [story] Controls panel — deeper Malli walk for nested :map and :vector schemas
description: Story SOTA audit follow-up (P3 polish). re-frame.story.ui.controls auto-derives widgets for :string / :int / :double / :boolean / :enum but stops there. Nested :map collapsible groups + :vector repeaters are in tools/story/spec/001-Authoring.md §Schema-derivation pipeline but deferred to Stage 6 expansion (never landed). Limits the 'zero argtypes' promise for non-trivial component schemas — anything with a {:items [...]} or {:nested {...}} arg falls back to inert :text widgets. Cross-references ai/findings/story-sota-audit-2026-05-13.md F7.

title: [story] Trace × scrubber cross-reference — trace highlights events leading to scrubbed epoch
description: Story SOTA audit follow-up (P3 differentiator). The scrubber (re-frame.story.ui.scrubber) pins epoch snapshots; the trace panel (re-frame.story.ui.trace) captures events. They currently render independently. Tying them so 'scrub to epoch N, see the trace that led to that epoch + highlight the row that landed N' is a combination no JS playground has. Scope: when scrubber selection changes, the trace panel filters its buffer to events with timestamp <= selected epoch + visually marks the event whose post-effects produced the epoch. Consumes existing ratoms only — no new shell-state slot. Cross-references ai/findings/story-sota-audit-2026-05-13.md F6.

title: [story] 'Open in editor' affordance per variant + per assertion

title: [story] Actions panel — filter trace buffer to user-action dispatches
description: Story SOTA audit follow-up (P3 polish). Storybook's Actions panel separates user-action dispatches from internal cascade so designers see 'what did the user do?' at a glance. title: [spec] tools/10x/spec/008-Embedding-Contract.md — add Option-C frame-provider isolation language from rf2-tijr
description:
Per rf2-tijr (2026-05-12 architecture lock, Option C hybrid):

> 'Causa's panel renders inside the host frame for zero-config mount; STATE isolation via an internal frame-provider wrapper that re-anchors descendants to :rf/causa. Subs/dispatches/machines/app-db all isolated. Registrations use :rf.causa/* namespace prefix to avoid collision with host (registrar is process-global; frames isolate state but not the kind+id registrar key).'

011-Launch-Modes.md captures the in-app overlay + hybrid decision. **008-Embedding-Contract.md does NOT** — verified grep 2026-05-13 shows zero hits for 'isolation', 'frame-provider', 're-anchor', 'host frame', 'state isolation'.

## Action

Update tools/10x/spec/008-Embedding-Contract.md to document:
1. State isolation mechanism: internal frame-provider wraps Causa's panels; descendants re-anchor to :rf/causa frame.
2. Registry-key isolation: :rf.causa/* namespace prefix for all event/sub/effect/cofx IDs.
3. Adapter resolution: re-frame.substrate.adapter/current-adapter; per-substrate escape-hatch sites enumerated.
4. Cross-reference 011 for launch modes.

## Cross-refs

- rf2-tijr (closed 2026-05-13) — the lock
- tools/10x/spec/011-Launch-Modes.md — already captures the in-app overlay decision
- rf2-5aw5v — Causa v1.0 impl epic; this spec doc work is a prerequisite cleanup before impl dispatches
notes: Direction-set per Mike's 2026-05-13 check-in. Small spec-text addition.

title: [docs] Conventions: document the configure / set-! / per-frame-metadata partition
description:
Per API elegance audit (rf2-6abd1) Gap 1 + Mike walkthrough decision 2026-05-13: B = document the three-bucket partition, NOT pool everything under (configure).

## Current state (three orthogonal config surfaces)

1. (rf/configure {:foo bar}) — keyword-keyed fn for global runtime knobs.
2. (rf/set-schema-validator! ...) / (rf/set-schema-explainer! ...) / (rf/install-adapter! ...) — dedicated set-! fns for adapter-pluggable hooks.
3. :fx-overrides + similar per-frame metadata keys passed via with-frame opts or (reg-frame) — per-frame override metadata.

Today these are scattered; the user-facing 'where do I configure X?' question has three different answers with no documented rule.

## Decision

Document the partition in spec/Conventions.md (alongside the §Naming bang rule that rf2-wmgp8 just shipped). Three buckets with a one-line rule each:

- **configure** = process-level runtime knobs that apply globally (e.g. debug-mode, performance-instrumentation enabled).
- **set-! fns** = adapter-pluggable hooks where the consumer needs a strong reference to a specific implementation (schema validator, schema explainer, adapter).
- **per-frame metadata** = overrides scoped to a single frame's lifetime, expressed in the frame's :overrides map at creation/with-frame time (e.g. :fx-overrides for test-side fx stubs).

## Action

Edit spec/Conventions.md — add new section '## Configuration surfaces: configure vs set-! vs per-frame metadata' near the §Naming bang rule. Enumerate the three buckets with one example each, and the decision tree for slotting a new config option.

## Cross-refs

- ai/findings/api-elegance-review-2026-05-13.md (audit Gap 1)
- spec/Conventions.md §Naming (the analogous rule that rf2-wmgp8 just shipped)
- Cross-link from API.md §Configure row to this section.
notes: Direction-set per Mike 2026-05-13. Disjoint surface from the in-flight API.md batched cleanup; can dispatch in parallel.
close_reason: PRs merged 2026-05-13 batch landing

title: [docs] Document cofx-wrapping pattern more visibly (skill + Guide ch.05)
description:
Per rf2-hyh5w decision 2026-05-13: rf2-gw8j (cofx-from-sub helper) closed as won't-ship because it would legitimise subscribing-inside-handlers. The ergonomic complaint (cofx wrapping is repetitive) is real, but the fix is **better docs** not new API surface.

## Action

1. **skills/re-frame2/reference/cross-cutting/cofx.md** (or wherever the cofx pattern lives) — add a worked example showing the wrap pattern:
   - "Need a sub value in a handler? Wrap it as cofx." 
   - Show the reg-cofx form, the interceptor injection at reg-event-fx, and how the handler reads it.
   - Reference: rf2-gw8j (closed; cofx-from-sub WOULD have been the shortcut but is intentionally not shipped).

2. **docs/guide/05-coeffects.md** (or wherever coeffects are taught) — surface the cofx-from-sub idiom prominently. The chapter likely has it; verify the example is foregrounded.

3. **Cross-link** from the API.md row for `reg-cofx` to the guide chapter + skill reference.

## Pre-alpha

The audit's rationale for cofx wrapping over subscribing-inside-handlers is per-handler purity: handlers receive all inputs as part of the event coeffects map, not via implicit subscription reads. Document this principle, not just the mechanic.

## Cross-refs

- ai/findings/api-elegance-review-2026-05-13.md §Convenience-helper proposals
- ai/findings/user-ergonomics-review-2026-05-12.md §3.x
- rf2-gw8j (closed as won't-ship; this is the replacement)
- rf2-hyh5w (closed; decision bead)
notes: Direction-set. Replaces the API surface that rf2-gw8j would have shipped.

title: ci(docs): extend check_doc_slugs.py to also validate target .md files exist
description:
scripts/check_doc_slugs.py currently flags anchor-mismatches only. Per its in-script comment:

    # Missing target file is a different problem (and mkdocs'
    # own link check catches it for the rendered site).  We
    # only flag anchor mismatches.

The 2026-05-12 cross-link audit (ai/findings/docs-crosslink-audit-2026-05-12-rerun.md) surfaced a case the validator missed: docs/guide/17a-routing-reference.md:323 links to 19-where-next.md after #483 renamed the file to 20-where-next.md. The link's anchor was empty (link to file, not file#anchor), so the validator returned clean.

Suggested change: when a link has the form '[text](path/to/file.md)' (no '#anchor'), still resolve the target and emit a 'broken target' diagnostic if the file doesn't exist. Same for 'path/to/file.md#anchor' links — currently the validator skips anchor-checking when the target file is missing; it should emit a 'broken target' error instead.

This would have caught the 17a stale link automatically as part of the CI slug-validation gate (rf2-ftkz / rf2-sefq).

Discovered-from: rf2-6viod.

title: [template Q6] Verify current Tailwind major version (v4 vs v5) before locking template CSS opt-in
description:
Per the template walkthrough Q6 lock (2026-05-12): plain CSS default + `:css :tailwind` opt-in for Tailwind v4.

Mike asked during the walkthrough whether the opt-in should target Tailwind v4 or v5. As of Claude's knowledge cutoff (Jan 2026), Tailwind v4 is the current stable major (shipped early 2025: Oxide Rust engine, CSS-first config via `@theme`, dropped `tailwind.config.js`). I have no confident information about a v5.

**Action**: Before implementing the `:css :tailwind` template variant, verify:

1. **Current stable Tailwind major** via `https://tailwindcss.com/` and `https://github.com/tailwindlabs/tailwindcss/releases`.
2. **If v5 has shipped**: confirm its config model (still CSS-first? new directives?), bundle size, browser support, and migration story from v4.
3. **If v5 is in beta/RC**: weigh shipping the template against beta vs waiting for stable.
4. **CDN URL / npm coord** to bake into the generated `index.html` + `package.json`.

**Update** `ai/findings/re-frame2-template-design-2026-05-13.md §Q6` with the verified version. The doc currently says "v4" — confirm or correct.

**Pre-alpha**: ship against current stable; bumping in a follow-up is cheap.
notes:
@
**Verified 2026-05-13** via GitHub releases API + tailwindcss.com.

**Current stable major:** Tailwind v4 (no v5 exists or is in flight).
**Latest version:** v4.3.0, published 2026-05-08 (fresh — 5 days ago).
**v3.x:** still receiving back-port patches (v3.4.19, 2025-12-10) but
v4 is the lock target for the template.

**CDN (Play, dev only):**
`<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@tailwindcss/browser@4"></script>`

**npm coord (prod build):**
- `tailwindcss@^4.3.0`
- `@tailwindcss/vite@^4.3.0` (Vite path) — but for shadow-cljs the CLI
  variant `@tailwindcss/cli@^4.3.0` is more likely the right call;
  resolve in the impl bead.

**Migration story:** None. v4.3 is a minor over v4.2/v4.1. The big break
was v3→v4 a year ago (Oxide Rust engine, CSS-first `@theme {}` config,
dropped `tailwind.config.js`) — we are already targeting the v4-native
shape, so no migration concern.

**Design doc updated:** `ai/findings/re-frame2-template-design-2026-05-13.md §Q6`
now reflects v4.3.0 lock + CDN/npm coords.

Closing — version unambiguously confirmed.
@
close_reason: Verified: Tailwind v4.3.0 (2026-05-08) is current stable; no v5. Design doc §Q6 updated with CDN/npm coords. Impl bead can read locked version from rf2-gthro notes + design doc.

title: Canonicalise :frame over :frame-id in trace event :tags (drop aliasing)
description:
Per ai/findings/trace-surface-consumer-audit-2026-05-13.md Finding 7.

Spec 009 §Stable surfaces standardises on :frame as the per-frame-routing key under :tags. But Story's variant-event? reads `(or (get-in ev [:tags :frame]) (get-in ev [:tags :frame-id]))` — the :frame-id fallback hints at past drift. Spec is consistent on :frame; some emit sites in impl historically used :frame-id.

Fix:
1. Audit implementation/core/src/re_frame/**/*.cljc for :tags containing :frame-id and rename to :frame.
2. Remove :frame-id fallback in Story's variant-event? (tools/story/src/re_frame/story/ui/trace.cljs:101).
3. Audit pair2 runtime / MCP server for the same fallback pattern.
4. One-line addition to spec/009-Instrumentation.md noting the canonical name is :frame; ports MUST emit :frame, NOT :frame-id.

title: API.md: document :rf.error/no-such-handler :kind discriminator inline in error table
description:
Per ergonomics review §2.7 and elegance audit (rf2-6abd1) surface table: :rf.error/no-such-handler is one operation keyword overloading three failure modes — event dispatched with no registered handler, frame name lookup against a destroyed/missing frame, pair-frame surfaces hitting a stale id. The :kind tag discriminates (:event / :frame / :route) but spec/API.md L407 documents only the event case.

## Action

Tighten the row at L407 to inline the :kind discriminator:

| :rf.error/no-such-handler | A registrar-shaped lookup missed. Discriminated by :kind tag: :event (dispatch with no event handler), :frame (Tool-Pair surface against unregistered frame-id), :route (handle-url-change saw an unmatched URL). See [009 §Error categories — :rf.error/no-such-handler](009-Instrumentation.md#error-categories-initial-set) for per-:kind tag schemas. |

This row already exists (verified during audit) — close once verified the prose lands cleanly. Doc-only.

title: Audit recommendation: close rf2-ii6s (per-adapter start!) and rf2-gw8j (cofx-from-sub) as won't-ship
description:
Two Mike-blocked decision beads from the ergonomics review have audit recommendations from rf2-6abd1:

## rf2-ii6s — per-adapter start! (e.g. reagent-adapter/start!)

Audit recommendation: **close as won't-ship**.
- The explicit (rf/init! reagent/adapter) reads identically across adapters — a virtue.
- Convenience saves 2 chars at cost of:
  - every adapter ns adds a new exported symbol
  - docs grow a 'two ways to boot' paragraph
  - rf2-agql's explicit-only invariant is muddied
- The bundle-isolation argument from rf2-agql is structural; the convenience erodes the principle for marginal typing savings.

## rf2-gw8j — rf/cofx-from-sub helper

Audit recommendation: **close as won't-ship**; document the cofx-wrapping pattern more visibly in skills/.
- The helper would legitimise subscribing-inside-handlers — a pattern re-frame's docs avoid.
- Subs are for views; cofx are for handler input. The five-line cofx wrap is the right amount of friction to push authors toward the canonical pattern.
- If app authors have a real boilerplate problem, they can write the helper locally — it doesn't need to be framework surface.

## Action

Mike to confirm/reject the recommendations. If accepted: close rf2-ii6s and rf2-gw8j with the recommendation as close-reason.

From ai/findings/api-elegance-review-2026-05-13.md (rf2-6abd1).

title: API.md row tightening: frame-provider is Reagent component, not 'Fn'; bound-dispatcher signature mismatch
description:
Two surface-row mislabels in spec/API.md:

1. **frame-provider** (L77): rowed as 'Fn (Reagent component)'. The label 'Fn' is misleading — it's a Reagent component, not a fn callable from user code with positional args. Change to 'Component (Reagent)' for clarity; the UIx and Helix tables (L100, L120) correctly use 'Fn (UIx/Helix component)' but for the cross-runtime React-shape concept the row should communicate 'this is a component you put in hiccup'.

2. **bound-dispatcher** (L80): signature column says '(bound-dispatcher m) → (fn [event] ...)'. The actual impl (core.cljc:613-618) is no-arg: (defn bound-dispatcher [] (dispatcher)). Fix to '(bound-dispatcher) → (fn [event] ...)'.

Doc-only correction; no impl change. From ai/findings/api-elegance-review-2026-05-13.md (rf2-6abd1).
close_reason: merged in PR #495 (API.md batched cleanup) and PR #492 (bang rename); both landed in 2026-05-13 batch landing wave

title: Conventions doc: when a surface carries a bang (listener registrations) vs not (registrar / dispatch)
description:
Audit (rf2-6abd1) surfaces a naming asymmetry that's principled but undocumented:

- register-trace-cb! / remove-trace-cb! / clear-trace-cbs! / register-epoch-cb! (post rf2-1pqc) — bang.
- reg-event-db / reg-event-fx / reg-sub / reg-fx / reg-cofx / reg-frame / reg-flow / reg-route — no bang.
- dispatch / dispatch-sync / subscribe / unsubscribe — no bang.
- install-adapter! / dispose-adapter! / init-platform (ghost) — bang.
- set-schema-validator! / set-schema-explainer! / clear-subscription-cache! — bang.
- clear-event / clear-sub / clear-fx / clear-flow / reset-frame / destroy-frame — no bang.

The rule (audit's reverse-engineering):

1. **Registry-shaped registrations** (the registrar IS the side-effect abstraction): no bang. reg-* / clear-*.
2. **Listener registrations** (caller hands a fn to a global hook): bang. register-*-cb!.
3. **Adapter / platform installation** (process-level state mutation outside the registrar): bang. install-adapter!, set-schema-validator!.
4. **Dispatch and subscribe** (frame-relative side-effects but the side-effect is the 'normal mode of operation'): no bang. Same as 'IO is the program' — bang would be noise.

## Action

Add §Naming subsection to spec/Conventions.md documenting this rule. Surfaces the audit catalogued in the table above as the canonical examples. Future-additions slot into one of the four buckets.

From ai/findings/api-elegance-review-2026-05-13.md (rf2-6abd1).

title: Spec 009 — unify frame-exit machine-teardown dual-emit via :reason :parent-frame-destroyed tag
description:
Per ai/findings/trace-surface-consumer-audit-2026-05-13.md Finding 1.

Today on frame-exit machine teardown, frame.cljc emits TWO trace events per destroyed actor:
- :op-type :machine / :operation :rf.machine/destroyed-on-frame-exit
- :op-type :rf.machine.lifecycle/destroyed / :operation :rf.machine.lifecycle/destroyed

Spec 009 §:op-type vocabulary (the line discussing this) has its own future-direction note acknowledging the duplication smell. The fx-substrate's :rf.machine/destroyed event already carries a :reason tag (:rf.machine/finished / :explicit / :parent-unmount-cascade).

Fix: collapse to a single emit on the lifecycle channel with :reason :parent-frame-destroyed (or similar). Pre-alpha breaking change. Tools that subscribe to one channel and miss the other today (the smell driving this) get unified semantics.

Touch: spec/009-Instrumentation.md (drop the dual-emit prose; add :parent-frame-destroyed to the :reason enum), implementation/core/src/re_frame/frame.cljc emit site, audit consumers.

Cross-ref: ai/findings/trace-surface-consumer-audit-2026-05-13.md, parent rf2-usa3
close_reason: PRs merged 2026-05-13 batch landing

title: Spec 009 — rename :rf.frame/drain-aborted op-type from :event to :frame for lifecycle consistency
description:
Per ai/findings/trace-surface-consumer-audit-2026-05-13.md Finding 5.

Spec 009 documents :rf.frame/drain-aborted with :op-type :event (a lifecycle event, not an error). But every other frame-lifecycle event uses its own :op-type: :frame/created / :frame/re-registered / :frame/destroyed. Reserving :op-type :event for non-error lifecycle events conflicts with :event meaning 'an event was dispatched.'

Fix: rename to :op-type :frame to match the rest of the :frame/* lifecycle family. Pre-alpha — breaking but trivial.

Touch: spec/009-Instrumentation.md (§:op-type vocabulary + §Error categories + the lifecycle table), impl emit site (router.cljc), any consumer that filters specifically on this op-type (audit Story, pair2). Add MIGRATION.md note if downstream users have started consuming it.

Cross-ref: ai/findings/trace-surface-consumer-audit-2026-05-13.md, parent rf2-usa3
close_reason: PRs merged 2026-05-13 batch landing

title: Drop (rf/init!) no-arg arity — let language-level ArityException replace runtime ex-info
description:
Per rf2-agql there is no default-adapter registry: (rf/init!) with no args raises :rf.error/no-adapter-specified at runtime via ex-info. Pre-alpha: consider dropping the no-arg arity from the fn defn entirely, so the call fails at compile time (CLJS) / load time (CLJ) with a language-level ArityException.

## Argument for cutting the arity

- ArityException is more discoverable than a runtime ex-info — it fires earlier, the stack trace points directly at the user's call, IDEs flag it.
- The no-arg-form error message (core.cljc:847-851) is excellent but only fires if the user runs the code. ArityException prevents the call from ever being valid.

## Argument for keeping the arity (current state)

- The ex-info message is detailed: 'rf/init! requires an explicit adapter spec map. Require the adapter ns and pass its adapter var: (require ...) (rf/init! reagent/adapter). Per rf2-agql the default-adapter registry was removed; there is no implicit default.'
- ArityException would just say 'wrong number of args (0) passed to: re-frame.core/init!' — less educational.

## Resolution

Mike-blocked. Either:
(a) cut the arity and surface the helpful prose in the docstring + spec/006-ReactiveSubstrate.md §Adapter selection at boot;
(b) keep the arity for the prose; document the trade-off.

From ai/findings/api-elegance-review-2026-05-13.md (rf2-6abd1).
notes: Decision 2026-05-13: drop (rf/init!) no-arg arity. Reasoning: ArityException at compile time is more informative than runtime ex-info; docstring carries the educational content. Pre-alpha green-light. Hold dispatch until rf2-1pqc (in-flight, same core.cljc surface) lands.
close_reason: merged in 2026-05-13 wave

title: Spec 009 — add production-debugging guidance note (what's available when goog.DEBUG=false)
description:
Per ai/findings/trace-surface-consumer-audit-2026-05-13.md Finding 6.

Today Spec 009 §Production builds documents the elision mechanism but leaves silent the question 'so what DO I have in prod for post-mortem debugging?' A user spinning up Causa-MCP for production debugging will hit it and be surprised.

Fix: add a Spec 009 §Production debugging subsection noting:
- The trace surface is whole-surface elided; pulling the last N events from a prod session isn't supported by default.
- Apps that want prod observability use the Performance API surface (default-off, opt-in via re-frame.performance/enabled?) plus user-registered :on-error per-frame handler routed to monitoring.
- The escape hatch: apps can ship :advanced with :closure-defines {goog.DEBUG true} to keep the trace surface in prod, accepting the binary-size cost.

Doc-only, no code change.

Cross-ref: ai/findings/trace-surface-consumer-audit-2026-05-13.md, parent rf2-usa3
close_reason: PRs merged in batch landing wave 2026-05-13

title: Collapse dispatcher/subscriber and bound-dispatcher/bound-subscriber to one pair
description:
re-frame.core exports two name-pairs that do the same thing:

- (rf/dispatcher) / (rf/subscriber) — return a frame-bound dispatch/subscribe fn.
- (rf/bound-dispatcher) / (rf/bound-subscriber) — same. Literally (defn bound-dispatcher [] (dispatcher)). See core.cljc:613-623.

Per spec/API.md:
- L80-82: dispatcher / subscriber rowed under §View ergonomics with prose 'called during render → frame-bound dispatch fn'.
- L80 also rows bound-dispatcher with prose '(bound-dispatcher m) → (fn [event] ...)' — but the impl is the no-arg shape, NOT a 1-arity-m shape.

The duplication is a docs artefact. Pre-alpha: pick one pair.

## Options

(a) Keep dispatcher / subscriber, cut bound-dispatcher / bound-subscriber. Argument: shorter names; the 'bound-' prefix is redundant because the only fn that returns these is one that captures the current frame.

(b) Keep bound-dispatcher / bound-subscriber, cut dispatcher / subscriber. Argument: 'bound-' communicates the async-safety property (you can call this from a callback after the dynamic var has unwound) — the canonical Spec 002 §bound-fn / bound-dispatcher language.

(c) Keep both as canonical for different idioms — (dispatcher) for inside-render, (bound-dispatcher) for capture-and-pass-to-async-cb. Argument: matches Reagent practice.

## API.md row mismatch

L80 documents bound-dispatcher signature as (bound-dispatcher m) → (fn ...), but the impl is no-arg. Independent of which option chosen, the row needs correction.

Mike-blocked: this is a naming decision. From ai/findings/api-elegance-review-2026-05-13.md (rf2-6abd1).
notes: Decision 2026-05-13: cut bound-dispatcher + bound-subscriber; keep dispatcher + subscriber. Reasoning: shorter; the verb-form names already imply capture semantics; aliases are pure. Cross-ref ai/findings/api-elegance-review-2026-05-13.md B6. Hold dispatch until rf2-1pqc (in-flight bang rename, also touches core.cljc + API.md) lands to avoid 3-way merge conflict on shared surface.
close_reason: merged in 2026-05-13 wave

title: Cut :dom-source-annotations? configure key — its own API.md row says 'Setting this false has no effect'
description:
spec/API.md L555 documents (rf/configure :dom-source-annotations? <bool>) — and the row itself says 'historical opt-in flag; superseded by mandatory injection per Spec 006 §Source-coord annotation. Setting this false has no effect — the gate is interop/debug-enabled?'.

A no-op configure key is a footgun (user sets it, expects behaviour, gets nothing; no warning emitted) and burns the configure-key namespace. Pre-alpha — cut.

## Action

1. Strip the row from spec/API.md L555.
2. Cut the case branch from re-frame.core/configure (core.cljc:801 — actually the case has no :dom-source-annotations? branch, so check; the configure fn's case statement falls through to nil for unknown keys, which means the key is effectively already silently ignored).
3. Conformance harness: ensure no test asserts the configure call succeeds.
4. Update spec/006-ReactiveSubstrate.md if it cross-refs the configure key.

ADDITIVE-cut: removes a documented surface, but the surface has zero behaviour.

From ai/findings/api-elegance-review-2026-05-13.md (rf2-6abd1).

## Cross-refs

title: Decide: event-vector Clojure metadata as opt-channel — implement or strip
description:
Spec/002-Frames.md L359 says: "Clojure metadata on the event vector is accepted as a backwards-compat shim — see MIGRATION.md."

Grep across `implementation/core/src/re_frame/router.cljc` and `core.cljc` (the dispatch path) shows no `(meta event)` read — no implementation of the metadata channel exists. The promise dangles.

## Two outcomes

**Outcome A — Implement.**
Add a `(meta event)`-aware path in `router/dispatch!` that merges any vector-attached metadata into the dispatch envelope's opts map (the two-arg `(dispatch event opts)` form). Warn-once per call site that the metadata channel is a back-compat shim and the two-arg form is canonical.

**Outcome B — Strip the promise.**
The two-arg `(dispatch event opts)` form is the canonical mechanism per Spec 002 §The two-arg dispatch form. M-16 already deprecates `^:flush-dom` event-vector metadata — the only documented event-meta channel. Outside of that one case (covered by M-16's `:dispatch-later {:ms 0}` rewrite), there is no documented use for event-vector metadata in v2.

Outcome B is preferred — fewer dispatch paths, no shadow-channel for envelope opts, simpler runtime contract.

## Task

Decide; if Outcome B, edit spec/002-Frames.md L359 to remove the sentence and add a one-line footnote: "Event-vector metadata is not an opt-channel in v2; use the two-arg `(dispatch event opts)` form." If Outcome A, file an impl bead.

## Reference

- `ai/findings/back-compat-deprecation-audit-2026-05-13.md` §Per-surface table row 5
- Spec/002-Frames.md L331-359 (two-arg `dispatch` form)
- MIGRATION §M-16 (`^:flush-dom` removal — the one event-meta case v2 explicitly handles)
close_reason: actioned in PR #499 (batched back-compat audit cleanup); merged

title: Spec-strip: re-frame.core-legacy ns mention in spec/004-Views.md L321
description:
Spec/004-Views.md L321 says: "The CLJS reference's `re-frame.core-legacy` namespace continues to allow plain fns indefinitely; the warning is a *quality-of-life* signal, not a deprecation."

There is no `re-frame.core-legacy` namespace in `implementation/**/src/`. The actual home of the plain-fn permissiveness + warn-once is `re-frame.views.warn-once` (`implementation/core/src/re_frame/views/warn_once.cljs`).

## Task

Either:
- **Rename the spec mention** to `re-frame.views.warn-once` (matching the actual ns), or
- **Remove the sentence entirely** — the surrounding context already conveys that plain fns are permitted; the namespace-naming is incidental.

## Reference

- `ai/findings/back-compat-deprecation-audit-2026-05-13.md` §Spec scrub
- `implementation/core/src/re_frame/views/warn_once.cljs` (the actual home)
close_reason: already resolved by rf2-4lc9o PR #482; back-compat batch PR #499 only confirmed

title: Spec-clarify: :http in example fx is user-supplied, not framework-supplied
description:
`:http` is used as an example fx-id in many spec files but reads as if framework-shipped — because of brand association with re-frame's historical `:http-xhrio` libs and the un-namespaced form. The framework actually ships `:rf.http/managed` (Spec 014). `events.cljc` L69-79 *rejects* `:http` as a top-level effect-map key (per migration M-8).

## Affected files

- `spec/002-Frames.md` — L41, L101, L610-626, L748, L1075-1085
- `spec/007-Stories.md` — L86, L160, L263, L277, L363, L386
- `spec/008-Testing.md` — L22, L152-167

## Options

**Option A — One-line clarifying note at first-use sites.**
Append at the first occurrence in each file: "`:http` here is a placeholder for a user-supplied fx; the framework ships `:rf.http/managed` (see [014-HTTPRequests](014-HTTPRequests.md))."

**Option B — Rename example fx-ids to `:my-app/http` (or `:user-http`) everywhere.**
Removes the ambiguity entirely. The example domain becomes clearly user-namespaced.

Option B is preferred — examples should not use framework-adjacent un-namespaced keywords when a v2 canonical exists under a `:rf.http/*` namespace.

## Discipline

This is a doc-only spec edit. No impl changes. No bead chains.

## Reference

- `ai/findings/back-compat-deprecation-audit-2026-05-13.md` §Per-surface table row 10
- Spec/014-HTTPRequests.md (canonical `:rf.http/managed` surface)
close_reason: actioned in PR #499 (batched back-compat audit cleanup); merged

title: Document the permitted guide→spec link set
description:

- spec/Principles.md (existing permitted)
- spec/MIGRATION.md (existing permitted)
- spec/Pattern-AsyncEffect.md (PROPOSED — runnable convention, 8 occurrences)
- spec/Pattern-RemoteData.md (PROPOSED — runnable convention, 7 occurrences)
- spec/Pattern-Forms.md (PROPOSED — runnable convention, 4 occurrences)

Outside the permitted set, guide chapters should paraphrase the relevant snippet inline rather than link out.

Tasks:
1. Decide whether the proposed three Pattern-* docs qualify.
2. Write the policy into spec/SPEC-AUTHORING.md or docs/guide/README.md (author note).
3. Apply the policy retrospectively via the per-chapter surgery beads (rf2-4470 et al).

This is the prerequisite gate for the per-chapter surgery beads.
notes: Decision 2026-05-13: LOCKED. Permitted set = Principles + MIGRATION + Pattern-AsyncEffect + Pattern-RemoteData + Pattern-Forms. Ch.19 exempt (spec portal). Policy written to docs/guide/AUTHORING.md (new file). title: Guide spec-refs surgery: ch.05 + ch.06 + ch.07
description:
Surgery on guide→spec cross-references in event-pipeline cluster chapters (Coeffects / Views / Interceptors).

**Gate**: depends on rf2-1foi5 (permitted-set policy decision). Permitted set assumed: Principles, MIGRATION, Pattern-AsyncEffect, Pattern-RemoteData, Pattern-Forms. Pattern-* docs outside that set → PARA.

**Notation**: KEEP = preserve link; DROP = remove link entirely (prose stands); PARA = inline the linked snippet, remove the link.

## Chapter 05 — Coeffects (4 refs)
| Line | Target | Disposition | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| 97 | `001-Registration.md` | PARA | "(per Spec 001)" — citation; reg-* shape uniformity is the point and stated in prose. Drop the citation. |
| 97 | `010-Schemas.md` | PARA | Same line — citation for `:spec` metadata. Drop. |
| 249 | `002-Frames.md#per-frame-and-per-call-overrides` | PARA | Mid-prose forward reference to a deep-link. Paraphrase the one-line "override pattern" inline. |
| 282 | `002-Frames.md#per-frame-and-per-call-overrides` | DROP | "Further reading" bullet pointing into a Spec section. Reader who finished ch.5 doesn't need normative override grammar. |

## Chapter 06 — Views and frames (7 refs — coordinate with rf2-q2yv)
| Line | Target | Disposition | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| 76 | `004-Views.md` | PARA | "Both supported shapes are documented in Spec 004." → name the shapes inline (Form-1 / Form-2) and drop the citation. |
| 156 | `Pattern-AsyncEffect.md` | PARA | Promoted pattern, but mid-paragraph link is a deflection — paraphrase the one-line shape (registered fx as right answer for setTimeout/fetch) and remove. (Chapter-end Further-reading is fine.) |
| 166 | `Pattern-AsyncEffect.md` | PARA | Same — mid-paragraph deflection in per-frame animation note. |
| 170 | `004-Views.md#animations` | DROP | "Full normative contract: Spec 004 §Animations." Chapter has given prose; defensive citation. |
| 232 | `002-Frames.md#3` | PARA | "Full resolution chain (dynamic-var → React context → :rf/default) is specified in Spec 002 §3." Either inline the chain (already named inline) and drop, or keep chain and drop citation. PARA. |
| 310 | `002-Frames.md#frame-presets` | DROP | "Full grammar lives in Spec 002 §Frame presets." Chapter covers four presets; nothing useful behind the link. |
| 349 | `006-ReactiveSubstrate.md#source-coord-annotation` | PARA | Deep-link to source-coord contract. Ch.15 (linked on same line) covers the human story; drop the spec deep-link. |
| 353 | `012-Routing.md` | PARA | "(routing) … is in chapter 17 and Spec 012." Keep ch.17 pointer; drop Spec 012 — chapter covers it. |
| 396 | `013-Flows.md` | DROP | "Full contract: Spec 013 — Flows." Flows are introduced in two sentences here. |
| 397 | `Pattern-NineStates.md` | PARA | Not promoted. Inline the nine-state list (already done in same bullet) and drop. |

**⚠ rf2-q2yv interaction**: rf2-q2yv (ch.06 antipattern + animations) intentionally adds `spec/004` cross-links at L76 and L170. Audit classifies both PARA. The ch.06 surgery here would undo rf2-q2yv's intent. Confirm with Mike before applying L76/L170 changes — may need to keep those links as exceptions.

## Chapter 07 — Interceptors (7 refs)
| Line | Target | Disposition | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| 55 | `010-Schemas.md` | PARA | "(see Spec 010)" mid-sentence — drop the link, keep the concept. |
| 57 | `MIGRATION.md §M-21` | PARA | Migration rule citation in v1-reader callout. Let it say "the migration agent flags it" without rule-number link. |
| 98 | `002-Frames.md` (unwrap interceptor) | DROP | Mid-prose mention of where `unwrap` lives. Reader doesn't need to chase. |
| 218 | `spec/validate-at-boundary` | N/A | Code identifier (namespace), not a doc link. Skip. |
| 235 | `MIGRATION.md §M-17` | PARA | Same as L57 — migration-rule citation. Drop the link. |
| 245 | `010-Schemas.md#validation-order` | PARA | Parenthetical citation. Drop. |
| 285 | `002-Frames.md#per-frame-and-per-call-overrides` | DROP | "Further reading" pointer at chapter end. Repeat of ch.05 L282. |

Net: ch.05 = 0 KEEP / 1 DROP / 3 PARA. ch.06 = 0 KEEP / 2 DROP / 5 PARA (+ rf2-q2yv conflict). ch.07 = 0 KEEP / 2 DROP / 5 PARA.

title: [guide] Add dedicated Causa chapter after v1.0 release
description:
Held by Causa v1.0 implementation. After Causa lands as a published tool, add a dedicated guide chapter covering:

- The headline pitch (event-detail hero + causality strip + AI co-pilot, per the 13 §10 locks in \`tools/10x/spec/DESIGN-RATIONALE.md\`)
- The 16-panel inventory at a tutorial level (without re-narrating the spec)
- The hybrid launch model (in-app overlay primary; standalone-via-MCP for remote-attach)
- The "ask why and get an answer" workflow
- Cross-link to the Causa-MCP server skill once published

**Don't write this chapter until Causa actually ships** — guide content for vapourware ages poorly. When Causa v1.0 is released, this bead becomes actionable.

## Sequencing

- Held by: Causa v1.0 implementation epic (rf2-tijr → Causa-impl-epic to be filed)
- The brief mention in ch.15 §Tooling stays as-is until then; this bead is the dedicated-chapter elevation.

## Discipline

- NO AI attribution.
- Don't commit \`ai/\`/\`decision-beads.md\`/\`findings/\`.
- New chapter file in \`docs/guide/\` + mkdocs.yml nav. Pick the natural slot — likely between current ch.15 (Tooling) and ch.16 (Performance), or as ch.15a depending on prevailing convention at the time.

## Cross-references

- Causa spec corpus: \`tools/10x/spec/\`
- Causa name + decisions: \`tools/10x/spec/DESIGN-RATIONALE.md\`
- Sister sweep bead: the just-filed re-frame-10x → re-frame-causa rename sweep

title: [mkdocs] Verify Material theme search plugin enabled — mkdocs.yml has no explicit plugins: declaration
description: mkdocs.yml has no 'plugins:' declaration. The Material theme bundles a search plugin, and recent Material versions include search by default — but explicit declaration is best practice and protects against future Material version changes that flip the default. Verify the published site has a working search box (typical 'press / to search' affordance in Material); if missing, add 'plugins: [search]' to mkdocs.yml. The guide is 7000+ lines across 20 chapters — text search is the load-bearing navigation affordance for it. Audit context: ai/findings/guide-excellence-audit-2026-05-12.md (F7).
close_reason: Closed

title: [guide] ch.14 — add intra-chapter pointer after taxonomy table: skim common-scenarios on first read
description: ch.14 §Common scenarios (L240-401) runs six scenarios at 25-40 lines each, totalling ~200 lines of payload before §Testing error paths arrives. A reader who hits ch.14 because they have a specific error in front of them needs the scenario list; a reader doing a linear first-read does not. rf2-po8h covers chapter-level 'if you're skipping' callouts; this is the smaller intra-chapter scope: add a one-line pointer at L60 (after the taxonomy table) — 'if reading sequentially, skim to §Testing error paths after the taxonomy table; the six scenarios are reference for when you hit them.' Audit context: ai/findings/guide-excellence-audit-2026-05-12.md (F13).

P3.

title: Refactor release.yml deploy-leaf jobs into a matrix
description:
.github/workflows/release.yml contains 8 per-leaf deploy jobs (deploy-reagent, deploy-uix, deploy-helix, deploy-schemas, deploy-machines, deploy-routing, deploy-flows, deploy-http, deploy-ssr, deploy-epoch) that are structurally identical 80-95-line blocks differing only in:
- artefact name (string)
- :local/root path string (always '../core' or '../../core')

Total ~750 lines of copy-paste — every leaf gets the same checkout, JDK 17 setup, Clojure CLI setup, cache key, 'Install core to runner ~/.m2' step, ':local/root → :mvn/version' rewrite, and 'clojure -M:clein deploy' step.

Refactor to a single 'deploy-leaf' job with strategy.matrix.include providing per-leaf customisation (name, directory, local-root-path). Collapses to ~120 lines total.

Verification: dry-run on a release-recovery candidate; confirm every leaf publishes identically to the current per-job shape.

P3 polish; saves on maintenance burden when (eventually) adding another leaf.

title: Bump Clojure to 1.12.0 in implementation/core/deps.edn
description:
implementation/core/deps.edn pins org.clojure/clojure 1.11.1 and org.clojure/clojurescript 1.11.132.

tools/template/src/clj/new/re_frame2/{reagent,uix,helix}/deps.edn all pin 1.12.0 for the generated apps.

Wrong-way inversion: new apps scaffolded from our template run on a newer Clojure than the framework's own tests. If a 1.12 surface leaks into core (e.g. the new keyword-arg invocation syntax) the template smoke catches it but the framework's CI never exercises it.

Action: bump implementation/core/deps.edn to Clojure 1.12.0 + ClojureScript 1.12.55 (or latest). Inherit through :local/root for all other artefacts. Run JVM + CLJS + browser + elision + examples.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add LICENSE file to every published artefact (implementation/ + tools/)
description:
skills/ has 6 of 6 LICENSE files. tools/ has 0 of 5. implementation/** has 0 of 12.

The root license.txt covers the repo, and the :clein/build :license map in each deps.edn populates pom.xml — but published jars do not carry a LICENSE file in the artefact root unless added to :src-dirs / :resource-dirs / similar.

Matters for:
1. Automated license scanners (FOSSA, Snyk Licenses) that look for a LICENSE file in jar/npm-package roots.
2. Clojars artefact inspection — jar opens with no LICENSE in artefact root.

Affected artefacts:
- implementation/core, implementation/schemas, implementation/machines, implementation/routing, implementation/flows, implementation/http, implementation/ssr, implementation/epoch (8)
- implementation/adapters/reagent, /uix, /helix, /reagent-slim (4)
- tools/template, tools/story, tools/story-mcp, tools/pair2-mcp (4) — note tools/10x has no jar yet.

Total: 16 LICENSE files to add. Each is a copy of the root license.txt. Add to clein :resource-dirs (or :src-dirs) so it lands in the published jar.

P3 / pre-1.0 polish — should ship before 1.0.
close_reason: Closed

title: Skills: cross-reference :final? / :on-done from re-frame2/patterns/{boot,long-running-work,websocket}.md where lifecycle fits

Several pattern lifecycles are natural :final? use cases: Boot pattern's 'configure-then-handoff' is the canonical singleton-final shape; LongRunningWork's terminate-on-done step maps to :final?; child-machine flows under WebSocket exit cleanly via :on-done. A one-paragraph 'see state-machines/invoke.md §Final states' cross-reference in 2-3 pattern leaves would close the gap. Found in skills correctness audit 2026-05-12.

title: Skills: fix bare spec/<file>.md paths in SKILL.md files (depth-aware or redirect via SKILL-REDIRECT.md)

From skills/<name>/SKILL.md to repo-root spec/ the correct relative path is ../../spec/<file>.md. The depth-aware leaves under reference/fundamentals/ already use this discipline (e.g. ../../../../spec/009-Instrumentation.md from skills/re-frame2/reference/fundamentals/frames.md). When the skill is installed as a plugin in another repo, bare spec/ paths resolve nowhere. Options: (a) rewrite to ../../spec/<file>.md, (b) redirect every spec/ reference through SKILL-REDIRECT.md (which carries the canonical GitHub URLs). Note: rf2-5lcu may overlap — that bead is about mkdocs rewriting; this is about skill-internal references. Found in skills correctness audit 2026-05-12.

title: [clarify] Conventions :rf.frame/* row conflates gensym identifier namespace and trace-operation namespace
notes: spec/Conventions.md:17 row for :rf.frame/* conflates 'Frame lifecycle traces' AND 'anonymous gensym'd frame ids (e.g. :rf.frame/123)' — different things sharing one prefix-namespace. A user-registered frame (rf/reg-frame :rf.frame/my-test-frame ...) collides with the gensym namespace, but the prose doesn't say so. Direction: split the row into two — :rf.frame/<gensym> (identifier namespace, owned by make-frame) and :rf.frame/<operation> (trace-op namespace, owned by router/frame lifecycle); pin the linter rule.

title: [doc] Spec 002 drain! pseudocode omits destroyed-frame check that Edge cases #4 normatively requires
notes: spec/002-Frames.md:843-859 (drain! pseudocode) has no (when-not (:destroyed? (:lifecycle frame)) …) guard between dequeue and process-event!. spec/002-Frames.md:1025 (§Edge cases #4) normatively requires the check + :rf.frame/drain-aborted emit. Implementor copying the pseudocode would not implement the check. Direction: inline the check in drain! pseudocode, emitting :rf.frame/drain-aborted with :dropped-count.
