title: Tool-Pair "complete and self-contained" claim contradicted by missing write surface (pair2 reaches past listed primitives)
description:
Audit rf2-ehpu finding F8.

Quote (audit-tool-pair line 30): 'Tool-Pair line 125: "The runtime contract above is complete and self-contained. A pair-shaped tool … attaches to a running re-frame2 application using only the framework primitives listed below." This is overclaim. Pair2 reaches into (rf/handler-meta :frame frame-id) :app-db to mutate the underlying container ref (runtime.cljs line 136 — app-db-reset!); Tool-Pair lists (rf/get-frame-db ...) as the read API and never specifies a write surface, but the consumer needed one for an evolved-state-shape probe and reached past the listed primitives. Either Tool-Pair commits a write surface (e.g. a documented reset-frame-db! or a listed acceptance that (handler-meta :frame …) exposes a mutable container), or it removes the "complete" claim.'

Pair2 corroboration (audit §5): runtime.cljs line 136 — '(when-let [container (some-> (rf/handler-meta :frame frame-id) :app-db)] (reset! container v))'. Pair2 mutates the underlying container by reaching past get-frame-db into handler-meta :frame and grabbing the :app-db key. Tool-Pair never specifies that (handler-meta :frame …) exposes a mutable container; the consumer guessed.

Audit-criterion #7 (undocumented public surface): an implementation surface real consumers depend on (pair2) but the spec doesn't acknowledge or contract it. If the spec commits this as a contract (it currently doesn't), pair2 is forward-compatible; if not, pair2 will break on the first refactor that moves the container.

Resolution per audit R-L (large): (a) add (rf/reset-frame-db! frame-id new-db) to public Tool-Pair surface — pair2 needs it, story tools need it, conformance harnesses need it — gated on interop/debug-enabled?; or (b) drop the 'complete and self-contained' claim and document the (handler-meta :frame …) :app-db container access as the supported escape hatch with stability commitments.
close_reason: Stale in_progress cleanup — fix commits on origin/main per git log sweep 2026-05-13. Bead status was never flipped after PR merge.

title: Spec 009 listener-ordering: three statements disagree on whether listener invocation order is contract
description:
Audit rf2-ra02 finding F-4 (also surfaced as rf2-ehpu F5).

Quote (audit-009 line 48): '§Listener ordering carries two incompatible statements. §Resolved decisions §Listener ordering (line 670): "Order is registration order (a swap! assoc on a single atom). Tools must not depend on order." But (a) swap! assoc on a Clojure map does NOT preserve insertion order (a hash-map is unordered) — so the implementation hint is technically incorrect; (b) register-epoch-cb's §Invocation rules line 161 says flatly "Listener ordering is not contract"; (c) Tool-Pair line 163 cites "009 §Listener ordering" as authority for "Listener ordering is not contract." So the field reads "registration-order is the order, but tools must not depend on it, but also it is not contract, but also swap! assoc doesn't actually preserve registration order."'

Cross-spec drift (rf2-ehpu F5 / audit-tool-pair line 27): 009 §Subscription-and-consumption says 'every registered listener is invoked once per emitted trace event, in registration order'; 009 §Listener-invocation-rules says 'In-order'; 009 §Listener ordering says 'tools must not depend on order'. Tool-Pair line 163 cites 009 as authority for 'not contract'.

Internal contradiction per audit-criterion: spec contradicts itself; an AI implementor / tool author would write tests against three different interpretations.

Resolution per audit R-S3: 'Listener ordering is not contract; an implementation MAY iterate in any order; tools MUST NOT depend on order.' Drop the swap! assoc aside. Aligns with Tool-Pair's existing citation.
notes:
Closed by PR #254 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/254

Three Spec 009 statements disagreed on whether listener-invocation order is contract. Plus the implementation hint 'swap! assoc on a single atom' was technically wrong (Clojure maps don't preserve insertion order). Picked: ordering is NOT contract (simplest + truest framing). Swept all three statements:

- §Subscription / consumption (line 150): dropped 'in registration order'; added explicit not-contract clause.
- §Listener invocation rules (line 232): split 'in-order' into two bullets — events arrive in emission order; listener-invocation order is not contract.
- §Resolved decisions §Listener ordering (line 960): dropped the registration-order claim and the misleading swap! assoc parenthetical.

Cross-doc sweep: Runtime-Architecture.md (two refs) + Implementor-Checklist.md (one ref) also asserted 'in registration order' — reconciled. Tool-Pair line 438 cites 009 §Listener ordering — anchor preserved. Batched with rf2-sn1h + rf2-4o1c.4.
close_reason: Closed

title: reg-machine is shipped public surface but unmentioned in Spec 005 / CP-5-MachineGuide
description:
Audit rf2-goxn finding #6.

Quote (audit-005 line 21): 'reg-machine is shipped (machines.cljc:885) but unmentioned in 005 / CP-5. A real public surface used internally by spawn-fx; rf2-8bp3 plans to macro-ify for source-coord. Spec only documents reg-event-fx + create-machine-handler.'

Audit-criterion #7 (undocumented public surface): an implementation surface that pair2 / 10x v2 / users may reach for, but the spec doesn't acknowledge or contract it.

Note: rf2-8bp3 (in flight) plans to promote reg-machine to a macro for source-coord; that work should also fix the spec gap (audit §5 large bullet, line 89).

Resolution: document reg-machine in spec/005-StateMachines.md and CP-5-MachineGuide as a real public registration surface (versus the reg-event-fx + create-machine-handler form 005 currently documents).

title: Tighten Spec 000-Vision: keep ~8 Contract clauses, drop the rest, move addressee-flipped to SPEC-AUTHORING.md (resolves rf2-uuoj / rf2-ehml)
description: Decision (Mike, 2026-05-09 from rf2-ehml-review.md): Refine narrowly. Apply reviewer §6 recommendation. KEEPERS (8): C-000.13-15 capability-vs-mechanism, C-000.19 identity-primitive anti-patterns, C-000.25 capability declaration, C-000.26 PDS O(1) revert, C-000.27 artefact independence (no transitive :require core->feature), C-000.49-50 explicit-frame view + behavioural equivalence, C-000.51 substrate decoupling. REWORD: C-000.31 loosen to match prose where-possible hedge; C-000.34 inline C-000.36 caveat; C-000.49 grammatically broken second sentence. DROP all others (~43 clauses): paraphrases, addressee-flipped, premature normative content. PLACEMENT: ONE summary Contract block at the END of spec/000-Vision.md, not 16 blocks throughout. Block-style format (kept). ADDRESSEE-FLIPPED CLAUSES: C-000.20, .37-.40, .43-.44 are spec/harness-author obligations, not implementor. Move to new spec/SPEC-AUTHORING.md (or extend spec/Conventions.md if that fits the corpus better — agent picks). HARD HOLD: C-000.06 (unregistered fx MUST raise) and C-000.35 (production elision behavioural equivalence) MUST NOT land in 000 in this bead; they introduce normative content prose doesn't commit to. Need ratification in spec 002 (for .06) and spec 009 (for .35) first. Reviewer recommends those happen via separate decision beads at upstream-spec layer if at all. Filing them is out of scope for this bead. SPEC EDITS: spec/000-Vision.md (delete 43 inline blocks, add 1 summary block at end with 8 clauses, reword 3); new spec/SPEC-AUTHORING.md (or section in Conventions.md) for the 7 addressee-flipped clauses; spec/MIGRATION.md additive entry. CRITERION: per drafter's own observation - if you could write a fixture that fails when the prose's claim is violated, the prose wanted a Contract block. Apply strictly. Tests: each landed Contract clause's MUST/SHOULD must be checkable by either an existing test or a new fixture; if not, drop the clause. Additionally: append decision notes to rf2-uuoj + rf2-ehml referencing this bead as the resolution. Sources: findings/rf2-ehml-review.md (verdict, clause-level flags §5), findings/rf2-ehml-spec000-contract-draft.md (the 51-clause draft to cherry-pick from). Disposition: P3 follow-up to the rf2-uuoj pilot. Not pre-beta blocker. Direction set; dispatch.
close_reason: Merged via PR #137: 11 clauses in spec/000-Vision.md + 7 in spec/SPEC-AUTHORING.md + 2 held (C-000.06, C-000.35). Resolves rf2-uuoj + rf2-ehml.

title: Spec 011 / 009 - reconcile head-mismatch trace category with current hydration runtime
description:
Spec 011 and Spec 009 describe head-specific hydration divergence as a distinct trace operation: `:rf.warning/head-mismatch`. The current runtime routes head divergence through the generic `verify-hydration!` path and emits `:rf.ssr/hydration-mismatch`, using `:failing-id :rf.ssr/head-mismatch` to distinguish the case.

Possible necessary change: decide whether head mismatch deserves its own operation as currently specced, or whether the spec should adopt the current generic-hydration operation plus the `:failing-id` discriminator.

Evidence:
- spec/011-SSR.md:440, 467
- spec/009-Instrumentation.md:473, 588
- spec/API.md:365
- implementation/core/src/re_frame/ssr.cljc:379-416
- implementation/core/test/re_frame/ssr_end_to_end_test.clj:646-694
notes:
Closed by PR #254 — https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/254

Spec 011 + Spec 009 said :rf.warning/head-mismatch was the trace key. Runtime emits :rf.ssr/hydration-mismatch with :failing-id discriminating (:rf/hydrate for body, :rf.ssr/head-mismatch for head). Picked resolution (a) — adopt the runtime canonical shape; spec catches up to runtime since that's what users observe via 10x and similar tooling, and the discriminator pattern is more general than a per-case trace key.

Touched:
- spec/011-SSR.md: §Hydration-mismatch detection trace example now shows :failing-id :rf/hydrate; §Mismatch detection — head emits :rf.ssr/hydration-mismatch with :failing-id :rf.ssr/head-mismatch.
- spec/009-Instrumentation.md: :rf.ssr/hydration-mismatch category-table row now covers both cases via :failing-id; removed the parallel :rf.warning/head-mismatch row; recovery table split into body/head sub-rows; ssr.cljc emission list updated.
- spec/API.md: hydration-mismatch row mentions the discriminator; :rf.warning/head-mismatch row removed.
- spec/conformance/README.md + spec/conformance/fixtures/ssr-head-hydration.edn: :trace-not-emitted assertion now references :rf.ssr/hydration-mismatch + head discriminator.

No runtime change in this PR — runtime's verify-hydration! already emits the spec-aligned shape. Batched with rf2-sn1h + rf2-b4wt.
close_reason: Closed

title: Spec 011 - reconcile head hydration payload keys with current :rf/hydrate runtime
description:
The hydration payload schema and SSR conformance corpus include optional head payload keys `:rf/head` and `:rf/head-hash`, and Spec 011 says the client uses them for head-mismatch detection when present. The current `:rf/hydrate` runtime only reads `:rf/app-db`, `:app-db`, `:rf/render-hash`, and `:rf/version`; it does not read or persist `:rf/head` / `:rf/head-hash`, and the current SSR tests explicitly park head-hash under `:rf/render-hash` as a workaround.

Possible necessary change: either implement the published head-hydration payload contract, or tighten Spec 011 / Spec-Schemas / conformance so they no longer describe keys the runtime ignores.

Evidence:
- spec/011-SSR.md:64, 440-442, 467, 682
- spec/Spec-Schemas.md:642-644
- spec/conformance/README.md:231
- spec/conformance/fixtures/ssr-head-hydration.edn:2, 9, 32-35, 52-59
- implementation/core/src/re_frame/ssr.cljc:364-376
- implementation/core/test/re_frame/ssr_end_to_end_test.clj:655-666
notes:
PR: https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/257

Picked path (b) — tightened the spec to match the runtime. The :rf/head + :rf/head-hash payload keys were dropped from :rf/hydration-payload (v1) and Spec 011's head-hydration section. Head-mismatch detection rides on the unified :rf/render-hash channel; head-vs-body discrimination lives in :failing-id (canonical post rf2-4o1c.4). The dedicated head keys are reserved for :rf/hydration-payload-postv1 to land alongside reg-head when that surface ships as non-deferred.

Rationale: reg-head / render-head / active-head are explicitly deferred in MIGRATION.md; no implementation exists for the head producer side (no head emission to wire, no data-rf-head-hash); the conformance DSL has no :return-head op; the end-to-end test was already deliberately parking under :rf/render-hash as a workaround. Path (b) cleanly removes the paper contract; when reg-head lands the post-v1 payload keys can come with it.

Files touched: spec/011-SSR.md, spec/Spec-Schemas.md, spec/conformance/README.md, spec/conformance/fixtures/ssr-head-hydration.edn, spec/conformance/fixtures/ssr-head-emits.edn, implementation/ssr/test/re_frame/ssr_end_to_end_test.clj.

Tests: SSR (27/102 ✓), elision (all sentinels ✓), conformance corpus (✓ incl. :ssr/head-hydration + :ssr/head-emits).
close_reason: Resolved via PR #257 — picked path (b), tightened the spec to match the unified :rf/render-hash runtime; head-vs-body lives in :failing-id.

title: Spec 009 - reconcile fragment-change trace op name with runtime and routing spec
description:
Spec 009 still documents fragment-only routing traces under the older operation name `:route.url/fragment-changed`, but the runtime, migration table, Spec-Schemas, and current conformance fixture all use `:rf.route/url-changed` for the fragment-only case.

Possible necessary change: reconcile the trace vocabulary so Spec 009 names the same operation the runtime and the rest of the spec corpus use, or deliberately restore the older op if that is still the intended surface.

Evidence:
- spec/009-Instrumentation.md:89, 227
- spec/012-Routing.md:83, 336, 706
- spec/Spec-Schemas.md:211
- spec/conformance/fixtures/route-fragment-change.edn:4, 12, 35, 59-60
- implementation/core/test/re_frame/trace_test.clj:374-380
close_reason: Resolved via PR #162 — Spec 009 fragment-change trace op name aligned to runtime canonical (commit 6a784a8).

title: Spec 005 - decide whether dynamic machine spawn/destroy is real runtime lifecycle or trace-only
description:
Spec 005 describes dynamic actors spawned by `[:rf.machine/spawn ...]` as real runtime-managed machine instances: the runtime allocates an id, registers the handler, creates a snapshot under `[:rf/machines <id>]`, optionally dispatches `:start`, and `[:rf.machine/destroy actor-id]` tears the instance back down. The current `spawn-fx` / `destroy-machine-fx` implementation only emits `:rf.machine/spawned` / `:rf.machine/destroyed` traces and explicitly says full actor lifecycle is still the user's responsibility.

Possible necessary change: decide whether dynamic spawn/destroy is meant to be a full runtime capability or merely a trace-level placeholder. If the former, the lifecycle machinery is still missing; if the latter, Spec 005 should be tightened so it no longer promises registration/snapshot/message-routing semantics that the runtime does not perform.

Evidence:
- spec/005-StateMachines.md:1084-1171, 1801, 1883
- implementation/machines/src/re_frame/machines.cljc:963-981
close_reason: Already resolved by rf2-suue + rf2-t07u + rf2-6vmw (all 2026-05-09). machines.cljc:1705-2041 contains full lifecycle: handler registration, snapshot init at [:rf/machines <id>], :start dispatch, system-id binding, destroy mirror. spawn_registry_test.clj + 7 conformance fixtures directly assert runtime-managed shape. Audit cited stale line numbers (963-981 in trace-only era). 49/162/0 baseline.

title: Spec 005 / MIGRATION - reconcile public machine lifecycle fx ids with implementation
description:
Spec 005 §Spawning and MIGRATION §M-26 describe the canonical public lifecycle surface as `[:rf.machine/spawn ...]` and `[:rf.machine/destroy actor-id]`. The current implementation registers only the machine-internal fx ids `:spawn` and `:destroy-machine` in `implementation/machines/src/re_frame/machines.cljc`; there is no registered `:rf.machine/spawn` or `:rf.machine/destroy` alias.

Possible necessary change: reconcile the public surface with the runtime by either (a) adding the canonical public fx ids as aliases, (b) renaming the implementation to match the published ids, or (c) tightening the spec/prose so the public and machine-internal surfaces are explicitly different and the examples stop using the public ids.

Evidence:
- spec/005-StateMachines.md:1115, 1139, 1729, 1883
- spec/MIGRATION.md:1035-1036
- implementation/machines/src/re_frame/machines.cljc:963-984
close_reason: Resolved by rf2-m83v PR #175 — option (b) executed: implementation renamed to :rf.machine/spawn / :rf.machine/destroy (published id). Spec 005 + MIGRATION + Conventions + Spec-Schemas + API now consistent with impl.

title: re-frame-pair2: align parse-rf2-coord with actual data-rf2-source-coord format shipped by rf2-z7f7
description:
## Context

rf2-z7f7 (PR #135) shipped the `data-rf2-source-coord` DOM annotation. The actual emitted format (per the PR's verified DOM snippet):

```
data-rf2-source-coord="counter.core:counter-buttons:47:11"
```

Shape: `<ns>:<handler-id>:<line>:<col>` (4 colon-separated segments).

re-frame-pair2's `scripts/runtime.cljs` parser (`parse-rf2-coord`) was written speculatively before rf2-z7f7 landed and assumed:

```
'<ns>|<file>:<line>:<col>'
```

(per `re-frame-pair2/STATUS.md` known-unknown #3). The shapes differ — pair2's parser will not extract correct fields from real DOM annotations as shipped.

## Task

In `C:/Users/miket/code/re-frame-pair2/`:

1. Read the canonical format definition. Sources of truth (in re-frame2 repo at `C:/Users/miket/code/re-frame2/`):
   - `spec/006-Components.md` §Source-coord annotation.
   - `spec/Tool-Pair.md` §Source-mapping.
   - The implementation that emits the attribute (likely under `implementation/reagent/src/...` and/or `implementation/core/src/.../source_coord.cljc` — find via grep for `data-rf2-source-coord`).
   - PR #135 diff for context.
2. Verify what fields are extractable: `:ns`, `:handler-id` (new — wasn't in the original parser spec), `:line`, `:col`. Confirm format isn't variable per Form-1 / Form-2 / SSR cases.
3. Update `scripts/runtime.cljs` `parse-rf2-coord` to handle the actual format. Return all four fields. Handle missing-handler-id case if format degrades for some Reagent shapes (per the rf2-z7f7 fragment / interop fallback warning — those cases may emit with fewer segments or fall back to handler-meta lookup).
4. Update / add tests in pair2 that exercise the parser with real DOM samples. Use the snippet from PR #135 + at least one Form-2 case + one SSR case (read PR #135's test fixtures to crib representative samples).
5. Update `re-frame-pair2/STATUS.md`:
   - Remove or resolve known-unknown #3 (source-coord format) — it's now known.
   - Note any dependency on subsequent `:configure` knobs (Tool-Pair.md says the value format is opaque, so document the shape we're parsing as a contract that consumers should not depend on stability of).

## Out of scope

- Running the pair2 spike against a live re-frame2 app (that's the §8a spike work — separate effort tracked under rf2-9ra3).

## Verification

- Pair2 unit tests pass: `cd C:/Users/miket/code/re-frame-pair2 && (whatever its test command is — check package.json / SKILL.md / scripts/)`.
- Parser correctly extracts all four fields from the canonical sample `counter.core:counter-buttons:47:11`.

## Deliverable

Branch + PR in the `re-frame-pair2` repo (NOT re-frame2). If `re-frame-pair2` has no remote yet (it was created locally yesterday), commit on a feature branch and report back — Mike can decide whether to push or just keep local.

No AI attribution.

## Disposition

Pre-alpha follow-up to rf2-9ra3. Small, mechanical fix discovered while explaining pair2 status.
notes: 2026-05-09 bookkeeping: pair2-side parse-rf2-coord alignment work to land on the re-frame-pair2 repo (sibling repo, separate PR flow). re-frame2-side input is stable — data-rf2-source-coord format is locked by rf2-z7f7 (PR #135) shipped to spec/006-Components.md and Tool-Pair.md. Pair2-side merge awaiting Mike. Leaving in_progress.
close_reason: Stale-satisfied: rf2-fhz = h-macro dropped via rf2-n4um per spec/004-Views.md §h macro dropped; the V-2 expansion question is moot because the macro doesn't exist. rf2-7g2q = parser updated 2026-05-09 to canonical <ns>:<handler-id>:<line>:<col> shape per skills/re-frame-pair2/docs/TESTING.md self-attestation; bb-runnable tests at tests/runtime/parse_rf2_coord_test.clj.

title: Pilot RFC 2119 Contract blocks on spec/000-Specifications.md (draft + dual review) — rf2-ehml
description:
## Approach (decided in rf2-ehml notes)

Pilot the RFC 2119 Contract-block pattern on **spec/000-Specifications.md**. Two-phase: draft → independent dual-perspective review → Mike decides on rollout.

## Phase 1 — Drafter agent

Read:
- `spec/000-Specifications.md` (the target).
- RFC 2119 keyword conventions (MUST / SHOULD / MAY / etc).
- `bd show rf2-ehml` (parent decision bead — note prior parking in favour of empirical audit).
- `bd show rf2-7cco` (the empirical-audit pattern that landed — context for "what we're testing against").
- A handful of other spec files (002, 005) to see the prevailing prose style.

Output:
- `findings/rf2-ehml-spec000-contract-draft.md` — full revised 000 with Contract blocks added at appropriate points.
- Drafter explains in a §Designer-notes section: where they added blocks, why, what they considered adding-but-didn't.
- DO NOT modify the real `spec/000-Specifications.md`.
- DO NOT branch / PR — this is a draft for review.

## Phase 2 — Reviewer agent (dispatched separately when drafter returns)

Reviewer must be a different agent so they form an independent judgment.

Reviewer reads:
- The drafted version (`findings/rf2-ehml-spec000-contract-draft.md`).
- The original (`spec/000-Specifications.md`).
- RFC 2119.
- The empirical-audit pattern (`bd show rf2-7cco`) for the alternative-strategy comparison.

Reviewer writes findings to `findings/rf2-ehml-review.md` covering:
- §AI-implementation perspective. Does the Contract block layer reduce ambiguity? Are AI agents likelier to produce conformant impl from this version? Or does the formal layer just paraphrase prose without adding precision? Specific examples — quote sections that improved or didn't.
- §Human-readability perspective. Does the doc still read? Is narrative flow preserved? Or does the Contract block layer interrupt? Specific quotes.
- §Comparison vs the empirical-audit pattern. Which one would they recommend going forward, and why?
- §Recommendation. Roll out / refine / kill, with justification.

## Phase 3 — Mike decides

Mike reads draft + review. Decides:
- Adopt across all specs.
- Adopt only in some specs (which?).
- Refine the pattern (specific tweaks).
- Kill the pilot, stick with empirical-audit.

## Disposition

P3 — process improvement. Not pre-beta. Sequence does not gate other work.

## Recommendation (placeholder)

Wait for both phases to land before forming a view. The empirical-audit pattern is producing real drift-reduction signal already (per the rf2-7cco merge); the case for adding a second formal layer needs to clear a high bar.
notes: Refinement landed at PR https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/137 (rf2-04sm). The 51-clause draft was reduced to 11 clauses in 000-Vision (one summary Contract block at the end) plus 7 addressee-flipped clauses extracted to spec/SPEC-AUTHORING.md under the SA-N id scheme. C-000.06 (unregistered fx) and C-000.35 (production elision behavioural equivalence) were deliberately held — they need ratification in spec 002 / spec 009 first.
close_reason: Pilot complete: draft (Phase 1) + independent review (Phase 2) landed in findings/. Decision: refine narrowly. Outcome: rf2-04sm impl merged via PR #137.

title: Tool-Pair surface audit: walk re-frame-10x feature list against re-frame-2's Tool-Pair surfaces
description:
## Context

From rf2-tijr discussion: re-frame-10x v2 will consume re-frame-2's Tool-Pair surfaces (epoch-history, restore-epoch, register-trace-cb!, source-coord, etc.) instead of vendoring re-frame.

Before re-frame-2 freezes its Tool-Pair surface set for 1.0, we need to walk re-frame-10x's existing feature list and verify each feature has a viable rendering path on top of re-frame-2's current Tool-Pair surfaces — without vendoring or host-frame hacks.

Gaps surfaced by this audit influence what spec / Tool-Pair beads need to land before 1.0.

## Scope

Research-only. Write findings to `findings/rf2-tijr-toolpair-audit.md`. No code changes, no PR.

## Deliverable

1. Inventory of re-frame-10x v1 features (what each panel does, what data it surfaces).
2. Mapping of each feature → required re-frame-2 Tool-Pair surface(s).
3. Gap list — features where the required surface is missing, partial, or undecided.
4. Sizing each gap (small / medium / large) and a recommendation: file beads now (pre-1.0 blocker), defer to post-1.0, or kill the feature in v2.
5. Recommendation on the audit's most load-bearing finding.

## Disposition

P3 — research informs pre-1.0 spec work but isn't itself a blocker. Findings will be reviewed by Mike; bead-filing decisions follow.
notes: 2026-05-09 bookkeeping: top-3 audit findings have all been actioned: #1 sub-topology debugger surface -> rf2-8nzo (closed via PR #142); #2 Performance API instrumentation -> rf2-du3i (closed via PR #139); #3 :flow op-type + :rf.flow/* trace vocabulary -> rf2-2s1o (closed via PR #138). Plus rf2-90vq (PR #154) for trace category coverage. Remaining items in findings/rf2-tijr-toolpair-audit.md are stylistic / smaller-priority gaps that can be filed individually as work prompts arise. Leaving the audit bead itself open as the reference index until Mike confirms it can be closed.
close_reason: Audit complete. Top-3 recommendations all actioned: rf2-8nzo (sub-topology impl, PR #142), rf2-du3i (Performance API, PR #139), rf2-2s1o (:flow op-type, PR #138). Plus rf2-90vq + rf2-mmn5 sweep (PR #154). Findings doc remains as reference at findings/rf2-tijr-toolpair-audit.md.

title: re-frame-10x v2: repo placement + replace vendoring with multi-frame isolation
description: Refinement (2026-05-11): during alpha phase, re-frame-10x v2 lives at tools/10x/ for cohesion. Post-alpha, splitting individual tools (10x most likely) into their own repos remains an option per the original framing of this bead.
notes:
**Sub-decision 2 LOCKED 2026-05-12 (Mike): Option C (hybrid).** Causa's panel renders inside the host frame for zero-config mount; STATE isolation via an internal frame-provider wrapper that re-anchors descendants to :rf/causa. Subs/dispatches/machines/app-db all isolated. Registrations use :rf.causa/* namespace prefix to avoid collision with host (registrar is process-global; frames isolate state but not the kind+id registrar key).

**Substrate adapter resolution**: Causa uses whatever adapter (rf/init!) installed — resolved via re-frame.substrate.adapter/current-adapter. Pure hiccup needs no per-substrate code (Reagent/Reagent-slim/UIx/Helix all accept the same hiccup shape). Per-substrate switches confined to ~5 imperative escape-hatch sites (canvas refs, lifecycle hooks for memoisation / large-list virtualisation / DOM init). The rf2-9j2l adapter READMEs document each substrate's escape-hatch spelling — Causa's internal on-mount helper branches on (current-adapter) using a case statement.

No iframe; no multi-substrate; no Causa-shipped-as-its-own-substrate. Spec/006 single-active-substrate-per-process constraint preserved. Decision baked into tools/10x/spec/008-Embedding-Contract.md + tools/10x/spec/011-Launch-Modes.md if not already present (verify on next impl pass).

title: Consider Stately-style 'actor system' capability for spawned-machine coordination
description:
## Question

Should re-frame2 adopt a Stately-style 'system' surface (https://stately.ai/docs/system) for coordinating spawned machines?

## What Stately provides

- **Named-actor registry per system.** Actors get a `systemId` at spawn/invoke time; any other actor in the same system looks them up via `system.get('actorId')`.
- **System-relative addressing.** Cross-actor messaging is `sendTo(({system}) => system.get('notifier'), {type: :notify})` — the sender doesn't need a direct reference, only the name.
- **Implicit root actor → system.** `createActor(machine).start()` creates the system; descendants can be invoked/spawned under it. Only the root can stop the system.
- **System-level introspection.** `actor.system` is the entry point. Stately Studio integration for system-wide debugging is hinted as 'coming soon'.

## How this maps to re-frame2

- **Frame ≈ system.** Per-frame scoping (schemas, flows, routing, machines) already gives re-frame2 the per-instance container Stately gets from a 'system'. The frame is the natural home for an actor registry.
- **Spawned machines exist** (rf2-een2 :on-spawn data-relative, :spawn / :destroy-machine fxs in machines.cljc) but there is no named-machine discovery surface. Two spawned machines today coordinate via frame events, not point-to-point named addressing.
- **Dispatch model is broadcast-then-handled**, not point-to-point. Stately's sendTo is conceptually different from rf dispatch — adopting named-actor sendTo would be a new primitive sitting alongside dispatch, not replacing it.

## Concrete things this would unlock (if adopted)

- Named-machine lookup: (machine.system/get frame :notifier) returns the spawned machine ref.
- Targeted send: (machine.send :notifier {:type :notify}) — analogous to dispatch but addressed to a specific spawned machine, not the frame's event bus.
- Hierarchical machine introspection for Tool-Pair / epoch-history surfaces.
- Compositional patterns where parent machines spawn named children that other parts of the app message by name.

## Open questions / decisions needed

1. Is targeted point-to-point machine messaging a missing primitive, or is broadcast-then-handled-by-machine (current model) sufficient?
2. If yes — does it sit in spec 005 (machines) or warrant its own spec / EP?
3. Hierarchical actor trees: does Stately's parent/child machine model map onto re-frame2's spawned-machine concept, or is it a different shape?
4. System-level introspection: how does this interact with Tool-Pair epoch-history / register-trace-cb! surfaces?
5. Multi-frame story: re-frame2 already has per-frame scoping. Is the Stately 'system' just our 'frame', or is there a sub-system level (a system within a frame)?

## Disposition

Open question — Mike's call. Not pre-1.0 if it's a new spec/EP; could be pragmatic-add to spec 005 if it's just a named-machine lookup helper. Defer until Mike rules.
notes:
---

## Decision (2026-05-09, Mike)

**Option B** — opt-in :system-id named-machine addressing.

Implementation tracked under separate impl bead.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #136 — Stately-style actor system Option B (:system-id named addressing) + impl merged via rf2-suue.

## 2. Add examples/README.md

A top-level README in examples/ explaining how to run the examples. Likely already exists from rf2-0h9n (refresh example catalog) — this bead consolidates / updates if so.

Content:
- Where the orchestrator is (implementation/scripts/run-examples-tests.cjs).
- How to run all examples via Playwright: npm run test:examples.
- How to run a single example with shadow-cljs watch (shadow-cljs watch <id>).
- Per-example brief description (mirror examples/README.md from rf2-0h9n if it has this; otherwise add).

## Sweep targets

- implementation/shadow-cljs.edn — every 7Guis build target's source paths update from ../examples/seven_guis/<file>.cljs to ../examples/7Guis/<example>/<file>.cljs.
- implementation/scripts/run-examples-tests.cjs — spec-discovery glob picks up new locations (the rf2-kdvh glob examples/**/*.spec.cjs should already work; verify).

- spec/README.md — refresh the worked-examples row to use new paths.

After the move:
- npm run test:examples runs all 15 specs (no regression).
- Each example builds via shadow-cljs.
- Bundle isolation per rf2-p7va et al. preserved (counter still has 0 hits for unused features).
- Spec docs / guide cross-links resolve.

## Out of scope

- Don't rename the .cljs source files inside (cells.cljs stays cells.cljs).
- Don't touch the namespace identifiers (seven-guis.cells namespace stays the same — only the on-disk path changes... wait, namespace HAS to match path. Reconsider: if the file moves to examples/7Guis/cells/cells.cljs, the ns becomes cells.cells or seven-guis.cells.cells — pick a consistent rule).

## Naming question

7Guis or 7-guis for the folder? Original project is 7GUIs.com; 7Guis is the natural shortening. Mike specified 7Guis.
notes:

title: SSR head-element management surface (rf2-gr0n defer)
description:
The :head registry kind exists but no machinery — registration / rendering / active-head selection for SSR pages. Real production SSR feature; non-trivial impl. Defer post-v1.

Surface: reg-head, render-head, active-head — the trio advertised in spec/API.md and 011-SSR.md as v1 surfaces but with no impl, no test, and no fixture exercising them.

Discovered from rf2-gr0n during the API.md drift sweep — the SSR head trio was triaged as defer (not drop), since SSR-aware applications will need this. The API.md rows stay but the impl is post-v1.

title: Public init-platform — JVM-side substrate init equivalent (rf2-gr0n defer)
description:
The substrate adapter pattern (rf2-0hxm + rf2-4cb6) covers CLJS init via substrate adapter. JVM-side init for SSR / non-browser contexts may want an equivalent named entry. Currently init! covers all paths; init-platform would be a JVM-flavoured alias if pattern emerges. Defer post-v1.

A public fn that wraps current epoch-restore semantics in a fixture-shaped helper for tests. Useful for v1-style test code that called make-restore-fn to snapshot/restore between tests. Currently with-fresh-registrar and reset-runtime-fixture cover this; make-restore-fn would be a public ergonomic wrapper. Defer post-v1.

title: Promote remaining browser-only placeholder cross-spec tests
description:
Why this issue exists
The repo now has a browser-test runner, but several cross-spec tests are still placeholders that explicitly defer real DOM/browser assertions. Those cases sit on load-bearing UI/runtime boundaries rather than pure business logic.

What needs to be done
Convert the remaining placeholders in implementation/test/re_frame/cross_spec_cljs_test.cljs into real browser-run tests where feasible, especially:
1. frame destroy during render
2. plain Reagent fn under a non-default frame warning behavior
3. any remaining timer/render-path cases currently left as browser TODOs

Expected outcome
- The browser runner, not just node-test, covers these cases.
- Placeholder comments can be removed or narrowed.
- Real browser semantics become part of CI coverage for these interactions.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #166 — browser-only placeholder cross-spec tests promoted to real assertions (commit cbd06af). Halt-flagged sub-case for plain-fn-under-non-default-frame is tracked separately on rf2-d3k3.

title: Add restore-epoch tests for reactive surfaces after rewind
description:
Why this issue exists
poch_test.clj validates epoch recording and the basic estore-epoch happy/failure paths, but the coverage stops at raw app-db replacement. For Tool-Pair and time-travel debugging, the load-bearing question is whether materialized runtime surfaces observe the rewind correctly.

What needs to be done
1. Add tests showing that estore-epoch correctly updates already-materialized subscriptions.
2. Add tests for flows after restore:
- restored upstream inputs
- restored flow output paths
- no stale cached last-inputs / recompute state
3. Add route-slice coverage where a restored epoch changes :route and the observable routing state follows.
4. If there are known non-goals, pin them explicitly in tests or comments so time-travel semantics stay clear.

Current related coverage
- implementation/test/re_frame/epoch_test.clj covers app-db rewind and failure modes.
- implementation/test/re_frame/cross_spec_cljs_test.cljs has a Tool-Pair-style revert case, but not materialized subs/flows/routes after estore-epoch.
close_reason: Stale in_progress cleanup — fix commits on origin/main per git log sweep 2026-05-13. Bead status was never flipped after PR merge.

title: Revisit verifier and runtime-monitoring direction
description:
Why this issue exists
Revisit the verification / monitoring direction after the current implementation and spec work settles. The codebase already has strong ingredients for this work: conformance fixtures, pure machine-transition, structured traces, epoch history, and an AI/tool-facing spec surface. There are also concrete gaps to resolve before deeper verifier work is worth doing.
What needs to be done
1. Revisit the runtime/spec drift called out in the review:
- frame teardown vs scheduled drain race causing post-test NullPointerException
- trace contract drift between spec/API and implementation
- schema tooling surface drift, especially per-frame schemas and pp-schemas-digest
2. Evaluate a narrow formal-verification pilot around the pure seams:
- machine-transition
- compute-sub
- epoch replay / restore invariants
- routing / managed-http stale-result and retry invariants
3. Evaluate a runtime-monitoring path over trace / epoch output:
- define monitorable invariants over trace events and epoch records
- decide whether this should be an internal harness, a pair-tool feature, or a separate tool
4. Decide whether the long-term shape is:
- verified kernels with an unverified shell
- monitor-only enforcement over logs / traces
- or a hybrid approach
Notes from the review
- JVM tests passed (223 tests, 1047 assertions, 78/78 conformance fixtures) but a background drain thread still threw a NullPointerException after completion, indicating a frame-lifecycle / async-drain race worth fixing before replay/verification work builds on it.
- The repo is unusually well-positioned for this line of work because the spec is already first-class and much of the state evolution surface is data-driven and host-portable.
External sources to use when revisiting
- lemmafit: https://midspiral.com/blog/introducing-lemmafit-a-verifier-in-the-ai-loop/
- Building a React App with Formally Verified State: https://midspiral.com/blog/building-a-react-app-with-formally-verified-state/
- dafny-replay: https://github.com/metareflection/dafny-replay
- PObserve runtime monitoring: https://p-org.github.io/P/advanced/pobserve/pobserve/
Likely inspiration to assess
- external deterministic verifier loop for AI-generated logic
- verified state kernels with compiled/runtime integration
- replay / undo / authority / effect-orchestration kernels
- runtime monitoring over logs/traces against formalized invariants
close_reason: 2026-05-13 — defer to post-alpha. Originating drift gaps now resolved/in-flight (frame teardown race rf2-ynk7; trace contract drift rf2-g6ih4+rf2-97ah0+rf2-wvzgd merged; schema drift rf2-t0hq). Speculative verifier work belongs in v1.1+. External sources preserved in body for retrieval.

title: Cross-Spec doc §6 — reconcile :rf.warning/nav-fx-on-server vs :rf.fx/skipped-on-platform trace
description: Cross-Spec-Interactions.md §6 (Routing in SSR) says 'Navigation effects (:rf.nav/push-url, :rf.nav/replace-url) are no-ops on the server with a :rf.warning/nav-fx-on-server trace.' The runtime actually emits :rf.fx/skipped-on-platform (the generic platform-gate trace from re-frame.fx/handle-one-fx) — there is no nav-specific :rf.warning/nav-fx-on-server trace. Surfaced from rf2-bhhu while pinning §6 — the new fixture asserts what the runtime does. Either the prose should be updated to cite :rf.fx/skipped-on-platform (the generic trace is sufficient — the :fx-id tag distinguishes nav from other platform-gated fx) or a dedicated :rf.warning/nav-fx-on-server trace should be added. Recommendation: prose update; the generic trace is enough.
close_reason: Stale in_progress cleanup — fix commits on origin/main per git log sweep 2026-05-13. Bead status was never flipped after PR merge.

title: Cross-Spec doc §7 — reconcile route-not-found / error-projector contradiction
description: Cross-Spec-Interactions.md §7 (Route-not-found under SSR) says 'The error projector does NOT fire — route-not-found is normal control flow, not an error.' But the runtime (re-frame.routing/handle-url-change) explicitly emits :rf.error/no-such-handler, and re-frame.ssr/default-error-projector-fn maps that error category to {:status 404 :code :not-found ...} — the :ssr/error-known-mapping fixture pins exactly that behaviour. The new cross-spec-route-not-found-ssr-status.edn fixture (filed under rf2-bhhu) reflects the runtime, not the prose. Either the prose is wrong (and should be updated to say 'the projector DOES fire — its known-mapping turns route-not-found into the locked 404 public-error') or the runtime is wrong (and route-not-found should bypass the error projector with a dedicated 404 path). Recommendation: prose is wrong — the projector firing IS the wire-level signal that produces the 404; bypassing it would mean every host re-implements the not-found-→-404 mapping. Update §7 accordingly.

title: Machines :after must no-op under :ssr-server / :platform :server
description: Cross-Spec interaction #4 (Machines × SSR) specifies that :after is a no-op under :ssr-server: 'The entry action skips timer scheduling; the synthetic timer-elapsed event is never queued; the request frame is destroyed before any timer could fire anyway.' Today re-frame.machines/transition-with-actions schedules :after timers unconditionally — there is no platform-aware gating in machines.cljc. Surfaced from rf2-bhhu (cross-spec fixtures) — the rule is documented but the runtime doesn't yet enforce it. Resolution: thread the active frame's :platform into create-machine-handler (or read it from :rf/frame on the machine spec the handler stamps), skip the :rf.machine.timer/scheduled emission and the timer-arming branch when :platform = :server, and emit :rf.machine.timer/skipped-on-server (or similar) trace for visibility. Then author the corresponding cross-spec fixture (cross-spec-after-no-op-under-ssr.edn) and flip §4 to Pinned.

title: Conformance runner: realise reg-machine bodies for cross-spec error fixtures
description: Cross-Spec interactions #11 (machine action throws), #12 (effect handler throws inside machine action's :fx) and #17 (machine error inside SSR) cannot be pinned by conformance fixtures today because the runner only supports direct :machine-transition calls. The runtime's :rf.error/machine-action-exception handling lives inside re-frame.machines/create-machine-handler — which is only reached when the machine is registered via reg-machine and dispatched as an event. Surfaced from rf2-bhhu (cross-spec fixtures). Resolution: extend conformance_test.clj's realise-handlers to call rf/reg-machine for :fixture/registry :machine entries (with bodies in :fixture/handlers :machine), and add the missing :throw op to the machine-action body interpreter. Then author cross-spec-machine-action-throws.edn / cross-spec-machine-fx-handler-throws.edn / cross-spec-ssr-machine-error.edn and flip Cross-Spec-Interactions §11/12/17 to Pinned.

title: MkDocs build: stage examples/ + root README.md, or rewrite cross-tree refs (rf2-qvlf follow-up)
description:
rf2-qvlf agent surfaced 33 remaining build warnings after the guide-spec rewrite. The remaining patterns reference ../../examples/* and ../../README.md from inside guide/ and spec/ source files. The mkdocs build doesn't stage examples/ or the root README into docs/, so those links 404 on the published site.

## Two paths

1. **Stage more.** Update .github/workflows/docs.yml to stage examples/* and root README.md alongside the existing spec/ staging step. Adjust mkdocs.yml nav if examples should appear in the site.

2. **Rewrite to GitHub URLs.** Add another rewrite case in mkdocs_hooks.py: ../../examples/X -> https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/blob/main/examples/X (and similarly for README). Source stays GitHub-friendly; published site links go to the GitHub source-tree view.

Path 2 is simpler and probably right — the examples are CLJS apps, not docs to render. Embedding them in the site as docs would require a different treatment (probably extract their READMEs only, not the full source).

Path 1 is right if examples have READMEs we want users to read on the published site as a 'gallery of worked examples' page. That's a more substantial design move.

Recommend Path 2 unless there's a desire for a published examples gallery.

Discovered from rf2-qvlf (PR #98).

title: Guide ch.08 references :platforms #{:server} fx-gating; verify impl coverage
description:
Chapter 08 (v1 migration) §'Three v1 surfaces removed' lists :platforms #{:server} and :rf.fx/skipped-on-platform as part of the new surface.

The rf2-x13a guide-audit agent didn't find these in the impl test surface they checked. Could be:
- Already implemented in a sibling impl ns the agent didn't sweep.
- A forward-promise the guide is making.
- Missing impl that needs to land before v0.0.1.beta.

Investigation: grep -rn ':platforms.*:server\|:rf\.fx/skipped-on-platform' implementation/. If empty, file an impl bead. If present, the guide is correct.

Discovered from rf2-x13a guide audit.
notes:
Verified impl coverage. Both surfaces are present and exercised:

- :platforms #{:server} appears throughout SSR fxs in implementation/src/re_frame/ssr.cljc (lines 444, 464, 474, 484, 494, 512). :platforms metadata generally is plumbed via implementation/src/re_frame/fx.cljc :48 (active platform set on each fx).
- :rf.fx/skipped-on-platform fires from implementation/src/re_frame/fx.cljc :190, implementation/src/re_frame/routing.cljc :895/903/928. The trace event is asserted in implementation/test/re_frame/trace_test.clj :155, 305-308; implementation/test/re_frame/fx_test.clj :226, 232-247; implementation/test/re_frame/cross_spec_cljs_test.cljs :182-185.
- The epoch summariser (implementation/src/re_frame/epoch.cljc :273, 287, 299) maps :rf.fx/skipped-on-platform onto :outcome :skipped-on-platform.

Note: the bead said chapter 08 "Three v1 surfaces removed" lists :platforms #{:server} and :rf.fx/skipped-on-platform. A grep of docs/guide/08-from-re-frame-v1.md shows it does NOT name them (confined to chapters 03 and 07). So no doc fix is required either — the audit-bead's chapter pointer was loose.

Closing as no-op verified.

title: Please simplify the public documentation map and clarify ownership boundaries
description: The review found that the front-door documentation still asks readers to absorb multiple navigation taxonomies, while the ownership matrix still leaves some surfaces spread across several specs. Please simplify the public entry path and tighten ownership wording so there is a more obvious canonical home for views, subscriptions, and related companion material. This would make the documentation system reflect the same simplicity goals that the runtime design emphasizes.
notes:
Constraint (Mike, 2026-05-09): README's narrative is OFF LIMITS. Don't restructure it. Any additions to README for doc-map simplification go at the BOTTOM under a new section ## Information for AIs.

## Updated scope

### Concern 1 — front-door tiering

Don't change docs/guide/README.md's voice or spec/README.md's content. Don't tier 'human entry' vs 'AI entry' by editing the existing READMEs.

DO add a single new section at the bottom of repo root README.md titled ## Information for AIs. Content:
- A compact bulleted reading order for AI implementers / spec checkers / porting agents.
- 'Spec is the artefact' restated, with a direct pointer to spec/README.md.
- 'Pattern docs are worked examples; spec/* is normative; docs/guide/ is narrative.'
- Cross-link to spec/Conventions.md for naming / packaging / ownership rules.
- Maybe a short cheat-sheet: 'where does <surface> live?' → spec/Ownership.md.

This section is the AI-readable map the rest of the repo doesn't currently have. Narrative readers can skip it; the heading announces 'this is for tools'.

### Concern 2 — Ownership tighten

Spec 'canonical home' for each surface tightened. Non-canonical references in other specs become brief cross-links. This concern is unaffected by the README constraint — it's all spec-level.

## Out of scope

- Don't edit README's existing prose (the narrative).
- Don't restructure docs/guide/.
- Don't change spec/README.md's existing prose.
- Don't introduce new doc tiers (e.g. 'Tier 1 / Tier 2'). The narrative-vs-AI split is what we have.

title: Repo-wide sweep for broken cross-links in docs + code
description:
Sweep every Markdown file and source file in the repo for broken cross-references. Several recent reorgs (rf2-9hft promoted docs/specification/ -> spec/; rf2-heo2 removed get-view; rf2-d0pi swept reg-view; rf2-n4um dropped h; etc.) likely left stale links and anchor references in their wake.

## Scope

Markdown files:
- README.md
- spec/*.md (all)
- spec/conformance/README.md
- docs/guide/*.md (all chapters)
- examples/*/README.md
- implementation/README.md
- CLAUDE.md
- Pattern-*.md
- Any other .md surfaced by find

Code-level path/anchor references:
- Source-file docstrings that mention paths (e.g. 'see docs/specification/...').
- Comments referencing spec sections.
- Any test-fixture file path that's hard-coded.

## What's a 'broken' link

- File paths that don't resolve (e.g. ../specification/X.md after the promote-to-spec rename).
- Anchor references (#some-section) where the section heading was renamed or removed.
- External URLs that 404 (low priority — only flag obvious dead links like to-be-rewritten github URLs).
- References to symbols that have been removed (e.g. rf/get-view, rf/h).

## Process

1. Use a markdown link checker if available (lychee, markdown-link-check). Or write a small CLJS / bb script that parses each .md, extracts links/anchors, validates them.
2. For source-code references, grep for known stale paths:
   - 'docs/specification/' -> should be 'spec/' now (rf2-9hft)
   - '/get-view' -> should be '/view' (rf2-heo2)
   - 'rf/h ' or '(h ' -> removed (rf2-n4um)
   - 'reg-view :' (keyword form) -> defn-shape now (rf2-d0pi)
   - 're-frame.alpha' -> dissolved (rf2-7cb2)
3. For each broken reference, fix in place. Where the target exists under a new path/name, redirect. Where the target was removed entirely, remove the reference.
4. Where the broken link is in a closed bead description / historical notes — leave alone (don't rewrite history).

## Output

A report listing broken links found + fixed (one PR with all the fixes).

## Sequencing

Land AFTER all the in-flight reorgs (rf2-4v73, rf2-kdvh, rf2-zuwz, etc.) so the sweep catches everything in one pass. Otherwise the sweep gets stale immediately.

If any in-flight work surfaces ADDITIONAL broken-link patterns we don't know about (e.g. an agent moves files we didn't anticipate), this sweep catches them.

## Out of scope

- Don't restructure docs (that's other beads).
- Don't add new cross-references; only fix broken ones.

## Closes / related

- Catches drift from rf2-9hft, rf2-heo2, rf2-d0pi, rf2-n4um, rf2-7cb2, etc.
- Adjacent to rf2-au1b (Playwright final-pass) — both are end-of-cycle verification beads.
notes: 2026-05-13: ran full diagnostic. check_doc_slugs.py reports 0 broken links across 237 scanned files. Spot-checked README files (root + implementation/ + examples/) and mkdocs.yml nav — also clean. mkdocs build --strict surfaces 126 pre-existing warnings about cross-tree links from docs/guide → spec/ and tools/, all matching the same pattern: mkdocs only treats docs/ as its corpus so all sibling-tree references via ../spec/X.md and ../../tools/X/spec/Y.md are flagged. These resolve correctly at filesystem level (script confirms) and are not link-rot from the reorgs the bead was filed against. The reorgs (rf2-9hft promote, rf2-heo2 get-view removal, etc.) have already been swept clean. No PR to file — bead deliverable is verification + report. Leaving in_progress per task instruction; suggest closing as no-op once Mike reviews.
close_reason: Verification pass: check_doc_slugs.py scans 237 markdown files repo-wide; 0 broken links / 0 broken anchors. No fixes needed. The reorgs that triggered this bead (rf2-9hft promote-to-spec, rf2-heo2 get-view removal, rf2-d0pi reg-view sweep, rf2-n4um h removal, rf2-7cb2 alpha dissolve) were all cleaned by their landing agents. The 126 mkdocs --strict warnings are structural (docs/ vs spec/ tree separation by design), not link rot. CI guard at scripts/check_doc_slugs.py covers future drift.

title: Final-pass: Playwright verifies every example end-to-end after all reorgs land
description:
Final verification pass via Playwright. After all the example-related reorganisations and migrations land, run the full Playwright suite against every example and confirm end-to-end behaviour holds.

## Goal

Empirical proof that every example works as a user-facing app, not just that it compiles. Each example's Playwright spec exercises the golden path + at least one edge case, asserts user-visible state.

## Scope

Run npm run test:examples and verify ALL specs pass:

- counter
- todomvc
- realworld (post-rf2-o8t6 Spec 014 retrofit)
- login (post-rf2-wdu8 :http -> :rf.http/managed)
- nine-states
- routing
- managed-http-counter
- ssr
- state-machine-walkthrough
- 7guis: temperature, flight-booker, timer, crud, circle-drawer, cells

If any spec fails:
1. Identify root cause: example drift / spec drift / framework regression.
2. Fix in place.
3. Re-run.
4. Repeat until 100% pass rate.

## Success criteria

- All Playwright specs pass on a clean main checkout.
- No timer-style flakes (run 3-5 times to confirm stability).
- The orchestrator (implementation/scripts/serve-and-run-examples-tests.cjs) discovers every spec.
- Test report shows N specs run / N passed (no skipped, no flaked).

## Out of scope

- Don't add new specs; only verify existing ones.
- Don't refactor specs; if they need rewrites, file follow-up beads.
- Don't introduce new example-time behaviours; only verify documented behaviours.

## Sequencing — at the END

Land AFTER:
- rf2-4v73 (realworld test harness extraction)
- rf2-kdvh (playwright spec-per-example move)
- rf2-zuwz (examples best-practice audit)
- rf2-o8t6 (realworld Spec 014 retrofit)
- rf2-wdu8 (HTTP migration sweep)
- rf2-kx74 (examples per-substrate reorg, if it lands first)
- rf2-3yij / rf2-2qit (substrate adapters, if those land)

Effectively: this bead is the final-pass before a v1.0 cut, validating that every example demonstrates correct re-frame2 idioms in working state.

## Closes / related

- Sibling to rf2-zuwz (audit) but behavior-focused, not structure-focused.
- Last-line-of-defense check before cutting any release.

title: examples/playwright/ is misnamed — move specs into their respective example dirs
description:
examples/playwright/ is structurally odd. It's not an example; it houses Playwright spec files for the example apps. The dir-name suggests 'this is an example called playwright', which it isn't.

## Recommended fix

Option A: spec-per-example. Each spec moves into the example it tests:

  examples/counter/counter.spec.cjs
  examples/todomvc/todomvc.spec.cjs
  examples/seven_guis/cells/cells.spec.cjs
  ... etc.

Self-contained: example source + spec + README all in one dir. The orchestrator (implementation/scripts/serve-and-run-examples-tests.cjs) globs examples/*/*.spec.cjs (or examples/**/*.spec.cjs depending on nesting).

Alternative (Option B): top-level tests/e2e/ for all Playwright specs. Less self-contained but cleaner separation. Use only if Option A creates orchestrator complexity that's not worth the everyday navigation win.

## Sweep

- Move every spec file from examples/playwright/<name>.spec.cjs to its example dir.
- Update implementation/scripts/serve-and-run-examples-tests.cjs to glob the new locations.
- Update any package.json scripts that reference the old paths.
- Update any docs (README, examples/README.md if present) that link to the spec dir.

## Coordination with rf2-kx74

rf2-kx74 (examples reorg into per-substrate) was going to keep examples/playwright/ but tier it by substrate. That preserves the misnaming. Better: combine these two beads — the per-substrate reorg AND the spec-per-example move land together:

  examples/
    reagent/
      counter/
        core.cljs
        counter.spec.cjs
      todomvc/
        core.cljs
        todomvc.spec.cjs
    uix/
      counter/
        core.cljs
        counter.spec.cjs

If Mike wants this combined with rf2-kx74, this bead becomes a sub-task of that one. If standalone (cheap immediate cleanup before any substrate reorg), it ships first.

## Out of scope

- Don't change spec content; only move location.
- Don't delete examples/playwright/ until every spec has been moved + orchestrator updated + tests pass.

## Sequencing

Two paths:
1. Standalone: land before rf2-kx74. Cheap mechanical move; Mike sees the cleaner layout immediately.
2. Combined with rf2-kx74: pairs with substrate reorg; one PR.

Mike's call. (1) is faster; (2) is one less reshuffle.

title: Substrate adapter: Helix
description:
Add Helix as a Spec 006 substrate adapter, parallel to Reagent and UIx. Smaller scope than the UIx adapter — Helix has fewer users and serves the 'absolute minimal React wrapper' niche.

## Scope

### Adapter at re-frame.substrate.helix

Implement the Spec 006 adapter contract for Helix. Same shape as the UIx adapter:
- Subscription via useSyncExternalStore-wrapping hook.
- Component-local state via useState / useRef.
- Render scheduler delegated to React.
- Context via react/createContext + useContext.
- (rf/init! helix-adapter/adapter) at app entry.

### reg-view emission

Parallel macro: reg-view-helix that emits defnc. Same reasoning as UIx — separate macro per substrate; combine later only if necessary.

### Smaller curated example port

Smallest viable demonstration:
- counter

That's enough to prove the substrate adapter works. If demand surfaces, expand later.

## Triggers / sequencing

- Depends on rf2-kx74 (examples reorg) being settled — examples/helix/counter/ goes in the new substrate-tiered layout.
- Should land AFTER the UIx adapter so the Spec 006 contract has been exercised by two substrates and clear gaps surface.

## Cost estimate

~3-5 days of agent work. Adapter is similar shape to UIx; the example sweep is much smaller.

## Closes / related

- Reinforces the 'spec is substrate-agnostic' claim with a third reference.
- Pairs with rf2-kx74 (examples reorg) and the UIx-adapter bead.

## Why P3 (not P2)

UIx covers the modern-hooks-CLJS-React audience. Helix is the further validation, not the load-bearing port. Schedule when capacity allows; not blocking.
notes:
rf2-z9n1 resolved as (a)+(α) [2026-05-09]: same DOM-injection mandate as UIx (per rf2-3yij note). Helix adapter MUST ship :data-rf2-source-coord injection. See rf2-z7f7.

---

## Decisions (Mike, 2026-05-10)

Direction: port the UIx adapter (rf2-3yij PR #151) to Helix. All 8 UIx decisions transfer (same React/hooks substrate model):

1. Hook naming: `use-subscribe`.
2. Frame propagation: shared `re-frame.substrate.context` (already factored).
3. No auto-inject — components call `use-subscribe` directly.
4. `reg-view` macro stays Reagent-only; Helix users call `reg-view*`.
5. Source-coord DOM annotation via adapter wrap.
6. Render flush: `flush-views!` wrapping React's `act()`.
7. Smoke-test set: counter + login.
8. Helix version target: latest (1.x line). No back-compat.

Pre-alpha — no migration concerns.
close_reason: Already merged via PR #176. Helix substrate adapter shipped.

title: examples/ reorganisation: per-substrate top-level dirs (paired with first non-Reagent substrate)
description:
When the first non-Reagent substrate ships (UIx most likely; possibly Helix), reorganise examples/ into per-substrate top-level dirs.

## Trigger

Don't dispatch until the substrate-adapter bead is also being scheduled. The rename is paired with the new substrate's first canonical example so it lands as one consistent move; otherwise it's premature churn.

## Target layout

  examples/
    reagent/
      counter/
      todomvc/
      realworld/
      seven_guis/
      state_machine_walkthrough/
      nine_states/
      routing/
      ssr/
      managed_http_counter/
      login/
    <new-substrate>/
      counter/
      todomvc/
      managed-http-counter/
      frame-provider-demo/
      (curated subset; not 1:1 mirror)
    playwright/
      reagent/
        <existing specs moved here>
      <new-substrate>/
        <new specs>

Helix gets a third dir only if real demand surfaces.

## Sweep

- Move all existing examples/<name>/ into examples/reagent/<name>/.
- Move examples/playwright/ specs into examples/playwright/reagent/.
- Update implementation/scripts/serve-and-run-examples-tests.cjs path entries.
- Update implementation/shadow-cljs.edn build target paths.
- Update docs that link to examples (README, guide chapters, spec docs that reference examples).
- Update any cross-references in code (test files, example READMEs).

## Process

1. First commit: rename existing examples to examples/reagent/. Sweep references.
2. Second commit: add examples/<new-substrate>/counter/ as the first port — proves the substrate adapter works end-to-end.
3. Build out the new-substrate set incrementally over follow-up beads.

## Constraints

- DON'T share view code across substrates initially. Duplication is honest about substrate differences.
- DO share events/subs/fx if they're 100% substrate-agnostic (and don't import Reagent / UIx / Helix). For the v1.0 phase, prefer self-contained trees over clever cljc/per-substrate-views layouts.
- DON'T 1:1 mirror every Reagent example. Curated subset per non-canonical substrate.

## Cross-references

- rf2-vf8m (long-term repo split) — orthogonal but may interact if the substrate adapters ship as separate artefacts.
- Future bead: feat: substrate adapter for UIx (when filed, that bead is the trigger to schedule this reorg).

## Out of scope

- Don't touch implementation/ or docs/specification/ layouts. Examples-only reorg.
- Don't pre-organise before a non-Reagent substrate is greenlit.
close_reason: Resolved via PR #160 — examples reorganised under per-substrate top-level dirs (Reagent + UIx) (commits 3c80cc1, 4a0bf2c, 9acde89, d9a0479, 1d0d5e3).

title: [bug] reg-event-* silently ignores :interceptors when given as metadata-key inside the metadata-map
description:
Surfaced by rf2-w3vn during 7guis #6 (Circle Drawer) work. The example had  placed inside a metadata-map (the docstring/metadata position of reg-event-db), where it was silently ignored — undo/redo was effectively never enabled until the agent recognised the typo and moved interceptors to the positional slot.

Two paths:
1. Lint/warn at registration time when :interceptors appears in the metadata-map. Compile-time warning if possible; runtime warning otherwise.
2. Honor :interceptors in the metadata-map AS WELL as the positional slot. Same key, two valid positions.

Path 1 is the surface-shrinking choice (one canonical position). Path 2 is the user-friendly choice (Postel's law — accept either).

Recommend path 1: warn at registration. Either is mechanical to implement.

Discovered from rf2-w3vn (PR #86).

title: examples/realworld: extract inline tests to a proper test harness
description:
examples/realworld/ has tests mixed inline with the normal application code. Cleanup task: move tests to a proper test harness directory and verify they still pass.

## Scope

1. Audit examples/realworld/ for inline test code:
   - deftest forms inside source ns
   - testing/comment blocks containing assertions

title: RFC 2119 Contract block pilot in 002-Frames.md (resolves rf2-gpva)
description:
Per rf2-gpva Option B: pilot RFC 2119 normative phrasing in 002-Frames.md. Place the Contract block at the END of the doc (not the top) so the narrative reader keeps the existing reading experience.

## Scope (deliberately narrow)

Only docs/specification/002-Frames.md is touched. Other specs stay in narrative voice for now.

## Add a §Normative Requirements section at the end

Sketch:

  ## Normative Requirements

  This section enumerates the contract that an implementation MUST satisfy
  to claim conformance with Spec 002. The narrative above provides the
  rationale; the bullets below are the contract.

  Definitions (per RFC 2119):
  - MUST / REQUIRED / SHALL — absolute requirement.
  - MUST NOT / SHALL NOT — absolute prohibition.
  - SHOULD / RECOMMENDED — there may exist valid reasons in particular circumstances to deviate, but the full implications must be understood.
  - SHOULD NOT — same, in the negative.
  - MAY / OPTIONAL — truly optional; conformance-neutral.

  ### N-1. Frame-id is a keyword
  An implementation MUST treat frame-ids as keywords. Conformance: dispatch-envelope.edn

  ### N-2. Default frame is :rf/default
  An implementation MUST use :rf/default as the frame-id when no frame is specified. Conformance: dispatch-envelope.edn

  ... (continue extracting normative claims from the narrative above)

The agent's job:
1. Read 002-Frames.md end-to-end and extract every claim that's normative (where 'normative' means an implementation must do this thing).
2. Classify each as MUST / SHOULD / MAY based on:
   - MUST: contract-breaking if not honoured. e.g. dispatch envelope shape, drain semantics, frame isolation.
   - SHOULD: strongly recommended; deviation needs justification. e.g. dispatch ordering preferences, cofx convention.
   - MAY: optional or conformance-neutral. e.g. additional metadata slots, performance optimizations.
3. Write each as a numbered N-X bullet with one short paragraph + cross-link to its conformance fixture.
4. Add a §Definitions header explaining the keywords (per RFC 2119 §1).
5. Footnote-link each normative claim in the narrative back to its Contract entry: e.g. paragraphs that say 'the dispatch envelope shape' get a [N-1] footnote.

## Cross-references

- Conformance fixtures under docs/specification/conformance/fixtures/ — many already exist for Spec 002. Cross-link each Contract entry to the fixture(s) that pin it. Fixtures may need a small metadata addition (a :rf.spec/normative-id slot) so the cross-reference is mechanical. If that's heavy, just put the fixture filenames in the Contract entry's text.

## Out of scope

- Other spec docs stay in narrative voice. Only 002-Frames.md is touched.
- DO NOT touch 000-Vision.md or any spec-as-argument doc.
- Don't sweep cross-references in OTHER specs that point at 002-Frames; their existing references should still work because the narrative-side anchors don't move.

## Verification

- Read 002-Frames.md end-to-end after the edit. Narrative reading experience preserved (no Contract block at the top jarring the reader).
- §Normative Requirements section is mechanically grep-able for normative-id (N-1, N-2, ...).
- Each Contract entry has a conformance-fixture link.
- Spell-check, link-check.

## Deliverable

PR titled 'spec: 002-Frames Contract block (RFC 2119 pilot, rf2-gpva)'. No AI attribution.

After merge, observe for a few weeks. If observed value is real (no new ambiguity beads against 002, conformance-fixture cross-referencing is useful), file follow-up beads to scale to other specs (rf2-rfc2119-004-views, etc.). If no value, leave the pilot in place but don't scale.

## Closes / related

- Resolves rf2-gpva (decision: Option B, pilot in one doc with Contract block at the end).
- Future scale-up beads will be filed only if the pilot earns its keep.
notes: Reviewer §6 recommendation applied at PR https://github.com/day8/re-frame2/pull/137 (rf2-04sm). 11 keeper clauses landed in 000-Vision; 7 addressee-flipped clauses moved to a new spec/SPEC-AUTHORING.md (SA-N ids); 31 paraphrase / premature-normative clauses dropped; 2 (C-000.06, C-000.35) held back pending spec-002 / spec-009 ratification per the reviewer's §5 flag.
close_reason: Decision recorded (refine narrowly via rf2-uuoj review; applied via rf2-04sm / PR #137 merged); don't scale to other specs without empirical-audit driving it. See findings/rf2-ehml-spec000-contract-draft.md + findings/rf2-ehml-review.md.

title: Migration prompt: scan target project's deps and bump to latest released re-frame2
description:
The migration agent prompt should include a deps-update step. When migrating a v1 project to v2, examine the target's deps and update the re-frame coordinate to the latest released version of re-frame2 (if a release is available).

## Scope

The migration prompt likely lives in MIGRATION.md (or a separate agent-instructions file). Verify; check whether a deps-update step is already present.

If absent, add a step that instructs the agent to:

1. Inspect the target project's dependency files. Common shapes:
   - deps.edn — look in :deps and any :aliases ../:extra-deps for re-frame coordinates.
   - project.clj — look in :dependencies.
   - shadow-cljs.edn — look in :dependencies.
   - bb.edn — for Babashka tooling.
2. Identify any re-frame coordinate. Common shapes today:
   - re-frame/re-frame {:mvn/version "1.x.x"}
   - re-frame {:mvn/version "1.x.x"}  (older shorter form)
   - [re-frame "1.x.x"]  (lein vector form)
3. Look up the latest released re-frame2 version. Source TBD — once published, likely Maven Central / clojars. The agent's heuristic:
   - If the artefact label has been resolved (rf2-5sqd) to re-frame/re-frame v2.x: bump the version field to the latest v2.x.
   - If the artefact has been published as day8/re-frame-2: replace the coord entirely.
4. If no released re-frame2 version is available yet (pre-publication): leave the dep alone but flag in the migration report that the user must manually update once a release lands.

## Coupling with rf2-5sqd

This bead's wording depends on the rf2-5sqd outcome (artefact name). Either resolve in this bead's text up-front, or leave parameterised and update after rf2-5sqd lands.

## Sequencing

Should land after rf2-5sqd (so the prompt can name the actual coord). Until then, file with a placeholder / leave OPEN.

If pre-rf2-5sqd-decision, the bead's resolution is just 'add the step in a way that handles either artefact-name outcome' — i.e. detect re-frame at any of the known coord shapes, replace with whatever the published v2 artefact ends up being.
notes:
rf2-5sqd resolved (2026-05-09): artefact is day8/re-frame-2. The migration prompt's deps-bump step now has a concrete target:

- Detect any of: re-frame/re-frame {:mvn/version ...}, [re-frame "..."], re-frame {:mvn/version ...}.
- Replace with day8/re-frame-2 {:mvn/version <latest-released>}.
- If no released v2 yet, leave dep alone and flag in migration report.

title: Reconcile get-view vs view across spec and source (rf2-yl9n follow-up)
description:
rf2-yl9n introduced (re-frame.core/view id) as the runtime-lookup handle for registered views, per the rf2-iyzm Form-2 resolution. The implementation adds `view` as an alias of the existing `get-view`, and the spec corpus documents `view` in the new normative sections (Spec 001 §query API, Spec 004 §Calling a registered view, Conventions §Render-tree shape vs runtime lookup, Cross-Spec-Interactions §21).

The pre-existing `get-view` references throughout the spec corpus and source were NOT swept in rf2-yl9n. They remain valid (the function is unchanged) but the corpus now describes two names for the same handle, which is drift waiting to happen.

## Scope

Decide one of:

1. `view` is the canonical name; `get-view` is the deprecated alias (keep working, mark as deprecated, sweep references).
2. `view` and `get-view` are interchangeable aliases (document as such; pick one for examples; leave both indexed in API.md).
3. `view` is the canonical name; `get-view` is removed in a future major.

Once decided, sweep:

- docs/specification/004-Views.md — §"How registered views are used in hiccup", §"get-view — the canonical post-registration lookup", §"Alternative forms", §"The h macro", §"Resolved decisions §The h macro's expansion strategy", §"Hot-reload behaviour for re-registered views"
- docs/specification/Construction-Prompts.md — examples (rf/get-view ...) in CP scaffolds
- docs/specification/AI-Audit.md — references in the views-call-syntax discussion
- docs/specification/API.md — current row reads "view (alias of get-view)"; collapse to one row reflecting the chosen canon
- implementation/src/re_frame/core.cljc — the docstrings on `get-view` and `view`

## Sequencing

After rf2-yl9n PR merges. Mechanical sweep; no design uncertainty once Mike picks one of the three options.

## Discovered from

rf2-yl9n implementation. The original task's bead text spelled the lookup as `(rf/view id)` while the spec corpus had `(rf/get-view :id)` everywhere. rf2-yl9n added `view` as the documented form and an aliased fn; this bead reconciles the rest.
notes:
Resolved (Mike, 2026-05-08): Option 3 — remove get-view entirely. Per Mike: 'This is a new code base so we don't have to worry about deprecating anything.'

view is canonical and only. get-view is removed from impl + spec corpus. Implementation bead filed.

title: [bug] :sub-runs :result-changed? always true when sub recomputed
description:
Surfaced by re-frame-pair2 (rf2-9ra3) during port. The :sub-runs projection in :rf/epoch-record records :result-changed? for each sub recomputation — but the value is always true when the sub recomputed. The flag carries no information beyond 'this sub ran'.

Likely the value-equal recompute suppression (rf2-719e) handles the not-changed case by suppressing the :sub/run trace entirely — so any :sub-runs entry IS a changed result by construction. The :result-changed? slot is dead.

Either:
1. Remove :result-changed? from :sub-runs entries (it's structurally always true under current semantics).
2. Re-purpose :result-changed? to carry useful info (e.g., :input-changed? distinguishing 'input-graph fired but value-equal' vs 'inputs propagated novel values').
3. Document that :result-changed? is structurally true under current contract; consumers can drop the slot.

Discovered from re-frame-pair2 STATUS.md.

title: [bug] :effects projection captures only warning/error outcomes — successful fx attribution requires walking :trace-events
description:
Surfaced by re-frame-pair2 (rf2-9ra3) during port. The :rf/epoch-record :effects projection currently records only warning/error outcomes per fx; successful fx executions don't appear in the projection. Consumers wanting per-event :effects attribution have to walk the raw :trace-events stream and re-derive.

Either:
1. Make :effects record success outcomes too (trace event → projection entry on every dispatched fx, regardless of outcome).
2. Document the projection's current contract explicitly: success is implicit; only failure surfaces. Add a separate projection or helper for users wanting full attribution.

Discovered from re-frame-pair2 STATUS.md.

title: Spec-Schemas: :get-event-arg signature describes path-vector but impl + corpus use single-key
description:
Spec-Schemas.md (rf/handler-body-dsl) describes :get-event-arg as: [:tuple [:= :get-event-arg] :int [:vector :any]] -- (get-in (nth event N) path).

But the conformance impl (re-frame.conformance/resolve-value) and the conformance corpus (ssr-hydrate.edn) use the single-key form: [:get-event-arg N :key], not a path vector. After rf2-xb5o lands, the canonical form is [:get-event-arg N :key] / [:get-event-arg N :key default-val].

Update Spec-Schemas.md so the schema and prose match the impl + corpus + README:
  [:tuple [:= :get-event-arg] :int :keyword]                            ;; [:get-event-arg N :key]
  [:tuple [:= :get-event-arg] :int :keyword :any]                       ;; [:get-event-arg N :key default-val]

And the prose row: '(get (nth event N) :key)' or 'with optional default-val'.

Surfaced during rf2-xb5o implementation.

title: Conformance DSL: split :event-arg key-access into :get-event-arg (resolves rf2-pz9f)
description:
Per rf2-pz9f Option (a): make :event-arg's 3rd element always default-for-nil. The keyword-key-access overload moves to the existing :get-event-arg op.

## Changes

### Fixture migration

grep -rn '\[:event-arg [0-9] :' docs/specification/conformance/fixtures/ — every match is a candidate for key-access. Verify intent (read each fixture; if it expects to extract a key from a map result, migrate; if it expects a keyword as default-for-nil, leave).

NOT a migration:

implementation/src/re_frame_2/conformance.cljc (or wherever resolve-value lives — find via grep) — remove the type-dispatch branch on :event-arg's 3rd arg. The 3rd arg is now always default-for-nil. :get-event-arg is the only path for key-access.

### Docs

docs/specification/conformance/README.md — document both forms explicitly:
- [:event-arg n] / [:event-arg n default-val]: default-for-nil only.
- [:get-event-arg n :key] / [:get-event-arg n :key default-val]: key-access (with optional default).

Remove any prose describing the prior runtime-type-dispatch overload.

- Existing tests that exercise :event-arg with keyword 3rd-arg expecting key-access: migrate to :get-event-arg.
- Add fixture asserting [:event-arg 0 :some-keyword] returns the keyword as default (no longer key-access) when the resolved arg is a map containing that key.
- Add fixture asserting [:get-event-arg 0 :some-key] does the key-access.
